EDCS-05500684.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tritius, son of Tanginus, with Amoena as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500689.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Macrio annorum XXV hic est situs sit tibi terra levis soror faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05500689.translation: Quintus Caecilius Macrio, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sister had this made.
EDCS-05500689.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Macrio, commissioned by his sister, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500694.text_edition: \n Viriatus Tancini filius hic situs est\n
EDCS-05500694.translation: Viriatus, son of Tancinus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500694.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Viriatus, son of Tancinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500703.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II imperatori III consuli II proconsuli patri patriae optimo fortissimo providentissimoque principi ex argenti pondo X Decimo Iulio Celso Petronio Nigro IIviris decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05500703.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, the most excellent, most brave, and most provident prince, from ten pounds of silver, by decree of the decurions, by Decimus Julius Celsus Petronius Niger, duovir.
EDCS-05500703.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from an Italian municipality, dating to his reign (193-211 AD).

EDCS-05500705.translation: To Quintus Norbanus Capito, son of Quintus, aedile, duovir. Sulpicia Fausta, mother-in-law, and Iulia Quintilla, wife.
EDCS-05500705.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Norbanus Capito, aedile and duovir, by his mother-in-law and wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500706.text_edition: \n Cornelia Gai filia Uxamensis Argaelorum Materna hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05500706.translation: Cornelia Materna, daughter of Gaius, from Uxama, of the Argaeli tribe, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500706.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Materna, identified by her father and tribal origin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500734.translation: Gaius Allius Syrianus, father, and his wife, Allia Maxuma, daughter of Seranus, saw to the construction of this monument for Gaius Allius Quadratus, quaestor and member of the college of VIIIviri.
EDCS-05500734.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their son, Gaius Allius Quadratus, who held the offices of quaestor and VIIIvir, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500752.text_edition: \n Cn(aeo) Avito Saeco Lucius Aufidius Celer et Cornelia Flavina sacerdotes atiutorio sic parentum imperatore Antonino Pio Augusto II et Tineio Sacerdote II consulibus\n
EDCS-05500752.translation: Lucius Aufidius Celer and Cornelia Flavina, priests, [dedicated this] in the lifetime of their parents, during the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius Augustus, in his second consulship, and Tineius Sacerdos, in his second consulship.
EDCS-05500752.summary: A dedication made by priests Lucius Aufidius Celer and Cornelia Flavina, with a specific consular dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (139-161 CE).

EDCS-05500756.text_edition: \n Suttunio deo sacrum Lucius Aufidius Masculinus sesquiplicarius propria pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05500756.translation: Sacred to the god Suttunius. Lucius Aufidius Masculinus, sesquiplicarius, had this constructed at his own expense.
EDCS-05500756.summary: A votive inscription by Lucius Aufidius Masculinus, a soldier of the rank sesquiplicarius, dedicating a monument to the god Suttunius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500763.translation: To Caturonus, son of Tancinus, aged 7 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you, father Tancinus.
EDCS-05500763.summary: A tombstone for a young boy named Caturonus, erected by his father Tancinus, expressing the common wish for a light grave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500774.translation: Samacia, daughter of Perecatus, from Amocis, aged 14 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500774.summary: A tombstone for a young girl named Samacia, daughter of Perecatus from Amocis, who died at the age of 14, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500775.translation: Aemilius Marcellus, son of Marcus, from Arcobrigensis, aged 45 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Titus Peregrini and Crescens had this placed.
EDCS-05500775.summary: A tombstone for Aemilius Marcellus, a man of 45 years, with the monument erected by Titus Peregrini and Crescens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500776.translation: Avita, daughter of Avitus, from Cauria, aged 20 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. [Son?] Ruf[---] had this placed.
EDCS-05500776.summary: A tombstone for Avita, daughter of Avitus from Cauria, who died at 20 years old, with a partial inscription for a son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500778.translation: Longinus, son of Camalus, from Cauria, is laid here.
EDCS-05500778.summary: A simple tombstone recording the burial of Longinus, son of Camalus, from Cauria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500780.translation: [Ta]ncinus, son of Lacarus, aged 30 years, from Cauria, by his will. Tancina, daughter of Tancinus, is laid here.
EDCS-05500780.summary: A tombstone for Tancinus, son of Lacarus, who died at 30, buried according to his will, with his sister Tancina also mentioned, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500781.translation: Annia, daughter of Iucundus, aged 40 years. Albinilla, daughter of Albinus, aged 10 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Saturninus, son of Cilus, saw to its construction by will.
EDCS-05500781.summary: A tombstone commemorating two individuals, Annia and Albinilla, with the burial arrangements made by Saturninus according to his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500782.translation: Pisira, daughter of Cancilius, placed this stone for her daughter Apinae, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05500782.summary: This is a funerary marker (cippus) erected by a mother for her deceased daughter, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-05500783.translation: Avita, freedwoman of Silon and Vegetus, lies here; Marcius took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500783.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Avita, a freedwoman, and indicating that Marcius was responsible for her funerary monument.

EDCS-05500784.translation: Grovus, son of Medamus and Bassus, from the fort of Verium, aged 25, lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500784.summary: This inscription marks the burial of Grovus, a soldier (implied by 'castellum') who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05500785.translation: Bovanna, daughter of Caenonis, aged 50, and Bassus, son of Fuscinus, saw to the making of this.
EDCS-05500785.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Bassus for Bovanna, indicating their relationship (possibly spouses or family).

EDCS-05500786.translation: Sunua, daughter of Albon, mother, [erected this] for her daughters Camira, daughter of Adorus, and Paugende, daughter of Talabus, and Avela, daughter of Calaetus, [---]
EDCS-05500786.summary: This inscription likely records a funerary monument set up by a mother for her deceased daughters, with the mother's name partially preserved.

EDCS-05500788.translation: Flavinus, son of Flavius, aged 18; his wife Pomponia(?) took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500788.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavinus, erected by his wife, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-05500789.translation: Fuscinus, son of Fuscus Sextus, aged 13; his father Fuscus took care to have this made for his son.
EDCS-05500789.summary: A tombstone for Fuscinus, a young boy, erected by his father.

EDCS-05500792.translation: To Lucius Gutamus; Dobiterus, Vegetus, Vitulus, Murrius, sons of Medamus, [---]
EDCS-05500792.summary: An inscription listing several individuals, possibly dedicating something to Lucius Gutamus, with several names appearing to be patrons or patrons' sons.

EDCS-05500793.translation: [For his] wife, Caresius, son of L., made this.
EDCS-05500793.summary: A funerary inscription indicating a husband erected a monument for his deceased wife.

EDCS-05500794.translation: Flaccus [erected this] for Sunua, daughter of Cilicus, and for Camalus, son of Flaccus, who lies here.
EDCS-05500794.summary: This inscription records Flaccus dedicating a monument for his son Camalus and for Sunua, likely his wife.

EDCS-05500795.translation: Sunua, daughter of Useitus, 50 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500795.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Sunua, daughter of Useitus, at age 50, with a common epitaph wishing the earth to be light upon her.

EDCS-05500796.translation: Tertia, daughter of Nigrus, 12 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Coria, daughter of Capito, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500796.summary: A tombstone for Tertia, daughter of Nigrus, aged 12, arranged by her mother Coria, daughter of Capito.

EDCS-05500797.translation: [...], 45 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Titus Turellius Flavus [erected this] to his wife.
EDCS-05500797.summary: A tombstone recording the death of an unnamed woman aged 45, erected by her husband Titus Turellius Flavus.

EDCS-05500799.translation: To Lucius Valerius Severinus, of the tribe Quirina. Valeria Marcia, daughter of Marcus, [erected this] to her husband.
EDCS-05500799.summary: A dedication by Valeria Marcia to her husband, Lucius Valerius Severinus, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-05500800.translation: Venicca, daughter of Pisirus, 9 years old. Her father arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500800.summary: A tombstone for Venicca, daughter of Pisirus, aged 9, commissioned by her father.

EDCS-05500802.translation: To Vitellia Tertulla, daughter of Silonis. Antistia Vitalis [erected this] to her mother.
EDCS-05500802.summary: A dedication by Antistia Vitalis to her mother, Vitellia Tertulla, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-05500805.text_edition: \n Titanus Tongi filius annorum XI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Tongius pater fecit\n
EDCS-05500805.translation: Titanus, son of Tongus, 11 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father Tongius made [this].
EDCS-05500805.summary: A tombstone for Titanus, son of Tongus, aged 11, erected by his father Tongius.

EDCS-05500806.translation: Venustus, freedman of Vegetus, lies here. Niger arranged for this to be placed at his own expense. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500806.summary: A tombstone for Venustus, freedman of Vegetus, arranged by Niger, with the wish that the earth lie lightly upon him.

EDCS-05500808.translation: Boutia, daughter of Tanginus, 40 years old, and Vitalis, son of Fronto, 20 years old, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both. Pisocia, daughter of Cantona, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500808.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of Boutia (40) and Vitalis (20), arranged by Pisocia, daughter of Cantona.

EDCS-05500810.translation: Nero, son of Agrippa, 28 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother gave [this] to her son.
EDCS-05500810.summary: A tombstone for Nero, son of Agrippa, aged 28, erected by his mother.

EDCS-05500829.translation: Lucius Aemilius of the Qariqi tribe, a man from Clun, aged 36, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500829.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Aemilius, a man from Clun who died at 36, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500856.translation: Titus Titasidius of Norba lies here.
EDCS-05500856.summary: A tombstone for Titus Titasidius of Norba, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500857.text_edition: \n Vitulus Malgeini filius annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Domesticus fratri de suo faciendum curavit L\n
EDCS-05500857.translation: Vitulus, son of Malgeinus, aged 35, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Domesticus had this made for his brother at his own expense.
EDCS-05500857.summary: A tombstone for Vitulus, son of Malgeinus, erected by his brother Domesticus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500866.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attia Galatia, mother, made this at her own expense for her dearest daughter Attia Avita, daughter of Aulus, aged 24.
EDCS-05500866.summary: A funerary dedication by Attia Galatia for her daughter Attia Avita, who died at 24, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500868.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXVIII consul XIII pater patriae terminus Augustalis inter Mirobrigam Valutam et Bletisae vallem\n
EDCS-05500868.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 28th year, consul for the 13th time, father of the country, the Augustan boundary marker between Mirobriga, Valuta, and the valley of Bletisa.
EDCS-05500868.summary: An imperial boundary stone demarcating territory and bearing the titles of Emperor Caesar Augustus, dating to his 28th tribunician power year (equivalent to 87/88 AD).

EDCS-05500869.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 28th year, consul for the 13th time, father of the country, the Augustan boundary marker between the people of Bletisa, Mirobriga, and Salamanca.
EDCS-05500869.summary: An imperial boundary stone listing administrative divisions and bearing the titles of Emperor Caesar Augustus, dating to his 28th tribunician power year (equivalent to 87/88 AD).

EDCS-05500872.translation: Of Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Vespasian, Domitian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, emperor for the 2nd time, father of the country, consul for the 8th time, designated consul for the 9th time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500872.summary: An inscription recording building work or a dedication commissioned by the local council (decurions) in honor of Emperor Domitian, dating to his second consulship and tribunician power (likely 82 AD).

EDCS-05500873.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Pertinax Augustus, from the town council of Urunum, for 5 pounds of silver.
EDCS-05500873.summary: A dedication of 5 pounds of silver by the town council of Urunum to Emperor Septimius Severus Pertinax, likely dating to his reign (193-211 AD).

EDCS-05500880.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Julius Capito of Salamanca, aged 70. Julia Rusticana, his most devoted sister, had this made. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500880.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Julius Capito, erected by his sister Julia Rusticana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500881.translation: Lucius Accius Reburrus of Termes, aged 16, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Accius Reburrus and Atilia Clara had this made for their beloved and devoted son.
EDCS-05500881.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Accius Reburrus, erected by his parents Accius Reburrus and Atilia Clara, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500882.translation: Celsidius Albinus the father and Atilia Albina the mother had this made for their daughter Celsidia Serena, aged 10. Here she lies; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500882.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Celsidia Serena, erected by her parents.

EDCS-05500884.translation: To Julia Bassina, for her husband, who was kind.
EDCS-05500884.summary: A funerary dedication for a husband, erected by his wife Julia Bassina.

EDCS-05500885.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Here lies Julia Caesias, aged 40. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500885.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julia Caesias, likely erected by her family.

EDCS-05500886.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Thetis had this made for her husband, Gaius Julius Narcissus.
EDCS-05500886.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia Thetis for her husband, Gaius Julius Narcissus.

EDCS-05500888.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octav...
EDCS-05500888.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication to someone named Octav...

EDCS-05500906.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitia Attia made this for her husband, Lucius Annianus Placidas of Caesearobriga, aged 40, aedile, quaestor, and twice duovir, who was the best of husbands.
EDCS-05500906.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Annianus Placidas, who held several magistracies, erected by his wife Domitia Attia.

EDCS-05500907.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Here lies Domitia Attia of Caesarobriga, aged 27. May the earth lie lightly on you. Annii Tagana and Paulinus, sons, had this made.
EDCS-05500907.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitia Attia, erected by her sons.

EDCS-05500909.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Valerius Carcius, for his wife Sextilia Marcellea, daughter of Marcus, from Clunia, aged 18.
EDCS-05500909.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextilia Marcellea, erected by her husband Caius Valerius Carcius.

EDCS-05500910.translation: To the departed spirits. For Antonius Severus of Segisama, for Allia his mother, for Severa his sister, for Valeria his wife, for Severinus his son, aged 21, and for himself, aged 78. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-05500910.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Antonius Severus, his family members, and himself, indicating it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-05500911.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maternius Maternianus the heir had this made for his friend Lucius Antonius Rufinus, veteran, aged 49, who deserved well. Here he lies; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500911.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran named Lucius Antonius Rufinus, erected by his heir and friend Maternius Maternianus.

EDCS-05500915.translation: To the departed spirits. Ofelia, daughter of Rufinus Apina, erected this.
EDCS-05500915.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ofelia, erected by herself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500917.text_edition: \n Flaccus Ambati filius annorum LV hic situs est\n
EDCS-05500917.translation: Flaccus, son of Ambatus, aged 55, lies here.
EDCS-05500917.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Flaccus, son of Ambatus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500918.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia, daughter of Caelus Flavius, aged 25. Emuria, her mother, saw to its construction. She lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500918.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Flavia, daughter of Caelus Flavius, with her mother Emuria as the dedicator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500927.translation: To the departed spirits. Marius Lupercus, aged 33. Marius Castrensis, from his own resources, saw to its construction for his brother.
EDCS-05500927.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marius Lupercus, with his brother Marius Castrensis as the dedicator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500928.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Minicius Philadelphus, aged 61. Marcus Minicius Severus saw to its construction for his father.
EDCS-05500928.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Minicius Philadelphus, with his son Marcus Minicius Severus as the dedicator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500930.translation: To the departed spirits. Ramnia, aged 22. She lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500930.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Ramnia, aged 22, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500931.translation: To the departed [spirits]. Satur[...], son of Satur[...]. His wife, Marcus Co[...], aged [...], lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500931.summary: This funerary inscription, with significant lacunae, records the burial of a woman whose husband's name might be Marcus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500938.translation: To Gaius Antonius, son of Quintus [?]. Apolastus, aged 40. Antonius Phiaixa[?] and TOP[?] saw to its construction. She lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500938.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Apolastus, son of Gaius Antonius, with Antonius Phiaixa and TOP as dedicators, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500944.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Palphurius Laetinus, son of Marcus Palphurius Iasus, aged 48, lies here. Valeria Afra saw to its construction for her husband, the deceased.
EDCS-05500944.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Palphurius Laetinus, with his wife Valeria Afra as the dedicator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500954.translation: To the Holy God, Iovius Solutor. Baebius Crescens willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05500954.summary: This votive inscription records Baebius Crescens fulfilling a vow to the deity Iovius Solutor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500955.translation: To the departed spirits. Severa Mania, daughter of Lucius, who lived X[3] years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Severina, daughter of Severus, her mother, had this made.
EDCS-05500955.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severa Mania, set up by her mother Severina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500958.translation: Annius, son of Arcon, is buried here.
EDCS-05500958.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Annius, son of Arcon, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501027.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, by order of Furnia Turraniana, freedwoman of Gaius Furnius.
EDCS-05501027.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by a freedwoman named Furnia Turraniana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501028.text_edition: \n Publio Cincio Papiria Rufo Augusta militi legionis X Publius Cincius Papiria Tuscus patri suo et sibi per se de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05501028.translation: Publius Cincius Papiria Tuscus had this made for his father, Publius Cincius Papiria Rufus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, and for himself, at his own expense.
EDCS-05501028.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Publius Cincius Papiria Tuscus for his father, a soldier, and himself, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501717.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Barbatus, a slave who lived LI years, pious to his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. And Iunia Optatina, LXXV years old, pious to her own.
EDCS-05501717.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Barbatus, a slave, and Iunia Optatina, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502477.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming an individual, Galerio Valerio, possibly of senatorial or equestrian rank, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502586.translation: Cornelius Diogenes made this for Lucius Cornelius Paternus, aged LX years, his patron.
EDCS-05502586.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a freedman, Cornelius Diogenes, for his patron, Lucius Cornelius Paternus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600005.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 19th time, consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, rebuilt [this].
EDCS-05600005.summary: This is a building inscription recording the rebuilding of a structure by Emperor Hadrian, dated to his 19th tribunician power and 3rd consulship (circa 138 AD).

EDCS-05600011.translation: [Gaius] Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul designate for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, [built/repaired] 4 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600011.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording public works by Gaius Caesar (Caligula), indicating his titles and a measurement of 4 miles, likely from his reign (37-41 AD).

EDCS-05600026.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius, Pius, Germanicus, Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 8th time, acclaimed Imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, restored [this] for the 72nd time.
EDCS-05600026.summary: This is a comprehensive imperial titulature inscription recording the restoration of a structure by Emperor Septimius Severus, detailing his many honorific titles and ethnic conquests, likely dated to circa 202 AD.

EDCS-05600027.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, in his fifth year of tribunician power, consul for the third time, restored this. (Built) 102.
EDCS-05600027.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a structure by Emperor Hadrian, likely dating to his fifth tribunician power and third consulship.

EDCS-05600029.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan, grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, in his fifth year of tribunician power, consul, restored this.
EDCS-05600029.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a structure by Emperor Hadrian, noting his fifth tribunician power and consulship.

EDCS-05600038.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored this [structure] (CXVI).
EDCS-05600038.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a structure, identified by the number CXVI, by Emperor Trajan, likely in the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600040.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the third time, restored this [structure] (CXIIX).
EDCS-05600040.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a structure, identified by the number CXIIX, by Emperor Hadrian, likely in the period 120-128 CE.

EDCS-05600339.translation: In honor of Titus Rutilius Gallus Tuscillianus, son of Quintus Rutilius, nephew of Titus Manlius Martialis, their friend arranged this. Under the care of Lucius Paccius Marcianus and Lucius Gellius Tutor. Lucius Paccius Basileus, Publius Rutilius Antigonus, Titus Manlius Eutyches, Titus Manlius Eutychio, Titus Meclonius Cassius, Publicius Alexander Laetilianus of Balsium.
EDCS-05600339.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Titus Rutilius Gallus Tuscillianus by friends, with specific individuals listed as overseeing the work, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05600340.translation: To Titus Manlius Faustinus Balsensis, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, Faustina, daughter of Titus, his sister, to her most devoted brother, twice duovir, by decree of the decurions, a banquet was given.
EDCS-05600340.summary: This inscription records a banquet given by a sister, Faustina, to her brother, Titus Manlius Faustinus, who held the office of duovir, likely in a Roman colony.

EDCS-05600351.translation: Lucius Coranius Bubbus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is laid here.
EDCS-05600351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Coranius Bubbus, stating his name, filiation, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05600383.translation: Boutius, son of Loucinus, paid his vow to Jupiter Solutorius.
EDCS-05600383.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Boutius to Jupiter Solutorius.

EDCS-05600384.translation: [D]uccius(?), son of Contivai, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Solutorius.
EDCS-05600384.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Duccius(?) to Jupiter Solutorius.

EDCS-05600386.text_edition: \n Caenia Lupi filia Elanicum annorum XXX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05600386.translation: Caenia Lupi, daughter of Lupus, aged thirty, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600386.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caenia Lupi, stating her name, parentage, age, and a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-05600388.text_edition: \n Mentina Ambati filia nnorum LX hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05600388.translation: Mentina, daughter of Ambatus, aged 60, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600388.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Mentina, daughter of Ambatus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05600459.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Caecilia Marina, daughter of Lucius, of Ossobnob, aged 85, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600459.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Marina, daughter of Lucius, from Ossobnob, who died at 85.

EDCS-05600495.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of a god, pontifex maximus, consul for the 12th time, with tribunician power for the 19th time. Vicanus, son of Boutus, dedicated this sacred object.
EDCS-05600495.summary: A dedication to Emperor Augustus, listing his titles and consulships, made by Vicanus, son of Boutus.

EDCS-05600504.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Lucius Fabius Valerianus, aged 58. Julia Alexandrina made this for her most loving husband. He lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600504.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Fabius Valerianus, aged 58, with the monument erected by his wife, Julia Alexandrina.

EDCS-05600531.translation: To the deified Antoninus Pius Augustus, father of the country, the best and holiest prince of all ages. Quintus Talotius Silonianus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, from Collippone, a summoned soldier of his 6th Praetorian cohort, in the name of the order of citizens from Collippone, because they made him a decurion, having remitted his honorary payment and duties and burdens of public office. Dedicated by decree of the decurions on the 13th day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus for the 3rd time and Marcus Ummidius Quadratus for the 2nd time, with Quintus Allius Maximus and Gaius Sulpicius Silonianus as duovirs.
EDCS-05600531.summary: A dedication by the order of Collippone citizens to the deified Emperor Antoninus Pius and to Quintus Talotius Silonianus, a member of the order and a soldier, recognizing his appointment as decurion and the remission of his public duties, dated to 193 AD.

EDCS-05600532.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. To Diadumenus, freedman of Carisia Avita. Secunda [erected this].
EDCS-05600532.summary: A funerary epitaph for Diadumenus, freedman of Carisia Avita, with Secunda as the dedicator.

EDCS-05600549.translation: Caturus and Virius, sons of Coporus, and Boutia, from Meidubrigae (?), by their will had this made.
EDCS-05600549.summary: A funerary monument erected by heirs, Caturus, Virius, and Boutia, in accordance with the will of the deceased.

EDCS-05600552.text_edition: \n Promut annorum L hic sit-- est us\n
EDCS-05600552.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Promutus at the age of 50.

EDCS-05600554.text_edition: \n Dibus Manibus fecerunt parentes piissimo filio Ton annorum XVI\n
EDCS-05600554.translation: The parents made this for their most devoted son, Ton, aged 16.
EDCS-05600554.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by parents for their son Ton, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-05600557.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Valeria Avita dedicated this altar of her taurobolium as a returned gift for her birthday, with Doccyricus Valerianus as priest and Publicius Mysticus as archigallus.
EDCS-05600557.summary: A votive dedication of an altar for a taurobolium ritual, made by Valeria Avita on her birthday, with named priests.

EDCS-05600566.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gellia Patricia, aged 30, from Emerita, her mother made this; she lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600566.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gellia Patricia, set up by her mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600603.translation: Flavinus from Clun, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600603.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavinus from Clun, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600609.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Granius (?) Paternus, of the Quirina tribe, from Caesarobrigens, from the fortress of Cisellus, aged 65, his wife Ambatia set up this for her most devoted husband from her own resources.
EDCS-05600609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Granius Paternus, dedicated by his wife Ambatia, likely from a military context, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600615.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flaccus, son of Sula, aged 80. Marius, his son, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05600615.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flaccus, erected by his son Marius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600616.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus G. Polibius C. Amabilis saw to its construction from his own resources; he who asks you.
EDCS-05600616.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus G. Polibius C. Amabilis, who commissioned his own tomb, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600617.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Nerea lived 31 years. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600617.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Nerea, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600620.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Accius Diodatus commissioned this for his comrade.
EDCS-05600620.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed individual, commissioned by Caius Accius Diodatus, likely a soldier given the term 'contubernalis', dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600621.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Rufinus, son of Rufinus, aged 53. Rufinus, his son, saw to its construction. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600621.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufinus the younger, erected by his son Rufinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600622.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sege[---], son of Sege[---], aged 71. He lies here.
EDCS-05600622.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Sege[---], son of Sege[---], dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600624.translation: Uralo [---] Thallusa commissioned this for her patron from her own resources.
EDCS-05600624.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed patron, commissioned by Thallusa, likely a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600629.translation: Surisca fulfilled her vow, offered by Ambo[3]duitio.
EDCS-05600629.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Ambo[3]duitio to the deity Surisca.

EDCS-05601272.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesare Augusto Germanico pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate II imperatore II patre patriae consule III terminus Augustalis\n
EDCS-05601272.translation: By Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the second time, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time: the Augustan boundary marker.
EDCS-05601272.summary: This inscription is a boundary marker ('terminus Augustalis') dating to the reign of Emperor Claudius (41-54 AD), listing his titles.

EDCS-05601273.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the fifth time, consul, restored this.
EDCS-05601273.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a monument by Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and dating it to his fifth tribunician power year.

EDCS-05601403.translation: Turaga, daughter of Arconis, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601403.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Turaga, daughter of Arconis, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05900001.text_edition: \n Caius Cosconius Cai filius Galeria hic situs est\n
EDCS-05900001.translation: Gaius Cosconius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05900001.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Cosconius, identifying his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05900003.translation: Urbicus's wife is buried here: CRE(RE)MINA.
EDCS-05900003.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Cremina, identifying her as the wife of Urbicus.

EDCS-05900006.summary: This is a very brief dedication or gift inscription by a person named Primus.

EDCS-05900009.text_edition: \n Recommemoratio Cai Iuli Successiani Augustalis Emeritensis Exsuperanti liberti Ana bonis bene Barraeca\n
EDCS-05900009.translation: A commemoration of Gaius Iulius Successianus, Augustalis of Emerita, and his freedman Exsuperantius, for their good deeds, [---].
EDCS-05900009.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Iulius Successianus, an Augustalis from Emerita, and his freedman Exsuperantius, likely for benefactions.

EDCS-06100327.text_edition: \n CROVGAE NILAIGVI Clementinus GEI animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-06100327.translation: Clementinus, with a willing heart, fulfilled his vow to the holy goddess Crovga-Nilaigvi.
EDCS-06100327.summary: This is a votive inscription by Clementinus to the goddess Crovga-Nilaigvi.

EDCS-06900002.text_edition: \n Deae sanctae Fortunae Valerius Paternus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-06900002.translation: Valerius Paternus set up this in fulfillment of his vow to the holy goddess Fortuna.
EDCS-06900002.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valerius Paternus to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-07000578.text_edition: \n Tongeta Tulori filia annorum LX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis S T faciendum curav---\n
EDCS-07000578.translation: Tongeta, daughter of Tulorius, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. [---] saw to its construction.
EDCS-07000578.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tongeta, daughter of Tulorius, who died at age 60, indicating the construction was supervised by an unnamed individual.

EDCS-07000581.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul proconsul fecit milia LIII\n
EDCS-07000581.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, made this 53 miles [long].
EDCS-07000581.summary: An inscription recording the completion of a public work, likely a road, by Emperor Severus Alexander, dated to his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-07000582.text_edition: \n Calaetica Luci filia Severa nnorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Quintus Cornelius Vitulus coniugi suae dedicavit ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-07000582.translation: Calaetica, daughter of Lucius, Severa, aged 15, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Cornelius Vitulus dedicated [this] to his wife and saw to its construction.
EDCS-07000582.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calaetica Severa, daughter of Lucius, who died at age 15, dedicated by her husband Quintus Cornelius Vitulus.

EDCS-07000583.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Penti filius Turpio hic situs est\n
EDCS-07000583.translation: Caius Iulius Turpio, son of Penti, lies here.
EDCS-07000583.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Turpio, son of Penti.

EDCS-07000585.text_edition: \n Talticus Arconis filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis XXXX\n
EDCS-07000585.translation: Talticus, son of Arconis, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. 40 [years old?].
EDCS-07000585.summary: A funerary epitaph for Talticus, son of Arconis, likely indicating his age as 40.

EDCS-07000590.text_edition: \n Tancinus Ambati filius Aun annorum IV hic situs est Ambatus Tancinili filius filio\n
EDCS-07000590.translation: Tancinus, son of Ambatus, son of Aun, aged 4, lies here. Ambatus, son of Tancinilus, [dedicated this] to his son.
EDCS-07000590.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tancinus, son of Ambatus, who died at age 4, dedicated by his father Ambatus Tancinili.

EDCS-07100115.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Valerius Soldus veteranus legionis VII geminae nnorum LXX Lucius Domitius Aponius et Valeria Primula heredes ex testamento faciendum curaverunt hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07100115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Valerius Soldus, veteran of the Legio VII Gemina, aged 70. Lucius Domitius Aponius and Valeria Primula, his heirs, saw to its construction in accordance with the will. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07100115.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Soldus, a 70-year-old veteran of Legio VII Gemina, whose tomb was erected by his heirs Lucius Domitius Aponius and Valeria Primula.

EDCS-07400359.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus Iovi votum solvit animo libens\n
EDCS-07400359.translation: Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus paid his vow to Jupiter willingly.
EDCS-07400359.summary: A votive inscription recording Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-07400361.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Galeria V hic situs est Quintus Caecilius Cai filius Galeria hic situs est\n
EDCS-07400361.translation: Caius Iulius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here. Quintus Caecilius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-07400361.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of two individuals, Caius Iulius and Quintus Caecilius, both belonging to the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-07600212.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or ownership marker, referring to Lucius Cornelius Mitulus.

EDCS-07600213.text_edition: \n Lucius Sagaius Maxumi filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07600213.translation: Lucius Sagaius, son of Maxumus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07600213.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Sagaius, son of Maxumus.

EDCS-07600215.summary: A dedication to Cabura, daughter of Pucius.

EDCS-07600216.text_edition: \n Cusei Paetaico Boutius Turaiani animo libens posuit\n
EDCS-07600216.translation: Boutius, son of Turaianus, willingly placed this with free will.
EDCS-07600216.summary: A dedication by Boutius, son of Turaianus, indicating a voluntary offering.

EDCS-07600218.text_edition: \n Afina Tu filia annorum XX sit tibi terra levis mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07600218.translation: Afina, daughter of Tu, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her mother saw to its construction.
EDCS-07600218.summary: A funerary epitaph for Afina, daughter of Tu, aged 20, with her mother arranging the monument.

EDCS-07600219.text_edition: \n Iboena Alluqui filia annorum XXXV et Avitus Frontonis annorum XLII \n
EDCS-07600219.translation: Iboena, daughter of Alluquius, aged 35, and Avitus, son of Fronto, aged 42.
EDCS-07600219.summary: An epitaph recording the ages of Iboena, daughter of Alluquius, and Avitus, son of Fronto.

EDCS-07600220.translation: Of Pentilius, son of Camalus, aged 70.
EDCS-07600220.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Pentilius, son of Camalus.

EDCS-07600222.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rufinus, son of Rufus, with his age incompletely recorded.

EDCS-07600223.summary: An epitaph recording the name Pissirea, daughter of Tancinus.

EDCS-07600224.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Pentilius, aged 81.
EDCS-07600224.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld spirits for Pentilius, aged 81.

EDCS-07600231.summary: An epitaph possibly referring to a family or group named Viscanorum, belonging to Camalus, with the name Concilius.

EDCS-07600232.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased, Albinus.

EDCS-07600234.text_edition: \n Pudens Competri aras ei Collovesei Caieloni Cosigo sacravit\n
EDCS-07600234.translation: Pudens dedicated this altar to him, in honor of Competrius Caielonius of Collovesus, with respect to Cosigo.
EDCS-07600234.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Pudens to a deity or person, possibly in relation to local communities or individuals.

EDCS-07600235.text_edition: \n Lobaenus Mani nnrum VII pater filio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07600235.translation: Lobaenus, son of Mani, seven years old, his father arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07600235.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Lobaenus, whose father commissioned the monument.

EDCS-07600279.text_edition: \n Bassus Turobei eques alae Vettonum aram posuit Ataecinae votum solvit merito ]\n
EDCS-07600279.translation: Bassus, son of Turobeius, a cavalryman of the Vettones ala, set up this altar and fulfilled his vow to Ataecina with due merit.
EDCS-07600279.summary: This inscription records an altar erected by Bassus, a cavalryman, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Ataecina.

EDCS-08300359.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Cornelius, with significant parts missing.

EDCS-08300361.text_edition: \n Endovollico sacrum posuit Titus Annius VSR PATRV \n
EDCS-08300361.translation: Sacred to Endovellicus; Titus Annius [son of] VSR, his father, set [it] up.
EDCS-08300361.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sacred object or place to the deity Endovellicus by Titus Annius.

EDCS-08300368.translation: Apana, daughter of Apano, lies here.
EDCS-08300368.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased, Apana.

EDCS-08300371.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Galeria Rufus annorum XXV hic situs est et Iulia Gai filia Fundana hic sita est\n
EDCS-08300371.translation: Gaius Julius Galeria Rufus, aged 25, lies here, and Iulia Fundana, daughter of Gaius, lies here.
EDCS-08300371.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Galeria Rufus and Iulia Fundana.

EDCS-08300372.text_edition: \n Triborunni Titus Curiatius Rufinus libens animo dedit\n
EDCS-08300372.translation: Titus Curiatius Rufinus gave [this] to the Triboruni with a willing heart.
EDCS-08300372.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Titus Curiatius Rufinus to the Triboruni, possibly a local community or group.

EDCS-08300375.text_edition: \n Lari Couticivi Lari Coutioso Longonaroso Malgeinus Leuri filius Arbuensis votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-08300375.translation: Larus Couticivus, son of Larus Couticos, son of Longonarus, son of Malgeinus, of Arbuensis, fulfilled his vow with a willing heart.
EDCS-08300375.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Larus Couticivus, detailing his lineage.

EDCS-08300379.text_edition: \n Talabus Caenoni sic filius hic stitus sic est\n
EDCS-08300379.translation: Talabus, son of Caenoni, has been set up here.
EDCS-08300379.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Talabus, son of Caenoni, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300380.text_edition: \n Albinus Apili filius Taporus annorum XL hic situs est Turea Alboni filia uxor bene merenti faciendum curavit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300380.translation: Albinus, son of Apilius, Taporus, aged 40, is laid here. Turea, daughter of Albonius, his wife, took care to have this made for him, who deserved well. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08300380.summary: This funerary epitaph for Albinus, son of Apilius, aged 40, was erected by his deserving wife Turea, daughter of Albonius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300384.text_edition: \n us Flacci Cobelcus votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-08300384.translation: Flaccus Cobelcus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-08300384.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flaccus Cobelcus fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300388.text_edition: \n Licinio Polli filio Cilo Bouti filius heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08300388.translation: Cilo, son of Boutius, erected this for his son Licinius Pollius, the heir by the will.
EDCS-08300388.summary: This funerary inscription records Cilo, son of Boutius, erecting a monument for his son Licinius Pollius as the designated heir.

EDCS-08300391.text_edition: \n Paullo Lovesi filio Arania Craesoni filia socruo heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08300391.translation: The heir by the will erected this for his mother-in-law Arania, daughter of Craeson, wife of Paulus Lovesus.
EDCS-08300391.summary: This funerary inscription records an heir by will erecting a monument for his mother-in-law Arania, daughter of Craeson.

EDCS-08300393.text_edition: \n Flavus Marci annorum hic situs est sit tibi terra levis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08300393.translation: Flavus Marci is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. He took care to have this made.
EDCS-08300393.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavus Marci, with a wish for a light earth and an indication that he arranged for its construction.

EDCS-08300395.text_edition: \n Togae Rufus Vegeti filius votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-08300395.translation: Togatus Rufus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-08300395.summary: This is a votive inscription stating that Togatus Rufus willingly paid his vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400276.text_edition: \n Ladronus Dovai Bracarus castello Durbede hic situs est annorum XXX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08400276.translation: Ladronus, son of Dovaius, a Bracaran from the fort of Durbede, is laid here, aged 30. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08400276.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Ladronus, son of Dovaius, identified as a Bracaran from the fort of Durbede, aged 30.

EDCS-08400285.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Boudelus, son of Conceltus.

EDCS-08400286.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a funerary marker or a boundary stone, naming Turanus and Aluqui, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400287.summary: This inscription is a fragmented dedication to a person named Adoris, likely from an altar or funerary monument.

EDCS-08400290.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Priscino Prisci filio contubernales faciendum curaverunt sit tibi terra levis venisti ave legisti vale\n
EDCS-08400290.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. His comrades had this made for Priscino, son of Priscus. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Welcome, you who have read, farewell.
EDCS-08400290.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Priscino, son of Priscus, erected by his companions (contubernales), likely soldiers or members of a household, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-08400291.text_edition: \n Caturo Turendi filius VANGEI- votum solvit\n
EDCS-08400291.translation: Caturo, son of Turendus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400291.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Caturo, son of Turendus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08400292.translation: To Arentia, daughter of Equotulla, from Nicer, son of Arcon.
EDCS-08400292.summary: This appears to be a dedication or memorial inscription from Nicer, son of Arcon, to Arentia, daughter of Equotulla.

EDCS-08400293.text_edition: \n Tanginus Luci Bouti Laepo animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-08400293.translation: Laepo, son of Tanginus and Lucius Boutius, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400293.summary: This votive inscription records Laepo, son of Tanginus and Lucius Boutius, willingly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08400294.text_edition: \n Laepo votum solvit Bassus Viriati filius\n
EDCS-08400294.translation: Laepo fulfilled his vow. Bassus, son of Viriatus.
EDCS-08400294.summary: This is a votive inscription stating that Laepo fulfilled his vow, possibly mentioning Bassus, son of Viriatus, as the dedicator or recipient.

EDCS-08400295.text_edition: \n Placida Sciti filia annorum IIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Scitus Victoris filius et Avita Quadrati filia parentes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08400295.translation: Placida, daughter of Scitus, aged 4, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Her parents, Scitus, son of Victor, and Avita, daughter of Quadratus, had this made.
EDCS-08400295.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young child Placida, daughter of Scitus, erected by her parents Scitus and Avita.

EDCS-08400296.text_edition: \n S DAE CIVS - SALAC S Cumelius Corobulti filius Cularni fecit de Balatucelo natione\n
EDCS-08400296.translation: To the spirit of Daecius Salacus. Cumelius, son of Corobultus of the Cularni tribe, made this, from Balatucellus by nation.
EDCS-08400296.summary: This inscription records Cumelius, son of Corobultus, from the Cularni tribe, dedicating something to the spirit of Daecius Salacus.

EDCS-08400297.text_edition: \n Alfiduae sic Amae Luci Amni filis cippum fillis sic suis pater\n
EDCS-08400297.translation: Her father made this cippus for Alfidua, his daughter, thus for Ama, daughter of Lucius Amnis, thus for his own sons.
EDCS-08400297.summary: This inscription describes a father erecting a cippus (marker stone) for his daughter Alfidua, and also for his daughter Ama and his sons.

EDCS-08400298.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Iuliae Augustae Emeritae Caius Antistius Cai libertus Iucundus palmam ex pondo |(duarum unciarum) votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-08400298.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Julia Augusta Emerita. Gaius Antistius Iucundus, freedman of Gaius, willingly fulfilled his vow of a palm weighing two ounces.
EDCS-08400298.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Genius of the colony of Emerita by Gaius Antistius Iucundus, a freedman, offering a votive palm.

EDCS-08400301.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Caesiane sic annorum XXXVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Hector Corneliorum uxori piissimae fecit\n
EDCS-08400301.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Caesiane, laid here, lived for 37 years. May the earth be light on you. Hector made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-08400301.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Caesiane, set up by her husband Hector, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08400302.text_edition: \n Publius Curtius Faustilli l hic situs est Fabia Marci filia hic sita est VS\n
EDCS-08400302.translation: Publius Curtius Faustillus is laid here. Fabia, daughter of Marcus, is laid here. V. S. (vixit?).
EDCS-08400302.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Publius Curtius Faustillus and Fabia, daughter of Marcus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400307.text_edition: \n Maxumus Ulatici filius Taporus Nabiae sacrum\n
EDCS-08400307.translation: Maxumus, son of Ulaticus, and Taporus, son of Nabia, sacred offering.
EDCS-08400307.summary: An inscription recording two individuals, Maxumus and Taporus, who made a sacred offering, possibly in honor of a deity.

EDCS-08400308.summary: A brief funerary marker for Tuginocius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500317.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Avitus Olisiponensis nnum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-08500317.translation: Marcus Julius Avitus of Lisbon, 30 years old, is laid here.
EDCS-08500317.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Julius Avitus, a citizen of Lisbon, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-08500319.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Modestinae quae vixit nnos ittas \n
EDCS-08500319.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Modestina, who lived [a number] years.
EDCS-08500319.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modestina, with a lacuna for her age at death.

EDCS-08500320.text_edition: \n Auctus et Hermes deae magistri donum\n
EDCS-08500320.translation: Auctus and Hermes, masters of the goddess, a gift.
EDCS-08500320.summary: An inscription recording a gift made by Auctus and Hermes, identified as 'masters of the goddess', possibly related to a cult.

EDCS-08500321.text_edition: \n Camira Sunuae liberta annorum XXV hic sita est Docquirus Catueni et Sunua Docquiri liberti filiae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08500321.translation: Camira, freedwoman of Sunua, 25 years old, is laid here. Docquirus Catueni and Sunua Docquiri's freedwoman and daughter had this made.
EDCS-08500321.summary: A funerary epitaph for Camira, a freedwoman, set up by her patron Sunua and her patron's daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500323.text_edition: \n Tranquillo Silonis filio patri annorum L Siloni Tranquilli filio fratri nnrum XV Boutiae Mantai filiae matri nnrum XXXXVIII Avita Tranquilli filia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08500323.translation: To Tranquillus, son of Silon, father, 50 years old. To Silon, son of Tranquillus, brother, 15 years old. To the mother, daughter of Boutia Mantai, 48 years old. Avita, daughter of Tranquillus, had this made.
EDCS-08500323.summary: A funerary monument erected by Avita for her father Tranquillus, brother Silon, and mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500324.text_edition: \n Atilia Pupi l(iberta) Amoena Fonti animo libens posuit\n
EDCS-08500324.translation: Atilia Pupi's freedwoman Amoena willingly set this up for Fontus.
EDCS-08500324.summary: A dedication by a freedwoman named Amoena to a person named Fontus, likely set up during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500325.text_edition: \n Casabius Turrilli filius votum solvit libens merito reditus causa\n
EDCS-08500325.translation: Casabius, son of Turrillus, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved, concerning his return.
EDCS-08500325.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Casabius, son of Turrillus, for his safe return, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500326.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Metello Aulo Licinio consulibus Decimus Iulius Decimi filius Galeria Cilo hospitium fecit sibi liberis posterisque suis cum Nigro et Rufo et Prisco Turdulis Veteribus liberis posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-08500326.translation: In the consulship of Quintus Caecilius Metellus and Aulus Licinius, Decimus Iulius Cilo, son of Decimus, of the Galeria tribe, established a relationship of hospitality with Niger, Rufus, and Priscus, the Veteres Turduli, for himself, his children, and their descendants.
EDCS-08500326.summary: This inscription records a formal agreement of hospitality established between Decimus Iulius Cilo and members of the Veteres Turduli family during the consulship of Q. Caecilius Metellus and A. Licinius, likely in the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08500327.text_edition: \n Quinto Sulpicio Camerino Caio Poppaeo Sabino consulibus Decimus Iulius Marci filius Galeria Cilo hospitium fecit cum Lugario Septanii filio ex Turduleis Veteribus eumque et leiberos posterosque eius in fidem clientelamque suam recepit leiberorum posterorumque suorum egit Lugarius Septanii\n
EDCS-08500327.translation: In the consulship of Quintus Sulpicius Camerinus and Caius Poppaeus Sabinus, Decimus Iulius Cilo, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, established a relationship of hospitality with Lugarius, son of Septanius, from the Veteres Turduli, and took him and his children and descendants into his protection and patronage; Lugarius, son of Septanius, acted for his children and descendants.
EDCS-08500327.summary: This inscription details a hospitality agreement and reception into patronage between Decimus Iulius Cilo and Lugarius, son of Septanius, from the Veteres Turduli, during the consulship of Q. Sulpicius Camerinus and C. Poppaeus Sabinus.

EDCS-08500328.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Amoena, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-08500329.text_edition: \n Cumelius Medueni filius nnorum XXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500329.translation: Cumelius, son of Meduenus, aged 30, lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08500329.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Cumelius, son of Meduenus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-08500330.text_edition: \n Culva Paugendiae filia hic sita est hic tumulus levis sit\n
EDCS-08500330.translation: Culva, daughter of Paugendius, lies here; may this mound rest lightly.
EDCS-08500330.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Culva, daughter of Paugendius.

EDCS-08500331.text_edition: \n Doqirus Turei filius annorum C Ibdoena Talotis filia annorum C hic siti sunt vobis terra levis Vegetus \n
EDCS-08500331.translation: Doqirus, son of Tureius, aged 100, and Ibdoena, daughter of Talotis, aged 100, lie here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Vegetus.
EDCS-08500331.summary: This tombstone records the burial of two individuals, Doqirus and Ibdoena, both aged 100, with a dedication by Vegetus.

EDCS-08500332.translation: Talticus, son of Venatus, Pepo, son of Esui, aged...
EDCS-08500332.summary: This incomplete inscription appears to be a tombstone recording the names and partial ages of Talticus and Pepo.

EDCS-08500333.text_edition: \n Caturonis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis ER A\n
EDCS-08500333.translation: Caturonis lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. ER A.
EDCS-08500333.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caturonis, with some unclear accompanying letters or abbreviations.

EDCS-08500334.text_edition: \n Deae sanctae sacrum Lucius Caelius Philinus animo libens posuit\n
EDCS-08500334.translation: Sacred to the holy goddess. Lucius Caelius Philinus willingly placed this.
EDCS-08500334.summary: This is a dedication to a sacred goddess by Lucius Caelius Philinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500335.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaio M Zosimo beneficiario legionis VII geminae piae felicis natione Italo stipendior<um=ON> XX qui vixit ann<i>s XXXVII mensibus VII diebus XXXXVIIII Iunia Vera coniugi benignissimo p<iissimo=VRCINIO> posui<t=N> hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08500335.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunia Vera dedicated this to her most kind and loving husband, Gaius Marius Zosimus, a beneficarius of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, an Italian by birth, with 20 years of service, who lived for 37 years, 7 months, and 49 days. May the earth rest lightly on him here.
EDCS-08500335.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Marius Zosimus, a soldier, set up by his wife Iunia Vera, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500338.text_edition: \n Titus Cornelius Felix Valeria Quinti filia Tusca uxor Caius Valerius Lucanus frater Titus Cornelius Tuscus filius Alfia Maura hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-08500338.translation: Titus Cornelius Felix, the wife Tusca, daughter of Quintus, and Titus Cornelius Tuscus, the son, and Alfia Maura, are buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you all.
EDCS-08500338.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Cornelius Felix, his wife Tusca, their son Titus Cornelius Tuscus, and Alfia Maura, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500339.text_edition: \n Caius Marius Tyius annorum LXV Lucceia Laudice sic marito optimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08500339.translation: Lucceia Laudice had this funerary monument built for her husband, Gaius Marius Tyius, aged 65.
EDCS-08500339.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucceia Laudice commissioning a monument for her husband Gaius Marius Tyius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500340.text_edition: \n Publio Vario Quinti filio Papiria Liguri Liciniae Cai libertae Thelidi Publio Vario Publi filio Papiria Severo Variae Publi filiae Avitae de suo faciendum curavit [Quintus Iulius ] socero socrui socerino uxsori sic [dedicavit] Iulia Quinti filia Severa hoc monumentum sive sepulcrum exterum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-08500340.translation: Publius Varius Severus, son of Publius Varius, and Varia Avita, daughter of Publius, freedwoman of Licinia, had this built at their own expense for Quintus Julius, father-in-law, mother-in-law, father-in-law's father, and wife. Iulia Severa, daughter of Quintus, dedicated this monument or outer tomb; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-08500340.summary: This funerary monument inscription, likely from the Imperial period, records Publius Varius Severus and Varia Avita commissioning a tomb for various relatives, with Iulia Severa also involved in the dedication.

EDCS-08500341.text_edition: \n onis filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Maxsuma sic Calui filia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08500341.translation: Son of [---] is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you. Maxsuma, daughter of Calvus, had this made.
EDCS-08500341.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed son, commissioned by his mother Maxsuma, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500342.text_edition: \n a hic sita est Hilarus matri\n
EDCS-08500342.summary: This fragment of a funerary inscription records Hilarus burying his mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500343.summary: This very brief inscription identifies Lepidus Hispanus as a soldier, likely from the Roman army.

EDCS-08500344.text_edition: \n Avillius Marci libertus Philargurius hic situs est Hilarus conlibertus de sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08500344.translation: Avillius Philargurius, freedman of Marcus, is buried here. Hilarus, a fellow freedman, had this made with his own money.
EDCS-08500344.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Avillius Philargurius, a freedman, with the monument commissioned by his fellow freedman Hilarus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500345.text_edition: \n Coutius Cuocicorum libertus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08500345.translation: Coutius, freedman of the Cuocici, is buried here.
EDCS-08500345.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Coutius, a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500346.text_edition: \n Lucius Titius Cai filius Iovi Solutorio porticum at aram votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08500346.translation: Lucius Titius, son of Caius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Iovius Solutorius by building a portico near the altar.
EDCS-08500346.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Titius fulfilling a vow by building a portico for the god Iovius Solutorius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08500347.text_edition: \n Cololeus Loucini filius annorum C hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500347.translation: Cololeus, son of Loucinus, aged 100, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500347.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cololeus, who died at the age of 100.

EDCS-08500349.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Apana Triti annorum LX Apana Clouti filia annorum V Amaenea matri et filiae faciendum curavit hic sitae sunt sit tibi sic terra levis\n
EDCS-08500349.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Apana Triti, aged 60, and Apana Clouti, daughter, aged 5. Apana Amentia had this made for her mother and daughter, who are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500349.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Apana Triti and her young daughter Apana Clouti, with the monument commissioned by Apana Amentia.

EDCS-08500350.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonius Alionus annorum LXXV hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500350.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Antonius Alionus, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500350.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Antonius Alionus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-08500351.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maternae Materni annorum LII hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500351.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Maternae, daughter of Maternus, aged 52, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500351.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Maternae, who died at the age of 52.

EDCS-08500352.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Firmonis Flacci filii sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500352.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Of Firmonis, son of Flaccus. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500352.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Firmonis, son of Flaccus.

EDCS-08500353.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maganae annorum LXXX hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500353.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Magana, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500353.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Magana, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-08500354.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeriae Arqui annorum LX hic sita tibi terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum Aemili annorum XV hic sit-- tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500354.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Valeria, daughter of Arquus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Dedicated to the departed spirits. Of Aemilia, aged 15. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500354.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Valeria, daughter of Arquus, aged 60, and also Aemilia, aged 15.

EDCS-08500355.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cauelia, daughter of Vironus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-08500356.text_edition: \n Magana Clouti annorum LXV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500356.translation: Magana, daughter of Cloutus, aged 65. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500356.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Magana, daughter of Cloutus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-08500357.text_edition: \n Aro Arconis annorum LX terra levis\n
EDCS-08500357.translation: Aro, son of Arcon, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-08500357.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aro, son of Arcon, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-08500358.text_edition: \n Tritanus Vironi servus annorum XX hic situs est tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500358.translation: Tritanus, slave of Viron, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500358.summary: This is a tombstone for Tritanus, a slave, with a common funerary wish, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Circius, a child, with an abbreviation of unknown meaning, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500418.summary: This is a funerary inscription, incomplete, recording the burial of a woman named Caecilia and possibly another person, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600310.text_edition: \n Deae sanctae votum animo libens solvit Caius Valerius Rufus Caepio\n
EDCS-08600310.translation: Gaius Valerius Rufus Caepio willingly fulfilled his vow to the holy goddess.
EDCS-08600310.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled by Gaius Valerius Rufus Caepio to a goddess, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600311.text_edition: \n Camira Maxumi sic filia annorum XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600311.translation: Camira, daughter of Maximus, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08600311.summary: This is a tombstone for Camira, a young woman, with a common funerary wish, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600321.text_edition: \n Apano Cilei filio hic situs est statuerunt serores sic et vidua\n
EDCS-08600321.translation: Apano, son of Cileius, lies here. His sisters and his widow erected [this monument].
EDCS-08600321.summary: This is a tombstone for Apano, erected by his female relatives, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600325.text_edition: \n Iulia Luci filia Amoena annorum XII hic sita est mater de suo fecit\n
EDCS-08600325.translation: Iulia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, aged 12, lies here. Her mother made this at her own expense.
EDCS-08600325.summary: This is a tombstone for a young girl, Iulia Amoena, funded by her mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600326.text_edition: \n Quinti filius Galeria s annorum XIX Iulia mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600326.translation: The son of Quintus, Galeria [?], aged 19. Iulia, the mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-08600326.summary: This is a tombstone for a young woman, possibly named Galeria, whose mother commissioned the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600327.text_edition: \n Quinto Bovio Atimetioni Victori Quinti Bovi Marciani nnorum LXX Quintus Bovius Potitus patri optumo\n
EDCS-08600327.translation: To Quintus Bovius Atimetius Victor, aged 70, son of Quintus Bovius Marcianus. To Quintus Bovius Potitus, his most excellent father.
EDCS-08600327.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring two individuals, possibly father and son, with full name and age given for one, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600328.text_edition: \n Licinia Publi filia Maxsuma sic Marcus Antistius Marci filius Galeria Facundus hic siti sunt Cornelia Titi filia Boutia quius sic posita est in prima parte imago viva se faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600328.translation: Licinia Maxsuma, daughter of Publius, and Marcus Antistius Facundus, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, lie here. Cornelia Boutia, daughter of Titus, who lies here, [this] likeness of her living self had her mother see to its construction.
EDCS-08600328.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of two individuals, Licinia Maxsuma and Marcus Antistius Facundus, and a third, Cornelia Boutia, whose mother commissioned her portrait, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600329.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Quinti filio Galeria Frontoni annorum XVII Iulia Quintilla mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600329.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Quintilla, mother, had this made for her son Quintus Julius Fronton, 17 years of age.
EDCS-08600329.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother's dedication for her deceased son, Quintus Julius Fronton, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-08600330.text_edition: \n Reburrus Tusci filius et Tusca Tusci filia\n
EDCS-08600330.translation: Reburrus, son of Tuscus, and Tusca, daughter of Tuscus.
EDCS-08600330.summary: This short inscription likely identifies two individuals, Reburrus and Tusca, both children of a person named Tuscus, possibly on a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-08600331.summary: This inscription likely identifies an individual named Mascellus, son of Macrinus, potentially on a tombstone or as an owner's mark.

EDCS-08600333.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aufidiae Rusticae annorum XVII filiae Quintus Ligurius Rusticus ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600333.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Ligurius Rusticus had this placed for his daughter Aufidia Rustica, 17 years of age.
EDCS-08600333.summary: This funerary inscription records a father's dedication for his deceased daughter, Aufidia Rustica, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-08600334.text_edition: \n MIOMELI Catro Tangini filius ALVA votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-08600334.translation: Catro, son of Tanginus, willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600334.summary: This inscription records Catro, son of Tanginus, fulfilling a votive offering, likely to a deity.

EDCS-08600335.text_edition: \n Dobiteina Doquiri filia Iovi Supremo Summo votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-08600335.translation: Doquiteina, daughter of Doquir, willingly and gladly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Supreme and Highest.
EDCS-08600335.summary: This inscription records Doquiteina, daughter of Doquir, fulfilling a votive offering to Jupiter.

EDCS-08600336.text_edition: \n Veratia Verati filia hic sita est mater ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600336.translation: Veratia, daughter of Veratus, lies here; her mother had this placed.
EDCS-08600336.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Veratia, daughter of Veratus, with her mother responsible for the monument.

EDCS-08600337.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the name Virius Alluquius, son of, suggesting it's part of a personal dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-08600339.text_edition: \n Paullini sic Pa M F IM\n
EDCS-08600339.translation: Paullinus, son of Paullus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-08600339.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Paullinus, identifying him as the son of Paullus and grandson of Marcus.

EDCS-08600340.text_edition: \n Graecinius Langon nnorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600340.translation: Graecinius Langon, 35 years of age, lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600340.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Graecinius Langon at age 35 and includes a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08600341.text_edition: \n Accae Celeris Statulci uxori Maturus et Pompeia \n
EDCS-08600341.translation: To Acca Celeris, wife of Statulcus. Maturus and Pompeia [did this].
EDCS-08600341.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Acca Celeris, wife of Statulcus, by Maturus and Pompeia.

EDCS-08600342.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antistiae Luci filiae Verecundae annorum XVIII Antistius Eucarpio pater filiae piissumae sic faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600342.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antistius Eucarpus had this funerary monument erected for his most dutiful daughter, Antistia Verecunda, aged 18.
EDCS-08600342.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Antistia Verecunda, aged 18, by her father Antistius Eucarpus.

EDCS-08600343.text_edition: \n Rufria Quintilla nnorum LV hic sita est Marcus Luc\n
EDCS-08600343.translation: Rufria Quintilla, aged 55, is buried here. Marcus Luc[---]
EDCS-08600343.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufria Quintilla, aged 55, with the beginning of another name.

EDCS-08600344.text_edition: \n Lucius Publicius Placidus nnorum XXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Atilia Firmilla uxor ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600344.translation: Lucius Publicius Placidus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His wife, Atilia Firmilla, had this erected.
EDCS-08600344.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Publicius Placidus, aged 40, erected by his wife Atilia Firmilla.

EDCS-08600345.text_edition: \n Numeria Quinti liberta Prima sibi et suis posterisque Caius Rubrius Proculus Papiria annorum VI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600345.translation: To Numeria Prima, freedwoman of Quintus, for herself and her family and descendants. Caius Rubrius Proculus [and] Papiria, aged 6, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600345.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numeria Prima, freedwoman of Quintus, and Caius Rubrius Proculus and Papiria, who was aged 6.

EDCS-08600346.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Alecxander sic filius pius patri suo annorum XXX hic situs hest sic sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600346.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Alecxander, most dutiful son, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600346.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alecxander, aged 30, described as a dutiful son.

EDCS-08600347.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Euhodia Mellini verna annorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Euhodus et Callityche filiae pientissumae sic\n
