EDCS-23001979.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial plot dimensions for Gaius Salvius, son of Quintus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002159.text_edition: \n Caius Norbanus Tancinus Luci divinae votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-23002159.translation: Gaius Norbanus Tancinus willingly fulfilled a vow to Lucius Divinius.
EDCS-23002159.summary: This is a dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Norbanus Tancinus to Lucius Divinius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002189.translation: Vibulenia Placidia, daughter of Gaius, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23002189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibulenia Placidia, daughter of Gaius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002400.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto filio Luci Septimi Severi ordo Salmanticensis\n
EDCS-23002400.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus. The council of Salamanca [dedicates this].
EDCS-23002400.summary: This is a dedication from the council of Salamanca to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, dating to his reign (177-192 AD).

EDCS-23900194.text_edition: \n Domine Megale Invicte tu qui Attidis corpus accepisti accipias corpus eius qui meas sarcinas supstulit qui me compilavit de domo Hispani illius corpus tibi et animam do dono ut meas res inveniam tunc tibi ostiam quadripedem dono Attis voveo si eas iure invenero domine Attis te rogo per tuum Nocturnum ut me quam primum compotem facias\n
EDCS-23900194.translation: Mighty Lord Invictus, you who received the body of Attis, receive the body of him who carried my belongings and stole from me from the house of that Spaniard. I give his body and soul to you as a gift, so that I may find my things. Then I will give you a four-footed beast. Attis, I vow to you, by your Nocturnus, that you may make me successful as soon as possible.
EDCS-23900194.summary: This is a complex votive inscription related to theft and recovery, invoking the deity Attis and his aspect Nocturnus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23900195.text_edition: \n tori libertus nnorum LIX Veneria liberta faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-23900195.translation: Torus, her freedman, lived fifty-nine years. Veneria, his freedwoman, took care to have this made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-23900195.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Torus and commissioned by his freedwoman, Veneria, who also lived as a freedwoman.

EDCS-23900200.text_edition: \n Marci Iuli Felicis Marci Iuli Severi\n
EDCS-23900200.translation: Of Marcus Julius Felix, of Marcus Julius Severus.
EDCS-23900200.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording names, possibly of a family or funerary dedication.

EDCS-23900203.translation: Amoena, daughter of Maelgeinus, is buried here.
EDCS-23900203.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Amoena, daughter of Maelgeinus.

EDCS-23900209.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Laberiae Marci filiae Avitae annorum XXXV Hel<v=B>ius Avitianus pater filiae pientissimae faciendum <c=Q>(uravit) \n
EDCS-23900209.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Laberia Avita, daughter of Marcus, aged thirty-five years. Her father, Helvius Avitianus, took care to have this made for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-23900209.summary: A tomb inscription dedicating a monument to Laberia Avita by her father, Helvius Avitianus.

EDCS-23900210.text_edition: \n quem faciendum curavit et Atilus Bovi Tapurus Miranus \n
EDCS-23900210.translation: And Atilus Bovi Tapurus Miranus, who he took care to have made.
EDCS-23900210.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly recording the commissioner of a monument, Atilus Bovi Tapurus Miranus.

EDCS-23900219.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that possibly records a name and a location.

EDCS-23900229.text_edition: \n Votum aviae paternae Norbana Maxuma Belon(a)e libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-23900229.translation: Norbana Maxuma willingly paid a vow to her paternal grandmother, Belona.
EDCS-23900229.summary: An inscription recording Norbana Maxuma fulfilling a vow to her paternal grandmother, Belona.

EDCS-24500818.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus.
EDCS-24500818.summary: A dedication mentioning Tiberius Caesar as the son of the deified Augustus, likely part of an official inscription.

EDCS-24500822.text_edition: \n Caecilia Quinti filia Tittia hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-24500822.translation: Caecilia Tittia, daughter of Quintus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24500822.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Caecilia Tittia, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-24500831.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Victor annorum XVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-24500831.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Victor, aged seventeen years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His mother took care to have this made.
EDCS-24500831.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Victor, commissioned by his mother.

EDCS-24500843.text_edition: \n C Luci filia Campana annorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-24500843.translation: C Campana, daughter of Lucius, aged 15, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24500843.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Campana, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27800021.summary: This inscription is a fragmented personal name, possibly recording a person's name and patronymic or origin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300356.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely recording the name Lucius Rufinus, possibly with an abbreviated cognomen or status, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300364.text_edition: \n Caesar Vespasianus (depalationem) fecit Ro et Dep iudicatum\n
EDCS-28300364.translation: Caesar Vespasian made the depalation, judgment was given at Ro and Dep.
EDCS-28300364.summary: This inscription records a public work (depalation, likely drainage or leveling) undertaken by Emperor Vespasian and a legal judgment, possibly from the Flavian period (AD 69-96).

EDCS-28300478.text_edition: \n Munnia Marciana nnorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Munnius Marcianus pater et Iulia Candida mater filiae pientissimae tetrastylum cum sign<o=V> Piaetatis fecerunt Lucius Munnius Marcianus nnorum LXXV Iulia Candida marito merentissimo istatuam posuit tia Candida nnorum plus LXX aram et statuam mihi posuit \n
EDCS-28300478.translation: Munnia Marciana, aged 25, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Munnius Marcianus her father and Iulia Candida her mother made the tetrastyle with the statue of Pietas for their most devoted daughter. Lucius Munnius Marcianus, aged 75, placed a statue for his most deserving husband. Iulia Candida, aged over 70, placed an altar and a statue for me.
EDCS-28300478.summary: This is a complex funerary monument honoring multiple family members, including a daughter, parents, and a husband, with several dedications and statues, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300484.translation: o AFR took care to have it made.
EDCS-28300484.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription indicating that someone, possibly abbreviated as 'AFR', supervised the construction of a monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300485.translation: Valens willingly paid his vow to Aetius.
EDCS-28300485.summary: This inscription records a votive offering or payment made by Valens to Aetius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300486.text_edition: \n Lucius us N A monumentum posuit\n
EDCS-28300486.translation: Lucius (son of ---) N A placed the monument.
EDCS-28300486.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording Lucius as the dedicator of a monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of Flavia [---], aged 45, his wife [---]. Flavius Paternus, her husband [---].
EDCS-28400030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Flavia, dedicated by her husband Flavius Paternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400057.translation: Sacred to the spirits. Iulia Dionysias, aged 43. Tiberius Claudius Badiolus had this made for his most devoted mother. She is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28400057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Dionysias, dedicated by her son Tiberius Claudius Badiolus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400092.text_edition: \n Norbana Fusci filia Fusca nnm LXV hic sita tibi sit terra levis filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28400092.translation: To Norbana Fusca, daughter of Fuscus, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her sons had this made.
EDCS-28400092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Norbana Fusca, a woman aged 65, whose sons commissioned the monument, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400093.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Calpurnius Clementinus annorum LXXV Iulia Vitula socero pio faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-28400093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Calpurnius Clementinus, aged 75, had this made for his pious father-in-law. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28400093.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintus Calpurnius Clementinus, aged 75, dedicating a monument to his father-in-law, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28700548.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flaccilla (?).
EDCS-28700548.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph, possibly for a woman named Flaccilla, dedicated to the departed spirits, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28700549.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Medugena, daughter of Terentius, aged 15, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700549.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Medugena, daughter of Terentius, aged 15, who lies here, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28700550.translation: To Herculea, daughter of Arquicus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700550.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Herculea, daughter of Arquicus, aged 50, who lies here, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28700555.translation: To Commoda, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700555.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Commoda, aged 30, who lies here, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29700145.translation: To our lord Flavius Dalmatins, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700145.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription addressed to the Emperor Flavius Dalmatins, likely dating to the Late Roman period.

EDCS-30000460.text_edition: \n Triteus Turaos Peicai libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-30000460.translation: Triteus Turaos happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000460.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Triteus Turaos fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30000462.text_edition: \n Bandu Picio Pellico Tangini filius votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-30000462.translation: Bandu Picio, son of Pellicus Tanginus, happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000462.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Bandu Picio, son of Pellicus Tanginus, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30000470.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio nobilissimo Caesari filio Imperatoris Caesaris Messi Quinti Traiani Deci Pii Felicis Augusti res publica Emeritensium devota numini maiestatique eorum\n
EDCS-30000470.translation: To Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, the public community of the people of Emerita, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-30000470.summary: This is a public dedication from the city of Emerita to the imperial family, specifically honoring Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, son of Emperor Decius, dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000471.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Aulus.
EDCS-30000471.summary: This is a workshop or product label from the workshop of Gaius Aulus.

EDCS-30000472.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Aulus.
EDCS-30000472.summary: This is a workshop or product label from the workshop of Gaius Aulus.

EDCS-30000473.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Aulus.
EDCS-30000473.summary: This is a workshop or product label from the workshop of Gaius Aulus.

EDCS-30000474.summary: This is a workshop or product label from the workshop of Gaius (Aulus).

EDCS-30000475.summary: This inscription seems to indicate ownership or origin from Publius Asinius Feridius, possibly a craftsman or merchant.

EDCS-30000480.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Aemilius Amandae libertus Iucundus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Cnaeus Aemilius Servolus annorum XXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Cnaeus Aemilius Cnaei filius Papiria Callaecus annorum XXIIX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-30000480.translation: Gnaeus Aemilius Iucundus, freedman of Amanda, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Gnaeus Aemilius Servolus, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Gnaeus Aemilius Callaecus, son of Gnaeus, of the Papiria tribe, aged 28, is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-30000480.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of three individuals, Gnaeus Aemilius Iucundus, Gnaeus Aemilius Servolus, and Gnaeus Aemilius Callaecus, with wishes for their peaceful repose.

EDCS-30000481.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Valerius Bespa nnorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Aelia Lupa in honorem Aeli Menophili et Caeciliae Graeculae fecit\n
EDCS-30000481.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius Bespa, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Aelia Lupa made this in honor of Aelius Menophilus and Caecilia Graecula.
EDCS-30000481.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Valerius Bespa and was set up by Aelia Lupa in memory of Aelius Menophilus and Caecilia Graecula.

EDCS-30000482.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Messia Titi liberta Maura Emeritensis nnorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Titus Messius Maurio matri piissimae \n
EDCS-30000482.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Maura Emeritensis, freedwoman of Titus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Titus Messius Maurio made this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-30000482.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maura Emeritensis, erected by her son Titus Messius Maurio.

EDCS-30000483.text_edition: \n Sunua Laeti filius annorum XV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Cesea mater de sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30000483.translation: Sunua, son of Laetus, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Cesea, his mother, arranged for this to be made at her own expense.
EDCS-30000483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sunua, paid for by his mother Cesea.

EDCS-30000484.text_edition: \n Quintus Caenoni filius annorum XII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Camira mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30000484.translation: Quintus, son of Caenonius, aged 12, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Camira, his mother, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-30000484.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus, paid for by his mother Camira.

EDCS-30000485.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Kaliope nnorum X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-30000485.translation: To the departed spirits. Kalliope, aged 10 years, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-30000485.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a girl named Kalliope, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600041.text_edition: \n Aleinius Tongi filius Genio Miaici aram pos<u=S>it \n
EDCS-33600041.translation: Aleinius, son of Tongius, erected this altar to the Genius of Miaicus.
EDCS-33600041.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to a genius, likely from the Roman Imperial period, possibly in a rural setting.

EDCS-33600042.text_edition: \n Gaio Sentio Quirina Capitoni R ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-33600042.translation: Gaius Sentius had this put in place for Quirinus Capitolinus.
EDCS-33600042.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Gaius Sentius for Quirinus Capitolinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600043.text_edition: \n La Ti filius Cluniensis annorum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-33600043.translation: La, son of Ti, from Clunia, aged 30 years, is placed here.
EDCS-33600043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man from Clunia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600047.text_edition: \n Ammae Araceleni sacrum Clemens Celeris Licinius Cili de suo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-33600047.translation: Clemens, Celeris, and Licinius had this sacred dedication made at their own expense.
EDCS-33600047.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication made at the expense of three individuals, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600048.text_edition: \n Arco Tureni filius et Tangina Bealoni filia hic situs est!\n
EDCS-33600048.translation: Arco, son of Turenius, and Tangina, daughter of Bealon, are placed here!
EDCS-33600048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a couple, Arco and Tangina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600051.text_edition: \n matri suae Cutece Auniae liberta faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-33600051.translation: had this made for his mother, Cutece, freedwoman of Aunius.
EDCS-33600051.summary: This inscription records someone commissioning a monument for his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Haretusa vixit nnos IIII menses V pia in suis hic sita est tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-33600053.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Aurelia Haretusa lived 4 years, 5 months. Pious, she is placed here among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-33600053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aurelia Haretusa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600054.text_edition: \n Quintus Pomponius Potentinus Sergia hic situs est Caius Pomponius Potentinus miles cohortis IIII praetoriae testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-33600054.translation: Quintus Pomponius Potentinus and Sergia are placed here. Gaius Pomponius Potentinus, a soldier of the 4th Praetorian Cohort, ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-33600054.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial arrangements for Quintus Pomponius Potentinus and Sergia, with a soldier from the Praetorian Guard ordering their own monument in his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600055.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator XVIII consul XIII censor perpetuus pater patriae pon\n
EDCS-33600055.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Vespasian, Domitianus Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 8th time, commander for the 18th time, consul for the 13th time, perpetual censor, father of the country, placed...
EDCS-33600055.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the titles and honors of Emperor Domitian, dating to his reign (81-96 AD).

EDCS-34300017.text_edition: \n Cadiae Tangini Selocae Maelouxori R MAI faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-34300017.translation: Cadia Tangini Seloca of Maelouxor had this made.
EDCS-34300017.summary: This inscription records an order for construction by Cadia Tangini Seloca, likely a woman of local origin, possibly for a funerary monument.

EDCS-34300018.text_edition: \n Cabureina Maelonis filia fratri ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-34300018.translation: Cabureina, daughter of Maelo, had this made as directed by her brother's will.
EDCS-34300018.summary: This inscription records an order for construction by Cabureina, initiated by her brother's will, likely for a funerary monument.

EDCS-34300021.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Bruttius Segontius, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-34300022.translation: To Fuscus, son of Bolbus; to Procula, daughter of Fuscus [---]
EDCS-34300022.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Fuscus and Procula, possibly a family tombstone, with incomplete information.

EDCS-34300026.text_edition: \n Arantonius Doquiri filius annorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-34300026.translation: Arantonius, son of Doquir, is buried here.
EDCS-34300026.summary: This inscription records the burial of Arantonius, son of Doquir, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-34300036.translation: Camala, daughter of Tai, is buried here.
EDCS-34300036.summary: This is a simple tombstone recording the burial of Camala, daughter of Tai.

EDCS-34300040.translation: Colea, daughter of Mani, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300040.summary: This is a tombstone for Colea, daughter of Mani, who died at age 60, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-34300041.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Longiniana and Maria Sunua had this made for their husband.
EDCS-34300041.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the spirits of the departed, records Longiniana and Maria Sunua commissioning a monument for their husband.

EDCS-34300042.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lubane, daughter of Amoena, aged 32. Amoena had this made for her daughter.
EDCS-34300042.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the spirits of the departed, records Amoena commissioning a monument for her daughter Lubane, who died at age 32.

EDCS-34300043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Siloni Docquiri filio annorum L Sila Silonis marito faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-34300043.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sila, wife of Silon, had this made for her son Siloni, son of Docqirus, aged 50.
EDCS-34300043.summary: This inscription, dedicated to the spirits of the departed, records Sila commissioning a monument for her son Siloni, who died at age 50.

EDCS-34300044.translation: Dobiteina, daughter of Maelo, is buried here.
EDCS-34300044.summary: This is a tombstone for Dobiteina, identified by her father's name, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-34300045.text_edition: \n Iuliae Quinti filiae Modestae Lucio Iulio Rufini filio Quirina Fraterno Lucius Iulius Cutaecus heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-34300045.translation: Lucius Julius Cutaecus, as heir according to the will, took care to have this made for Julia Modesta, daughter of Quintus, and for Lucius Julius Fraterno, son of Quirinus.
EDCS-34300045.summary: This epitaph records the construction of a monument by an heir in memory of Julia Modesta and Lucius Julius Fraterno, likely a family dedication.

EDCS-34300090.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Philete, mother, had this made for her most dutiful daughter Superata.
EDCS-34300090.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) by a mother for her daughter, Superata, highlighting filial piety.

EDCS-34300101.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Julius Reburrus, father, and Iulia Iusta, mother, [dedicated this] to their most dutiful son, Lucius Julius Iustus, aedile, aged 28.
EDCS-34300101.summary: This epitaph honors Lucius Julius Iustus, an aedile who died at 28, by his grieving parents.

EDCS-34300562.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anna, aged 20, [...
EDCS-34300562.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Anna, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34300563.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vironus, aged 60/80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300563.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Vironus, specifying his age and including a common epitaph wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34300574.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Annius Capito, aged 60. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius, aged 3.
EDCS-34300574.summary: This inscription appears to record two separate burials: one for Annius Capito and another for a young child named Aurelius.

EDCS-34300876.translation: Tancinus, son of Dovilo, from Caura, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Talticus, son of Caenon.
EDCS-34300876.summary: This is a tombstone for Tancinus, son of Dovilo, who died at 30, with an additional name, Talticus, son of Caenon, possibly related.

EDCS-34300951.translation: Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, consul for the third time, tribune of the plebs for the third time.
EDCS-34300951.summary: This is a fragment of a public inscription recording the honors and offices held by Marcus Agrippa.

EDCS-34301044.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus [Valerius Maximianus?], chief priest, [greatest?] Emperor, [most?] courageous Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-34301044.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Emperor Maximian, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-34301047.translation: Brevior. Men of 105 years are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Potitus, son of Vegetus, erected this dedication.
EDCS-34301047.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of elderly men, erected by Potitus in honor of Vegetus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301048.translation: To Venus Augusta. Antestia, freedwoman of Anuis, gladly paid her vow.
EDCS-34301048.summary: A dedication to the goddess Venus Augusta, offered by her freedwoman Antestia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301061.translation: Secunda, daughter of Abentina, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. C. N. had this made by his will.
EDCS-34301061.summary: A tombstone for Secunda, aged 18, erected by an unknown individual (C. N.) through their will, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301062.translation: Gracilis, son of Arconis (?), aged [3], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34301062.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Gracilis, son of Arconis (?), whose age is partially lost, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301063.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Rufina, daughter of Rufus, aged 70, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Norbana Modesta (?), daughter [...], erected this.
EDCS-34301063.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Rufina, aged 70, erected by Norbana Modesta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301064.translation: [Emperor] Caesar Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 28th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the country, the Augustan boundary between the [3]obrigenses and the Avilenses.
EDCS-34301064.summary: A milestone or boundary marker commissioned by Emperor Augustus, defining the territory of the Avilenses and [3]obrigenses, likely dating to his later reign.

EDCS-34500972.translation: In the consulship of Nero Claudius Caesar III, Vitulus and Proculus, brothers of Valerius from Tarmestinum, gladly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-34500972.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by the brothers Vitulus and Proculus of the Tarmestini family during the third consulship of Nero Claudius Caesar, likely from the early to mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-34900010.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Callec us, aged 13 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. // Sacred to the Divine Shades. Apruncula, aged 6 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900010.summary: A funerary inscription with two separate burials, Callec us (13) and Apruncula (6), both recorded as lying here with the wish for light earth, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900029.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Batia, son of Patricius (?), servant of the Emperor (?), aged 50, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900029.summary: A tombstone for Batia, son of Patricius (?), possibly an imperial servant, aged 50, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900030.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia, daughter of Slavi, aged 59, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900030.summary: A tombstone for Flavia, daughter of Slavi, aged 59, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900032.translation: To Annia Reburrina, aged 34, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-34900032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Reburrina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900067.translation: To Amania, daughter of Virio, aged 30, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-34900067.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amania, daughter of Virio, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900094.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger, freedman of our lord Caesar.
EDCS-34900094.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Constantius II, likely made by his freedman, dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-34900095.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, son of Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, restored this (structure).
EDCS-34900095.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Emperor Trajan, likely commemorating a restoration project, dating to his reign (98-117 CE).

EDCS-34900097.translation: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, father of the soldiers, with tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva.
EDCS-34900097.summary: This is a lengthy titulature of Emperor Caracalla, recording his extensive lineage and imperial honors, dating to around 213 CE.

EDCS-34900121.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Aemilia Luculla, daughter of Lucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900122.translation: Lucius Annius Rufus and Annia Sabina, daughter of Lucius. Titus Julius Modes, son of Titus, placed this (tomb) from his own funds.
EDCS-34900122.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Annius Rufus and Annia Sabina, with Titus Julius Modes paying for the monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900124.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Moschis, aged 30, is buried here, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Valerius Daphnus, for his excellent wife, and Lucius Valerius Caecilianus, for his most dutiful mother, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-34900124.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Valerius Daphnus and Lucius Valerius Caecilianus for their wife and mother, Caecilia Moschis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900128.translation: Dobitena, daughter of Saellus, is buried here.
EDCS-34900128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dobitena, daughter of Saellus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900130.translation: Marcus Flavius Primus made a memorial for himself and for his patron Marcus Flavius Rufus with honor.
EDCS-34900130.summary: This inscription records a memorial commissioned by Marcus Flavius Primus for himself and his patron, Marcus Flavius Rufus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900131.translation: Here lies [---]. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900131.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900132.translation: Dedicated to the divine shades. Lucius Iulius Amoenus, aged 24, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cassia Amoena made this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-34900132.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Amoenus, erected by his mother Cassia Amoena, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900134.translation: Lucius Iulius Rufus, Iulia Sabina, daughter of Lucius. They lie here. Titus Iulius, son of Titus, paid for this from his own funds.
EDCS-34900134.summary: A funerary inscription recording Lucius Iulius Rufus and Iulia Sabina, with Titus Iulius paying for the monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900137.translation: Dedicated to the divine shades. Lucceius Dorion, aged 35. Prosodia Severa, his wife, took care to have this made for her most devoted husband. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucceius Dorion, erected by his wife Prosodia Severa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900139.translation: [Titus Barbatius] Placidus took care to have this made for his wife from his own funds. Barbatia Placida, daughter of Titus, aged 4 years and 3 months, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Barbatia Quarta, freedwoman of Placidus, aged 37 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900139.summary: A funerary inscription for Barbatia Placida and her freedwoman Barbatia Quarta, with Titus Barbatius Placidus presumably initiating the monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900141.translation: For Valeria Maxuminae, aged 38 years, [---] days. Valerius Lupus for his incomparable wife. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Maxuminae, erected by her husband Valerius Lupus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900142.translation: [---] Gaius Vibius Clymenus lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900142.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Vibius Clymenus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900146.summary: A very fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for an individual named Pat[---], dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900158.translation: Dedicated to the divine shades. Setinus Epaphroditus, slave of Tiberius Claudius, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900158.summary: A funerary epitaph for Setinus Epaphroditus, a slave, erected by his master (implied), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900226.translation: Dedicated to the divine shades. Aiatio Aprocome, aged 70. Aiatia Eurodice made this for her father.
EDCS-34900226.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aiatio Aprocome, erected by his daughter Aiatia Eurodice, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900228.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sempronia Mart(a), aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sempronius Firminus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-34900228.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Mart(a), set up by her husband Sempronius Firminus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700018.translation: Atticus, freedman of Quintus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-35700018.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atticus, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700019.translation: Avelius Peiconis, son of, aged 60, and Avelea Cancili, daughter of, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly upon you both. Sunua, daughter of Avelius, and Ca[...
EDCS-35700019.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph records the burial of Avelius and Avelea, with Sunua likely setting up the monument for them, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700020.translation: Auna, daughter of Tancinus, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Trio Cantoni and Pinarea Cant[oni]...
EDCS-35700020.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph records the burial of Auna, daughter of Tancinus, with Trio Cantoni and Pinarea Cantoni possibly being her relatives or dedicators, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700021.translation: [...]VI, [...]VIII. [...] lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Boutia, daughter of Tanginus, the mother, saw to the making of this.
EDCS-35700021.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph records the burial of an unnamed individual, for whom Boutia, likely the mother, commissioned the monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700022.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph mentioning Bolosa, daughter of Proculus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700024.translation: Apana, to her son Catueno, son of Tancinus. Catueno, mother, saw to the making of this.
EDCS-35700024.summary: This funerary epitaph records a mother, Catueno, commissioning a monument for her son, Catueno, son of Tancinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35700027.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aibarus, son of Madui, aged 40, lies here.
EDCS-35700027.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Aibarus, son of Madui, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Iunius Theseus an(norum) L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Publicia marito optimo \n
EDCS-36400181.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Iunius Theseus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publicia made this for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-36400181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Theseus, set up by his wife Publicia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400184.text_edition: \n Hordine sic si iusto placuisset currere fatis hic non debuit mori annorum XX set sic potius ante pater et mater debuerunt esse sepulti infelices miserum tumulum faciendum curaverunt parentes hic erit nunc tumuli dominus hospitio miseros invitat parentes Lucius Avinius Tropimus pater et Lucceia Urbana mater Lucio Avinio Avito filio pientissimo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-36400184.translation: If fate had wished him to run his course justly, he would not have had to die at the age of 20; rather, it would have been better for his father and mother to have been buried first. The unhappy parents saw to the making of this wretched tomb, for now he will be the master of the tomb, inviting guests with his hospitality. The parents Lucius Avinius Tropimus and Lucceia Urbana made this for their most dutiful son Lucius Avinius Avitus.
EDCS-36400184.summary: This funerary epitaph laments the premature death of Lucius Avinius Avitus at 20, with his parents Lucius Avinius Tropimus and Lucceia Urbana commissioning the monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400185.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio ori Spuri filio o o hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-36400185.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius, son of Spurius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-36400185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius, son of Spurius, with a common Latin formula wishing the deceased a light burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400242.text_edition: \n Rufus Rufini filius Arospicis annorum L curavi faciando patrono meo Cabureia liberta hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-36400242.translation: Rufus, son of Rufinus from Arospicis, aged 50. I took care to have this made for my patron Cabureia, freedwoman. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-36400242.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cabureia, a freedwoman, commissioned by Rufus, son of Rufinus, who was her patron, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400243.text_edition: \n Mantaus Mogulini libertus nesis aram deae Nabiae Mutinacae merito libens fecit\n
EDCS-36400243.translation: Mantaus, freedman of Mogulinus, willingly and deservedly made this altar to the goddess Nabia Mutinaca.
EDCS-36400243.summary: An altar dedicated to the goddess Nabia Mutinaca by Mantaus, a freedman, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900041.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus
EDCS-37900041.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording the titles of a Roman emperor, possibly Trajan or Hadrian, dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900055.translation: Alfia [---]. [---] Alfia, freedwoman of Marcus [---]. [---] Alfia MA[---]. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-37900055.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph mentioning at least one woman named Alfia and possibly her freedwoman status, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900057.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Athenais lived for 18 years. Her most devoted grandmother made this. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-37900057.summary: A funerary epitaph for Athenais, who died at 18, erected by her devoted grandmother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900061.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [---] to Claudia? Artemidor[---], to my incomparable wife [---] R[---]
EDCS-37900061.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for an individual named Artemidor, dedicated to an 'incomparable wife', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900065.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. F. Aeliana lived for 66 years. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. [---]
EDCS-37900065.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aeliana, who lived to 66, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900072.translation: [---] Publi[---] MNESAR[---]. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-37900072.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for at least two individuals, possibly pluralized as 'they lie here', dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900073.translation: Monteia Saturnina, aged 28. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Tiberius Claudius Thalamus took care to have this made for his wife.
EDCS-37900073.summary: A funerary epitaph for Monteia Saturnina, commissioned by her husband Tiberius Claudius Thalamus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37900077.translation: Primigenia, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Seven feet along the second wall, ten feet in the field.
EDCS-37900077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Primigenia, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE, indicating her burial plot dimensions.

EDCS-37900178.summary: This fragment is too incomplete to provide a meaningful translation or summary, but appears to be part of a Latin inscription.

EDCS-37900185.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-37900185.translation: Armiger, faithful, aged 17 years and 7 months. Severa, his mother, to her most devoted son, [dedicated this] memorial in this place, [far from home].
EDCS-37900185.summary: This funerary epitaph, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE, records the death of a young man named Armiger and was dedicated by his grieving mother.

EDCS-37900229.translation: Here lies [---]dorus, son of Me[---], from the ship, from beneath the earth.
EDCS-37900229.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly in Greek or a mixture of Greek and Latin, recording the burial of an individual who died at sea and was recovered.

EDCS-37900231.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman period, recording the burial of a person named Montanus.

EDCS-38000004.translation: Domitia, a young girl, lived 1 year, 4 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-38000004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Domitia, recording her age at death, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-38100234.translation: To Marcus Sergius Tro[---], to his most indulgent wife
EDCS-38100234.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a dedication or funerary inscription, mentioning Marcus Sergius and his wife.

EDCS-38100235.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph, mentioning Publius Speratus and his age at death.

EDCS-38100238.translation: Valeria Aunia, daughter of Gaius, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Iulius Lupus, son of Gaius, from the Galeria tribe, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Gaius Iulius Potitus saw to the making [of these] for his wife and son.
EDCS-38100238.summary: This is a paired funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, recording the burials of Valeria Aunia and Lucius Iulius Lupus, with Gaius Iulius Potitus responsible for their monuments.

EDCS-38100240.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly a list of names or part of a larger text, making its purpose unclear.

EDCS-38100252.translation: To the departed gods. Felicia, a servant, aged 61. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her devoted husband took care to have this made.
EDCS-38100252.summary: A funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Felicia, who died at 61, erected by her husband, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100254.translation: Licinia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-38100254.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100255.translation: Norban(a) Rufina, daughter of Rufus, aged 2. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-38100255.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Norban(a) Rufina, aged 2, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100258.translation: Sextus Norbanus Rufinus, aged 10, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-38100258.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named Sextus Norbanus Rufinus, aged 10, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100260.translation: Norbana Maurila, daughter of Caius, aged 30, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-38100260.summary: A funerary epitaph for Norbana Maurila, daughter of Caius, aged 30, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100261.translation: Quadratus, son of Quadratus, aged 6. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His grandmother took care to have this made.
EDCS-38100261.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named Quadratus, aged 6, erected by his grandmother, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100263.translation: Tertia, daughter of Caenonis, aged 20, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-38100263.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertia, daughter of Caenonis, aged 20, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100272.translation: To the departed gods. Marcus [---]tius Cl[---]dian[us ---]onis [---]mai [---] is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. [---]a V BE [---]si VI [---]t
EDCS-38100272.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph to the 'departed gods' (Dis Manibus), mentioning a man named Marcus and some numerical notations, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100292.translation: [---]cius [---]us of Emerita; Restilia, daughter of Caius, of Emerita [---]a [---] are laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-38100292.summary: A funerary epitaph for a married couple, possibly named Restilia and her husband, both from Emerita (modern Mérida, Spain), likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38100293.translation: Tancinus, son of Matuenus, is laid here.
EDCS-38100293.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Tancinus, son of Matuenus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-38700007.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Victorina, possibly a funerary epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-39700075.translation: To Emperor Caesar Maximinus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, ranked CXVIII. To our lord Flavius Valerius [In]victus Augustus, noble, [ranked] CXVIII.
EDCS-39700075.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicating or honoring emperors Maximinus and Flavius Valerius Constantinus Augustus, dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-39900622.text_edition: \n Daeae sic sanctae Turibrigensi Publius Florius Venustus libens animo posuit\n
EDCS-39900622.translation: Publius Florius Venustus willingly set this up to the sacred Daeae of Turibrigis.
EDCS-39900622.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Florius Venustus to local deities called Daeae of Turibrigis, likely from Roman Britain.

EDCS-39900626.translation: Lucius Murrius Rufinus, aged 10, is laid here.
EDCS-39900626.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Murrius Rufinus, who died at the age of 10, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-39900627.translation: Lucretia, daughter of Quintus Anus, aged 30, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Tibrilius saw to its construction.
EDCS-39900627.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucretia, daughter of Quintus, who died at 30, with a standard epitaph and a funerary commission, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-39900628.translation: Alluquius, son of Protaida, is laid here.
EDCS-39900628.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone for Alluquius, son of Protaida, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-39900633.translation: Lucius Marcius Baccinus, freedman of Lucius, aged 55, is laid here. Kania Phyrallis, freedwoman of his wife, saw to its construction after his death.
EDCS-39900633.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Marcius Baccinus, aged 55, with his wife's freedwoman commissioning the monument after his death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-39900640.translation: Gaius Norbanus Rufus is laid here.
EDCS-39900640.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Norbanus Rufus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700171.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius Primus, aged 18, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Valeria Veneria, a dutiful mother, saw to its construction for her most excellent and most dutiful son.
EDCS-42700171.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Valerius Primus, aged 18, commissioned by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700173.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Asclepiace, aged 15 years and 5 months, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Vibius Asclepiades, brother, and Publicia Olivola, made this for their sweetest and most dutiful cousin.
EDCS-42700173.summary: This is a tombstone for Vibia Asclepiace, aged 15 years and 5 months, commissioned by her brother and cousin, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700176.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Vicarius Vitalis, servant of the Juventian youth group, lived for 3 years, 9 months, and 19 days. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cornelia Corinthia Anna saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-42700176.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of a young boy named Vicarius Vitalis, erected by Cornelia Corinthia Anna, likely his mother or caretaker, in the late Roman period.

EDCS-42700190.translation: ...ricia, his wife, saw to the making of this monument. Felicio, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. A home-born slave.
EDCS-42700190.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Felicio, a young man, possibly a slave, erected by his wife (name lost), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700197.summary: A brief funerary epitaph for Aelia Septuma, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700209.translation: For Philumenes... Decimus Caecilius Socrates made this for his exceedingly rare wife and for himself while alive.
EDCS-42700209.summary: A funerary and self-dedicatory inscription for Philumenes, commissioned by Decimus Caecilius Socrates for his wife and himself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700212.translation: ... Secundus... aged 60... daughter aged 3 and... They lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Venusta saw to the making of this monument according to the will; this monument will not follow the heir.
EDCS-42700212.summary: A funerary inscription for Secundus, his daughter, and another individual, with Venusta arranging the burial according to a will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700224.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Quintus, son of Quintus, who died at age 19, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700231.translation: ...aged 13, lies here... Saturni[3]ae Vitali[3], aged 13, lies here...
EDCS-42700231.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for at least two individuals, possibly two girls named Saturni[3]ae and Vitali[3], both aged 13, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700232.translation: ... his wife saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-42700232.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating a wife commissioned a monument, likely funerary, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-42700242.summary: A heavily fragmented inscription, possibly part of a name or title, with no clear context or date discernible.

EDCS-42700282.translation: The eleventh day before the Kalends of August (July 22nd) at Postumino.
EDCS-42700282.summary: A date and location reference, possibly related to an event, contract, or administrative record from the Roman period.

EDCS-42700298.translation: Emerita, Roman citizen? A[---]. Julius Zosimus [...] placed [it].
EDCS-42700298.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Zosimus, who may have been a Roman citizen from Emerita, with the dedicator and full relationship of the deceased to the dedicator missing.

EDCS-42700407.translation: To the Invincible God, for the health of Gaius Julius [---].
EDCS-42700407.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to the god Invictus for the health of Gaius Julius, likely from Emerita.

EDCS-42700408.translation: To the Invincible God, Gaius Camilius Superatus willingly placed [this].
EDCS-42700408.summary: This is a votive inscription from Emerita where Gaius Camilius Superatus dedicates something to the god Invictus out of free will.

EDCS-42700419.translation: Tiberius Claudius Fronto, veteran of the Papiria tribe, Roman knight, subdecurion of the Taurus cavalry wing, standard-bearer of the baggage train, aged 80, lies here for himself and his family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700419.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Fronto, a Roman knight and veteran of the Taurus cavalry wing, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-42700428.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sentius Victor erected [this] for his dearest wife Sentia Amarantis, who lived 17 years, aged 45.
EDCS-42700428.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Sentius Victor for his wife Sentia Amarantis, who died after 17 years of marriage and was 45 years old, likely from Emerita.

EDCS-42700430.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Here lies Herennius, slave of the colony of Emerita, aged 27. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucceia Herennia, mother, made [this].
EDCS-42700430.summary: This is a tombstone for Herennius, a slave of Emerita, erected by his mother Lucceia Herennia.

EDCS-42700435.translation: Titus Manlius, son of Lucius, from Segobriga, lies here.
EDCS-42700435.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Manlius, a native of Segobriga, who lies buried in Emerita.

EDCS-42700442.translation: To the Divine Shades, in honor and memory of Afinius Nepos.
EDCS-42700442.summary: This is a posthumous dedication to the gods of the underworld for Afinius Nepos.

EDCS-42700446.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Bassina, aged 10 [---] [---].
EDCS-42700446.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone for Bassina, a child aged 10, likely from Emerita.

EDCS-42700454.translation: Gaius Herennius Saturninus, aged 60, for whom his devoted wife placed [this] for her husband, a distinguished man. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700454.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a devoted wife for her husband, Gaius Herennius Saturninus, a distinguished man of 60, likely from Emerita.

EDCS-42700456.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Festiva lived for 35 years. Porcus made this for his most devoted wife. She lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700456.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Festiva at 35 years old and its dedication by her husband, Porcus.

EDCS-42700458.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Firmana, 32 years old, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Titus Flavius Amethystus and Titus Flavius Beryllus took care to have this made for their most holy wife and most devoted mother, respectively, in their gardens.
EDCS-42700458.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Firmana at 32 years old, dedicated by her husband and son.

EDCS-42700472.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [A name or title missing] Lucceius Restutus, 55 years old, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Attenia Thetis took care to have this made for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-42700472.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucceius Restutus at 55 years old, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-42700474.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcia Tyche nnorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Marcius Laetinus et Marcius Reburrus liberti patronae piissimae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-42700474.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Tyche, 50 years old, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Marcius Laetinus and Marcius Reburrus, freedmen, took care to have this made for their most devoted patroness.
EDCS-42700474.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Marcia Tyche at 50 years old, dedicated by her freedmen.

EDCS-42800001.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Saturnina, 45 years old, incomparable wife, excellent doctor, most holy woman. Cassius Philippus, her husband, for her merits. She lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42800001.summary: A funerary epitaph honoring Julia Saturnina, a 45-year-old doctor, dedicated by her husband for her merits.

EDCS-43200023.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decumus Callaburi, son, 110 years old, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His sons took care [of this].
EDCS-43200023.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Decumus Callaburi at the remarkable age of 110, dedicated by his sons.

EDCS-43200024.translation: Uprila Severa, slave, 2 years old. Fortunata, mother, made this.
EDCS-43200024.summary: A funerary epitaph for a two-year-old slave girl named Uprila Severa, made by her mother.

EDCS-43200025.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Crescentius, 6 years old [illegible]. Cefrinus, 9 years old. They lie here.
EDCS-43200025.summary: A funerary inscription for two children, Crescentius (6) and Cefrinus (9), who are buried together.

EDCS-43200026.translation: Macrius Tancini and Betua Cosuri, daughter of Titus, took care to have this made from their own resources by testament. They lie here. May the earth rest lightly upon you both.
EDCS-43200026.summary: A funerary epitaph for Macrius Tancini and Betua Cosuri, commissioned by testament, who lie buried together.

EDCS-43200062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Saturninus, son, of the Quirina tribe from Aquileia, 22 years old. Severa, daughter of Severus Flaccus, took care to have this made for her pious nephew.
EDCS-43200062.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Saturninus, a 22-year-old man from Aquileia, dedicated by his aunt.

EDCS-43300042.translation: Tongius, son of Lucius Coutius, saw to the making of this for his wife.
EDCS-43300042.summary: This funerary inscription records that Tongius commissioned a monument for his wife.

EDCS-43300061.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Iulio Crispo nobilissimo Caesari CX\n
EDCS-43300061.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Crispus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-43300061.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Julius Crispus, a Caesar, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-43300064.translation: Flavus and Duitera, freely, made a vow.
EDCS-43300064.summary: This dedication records a vow made by Flavus and Duitera.

EDCS-43300072.translation: Mearus, son of Meiduenus, willingly fulfilled his vow to the god Quangeius.
EDCS-43300072.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Mearus, son of Meiduenus, fulfilling a vow to the god Quangeius.

EDCS-43300079.text_edition: \n sus annorum? V ex testamento hic siti sunt tibi terra levis SER OR faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-43300079.translation: A [child?] of [---] years, V. They are buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. SER OR saw to its construction.
EDCS-43300079.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a person, possibly a child, with a relative named SER OR responsible for the monument, and includes a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-43300080.text_edition: \n Quintus Servilius Quinti filius Papiria Celer annorum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-43300080.translation: Quintus Servilius Celer, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-43300080.summary: This is a standard tombstone inscription recording the name, parentage, tribe, age, and burial location of Quintus Servilius Celer.

EDCS-43300081.text_edition: \n sit-- sit tibi terra levis Caesilia Anulla amica de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-43300081.translation: May the earth rest lightly upon you. Caesilia Anulla, a friend, saw to the making of this from her own resources.
EDCS-43300081.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caesilia Anulla commissioned a grave marker for someone, possibly a friend, out of her own funds.

EDCS-43300082.text_edition: \n Quintus Artorius Quinti filius Papiria Vetto annorum V hic situs sit tibi terra levis frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-43300082.translation: Quintus Artorius, son of Quintus, aged 5, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His brother saw to its construction.
EDCS-43300082.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a child, Quintus Artorius, aged 5, with his brother responsible for the monument.

EDCS-43300086.translation: [Seppius] Titu[rius?], aged [3?], is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His grandmother saw to its construction.
EDCS-43300086.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Seppius Titu[rius?], with his grandmother responsible for the monument.

EDCS-43300089.translation: Abimp[---], a slave, is buried here.
EDCS-43300089.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a slave named Abimp[---].

EDCS-43300105.translation: Fuginus, son of Angetus, lies here, a fellow member and brother to Angetus.
EDCS-43300105.summary: A funerary inscription for Fuginus, son of Angetus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire, mentioning his fraternal and collegial connections.

EDCS-43300129.text_edition: \n Fatranis Vegetus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Apana\n
EDCS-43300129.translation: Fatranis Vegetus lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300129.summary: A simple tombstone inscription for Fatranis Vegetus, likely from the Imperial period, wishing him a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300135.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Quintus V() R() lived 45 years; he lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Norbana Maxima had this made for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-43300135.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus V() R(), likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, commemorating him and erected by his wife, Norbana Maxima.

EDCS-43400556.translation: Avia Martia, daughter of Rufus, aged 65, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. L() Modesta, daughter, had this made.
EDCS-43400556.summary: A funerary inscription for Avia Martia, aged 65, likely from the Imperial period, with a daughter L() Modesta as the dedicator.

EDCS-44100063.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or memorial, to a son of someone named Constantii.

EDCS-44100074.translation: Decimus Helvius Ligur lies here, in the field 8 feet [deep], in front 14 feet [wide].
EDCS-44100074.summary: A funerary inscription for Decimus Helvius Ligur, likely from the Roman Imperial period, which also records the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-44100086.translation: [3 Iustulus?], son of Publius, Papiria Aper, veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina, Pious and Faithful, aged 65, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100086.summary: A tombstone inscription for Aper, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina, who died at age 65, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-44100087.translation: Lucius Helvius Rebilus, of the Papirian tribe, Augustus's man, veteran of the Sixth Legion Victrix, Pious and Faithful, aged 55, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100087.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Helvius Rebilus, a veteran of the Sixth Legion Victrix and an Augustalis, who died at age 55, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-44100089.translation: Sertoria Tulla, daughter of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-44100089.summary: A simple tombstone inscription for Sertoria Tulla, daughter of Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44500123.translation: Naltivi Nudira Bitaciu, Boutia Endoseli, Pucius
EDCS-44500123.summary: A fragmentary inscription with several personal names, possibly a list of individuals or a funerary record, its exact nature is unclear due to damage.

EDCS-44800407.translation: Here lies; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Publicius Vitalio and Publicius Flavianus set this up for their father.
EDCS-44800407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording the dedication of a tomb by two sons for their deceased father.

EDCS-45000126.translation: For Jupiter, he set up an altar out of concern for his own welfare, and willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-45000126.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording the dedication of an altar to Jupiter for the dedicator's health.

EDCS-45000143.translation: Lancius, son of Tanginus, lies here.
EDCS-45000143.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, identifying the deceased and his father.

EDCS-45200067.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Pater Patriae, Tribune for the 5th time, Emperor for the 7th time, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar, Prince of Youth, son of our Lords Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, the most valiant and most noble Prince, restored (this) XXXIIX.
EDCS-45200067.summary: This is a monumental inscription, likely from the 3rd century AD, recording the restoration of a public monument by the emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus.

EDCS-45200110.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-45200110.summary: This is a dedication inscription, likely from the late Roman Empire, possibly honoring Emperor Probus.

EDCS-46600190.translation: To Constantius Pius Felix Semper Augustus, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-46600190.summary: This is a dedication inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, honoring Emperor Constantius.

EDCS-48700036.translation: Gaius Licinius Helixs, freedman, 120 years old, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-48700036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording the death of a freedman who lived to a remarkable age.

EDCS-49300142.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, naming a freedwoman and possibly her patron.

EDCS-49800417.translation: Togae Veienta Tusca willingly paid her vow for the victory of Garicus Fuscinus.
EDCS-49800417.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording a woman's vow paid for a military victory.

EDCS-50900004.translation: [...]mi, son of [...], 60 years old, made this while alive. [...]oni, wife of [...], here lies; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-50900004.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording a self-made tomb and its intended occupants.

EDCS-51400899.translation: To Odriria, who lived 21 years, 2 months, and 11 days. Postumus to his most deserving wife, the first of her youth, beautiful in form and excellent, perished... a girl like herself...
EDCS-51400899.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Odriria, who died young, and was erected by her husband Postumus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-52900117.translation: Dedicated to the gods of the underworld. Glaucus, aged 43, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-52900117.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caecilius Glaucus, who died at the age of 43, and was likely erected in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53200199.translation: Aemilia Amanda, freedwoman of Haline, lies here.
EDCS-53200199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Aemilia Amanda, freedwoman of Haline, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400985.summary: This is a dedication inscription, possibly on a monument or statue base, with the dedicant identified as Hemeros, son of Quintus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400990.translation: Atta Allonica, wife of Ammonis Boutogenes, lies here.
EDCS-55400990.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Atta Allonica, wife of Ammonis Boutogenes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400991.translation: Lucius Arrunitius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-55400991.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Arrunitius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400992.translation: Rufus willingly fulfilled his vow to the Arbaria (?) gods.
EDCS-55400992.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Rufus fulfilling a vow to the gods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400994.translation: Publius Petronius Firmianus, aged 16, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-55400994.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Petronius Firmianus, who died at the age of 16, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400995.translation: Gaius Calvus, aged 55, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sextia, daughter (?) of Calvus...
EDCS-55400995.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Calvus, aged 55, and possibly his relative Sextia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400996.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Quintus Catius, with his age partially lost, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400997.translation: [Name] Perpetuus is buried here, aged 75 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-55400997.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph recording the burial of a man named Perpetuus and wishing him a light burial.

EDCS-55400998.translation: [His/Her] mother took care to have this made. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-55400998.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph indicating that a mother commissioned the monument and wishing the deceased a light burial.

EDCS-55401008.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Julius Fortunatus, aged 28, buried in Rome. Claudia Vitalis, his mother, and Julia, his sister, [erected this].
EDCS-55401008.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Fortunatus, mentioning his age, place of burial, and the family members who dedicated it: his mother Claudia Vitalis and sister Julia.

EDCS-55702052.translation: For Atimetus, physician, slave of Marcus Julius Rufus. Nothus, a student, slave of Caius Heius Primus.
EDCS-55702052.summary: This is an epitaph commemorating Atimetus, a physician and slave of Marcus Julius Rufus, and Nothus, a student and slave of Caius Heius Primus.

EDCS-55702054.text_edition: \n Macer Rufinus Macer sus us us o Marcus Iulius Quintus Iulius Marci filius Iulius Cai filius Marcus Iulius Cai filius Caius Umerius Cai filius Marcus Aemilius Marci filius Lucius Aemilius Marci filius Lucius Flavius Luci filius V Lucius Marcius Luci filius Publius Marcius Luci filius Lucius Fabius Aspectus Marcus Manilius Cai filius Caius Iulius Cai filius Satu\n
EDCS-55702054.translation: Macer Rufinus, Marcus Julius Quintus, son of Julius, son of Caius, Marcus Julius, son of Caius, Gaius Umerius, son of Caius, Marcus Aemilius, son of Marcus, Lucius Aemilius, son of Marcus, Lucius Flavius, son of Lucius V, Lucius Marcius, son of Lucius, Publius Marcius, son of Lucius, Lucius Fabius Aspectus, Marcus Manilius, son of Caius, Gaius Julius, son of Caius, Satu.
EDCS-55702054.summary: This inscription is a complex list of names, likely representing a family or a group associated with a property, possibly related to a legal document or land ownership.

EDCS-55702063.translation: For Petronia Prisca, aged 12 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Her mother, Petronia Secunda, [erected this].
EDCS-55702063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Prisca, who died at age 12, erected by her mother Petronia Secunda.

EDCS-56500211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [Name], a veteran of the cohort I milliary, of [origin]. Julia Bagai, aged 61 years. [A]n eternal home. To my wife Alli[a?]. Also [lived] 55 years. For the sake of remembrance [I] erected this monument so that an unknown lineage might become known, for you who are reading, when your time comes, will also be here.
EDCS-56500211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Bagai, wife of a veteran, detailing her age and intended to preserve the memory of her lineage, with a reflection on mortality.

EDCS-58800003.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marciana[e] [Name] erected a memorial. [Name]lovia, aged 65 years.
EDCS-58800003.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marciana, with a memorial erected by an individual whose name is partially preserved, and stating her age.

EDCS-58800005.translation: Gaius Norbanus Rufinus, son of Lucius, aged eighty years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-58800005.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Norbanus Rufinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period, recording his name, age, and burial location, with a standard epitaph formula.

EDCS-58800006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Britta, daughter of Alienus, aged [---] years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-58800006.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph for Britta, daughter of Alienus, dating to the Roman Imperial period, with standard funerary formulae.

EDCS-58800013.translation: Livinius Eutiches, 6 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-58800013.summary: This is a tombstone for a child named Livinius Eutiches, who died at the age of six.

EDCS-58800014.translation: Cilius, son of Pisirus, is buried here.
EDCS-58800014.summary: This is a tombstone for Cilius, son of Pisirus, with the rest of the inscription missing.

EDCS-58800015.translation: Julia Paula, daughter of Caenon, is buried here.
EDCS-58800015.summary: This is a tombstone for Julia Paula, daughter of Caenon.

EDCS-58800019.translation: Gaius Allonius Severinus had this made for his wife Sila, a most excellent woman from Norba.
EDCS-58800019.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Allonius Severinus to his wife Sila, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-59500351.translation: Lucius Aufidius Rebilus, son of Lucius of the Galeria tribe, is buried here. Aufidia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-59500351.summary: This is a double tombstone for Lucius Aufidius Rebilus and Aufidia Amoena, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300111.translation: To the departed spirits. Pompeius, son of Augustinus, had this made for Pompeius Tripo and Pompeius Doriccius.
EDCS-60300111.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated to the departed spirits, was erected by Pompeius, son of Augustinus, for Pompeius Tripo and Pompeius Doriccius, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-60300453.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary or votive, to a woman named Eulalia.

EDCS-60700036.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name and parentage of an individual.

EDCS-60700037.translation: Lucius Goutius Areni, son of Arenus, lies here.
EDCS-60700037.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Lucius Goutius, son of Arenus, likely from the Roman Imperial period, stating his name and burial.

EDCS-60700128.translation: Rufus, son of Caius Tutor, 30 years old. Rufinus, son of Rufus, 5 years old, are buried here. Rufina (?) for her brother, at her own expense. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-60700128.summary: This is a tombstone recording the deaths of Rufus and his young son Rufinus, with a dedication by Rufina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800091.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maganus, son of Nigrinus, aged 60, had this made. To his brother Cabureina Nigrini, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-60800091.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Maganus and his brother, with the latter's burial arranged by Maganus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61200002.translation: To the god and goddess, Uliso Igotereuno and Ulisoniae. Aquilius Modesti, son of Modestus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-61200002.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to a pair of deities, likely local ones, by Aquilius Modesti, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61200003.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Anna, mother of Coro, made this for Atte[t]icus.
EDCS-61200003.summary: A funerary inscription recording the dedication of a monument by Anna for her son Atte[t]icus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61400238.translation: Tongius, son of Arcon, set up this altar to the god Quangeius and fulfilled his vow in the sanctuary.
EDCS-61400238.summary: A votive inscription recording the erection of an altar to the god Quangeius by Tongius, son of Arcon, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-62900326.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Helius had this made for his most dutiful son, Caius Valerius Fortunatus, aged 22.
EDCS-62900326.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a father to his son, Caius Valerius Fortunatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000003.translation: Ma[ur]a(?) Severa, a slave girl, aged 30, lies here. Saturninus had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-63000003.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a slave woman, Maura, aged 30, with the monument erected by Saturninus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000011.translation: Masonius, aged 52, lies here, and Laurentius, aged 51, lies here.
EDCS-63000011.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of two individuals, Masonius and Laurentius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000026.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. SEPELICIA
EDCS-63000026.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly a praenomen or nomen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000156.translation: Pastor set up this altar to Jupiter and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-63000156.summary: A votive inscription recording the erection of an altar to Jupiter by Pastor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63100005.translation: Titus H. Flaccus, aged 80, a veteran of the VI Legion Constantis Fidele, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His wife, Iulia, daughter of Caius Modesta, made this according to his will.
EDCS-63100005.summary: A funerary monument for a veteran of the VI Legion, Titus H. Flaccus, with his wife Iulia making arrangements according to his will, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.
