EDCS-03700398.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Hercules, funded and arranged by Gaius Flavius, son of Aulus.

EDCS-03700399.translation: Pei Lurta, daughter of Conceltus, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-03700399.summary: This is a tombstone for Pei Lurta, daughter of Conceltus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-03700402.translation: Turaesius, son of Turcaudus, and Pans, son of Maeilo, may the earth lie lightly upon them.
EDCS-03700402.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Turaesius and Pans, with a common wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-03700403.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely identifying an individual named Caeno, son of Loucinus, possibly on a tomb or property marker.

EDCS-03700405.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturninus, son of Boutus, which is incomplete regarding his age.

EDCS-03700408.translation: Lucius Norbanus Rufus, son of Lucius, aged 55.
EDCS-03700408.summary: This is an inscription, likely funerary, identifying Lucius Norbanus Rufus, son of Lucius, and his age.

EDCS-03700409.translation: Iulia Nigra Hospita, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Norbanus Celer, son of Lucius, aged 3, is here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700409.summary: This is a paired tombstone for two individuals, Iulia Nigra Hospita (aged 20) and Lucius Norbanus Celer (aged 3), both accompanied by a wish for peace.

EDCS-03700412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ursa, daughter of Iulia Teroli.

EDCS-03700415.translation: Maxsumia Libisina, daughter of Gaius, is here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her son had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-03700415.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxsumia Libisina, erected by her son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700416.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baso, son of Caius.

EDCS-03700417.translation: Sacred to [Turculla]. Norbana Quintilla fulfilled her vow willingly and set this up.
EDCS-03700417.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Turculla by Norbana Quintilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700418.translation: P. Pontina and Q. P. Pontina Niger arranged for this to be made for their mother, and for themselves. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700418.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a mother and her children, erected by P. Pontina and Q. P. Pontina Niger, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700419.translation: To Gaius Julius Arcus, son of Cilisu. Salicius, for his father.
EDCS-03700419.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Arcus, son of Cilisu, dedicated by Salicius.

EDCS-03700421.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Acilius Liberalis willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700421.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Acilius Liberalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700422.translation: Modestinus, son of Lucius, willingly set this up for Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-03700422.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Modestinus, son of Lucius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700425.translation: Vicanus, son of Talabarus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sons had this made.
EDCS-03700425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vicanus, son of Talabarus, erected by his sons, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700426.translation: son of Cumelus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700426.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed individual, son of Cumelus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700428.translation: Gracilis, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03700428.summary: This inscription identifies Gracilis as a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-03700429.translation: Lucius Antestius Celer is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700429.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Antestius Celer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700434.translation: Lucius Norbanus Rufus, an altar to Jupiter.
EDCS-03700434.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lucius Norbanus Rufus.

EDCS-03700435.translation: Galla, freedwoman of Maxsuma, aged 70, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Her freedmen and freedwomen had this made.
EDCS-03700435.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Galla, a freedwoman, and states that her freed dependents commissioned the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700436.translation: Brita, freedwoman of Marcus, Nova, aged 75, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700436.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Brita, a freedwoman, and Nova, likely an associated individual, who died at age 75.

EDCS-03700437.translation: O AN Quadratus, a vow to the goddess Belona, an altar.
EDCS-03700437.summary: This inscription records a votive offering of an altar to the goddess Belona by someone identified as O AN Quadratus.

EDCS-04900482.translation: Festellus, servant of God, lived 6 years, rested in peace on the 7th day before the Kalends of March, in the year 65 of the era [AD 131/132]. Pray for me.
EDCS-04900482.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Festellus, a child and servant of God, in AD 131/132, and includes a plea for prayers.

EDCS-04900483.translation: Leopardus, servant of God, lived 50 years and 32 days, departed in peace on the 6th day before the Kalends of January, in the year 64 of the era [AD 130/131].
EDCS-04900483.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Leopardus, a servant of God, in AD 130/131, at the age of 50 years and 32 days.

EDCS-04900485.translation: [---], servant of God, lived 45 years, rested in peace on the 12th day before the Kalends of December, in the year 64 of the era [AD 130/131].
EDCS-04900485.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an individual, identified as a servant of God, in AD 130/131.

EDCS-04900489.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Clodia Compse, aged 40, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Clodia F mater, sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iuventius, aged 48, Claudia Gerontia, and Valerius Herenianus, perpetual priests, made this monument; overcome by fate, may your name be safe beneath the underworld; here is your seat, here your earth, here your eternal home.
EDCS-04900489.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the burial of Clodia Compse and Iuventius, commissioned by their parents who were perpetual priests, and includes a lengthy epitaph expressing wishes for their afterlife.

EDCS-04900497.translation: To Mascellius Hermetus, a pious father, I placed this; may the earth lie lightly upon you as you lie buried here.
EDCS-04900497.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mascellius Hermetus, likely set up by a relative, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900498.translation: Aemilius Aemilianus, a most accomplished man and governor of the province of Further Lusitania, dedicated this to the god Jupiter for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-04900498.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Aemilius Aemilianus, governor of Lusitania Superior, for his well-being and that of his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900499.translation: Camira, daughter and freedwoman of Tertia, aged 35, G Gargenna arranged for this to be made at his own expense.
EDCS-04900499.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camira, a freedwoman, whose monument was commissioned by G Gargenna, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900500.translation: Mana, daughter of Maelo, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900500.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mana, daughter of Maelo, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900501.translation: Cabura, daughter of Boutus, lies here; Caturos, son of Tafi, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-04900501.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cabura, daughter of Boutus, with the monument erected by Caturos, son of Tafi, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900502.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Constitus Modestus, son of Turpencus, aged 86, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900503.translation: To the spirits of the departed, C N Papiria, aged 50, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. V.
EDCS-04900503.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for C N Papiria, aged 50, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900505.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Faracius Severus, aged [XX?], lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900505.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Faracius Severus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900533.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cloutius, aged 60. To the spirits of the departed. Docinia lies here.
EDCS-04900533.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cloutius, aged 60, and Docinia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900536.translation: Anderenus, son of Tritus, aged 15. Monument.
EDCS-04900536.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and monument for Anderenus, son of Tritus, aged 15, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900541.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription identifying a woman and her father.

EDCS-04900543.translation: Ambatus, son of Divilius, aged 25, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900543.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambatus, son of Divilius, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-04900545.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Avitus, son of Avitus, aged 30.
EDCS-04900545.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Avitus, son of Avitus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-04900546.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ilos, aged 10.
EDCS-04900546.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Ilos, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-04900547.translation: To the spirits of the departed. A monument for Reburrus, aged...
EDCS-04900547.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Reburrus, though the specific age is missing.

EDCS-04900550.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Doviterus, aged 10.
EDCS-04900550.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Doviterus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-04900551.translation: ... son of Caius, aged 30, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900551.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of a man, identified as the son of Caius, who died at 30.

EDCS-04900553.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Reburrinus, son of Reburius, aged 22, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900553.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Reburrinus, son of Reburius, died at the age of 22.

EDCS-04900563.translation: Modestinus willingly fulfilled his vow to Liber Pater.
EDCS-04900563.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Modestinus to the god Liber Pater.

EDCS-05000397.translation: Exsoderus the presbyter lived 85 years and rested in peace on the fourth day before the Kalends.
EDCS-05000397.summary: This inscription is a Christian epitaph recording the lifespan and death date of Exsoderus, a presbyter.

EDCS-05000401.translation: To Numerius Clitus, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05000401.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerius Clitus, likely from Roman Africa, expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-05000404.translation: Modestus, son of Pollius, arranged for this to be made at his own expense for Rufinus Callaeus and Camala, his father and mother.
EDCS-05000404.summary: This is a funerary dedication commissioned by Modestus for his parents, Rufinus Callaeus and Camala, indicating filial piety.

EDCS-05000406.translation: Cornelius Severus gladly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-05000406.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Cornelius Severus fulfilling a promise made to the god Jupiter.

EDCS-05000410.translation: Lucius Flavius Verecundus of Pacensis, aged 60, is buried here.
EDCS-05000410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Flavius Verecundus, recording his name, origin, and age at death.

EDCS-05000420.translation: Afelia, his grandmother, made this at her own expense for [---], aged 16, who is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05000420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and monument for a 16-year-old, commissioned by his grandmother, Afelia.

EDCS-05000424.translation: Elandetus, son of Tirius, [made this] for his son Tatianus, aged 2, who is buried here.
EDCS-05000424.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tatianus, a young child, commissioned by his father, Elandetus.

EDCS-05200320.translation: Sacred to Health. Appius N. Auca Piasirus placed [this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05200320.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Salus (Health) by Appius N. Auca Piasirus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05200325.translation: Caeno, son of Bovi, is buried here.
EDCS-05200325.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Caeno, son of Bovi.

EDCS-05200327.translation: To the departed spirits. Gallia Avita [dedicated this] for Publius Placidius Sabinus, aged 35.
EDCS-05200327.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely commissioned by Gallia Avita for Publius Placidius Sabinus, mentioning his age.

EDCS-05200328.translation: The freedmen and heirs of C.I. Rusticus and Tarria carried out the construction according to the will.
EDCS-05200328.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a funerary obligation by the freedmen and heirs of C.I. Rusticus and Tarria, as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-05200329.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Oculatia, mother, took care to place this for Rufina, daughter of Lubaccus, aged 40. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05200329.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rufina, set up by her mother Oculatia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200331.translation: Rufinus, son of Rufus, of the Aelatius tribe, willingly fulfilled his vow to Albucelaincus Efficax.
EDCS-05200331.summary: A votive inscription dedicating something to Albucelaincus Efficax, fulfilled by Rufinus, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200332.translation: Valerius Placedus placed this from the spring of the garden.
EDCS-05200332.summary: An inscription recording the placement of something by Valerius Placedus, possibly related to a fountain or water source, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200334.translation: Maelo, son of Albonius, is buried here.
EDCS-05200334.summary: A simple burial inscription for Maelo, son of Albonius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200335.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Valerius Reburrus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200337.translation: His wife took care to have this made for Sulla Lucrio, from Orosucelae, from Oppidum, of the Lancienses.
EDCS-05200337.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her husband Sulla Lucrio, from a region including Orosucelae and Oppidum, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200338.translation: Quadratus, son of Curius, is buried here.
EDCS-05200338.summary: A simple burial inscription for Quadratus, son of Curius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200343.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred. Marcus Ulpius Lupus, freedman of Augustus, to his wife Vipsania Attica, aged 28.
EDCS-05200343.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vipsania Attica, set up by her husband Marcus Ulpius Lupus, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05500001.translation: The public body of Ossonobensis, devoted to his divine majesty and authority, by decree of the order, [dedicates this] to the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, supreme pontiff, father of the country, holding tribunician power for the third time, consul.
EDCS-05500001.summary: A dedication by the town of Ossonobensis to Emperor Valerian, likely dating to his third tribunician power (255-256 AD).

EDCS-05500002.translation: Marcus Cornelius Eridanus and Caius Iunius Receptus gave this gift from their own money in honor of their holding the office of the IIIIvirate.
EDCS-05500002.summary: A dedication of a gift by two individuals, Marcus Cornelius Eridanus and Caius Iunius Receptus, to commemorate their service as IIIIviri, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500003.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Annius Romulus, aged 28, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500003.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Gaius Annius Romulus, aged 28.

EDCS-05500004.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caturica Prima, a most devoted wife, who lived 25 years and 8 months. Lucius Calpurnius Theodorus, her husband.
EDCS-05500004.summary: A tombstone honoring Caturica Prima, wife of Lucius Calpurnius Theodorus.

EDCS-05500005.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunia Herennia, aged 6.
EDCS-05500005.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Iunia Herennia, aged 6.

EDCS-05500006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Teia, grieving.
EDCS-05500006.summary: A tombstone with a brief dedication by Teia, indicating grief.

EDCS-05500009.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Trophime.
EDCS-05500009.summary: A tombstone with a simple dedication to Trophime.

EDCS-05500011.summary: An inscription that appears to be an insult or epithet for Cefalus.

EDCS-05500012.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Manlius Lacon, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500012.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Titus Manlius Lacon, aged 55.

EDCS-05500013.translation: Sacred to Augusta Fortuna. Annius Primitivus, because of his office as a IIIIvir, gave and dedicated this from his own money, after holding a boat race and providing gifts for boxers, and also to the citizens.
EDCS-05500013.summary: A dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Annius Primitivus, celebrating public games and banquets held during his tenure as a IIIIvir.

EDCS-05500014.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caturicus Lupatus lived 18 days. Caturica Agatemera placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-05500014.summary: A tombstone set up by Caturica Agatemera for her husband Caturicus Lupatus, who died after 18 days.

EDCS-05500015.translation: By decree of the decurions. The people of the municipium of Myrtillanos, through Gaius Julius Marinus [---] [---] Gaius Marcius Optatus, IIvir.
EDCS-05500015.summary: A public inscription recording a decree of the town council of Myrtillanos, likely concerning the installation of Gaius Marcius Optatus as IIvir.

EDCS-05500016.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sertorius Nigellius set this up for his mother, Aemilia L.
EDCS-05500016.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sertorius Nigellius for his mother, Aemilia L.

EDCS-05500017.translation: Lucius Firmidius Peregrinus of Utica lived for 60 years; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Firmidius Peregrinus, who lived for 60 years and was buried in Utica.

EDCS-05500018.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Herennia Secundina lived for 55 years; Publius Felix set this up for his deserving guest.
EDCS-05500018.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Publius Felix for his deserving guest, Herennia Secundina.

EDCS-05500019.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sextia Filumena set this up for her most devoted son, Quintus Iulius Lup, who lived for 3 years and some months; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500019.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Sextia Filumena for her son, Quintus Iulius Lup, who died at the age of 3 years and some months.

EDCS-05500020.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [Name illegible] lived for 1 year and some months; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500020.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an individual who lived for 1 year and some months.

EDCS-05500021.translation: To Aesculapius, the god. Caius Attius Ianuarius, a physician from Pacensis, bequeathed this in his will for his merits with the most distinguished order, because it granted him a four-day festival; Fabius Isas, his heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500021.summary: This inscription records a bequest made by the physician Caius Attius Ianuarius to the god Aesculapius, with the construction of the monument carried out by his heir, Fabius Isas.

EDCS-05500022.translation: Sacred to Mars, in honor of Gaius Pagus Marinus, for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-05500022.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars, set up in honor of Gaius Pagus Marinus for his brother.

EDCS-05500023.translation: Sacred to Victory Venus Augusta, in honor of Lucilia Lepidina, for her most devoted daughter Flavia Titia.
EDCS-05500023.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victory Venus Augusta, set up by Flavia Titia in honor of her daughter Lucilia Lepidina.

EDCS-05500026.translation: To Gaius Cocilius Fuscus, my father, from the will of Cocilicia Pastos.
EDCS-05500026.summary: This inscription records a monument erected from the will of Cocilicia Pastos for her father, Gaius Cocilius Fuscus.

EDCS-05500027.translation: To Gaius Pagusicus Valerianus, from his own will. Scribonia Maxima, daughter of Gaius, saw to its construction as his heir.
EDCS-05500027.summary: This inscription records a monument erected from the will of Scribonia Maxima for Gaius Pagusicus Valerianus, whom she served as heir.

EDCS-05500028.translation: To Pagusigae, daughter of Lucius, Fundana, Caius Pagusicus Lucianus [erected this] to his excellent sister.
EDCS-05500028.summary: This is a dedication from Caius Pagusicus Lucianus to his sister Pagusigae Fundana.

EDCS-05500029.translation: To Quintus Scribonius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe. His father, Paterno Lucius Scribonius Saturninus [erected this].
EDCS-05500029.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Lucius Scribonius Saturninus for his son Quintus Scribonius.

EDCS-05500030.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Marcellina, daughter of Caius, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500030.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iulia Marcellina, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05500031.translation: Claudius Thalassinus, aged 50, [is buried] here for his most deserving wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500031.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for the wife of Claudius Thalassinus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05500033.translation: To Isis the Mistress. Marcus Octavius Theophilus, freedman of Octavia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, paid his vow willingly with all his heart.
EDCS-05500033.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Isis by the freedman Marcus Octavius Theophilus.

EDCS-05500034.translation: To Lucius Porcius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice chief magistrate, prefect, chief magistrate for life, and priest of the deified emperors, for his merits the plebs, by contributing money, was content with the honor of this inscription and remitted the expense.
EDCS-05500034.summary: This is a dedication honoring Lucius Porcius for his merits, with the expenses covered by public subscription.

EDCS-05500035.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Bocchus, son of Caius, priest of the province, military tribune, for his merits in the colony of Scallabis.
EDCS-05500035.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Cornelius Bocchus for his services to the colony of Scallabis.

EDCS-05500036.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Laeonica, aged 31. Quintus Iulius Marcianus had this made for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-05500036.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iulia Laeonica, erected by her son Quintus Iulius Marcianus.

EDCS-05500037.translation: Lucius Rubrius Priscinus, aged 26, is buried here.
EDCS-05500037.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Rubrius Priscinus, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-05500038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mumia Cupita, daughter of Lucius, aged 44. Quintus L. N., and Antonia Fundana, and Mumia Rufina placed [this] for their most dutiful mother and sister. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500038.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Mumia Cupita, erected by her children and mother.

EDCS-05500039.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mumia Fundana erected this for her freedman Mumius Chresimus, aged 16, deserving. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500039.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a freedwoman for her deceased freedman, Mumius Chresimus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-05500040.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. L. Gallus, aged 45. May it lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for L. Gallus, aged 45.

EDCS-05500041.translation: Lucius Iulius Valens, freedman of Lucius and Titus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Copirus [erected this] for his brother.
EDCS-05500041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Copirus for his brother, Lucius Iulius Valens, a freedman, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05500042.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Fabianus, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Fabianus, aged 38.

EDCS-05500043.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. The most dutiful sons erected this for their deserving Mario Antiochus, aged 80. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by sons for their father, Mario Antiochus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-05500044.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Vibius Callistratus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vibius Callistratus, aged 40.

EDCS-05500045.translation: Alphurica Cenesis willingly gave this gift from her youth.
EDCS-05500045.summary: This inscription records a gift willingly given by Alphurica Cenesis.

EDCS-05500046.translation: Sacred to Serapis the All-God. In honor of Gaius Marius Priscianus, Stelina Prisca, his most indulgent mother, gave this gift.
EDCS-05500046.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Serapis and honor for Gaius Marius Priscianus by his mother, Stelina Prisca.

EDCS-05500048.summary: This is a very brief inscription mentioning Pax Iulia and Quintus Petronius.

EDCS-05500049.translation: To Marcus Aurelius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, twice leader, priest of Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, prefect of the armourers...
EDCS-05500049.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication honoring Marcus Aurelius, who held multiple civic and religious offices, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05500050.translation: To Modestus, slave, from his own means, for Clodius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, Quadratus, aedile.
EDCS-05500050.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a slave to his master, who was an aedile.

EDCS-05500051.translation: To Lucius Clodius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, Salvius, flamen of the deified Augusti.
EDCS-05500051.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Clodius Salvius, who held the priestly office of flamen of the deified Augusti.

EDCS-05500052.translation: Gaius Julius, son of Caius, twice chief magistrate, twice prefect of the engineers, to both senates
EDCS-05500052.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Julius, who held multiple public offices including twice being chief magistrate and prefect of the engineers.

EDCS-05500053.translation: To Gaius Julius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, Pedo, chief magistrate, flamen of the deified Augusti, for the republic well administered and for the grain supply aided by money contributed, the plebs, by means of a common fund.
EDCS-05500053.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the plebs to Gaius Julius Pedo, a chief magistrate and flamen, for his good governance and support of the grain supply.

EDCS-05500061.translation: Candialla, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500061.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Candialla, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-05500062.translation: Whoever you are, traveler, who passes by, after you read the boundary stone, you will grieve that I died in my twentieth year, and if you have senses, I will pray for your rest, which was not granted to me; enjoy life if it pleases you to weep, why do you sigh? Inachus rightly makes this; rather hurry, for you yourself will be read. Nice lived 20 years.
EDCS-05500062.summary: This is a lengthy epitaph of a young person named Nice, who died at 20, lamenting their premature death and urging the passerby to cherish life.

EDCS-05500063.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Shades. Iulia Paterna, aged 15. Iulia Terspichore, her most obedient freedwoman. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500063.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the Divine Shades for Iulia Paterna, aged 15, and her freedwoman Iulia Terspichore.

EDCS-05500064.translation: To Gaius Julius Avitus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, his brother, Iulius Sabinus.
EDCS-05500064.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iulius Sabinus to his brother, Gaius Julius Avitus.

EDCS-05500065.translation: Gaius Julius Maxumus, aged 27, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500065.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Maxumus, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-05500066.translation: To Quintus Julius Salvius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05500066.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Quintus Julius Salvius.

EDCS-05500067.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Hel Aerianus, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500067.summary: A tombstone for Hel Aerianus, aged 12, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500068.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Patricius Exodiarius, aged [---].
EDCS-05500068.summary: A tombstone fragment for Patricius Exodiarius, an actor of the pantomime, whose age is incomplete.

EDCS-05500069.translation: To Quinto Peticio Rufo, son of Titus. His mother [erected this] for her son.
EDCS-05500069.summary: A dedication by a mother to her son, Quinto Peticio Rufo, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sextilia, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500070.summary: A tombstone for Sextilia, aged 30, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sulpicia Iuliana lived 28 years. Sulpicius Monius [erected this] for his wife Sulpicia V. His mother.
EDCS-05500071.summary: A tombstone for Sulpicia Iuliana, aged 28, set up by her husband and mother.

EDCS-05500076.translation: To the holy goddess Turubricensis. Lucius Axonius paid his vow.
EDCS-05500076.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Turubricensis by Lucius Axonius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500078.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Fabricius Frontonus, aged 26. Fabricius Gallio, his father, and Albura, his mother, had this made for their most dutiful son. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500078.summary: A tombstone for Fabricius Frontonus, aged 26, erected by his parents, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500079.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Felicius, aged 45, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500079.summary: A tombstone for Felicius, aged 45, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500080.summary: A tombstone fragment for Iulia Avito, whose age is incomplete.

EDCS-05500081.translation: To Caius Julius Atlondus, son of Caius.
EDCS-05500081.summary: A dedication to Caius Julius Atlondus, son of Caius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500082.translation: Marcus Iulius Marcellus is buried here.
EDCS-05500082.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription identifying the deceased Marcus Iulius Marcellus.

EDCS-05500083.translation: Marcella lived thirty-two years, content with one husband. Iulius Marinus made this for his most devoted wife. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500083.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marcella, who lived 32 years, and was erected by her husband Iulius Marinus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500084.translation: Meranius Victus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Lobessa arranged for this to be placed for her husband.
EDCS-05500084.summary: This tombstone marks the burial of Meranius Victus, who died at 25, and was arranged by his wife Iulia Lobessa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500085.translation: To the divine spirits. Musice lived 38 years. Her husband made this. [---]. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-05500085.summary: This epitaph, dedicated to the divine spirits, records the death of Musice at 38 and was erected by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500088.translation: [M Pret nus?] lived 75 years. His brother placed this.
EDCS-05500088.summary: This tombstone records that [M Pret nus?] lived 75 years and was erected by his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500096.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Lama, 35. [---] erected this. [---].
EDCS-05500096.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the divine spirits, records the death of Lama at 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500097.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Dignitas lived 25 years. Chryseros her husband erected this. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500097.summary: This epitaph, dedicated to the divine spirits, records the death of Dignitas at 25 and was erected by her husband Chryseros, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500098.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Marius Letoides lived 85 years. He is buried here in this monument. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500098.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the divine spirits, records the death of Marius Letoides at 85 and identifies the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500099.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Maria Euprepia, who conceded to fate, lived 45 years. Modestus placed this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-05500099.summary: This epitaph, dedicated to the divine spirits, records that Maria Euprepia died at 45 and was erected by her husband Modestus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500100.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Musa lived 60 years. Livia, her freedwoman, placed this. This is [her monument]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500100.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the divine spirits, records the death of Musa at 60 and was erected by her freedwoman Livia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500101.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Perennia placed this; she was 35 years old.
EDCS-05500101.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Perennia, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-05500102.translation: By decree of the town councillors of Florentis.
EDCS-05500102.summary: This inscription records a decision made by the local council of Florentis.

EDCS-05500104.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Terentius Chrysogonus, aged 37, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Franciscus Iosephus Oliveira restored this in the year of our Lord 1724.
EDCS-05500104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentius Chrysogonus, aged 37, with a later restoration in 1724.

EDCS-05500106.translation: Of Quintus Cornelius Mernus, son of Publius.
EDCS-05500106.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely recording the name and filiation of Quintus Cornelius Mernus.

EDCS-05500107.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Misinius Phanstianus lived 35 years. Milfus Sulpicius Pater took care to have this placed.
EDCS-05500107.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Misinius Phanstianus at age 35, and credits Milfus Sulpicius Pater with arranging the monument.

EDCS-05500108.summary: This inscription likely identifies Iulia Brocina, providing her filiation.

EDCS-05500109.translation: Caius Albius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, Albicus.
EDCS-05500109.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Caius Albius.

EDCS-05500110.translation: Iulia Procula, daughter of Caius, aged 70, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Procula, aged 70.

EDCS-05500112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mercator, aged 32, his deserving wife placed this for her husband. He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500112.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Mercator at age 32, placed by his wife.

EDCS-05500113.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Martiali Secundina, his sister, took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500113.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Secundina arranged for the monument of her brother, Martialis.

EDCS-05500114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonia Helice, aged 37, the wet-nurse of Marcus Antonius Maximus, placed this (monument) for her mother, who rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500114.summary: A funerary monument set up by a mother for her deceased daughter, identified by her relationship to a specific individual, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500115.translation: Gaius Blossius Saturninus, aged 33, a resident of Balsium, from Afer (Africa?) and Arniensis, (dedicated this) for his most dutiful daughter, who rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500115.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a father for his deceased daughter, mentioning his origins and residency, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500116.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Polibius, aged 72, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500116.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Polibius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500117.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating an inheritance or a testament, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500118.translation: Severus, a Roman equestrian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled (his) vow.
EDCS-05500118.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Severus, an equestrian, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500121.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Catina Marcia Aciliana, a most distinguished woman, with her own money made (this monument) for Canidia Albina, a woman of most distinguished memory, and her nephew, Catinius Canidianus, a man of most distinguished memory.
EDCS-05500121.summary: A funerary monument funded by a woman for her deceased mother and nephew, emphasizing their distinguished status, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500122.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Quintus Iulius Maximus, a most distinguished man, quaestor of the province of Sicily, tribune of the plebs, legate of the province of Gallia Narbonensis, designated praetor, aged 45. For his most excellent husband. For Quintus Iulius Clarissimus, a most distinguished youth, a commissioner for the upkeep of roads, aged 21. For Quintus Iulius Nepotianus, a most distinguished youth, a commissioner for the upkeep of roads, aged 20. Calpurnia Sabina (dedicated this) for her sons.
EDCS-05500122.summary: A complex funerary monument set up by a wife/mother for her deceased husband and two sons, listing their careers and ages, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500123.translation: Avita, daughter of Quintus, a commissioner for litigious judgments, her mother, also dedicated (this).
EDCS-05500123.summary: A funerary epitaph where a daughter, who held a public office, dedicates a monument to her deceased mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500124.translation: For Laberia Galla, daughter of Lucius, flaminica of the municipality of Ebora, flaminica of the province of Lusitania. Lucius Laberius Artemas, Lucius Laberius Gallaecus, Lucius Laberius Abascantus, Lucius Laberius Paris, Lucius Laberius Lausus, freedmen.
EDCS-05500124.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Laberia Galla, a priestess of both local and provincial rank, by her freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500125.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Antonius Flavius, son of Caius, a junior decurial officer (VIvir iunior) and awarded the golden torc and double ration for bravery, hastatus of the Second Augustan Legion. Iunia Verecunda, perpetual flaminica of the municipality of Ebora, mother, saw to the making (of this monument).
EDCS-05500125.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by a mother for her deceased son, a military officer recognized for bravery, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500126.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Asinius Florentinus, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500126.summary: A funerary epitaph for Asinius Florentinus, indicating his age and burial location.

EDCS-05500127.translation: Iulia Munilla, daughter of Rufi, is buried here. Iulia Galla is buried here.
EDCS-05500127.summary: A simple epitaph recording the burial of two individuals, Iulia Munilla and Iulia Galla.

EDCS-05500128.translation: Caius Norbanus Iunius Dexter, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, made this for Lucius Iunius Rullus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05500128.summary: An epitaph stating that Caius Norbanus Iunius Dexter erected a monument for Lucius Iunius Rullus.

EDCS-05500129.translation: Manilia Maxuma, daughter of Marci, aged 12, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Vibius Tancinus took care to have this made for his relative.
EDCS-05500129.summary: A funerary epitaph for the child Manilia Maxuma, with a note that Caius Vibius Tancinus arranged for its creation.

EDCS-05500130.translation: Publius Staius Meridianus, freedman of Publius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500130.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Staius Meridianus, noting his status as a freedman.

EDCS-05500131.translation: Lucius Caecilius, son of Cai, made this for his wife Maelia Tertulla.
EDCS-05500131.summary: An epitaph indicating that Lucius Caecilius dedicated a monument to his wife, Maelia Tertulla.

EDCS-05500132.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sisenandus and Iesabille, servants of Christ, made this in the name of the Lord for the memoria of Calchisia, daughter of Cai, flaminica of the province of Lusitania, his very dutiful daughter, and for Maria Sidonia, daughter of Lucius, his dearest granddaughter, and for Aponius Lupianus, a deserving husband, and for themselves, a construction which their unhappy mother Iunia Leonica arranged for her dears.
EDCS-05500132.summary: A complex funerary inscription dedicated to multiple family members and including a Christian invocation, likely from the later Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-05500134.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marius Quintilianus, son of Quinti, of Olisipo, aged 18, is buried here. His mother Iulia Rufina and his father Marius Maximus took care to have this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marius Quintilianus, aged 18, with his parents identified as the ones who arranged for its creation.

EDCS-05500135.translation: To the god Carneus Calanticensis. Caecilia, daughter of Quinti, happily fulfilled her vow. [NI CVIS R C]
EDCS-05500135.summary: A votive inscription to the deity Carneus Calanticensis, dedicated by Caecilia.

EDCS-05500136.translation: To the god Carneus Calanticensis. Hermes happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05500136.summary: A votive inscription to the deity Carneus Calanticensis, dedicated by Hermes.

EDCS-05500137.summary: This is a votive dedication to the deity Endovellicus by Albia Ianuaria.

EDCS-05500138.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Antonia Manliola, daughter of Lucius, freely gave a silver statue in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500138.summary: This inscription records a votive offering of a silver statue to Endovellicus by Antonia Manliola.

EDCS-05500139.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Arrius Badiolus willingly made this, from the sanctuary, at the command of the divine will.
EDCS-05500139.summary: Arrius Badiolus willingly erected this monument at the sanctuary of Endovellicus, following divine instruction.

EDCS-05500140.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Blandus, slave of Caelia Rufina, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500140.summary: Blandus, a slave belonging to Caelia Rufina, fulfilled a vow to Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500141.translation: To the most excellent and present divine will of Endovellicus, Sextus Cocceius Craterus Honorinus, a Roman knight, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500141.summary: Sextus Cocceius Craterus Honorinus, a Roman knight, made a dedication to Endovellicus, likely for the god's protection or favor.

EDCS-05500142.translation: To Endovellicus, from Critonia Maxuma, in fulfillment of a vow for Critonia, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-05500142.summary: Critonia Maxuma made a vow to Endovellicus on behalf of Critonia, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-05500143.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Hermes, slave of Aurelia Vibiae Sabina, a marble worker, willingly placed this.
EDCS-05500143.summary: Hermes, a slave and marble worker belonging to Aurelia Vibiae Sabina, erected this monument to Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500144.translation: Lucius Iulius Novatus paid a vow to Endovellicus for the health of his Manilia Vivennius Venusta.
EDCS-05500144.summary: Lucius Iulius Novatus dedicated a vow to Endovellicus for the well-being of Vivennius Venusta, who is identified as his Manilia (a term of endearment or relationship).

EDCS-05500145.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Marcus Iulius Proculus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500145.summary: Marcus Iulius Proculus fulfilled a vow made to the deity Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500146.translation: To the sacred entity of Endovellicus, Iunia Eliana, mother of Elvia Ylias, willingly set up the vow undertaken for her daughter.
EDCS-05500146.summary: Iunia Eliana, acting on behalf of her daughter Elvia Ylias, fulfilled a vow to Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500147.translation: To the holy god Endovellicus, Marcus V M willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500147.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus V M to the god Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500148.translation: Sacred to Endovellicus, Pomponia Marcella willingly erected this, in accordance with religious practice and by order of the divine will.
EDCS-05500148.summary: This inscription records Pomponia Marcella dedicating a monument to Endovellicus following divine instruction.

EDCS-05500149.translation: Quintus Sevius Firmanus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god Endovollico.
EDCS-05500149.summary: This inscription records Quintus Sevius Firmanus paying a vow to the god Endovellico.

EDCS-05500150.translation: Sitnia Victorina, daughter of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, took care to have this dedicated to Endovellico by Quitus Sitonius Equestris, her father, after a vision.
EDCS-05500150.summary: This inscription records Sitnia Victorina dedicating a monument to Endovellico, inspired by a vision and her father's instruction.

EDCS-05500151.translation: Sacred to Endovellicus, Terentia, daughter of Gaius, a statue.
EDCS-05500151.summary: This inscription records Terentia dedicating a statue to Endovellicus.

EDCS-05500152.translation: Tusca Olia, daughter of Taurus, willingly paid her vow to Enobolico for Quintus Statorius Taurus.
EDCS-05500152.summary: This inscription records Tusca Olia paying a votive offering to Enobolico on behalf of Quintus Statorius Taurus.

EDCS-05500153.translation: Quintus Helvius Silvanus willingly paid his vow and erected this to Proserpina.
EDCS-05500153.summary: This inscription records Quintus Helvius Silvanus dedicating a monument and vow to Proserpina.

EDCS-05500154.translation: Gaius Julius Parthenopaeus willingly paid his vow to holy Proserpina, as he vowed.
EDCS-05500154.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Parthenopaeus fulfilling a vow to Proserpina.

EDCS-05500155.translation: Caius Vettius Silvanus willingly paid his vow to Proserpina the Saviour, for his wife Plautilla Eunoide and for himself, after she was restored.
EDCS-05500155.summary: This inscription records Caius Vettius Silvanus dedicating a votive offering to Proserpina for his wife's recovery.

EDCS-05500156.translation: Aunia, daughter of Amminus, aged 20 years, lies here; her brothers Avitus and Surus took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500156.summary: This inscription records the burial of Aunia, daughter of Amminus, aged 20, with her brothers Avitus and Surus arranging the monument.

EDCS-05500157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Laudice placed this for her most devoted husband, Publius Petronius Cautinus, aged 91. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500157.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, including his age at death.

EDCS-05500158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pultarius, aged 23, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Semne and Semnus placed this for their mother.
EDCS-05500158.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man, set up by his mother and another person.

EDCS-05500159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rufinus, son of Rufus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Norbana, his mother, saw to the construction of the monument for her most dutiful son. Valeria Avita placed the inscription for her most indulgent husband.
EDCS-05500159.summary: A funerary epitaph for a man, commissioned by his mother and with an additional inscription by his wife.

EDCS-05500160.translation: For Fontanus and Fontana. Albia Pacina gladly paid a vow for the safety of Albius Faustus.
EDCS-05500160.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to deities by a woman for the well-being of a man.

EDCS-05500162.translation: Anthymus, slave of Reb(ius) Scientinus, gladly paid a vow.
EDCS-05500162.summary: A votive inscription by a slave for his owner's well-being.

EDCS-05500163.translation: Cacalo, freedman of Avilius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500163.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying a deceased freedman.

EDCS-05500164.translation: Helvia, daughter of Marcus, lived [X] years.
EDCS-05500164.summary: A fragmentary epitaph providing the name and parentage of a woman, with her lifespan indicated.

EDCS-05500165.translation: Severus, son of Fel[ix] Cephalon, aged [X] years.
EDCS-05500165.summary: A fragmentary epitaph providing a man's name, parentage, and indicating his age.

EDCS-05500166.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Amoena, aged 32, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Pomponius Maxumus had the monument constructed for his dearest wife.
EDCS-05500166.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, including her age at death.

EDCS-05500167.summary: A fragmentary epitaph identifying a mother and daughter.

EDCS-05500169.translation: Quintus Capito and his son Quintus, the heirs, set this up in accordance with his will for Publius Cornelius Quintus Macrus, who was granted citizenship by the deified Claudius, and served as quaestor and as a *duovir*.
EDCS-05500169.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by heirs for Publius Cornelius Quintus Macrus, a former magistrate who was granted citizenship by Claudius.

EDCS-05500171.translation: Amoenus, the slave of Iulia, aged two, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Amoenus, my mother.
EDCS-05500171.summary: This is a tombstone for a young slave named Amoenus, set up by his mother.

EDCS-05500172.translation: Gaius Licinius Verus, son of Medugenus, from Clunia.
EDCS-05500172.summary: This is a tombstone or a dedication identifying Gaius Licinius Verus and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05500174.translation: Lucius Voconius Fuscus lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Lucius Voconius Tertius arranged for this to be made for his brother from his own funds.
EDCS-05500174.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Lucius Voconius Tertius for his deceased brother, Lucius Voconius Fuscus.

EDCS-05500175.translation: Lobesa, daughter of Lovesus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500175.summary: This is a tombstone for Lobesa, daughter of Lovesus.

EDCS-05500176.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Minatia Faustina, aged 40, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Antonius Romulus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-05500176.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Minatia Faustina by her husband, Antonius Romulus.

EDCS-05500177.translation: Sextus Aebutius Rufinus, son of Sextus, of the Papirian tribe, aged 45, is here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His son arranged for this to be set up for his father.
EDCS-05500177.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Aebutius Rufinus, set up by his son.

EDCS-05500178.translation: Avitus, son of Proculus, fulfilled his vow with a willing heart to the Nymphs for the safety of Flaccilla, his wife of Flaccus.
EDCS-05500178.summary: This is a votive inscription by Avitus to the Nymphs for the health of his wife.

EDCS-05500179.translation: Sica, daughter of Maelo, lies here.
EDCS-05500179.summary: This is a tombstone for Sica, daughter of Maelo.

EDCS-05500181.translation: Maxumus, son of Talabarius, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500181.summary: This is a tombstone for a young boy named Maxumus.

EDCS-05500183.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Asclepius by Caius Licinius Decimianus.

EDCS-05500184.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius Augustus, Marcus Cossutius Macrinus, worshippers of the Lares Maliae and Malioli, donated it.
EDCS-05500184.summary: This inscription records a donation to the god Aesculapius Augustus by Marcus Cossutius Macrinus, a worshipper of the Lares.

EDCS-05500186.translation: Marcus Baebius, son of Marcus, gives this to Concordia for the townsfolk of the municipality of Felicitas Iulia.
EDCS-05500186.summary: This inscription records a gift to the goddess Concordia by Marcus Baebius for the citizens of Felicitas Iulia.

EDCS-05500187.translation: Marcus Iulius Primus, with a willing spirit, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter for the health of Marcus Cassius Firmus.
EDCS-05500187.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter by Marcus Iulius Primus for the health of Marcus Cassius Firmus.

EDCS-05500188.translation: Titus Licinius Amaranthus, with a willing and deserved spirit, fulfilled his vow to the Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-05500188.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Mother of the Gods by Titus Licinius Amaranthus.

EDCS-05500191.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Caius Iulius Augustalis gave this gift.
EDCS-05500191.summary: This inscription records a gift to the god Mercury Augustus by Caius Iulius Augustalis.

EDCS-05500192.translation: To the deified Augustus. Caius Arrius Optatus and Caius Iulius Eutichus, Augustales.
EDCS-05500192.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Caius Arrius Optatus and Caius Iulius Eutichus, members of the Augustales.

EDCS-05500196.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, grandson of the deified Nerva, son of the deified Trajan Dacicus Parthicus, consul for the third time, holder of tribunician power for the fifth time. Felicitas Iulia Olisipo, by decree of the decurions, through Marcus Gellius Rutilianus and Lucius Iulius Avitus, the II viri.
EDCS-05500196.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the city of Olisipo (Lisbon) to Emperor Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, likely from the 2nd century CE, recorded by local magistrates.

EDCS-05500199.translation: Titius Quadratus, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-05500199.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Titius Quadratus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-05500202.translation: To Gaius Caecilius, son of Quintus, of the Galerian tribe, the aedile. His wife Peticia, daughter of Publius, of the Tyscan tribe.
EDCS-05500202.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Caecilius Gallus, an aedile, by his wife Peticia.

EDCS-05500203.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vibia Maxima, grandmother, and Maria Procula, mother, content with the honor, dedicated this at their own expense to Lucius Cantius Marinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile.
EDCS-05500203.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Cantius Marinus, aedile, set up by his grandmother and mother.

EDCS-05500204.translation: To Quintus Iulius Plotius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen of Germanicus Caesar, and perpetual flamen of Iulia Augusta.
EDCS-05500204.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Iulius Plotius, who held multiple priesthoods and magistracies in his town.

EDCS-05500206.translation: Augustalis perpetuo. Caius Heius Primi libertus Nothus and Heia Primi liberta Elpis, Heia Notha Secunda, Caius Heius Nothi filius, Galeria Primus Cato, Heia Nothi filia Chelido, Titus Heius Nothi filius, Galeria Glaphyrus Nothianus.
EDCS-05500206.summary: This text lists multiple individuals, including freedmen and their descendants, who likely held the position of Augustalis.

EDCS-05500208.translation: To Marcus Petronius Bassus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500208.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Petronius Bassus, a local official, as decided by the town council.

EDCS-05500209.translation: To Caius Tenatius Iustus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500209.summary: This inscription honors Caius Tenatius Iustus, a local official, as decided by the town council.

EDCS-05500210.translation: Aemilius Niger, son of Aemilius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500210.summary: This is a simple burial marker for Aemilius Niger.

EDCS-05500211.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Afra, aged 26, is buried here. Her husband Vettus placed this.
EDCS-05500211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Afra, placed by her husband.

EDCS-05500212.translation: Caius Antonius Urius, son of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500212.summary: This is a simple burial marker for Caius Antonius Urius.

EDCS-05500213.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Marinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, by his heirs according to his will.
EDCS-05500213.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Aurelius Marinus, who was commemorated by his heirs as specified in his will.

EDCS-05500214.translation: In memory of Arria Avita, mother. Quintus Cassius Arrianus dedicated this.
EDCS-05500214.summary: This inscription is a memorial dedicated by Quintus Cassius Arrianus to his mother, Arria Avita.

EDCS-05500215.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caecilius Hermetianus, son of Publius, lived 2 years, 10 months, 17 days. Publius Caecilius VII Silicianus, his brother, lived 7 years, 3 months, 7 days. His father Hermes made this.
EDCS-05500215.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of two young children, Caecilius Hermetianus and his brother Publius Caecilius VII Silicianus, made by their father Hermes, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500216.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caecilius Optatinus, aged 37, lies here. Iulia Orne had this made for her excellent kinsman.
EDCS-05500216.summary: A tombstone for Caecilius Optatinus, paid for by Iulia Orne, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500217.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Cassius Calvus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500218.translation: To Cassius Gallus, son of Caius, an excellent man. (Dedication to) DE TVDII
EDCS-05500218.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument to Cassius Gallus, son of Caius, by an unknown dedicator, possibly from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500219.translation: Cocceia Chreste, daughter of Lucius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500219.summary: A tombstone for Cocceia Chreste, daughter of Lucius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500220.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Cominius Expectatus. Iustus and Augustanus, with Cominia their mother, for their excellent father.
EDCS-05500220.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Cominius Expectatus, dedicated by his children Iustus and Augustanus and their mother Cominia, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500221.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Gamica, aged 25, and Lucius Cornelius Victorinus, aged 30, lie here, for brother and sister.
EDCS-05500221.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Gamica and Lucius Cornelius Victorinus, likely siblings, with their ages specified, dating possibly to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500222.translation: Curia Fundana, daughter of Sextus, lies here. Trebonius Tuscus, her husband, and Amoena, her mother, had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-05500222.summary: A tombstone for Curia Fundana, daughter of Sextus, funded by her husband Trebonius Tuscus and mother Amoena, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500223.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Quintus Fabi Festivus, aged 40, and his brother Quintus Fabi Euelpistus, aged 30. Buried in the city of Italica. Quintus Fabius Zosimus, brother, had this made.
EDCS-05500223.summary: A tombstone for two brothers, Quintus Fabi Festivus and Quintus Fabi Euelpistus, buried in Italica and commissioned by their brother Quintus Fabius Zosimus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500224.translation: Fabius Rufus, son of Iustus, Galeria tribe, from Clunia, aged 40, and Caecilia Gemina, daughter of Scapula, aged 18, wife. Of Olisipo. Sempronia Rufina, mother, had this mausoleum made at her own expense.
EDCS-05500224.summary: A tombstone for Fabius Rufus and his wife Caecilia Gemina, commissioned by Fabius Rufus's mother Sempronia Rufina, indicating their origins and ages, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500225.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Geminia Marcella as a mother.

EDCS-05500226.translation: Graptus, the slave of Lucceia Cinnamis, aged 13, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. 30 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-05500226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Graptus, a slave, indicating his age and burial plot size, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500227.translation: Quintus Hirrius Marci filius Galeria, Maternus is buried here.
EDCS-05500227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maternus, son of Quintus Hirrius, identifying his father and tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05500228.translation: Iulia Aequa, daughter of Quitus, mother.
EDCS-05500228.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Iulia Aequa as a mother and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500229.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Maxima, my only daughter, aged 30, is buried here. Maxima, the mother, took care to have this monument made; she will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-05500229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Maxima, set up by her mother Maxima, specifying her age and that the monument will not pass to an heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500230.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Severa, aged 55, is buried here. Iulia Orne made this for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-05500230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Severa, set up by her daughter Iulia Orne, specifying her age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500231.translation: Iulia Thymele, wife of Montanus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500231.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Thymele, mentioning her husband Montanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500232.translation: Iulia Materna Tusca, aged [---], is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500232.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Materna Tusca, with a standard wish for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500233.translation: Iulia Tusca, daughter of Caius, Postumia, aged 18, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Her mother made it.
EDCS-05500233.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Tusca Postumia, daughter of Caius, specifying her age and that her mother erected the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500234.translation: Gaius Iulius Clemens, son of Gaius Iulius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500234.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Clemens, identifying his father and tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05500235.translation: Gaius Julius Felix Cappadox and his wife Iulia Severa.
EDCS-05500235.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Julius Felix Cappadox and his wife Iulia Severa.

EDCS-05500236.translation: Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, designated aedile, lies here.
EDCS-05500236.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Rufinus, designated aedile, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05500237.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Publius Julius Seranus.

EDCS-05500238.translation: To Quintus Julius Silvanus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, after his death, his brother Gaius Julius Rufus [erected this].
EDCS-05500238.summary: This is a posthumous funerary dedication by Gaius Julius Rufus for his brother Quintus Julius Silvanus.

EDCS-05500239.translation: [Perhaps referring to a funerary donation, '...nae Iuniae um cum vit tradidit que C']
EDCS-05500239.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that seems to record a funerary donation, possibly related to Iunia.

EDCS-05500240.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lucius Junius Candidus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who lived 5 years and 6 months.
EDCS-05500240.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a child, Lucius Junius Candidus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-05500242.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Licinius Maternus, aged 7 years, lies here.
EDCS-05500242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Licinius Maternus, a child who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-05500243.translation: Gaius Lucceius Philogenes, aged 40 years, lies here.
EDCS-05500243.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Lucceius Philogenes, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-05500244.translation: Lucius Celer, freedman of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-05500244.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Celer, a freedman.

EDCS-05500246.translation: To the Divine Shades. His mother set this up for Maia Felicula, aged 26 years.
EDCS-05500246.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a mother for her daughter, Maia Felicula.

EDCS-05500247.translation: Marci Myrtilus, son of Marcus, is laid here.
EDCS-05500247.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying the deceased, Myrtilus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-05500248.translation: Munna, mother, placed this for her most devoted son Nigelioni, to the gods of the underworld and departed spirits.
EDCS-05500248.summary: This funerary epitaph records a mother's dedication to her deceased son, Nigelioni.

EDCS-05500249.translation: Sextus Numisius Philocalus, freedman of Sextus Numisius, is laid here. Sextus Numisius Nicephorus, son, eighteen years old, is laid here.
EDCS-05500249.summary: This epitaph records the burial of two individuals, Sextus Numisius Philocalus and his son Sextus Numisius Nicephorus.

EDCS-05500250.translation: For Peticia, daughter of Publius, Tusca.
EDCS-05500250.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph for Peticia Tusca, daughter of Publius.

EDCS-05500251.translation: Quintus Pompeius Iustus, freedman of Vari.
EDCS-05500251.summary: This inscription identifies Quintus Pompeius Iustus, a freedman.

EDCS-05500252.translation: Quintus Pompeius Tiphus, son of Quintus Pompeius, is laid here. Antonia Omulla is laid here.
EDCS-05500252.summary: This epitaph records the burial of two individuals, Quintus Pompeius Tiphus and Antonia Omulla.

EDCS-05500253.translation: Sextus Pompeius Faustus is laid here. Caecilia Calimis made this for him.
EDCS-05500253.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Sextus Pompeius Faustus and identifies the dedicator, Caecilia Calimis.

EDCS-05500254.translation: For Sextus Pompeius, son of Lucius, [freedman?] of Galeria Scapula.
EDCS-05500254.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or epitaph for Sextus Pompeius, son of Lucius, possibly related to Galeria Scapula.

EDCS-05500255.translation: To the gods of the underworld and departed spirits. Iulius Papianus for his most excellent wife Quintilla, daughter of Quintus Postumius, and for himself, most dear.
EDCS-05500255.summary: This funerary epitaph is a joint dedication by Iulius Papianus to his wife Quintilla and himself.

EDCS-05500256.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld and departed spirits. Postumius Florianus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted brother Postumius Vicillio, aged thirty-five.
EDCS-05500256.summary: This funerary inscription records a brother's arrangement for a tomb to be made for his deceased sibling, Postumius Vicillio.

EDCS-05500257.summary: This inscription records the name of Primigenius, son of Caius Terentius, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-05500258.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Rhodanus, son of Marcus Vibius Terentianus, aged nine years.
EDCS-05500258.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Rhodanus, son of Marcus Vibius Terentianus, who died at the age of nine.

EDCS-05500259.translation: For Rubia Sabina, daughter of Quintus, Quintus Aemilius Flaccus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05500259.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Aemilius Flaccus for Rubia Sabina, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-05500260.translation: Caius Sempronius Pacatus, aged eighty years, is buried here.
EDCS-05500260.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Sempronius Pacatus, who died at the age of eighty.

EDCS-05500261.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for T. Nigrinus, aged four months, and his husband, aged seven years.
EDCS-05500261.summary: This is a funerary dedication to T. Nigrinus, who died at four months, and his husband, who died at seven years.

EDCS-05500262.translation: Marcus Tarquitius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, Maxumus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500262.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Tarquitius Maxumus, son of Marcus, belonging to the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-05500263.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, sacred. Nemesius had this made for Telemachus, aged forty years, his most devoted and deserving father.
EDCS-05500263.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Nemesius for his deserving father, Telemachus, aged forty.

EDCS-05500264.translation: Lucius Valerius Severus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged fifty years, is buried here. May the earth be light for you. His sons and Quintus Sertorius Calvus, a relative, had this monument erected for their father.
EDCS-05500264.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Valerius Severus, aged fifty, with a wish for his peaceful rest, and notes that his sons and relative erected the monument.

EDCS-05500265.translation: Lucius Varbius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Tanginus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500265.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Varbius Tanginus, son of Lucius, belonging to the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-05500266.translation: For Publius Ursius Priscus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, his devoted father had this monument erected after death for Publius Ursius, and for Quintus L. Demetrius.
EDCS-05500266.summary: This funerary inscription records a father's posthumous dedication for his son, Publius Ursius Priscus, and also for Quintus L. Demetrius.

EDCS-05500268.translation: To Sol and Luna, by Cestius Acidius Perennis, imperial legate with praetorian authority of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-05500268.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the gods Sol and Luna by a provincial governor in Lusitania, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500269.translation: To Sol Aeternus and Luna, for the eternity of the empire and the safety of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Augustus Pius, son of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, and of Publius Septimius Geta Nobilissimus Caesar, and of Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, Decimus Julius Coelianus, a freedman of Augustus, with his own property and Quintus Julius Saturninus, Quintus Valerius, and Antonius.
EDCS-05500269.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Aeternus and Luna for the welfare of the Roman Empire and the imperial family, including Septimius Severus and Caracalla, and was set up by several individuals, including a freedman of Augustus, likely during the Severan period.

EDCS-05500271.translation: To the divine shades. Caeciliana, aedile, wife of Quintus Caecilius, son of Quintus of the Galerian tribe, aged 40; and Avitus, son of Marcus Caecilius, son of Quintus of the Galerian tribe, aged 18. Julia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, made this monument at her own expense for her most excellent husband and most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500271.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a tomb by Julia Marcella for her husband, Caeciliana, an aedile, and her son, Avitus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500272.translation: To Gaius Gavius Rectus, son of Gaius of the Galerian tribe, aedile, aged 29.
EDCS-05500272.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Gaius Gavius Rectus, an aedile, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500273.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Cominius Atilianus, a Roman knight, aged 54, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Cominius Atilianus, a Roman knight, including a wish for his repose, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500274.translation: Julius Munii Bitalicus Augustalis lies here.
EDCS-05500274.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Julius Munii Bitalicus Augustalis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500275.translation: Augustus Eutichus, slave of Gaius Licassius Bassus, lies here. Linus, slave of Alteciniris, lies here.
EDCS-05500275.summary: This funerary inscription records two individuals identified as slaves, Augustus Eutichus and Linus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500276.translation: Quintus Flavius Quadratus, son of Marcus of the Galerian tribe, aquilifer (eagle-bearer) of the Legio II, made this monument for himself while alive; this monument, with a triclinium (dining couch), will not pass to the heir.
EDCS-05500276.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument built by Quintus Flavius Quadratus, an aquilifer of Legio II, specifying that it includes a triclinium and will not be inherited, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500277.translation: Lucius Aelius Aelianus, son of Lucius of the Galerian tribe, lies here. Lucius Seneca, father of Lucius Aelius, son of Sextus of the Galerian tribe, lies here. Cassia Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, mother of Lucius Aelius, lies here. Lucius Julius Aelianus, son of Lucius of the Galerian tribe, aged 14, lies here. Lucius Julius Iulianus, son of Lucius of the Galerian tribe, aged 25, lies here. Aelia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, lies here.
EDCS-05500277.summary: This extensive funerary inscription records the burial of multiple family members, including Lucius Aelius Aelianus, his father Lucius Seneca, his mother Cassia Quintilla, and two younger individuals named Lucius Julius Aelianus and Lucius Julius Iulianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500278.translation: Iulia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, lies here. Publius Clodius Avitus, son of Publius, aged 20, lies here. Lucius Julius Civis lies here.
EDCS-05500278.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Amoena, Publius Clodius Avitus, and Lucius Julius Civis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500279.translation: Attius Montanus had this made at his own expense and it was cared for; here he rests.
EDCS-05500279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attius Montanus, who paid for his own burial place, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500280.translation: Antonia Amoena rests here; Antonius Amoenus, her father, [erected this] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500280.summary: This funerary inscription honors Antonia Amoena, with her father Antonius Amoenus acting as dedicator following a decree by the local council, suggesting a public or semi-public role for Antonia or her family.

EDCS-05500281.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Maxima, aged 32. Caesia Amoena, her most dutiful mother, rests here.
EDCS-05500281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Maxima, set up by her mother Caesia Amoena, and includes her age, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500282.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aponia Juliana, daughter of Publius. Publius Aponius Julianus and his daughter Aponia Nicopolis [erected this].
EDCS-05500282.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Aponia Juliana by her father, Publius Aponius Julianus, and his other daughter, Aponia Nicopolis, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500283.translation: Apronia Maelia, daughter of Lucius, rests here.
EDCS-05500283.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Apronia Maelia, daughter of Lucius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500284.translation: Quintus Atilius Fundanus, son of Marcus, rests here.
EDCS-05500284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Atilius Fundanus, son of Marcus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500285.translation: Marcus Terentius Tuscus had this made for his wife Atinia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, for his father-in-law Marcus Terentius Galeria, and for his mother-in-law Aquila Terentia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-05500285.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Marcus Terentius Tuscus commissioned this monument for his wife, Atinia Amoena, and his in-laws, Marcus Terentius and Aquila Terentia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500286.translation: Decimus Atrius Rufus, son of Marcus, rests here.
