EDCS-05500286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Atrius Rufus, son of Marcus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500287.translation: Quintus Atrius Severus, son of Marcus, rests here.
EDCS-05500287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Atrius Severus, son of Marcus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500288.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Decia Avita, daughter of Decius, aged 27, rests here. Her mother, Iulia, had this made.
EDCS-05500288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decia Avita, aged 27, set up by her mother Iulia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500289.translation: Caecilia Maxuma, daughter of Lucius, aged 27, is laid here.
EDCS-05500289.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caecilia Maxuma at age 27.

EDCS-05500290.translation: Gaius Caecilius Gaetulicus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 17, is laid here.
EDCS-05500290.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Caecilius Gaetulicus at age 17.

EDCS-05500291.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is laid here. Iulia Maxsuma, daughter of Marcus, aged 27, is laid here.
EDCS-05500291.summary: A joint funerary inscription for Calpurnius Avitus and Iulia Maxsuma, who died at age 27.

EDCS-05500292.translation: To the departed spirits. Cassia, daughter of Lucius, lived 18 years, is laid here. Cassianus Pituscanus [erected this?].
EDCS-05500292.summary: A funerary inscription for Cassia, who died at age 18, possibly erected by Cassianus Pituscanus.

EDCS-05500293.translation: Titus Cassius, son of HI S F ACVRT, is laid here.
EDCS-05500293.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Titus Cassius, with an abbreviated patronymic.

EDCS-05500294.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Coelius Aquila, aged 16, is laid here. Quintus Coelius Cassianus had this erected for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500294.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Coelius Aquila, aged 16, erected by his father.

EDCS-05500295.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Cominius Fundanus.

EDCS-05500296.translation: Lucius Cominius Tancinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is laid here.
EDCS-05500296.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Cominius Tancinus.

EDCS-05500297.translation: Dillia Amoena, daughter of Titus, aged 31.
EDCS-05500297.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Dillia Amoena at age 31.

EDCS-05500298.translation: Marcus Fabricius Marcianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 22, is laid here. V. Florilla [erected this?].
EDCS-05500298.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Fabricius Marcianus, aged 22, possibly erected by V. Florilla.

EDCS-05500299.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marciana, daughter of Marcus Atilius, of the Galeria tribe, aged...
EDCS-05500299.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marciana, daughter of Marcus Atilius, likely from a tombstone, with her age partially preserved.

EDCS-05500300.translation: Iulia, daughter of Quintus, Fun; Quintus Iulius Severus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, are buried here.
EDCS-05500300.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia and Quintus Iulius Severus, specifying their parentage and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-05500301.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Caius Iulius Silvanus and Iulia Glauca made this for their parents, Iulian Labernaria, aged 37.
EDCS-05500301.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Iulius Silvanus and Iulia Glauca for their parents, Iulian Labernaria, who was 37 years old.

EDCS-05500302.translation: Iulia Maxsuma, daughter of Marcus, aged 27, is buried here.
EDCS-05500302.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iulia Maxsuma, daughter of Marcus, at the age of 27.

EDCS-05500303.translation: Iulia Severa, daughter of Marcus, is buried here; Caius Fabius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500303.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals: Iulia Severa and Caius Fabius, noting their parentage and tribal affiliation for the latter.

EDCS-05500304.translation: Iulia Severa, daughter of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500304.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Iulia Severa, daughter of Lucius, is buried at this location.

EDCS-05500305.translation: Iulia Tongeta, daughter of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500305.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Tongeta, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-05500306.translation: Iulia Tonceta, daughter of Caius, aged 20, is buried here. Iulia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500306.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Iulia Tonceta, aged 20, and states that her mother, Iulia Amoena, arranged for its construction.

EDCS-05500308.translation: Iulius Tanginus, son of Decimus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500308.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the burial of Iulius Tanginus, son of Decimus.

EDCS-05500309.translation: Marcus Iulius Civis, his mother Apronia, and his sister Iulia Avita are buried here.
EDCS-05500309.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Iulius Civis, his mother Apronia, and his sister Iulia Avita.

EDCS-05500310.translation: Marcus Iulius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500310.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Avitus.

EDCS-05500311.translation: Marcus Iulius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 68, is buried here.
EDCS-05500311.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Avitus, aged 68.

EDCS-05500312.translation: Marcus Iulius Gallus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here. Quintus Iulius Tongius made this.
EDCS-05500312.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Gallus, made by Quintus Iulius Tongius.

EDCS-05500313.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Iulius Macrinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05500313.summary: This is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Lucius Iulius Macrinus.

EDCS-05500314.translation: Caius Licinius Celsus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500314.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Licinius Celsus.

EDCS-05500315.translation: Lucius Licinius Proculus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 22. Caius Licinius Avitus, his father, (made this).
EDCS-05500315.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Proculus, aged 22, set up by his father Caius Licinius Avitus.

EDCS-05500316.translation: Marcus Licinius Quadratus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-05500316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Licinius Quadratus.

EDCS-05500317.translation: Marcus Licinius Tancinus is buried here.
EDCS-05500317.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Licinius Tancinus.

EDCS-05500318.translation: Lucius Licinius Temporanus, aged 35, is buried here. His mother cared for this.
EDCS-05500318.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Temporanus, aged 35, set up by his mother.

EDCS-05500319.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Loreius Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 16.
EDCS-05500319.summary: This is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Lucius Loreius Maximus, aged 16.

EDCS-05500320.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lusidius Proculianus, who rests here, aged 11. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Lusidius Rufinus, his father, saw to the erection of this altar.
EDCS-05500320.summary: A funerary altar dedicated to Quintus Lusidius Proculianus, aged 11, by his father Caius Lusidius Rufinus.

EDCS-05500321.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Orbiacianius rests here.
EDCS-05500321.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Orbiacianius.

EDCS-05500322.translation: Titus Plotius Capito, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, rests here.
EDCS-05500322.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Plotius Capito, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-05500324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pultarius Flavius, a slave, aged 25. Voluptas, his mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500324.summary: A funerary monument for Pultarius Flavius, a slave aged 25, commissioned by his mother Voluptas.

EDCS-05500326.translation: Sallvia Amoena, daughter of Publius, and Terentia Maxsuma saw to its construction for [their] mother.
EDCS-05500326.summary: A funerary commission by Sallvia Amoena and Terentia Maxsuma for their mother.

EDCS-05500327.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, sacred. To Taurilius, a most dutiful father, aged 80, and to Sotiris, [his] mother.
EDCS-05500327.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Taurilius, a father aged 80, and his mother Sotiris.

EDCS-05500328.translation: Terentia Maxsuma, daughter of Lucius, mother of Iulia Amoena, daughter of Caius, aged 27, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500328.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Amoena, aged 27, by her mother Terentia Maxsuma.

EDCS-05500329.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Terentius Primitius, aged 32. Iunia Festina, his mother, made this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500329.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Terentius Primitius, aged 32, made by his mother Iunia Festina.

EDCS-05500330.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caius Terentius Rufo, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 17. Caius Terentius Maxumus Terentianus, for his dutiful son.
EDCS-05500330.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Terentius Rufo, aged 17, dedicated by his father Caius Terentius Maxumus Terentianus.

EDCS-05500331.translation: Valerius Acro saw to the erection of this altar for Marcus Tullius Crescens, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, his mother-in-law, in accordance with the will. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500331.summary: A funerary altar commissioned by Valerius Acro, according to a will, for his mother-in-law Marcus Tullia Crescenta.

EDCS-05500332.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gaius Valerius Varanus, son of Gaius. Marcus Tatius [erected this].
EDCS-05500332.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Varanus, likely erected by Marcus Tatius.

EDCS-05500333.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus Valerius Gallio, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 33. His mother Licinia Maxuma saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500333.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Gallio, aged 33, erected by his mother Licinia Maxuma.

EDCS-05500335.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Successis. Rubria Sabina [erected this].
EDCS-05500335.summary: A funerary epitaph for Successis, likely erected by Rubria Sabina.

EDCS-05500336.translation: Marcus Aemilius Tuscus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 45. He arranged for this monument to be constructed in his will; he is buried here.
EDCS-05500336.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius Tuscus, aged 45, who arranged for his own burial monument in his will.

EDCS-05500337.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus Antonius Lupus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, of Olisipo. He is buried here.
EDCS-05500337.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Lupus of Olisipo, indicating he is buried at this location.

EDCS-05500338.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Celer, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, of Olisipo.
EDCS-05500338.summary: A funerary epitaph for Celer of Olisipo, son of Marcus.

EDCS-05500339.translation: For Marciana, daughter of Marcus, aged 22.
EDCS-05500339.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marciana, daughter of Marcus, aged 22.

EDCS-05500340.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Modesta, aged 30. Her mother Iulia Rufina set up [this monument] for her most dutiful daughter; she is buried here.
EDCS-05500340.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modesta, aged 30, erected by her mother Iulia Rufina, indicating she is buried here.

EDCS-05500341.translation: To Fortuna, Sabina willingly paid her vow.
EDCS-05500341.summary: A votive inscription recording Sabina fulfilling a vow made to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-05500342.translation: Sacred to Imperial Piety. Valerius Maximus set up these statues in memory of himself and of his daughters.
EDCS-05500342.summary: A dedication of statues to the personification of Imperial Piety, set up by Valerius Maximus for himself and his daughters.

EDCS-05500343.translation: His mother placed this for Gaius Attius Attianus Rufinus.
EDCS-05500343.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her son, Gaius Attius Attianus Rufinus.

EDCS-05500344.translation: Gaius Attius Rufus and his wife Sabinula for themselves.
EDCS-05500344.summary: This inscription records a tomb intended for Gaius Attius Rufus and his wife Sabinula.

EDCS-05500345.translation: Antonia Modesta, mother, and Lucius Avillius Celer, husband, arranged for this to be made in accordance with the will of Antonia Maxuma, sacred to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-05500345.summary: This is a funerary monument for Antonia Maxuma, arranged by her mother and husband according to her will.

EDCS-05500346.translation: Clemens, son of Optatus, aged 2 years. Optatus, brother of Turrio, lie here.
EDCS-05500346.summary: This inscription records the burial of a child, Clemens, and likely his father, Optatus, who is identified as the brother of Turrio.

EDCS-05500348.translation: Albonius willingly paid his vow for his son Saturninus while he was serving as a soldier.
EDCS-05500348.summary: This is a votive inscription where Albonius fulfilled a vow for his son Saturninus serving in the military.

EDCS-05500349.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Claudianus gave to Laberia Galla, daughter of Lucius, flaminica of Ebora, flaminica of the province of Lusitania, the expenses for the funeral, the burial plot, and a statue, by decree of the decurions of Collippones.
EDCS-05500349.summary: This inscription records the civic honors bestowed by the decurions of Collippones upon Laberia Galla, a flaminica, including funeral expenses, burial plot, and a statue, provided by Lucius Sulpicius Claudianus.

EDCS-05500350.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed for Quintus Naevidius Quirinus, son of Rufinus of Collipponensis, and his daughter Claudia Silvanilla. Lucius Sulpicius Claudianus [erected this].
EDCS-05500350.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Quintus Naevidius Quirinus and his daughter Claudia Silvanilla by Lucius Sulpicius Claudianus.

EDCS-05500351.translation: Dutia Avita, daughter, arranged for this to be made for Albura, daughter of Titus, sacred to the spirits of the departed, mother.
EDCS-05500351.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Dutia Avita for her mother Albura.

EDCS-05500352.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rufus, son of Rufus, and Rufina, daughter of Rufus, mother, set this up for Avita, daughter of Rufus, aged 17 years.
EDCS-05500352.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by a father and daughter for Avita, a daughter and sister, aged 17.

EDCS-05500354.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Avitus, aged 61 years. Aufidia [erected this].
EDCS-05500354.summary: This funerary inscription is for Flavius Avitus, aged 61, erected by Aufidia.

EDCS-05500355.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Frontonius Avitus had this set up for his most dutiful father, Marcus Frontonius Frontonius. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500355.summary: A son erected this tombstone for his devoted father, Marcus Frontonius Frontonius.

EDCS-05500356.translation: Marcus Granius Vegetus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Lobessa had this set up for her husband.
EDCS-05500356.summary: A wife erected this tombstone for her husband, Marcus Granius Vegetus.

EDCS-05500357.translation: To Quintus Laerius Scipio of the tribe Quirina, aged 30. Quintilla, mother, for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500357.summary: A mother dedicated this memorial to her dutiful son, Quintus Laerius Scipio.

EDCS-05500358.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rusticus, aged 26. Turpilla, mother, had this set up for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500358.summary: A mother erected this tombstone for her devoted son, Rusticus.

EDCS-05500359.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Tolia Maxima, aged 40. Claudius Tiberianus for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-05500359.summary: A husband erected this tombstone for his devoted wife, Tolia Maxima.

EDCS-05500361.translation: Sacred to Minerva, in memory of Carisia, daughter of Gaius, Quintilla.
EDCS-05500361.summary: A dedication to Minerva in memory of Carisia Quintilla.

EDCS-05500362.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Dutius Tancinus, daughter. Amoena, daughter of M. Silvanus, had this set up for her mother.
EDCS-05500362.summary: A daughter erected this tombstone for her mother, Dutius Tancinus.

EDCS-05500363.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Sulpicia Collipponensis, aged 35. Gallaecus, freedman of his Republic, had this set up for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-05500363.summary: A freedman erected this tombstone for his devoted wife, Sulpicia Collipponensis.

EDCS-05500365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Arruntia Montani, aged 60. Laberia Quinti filia Flava had this set up for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-05500365.summary: A daughter erected this tombstone for her devoted mother, Arruntia Montani.

EDCS-05500366.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For A. Rufinus, aged 17. Quintus A. Maximus, father, had this set up for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500366.summary: A father erected this tombstone for his devoted son, A. Rufinus.

EDCS-05500367.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Recepta, daughter, had this set up for her most devoted mother, Iulia Marciana, aged 60.
EDCS-05500367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her mother, Iulia Marciana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500368.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paternus, father, had this set up for his most devoted son, Iulius Paternianus, aged 20.
EDCS-05500368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son, Iulius Paternianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500369.translation: Quintus Servilius Avitus, heir of Gaius Servilius Laurus, his father, had this made from Sulpicia Avita, daughter of Lucius, according to her will.
EDCS-05500369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by an heir in accordance with the will of the deceased, Sulpicia Avita, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05500370.translation: To Terentia Camira, daughter of Quintus. Terentia Maxuma, daughter of Docquiricus, her mother.
EDCS-05500370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her daughter, Terentia Camira, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500373.translation: Vasega Marinianus, to one deserving well, paid his due with a willing spirit. I set this up as a deserved reward.
EDCS-05500373.summary: This is a dedication made by Vasega Marinianus to an unnamed recipient, likely a deity or a benefactor, expressing gratitude and fulfilling an obligation, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500374.translation: Uramus Viionisea, son of Vixxavius, aged 30. Aturam NXXXX Doquira LITTG.
EDCS-05500374.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording a person named Uramus Viionisea and possibly other individuals or place names, with some numerical data, likely a funerary or administrative text from a provincial area.

EDCS-05500375.translation: To the god Mars Neto. Valerius Avitus and Marcus Turranius Sulpicius, from the village of Baedorus, of the tribe Pintonum.
EDCS-05500375.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars Neto by two individuals, Valerius Avitus and Marcus Turranius Sulpicius, who are identified by their origin and tribal affiliation, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500376.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Flavus, father, and Antonia, mother, and Flavia, sister, had this set up for their most devoted son and brother, Antonius Flavius, aged 40.
EDCS-05500376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the parents and sister for Antonius Flavius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500377.translation: To Aurelia Avita Restituta, daughter of Gallus, aged five. Marcus Gallius Avitus, for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-05500377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, Aurelia Avita Restituta, set up by her father, Marcus Gallius Avitus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500378.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Musaeus had this set up for his most devoted son, Aurelius Rufinus, aged 17.
EDCS-05500378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son, Aurelius Rufinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500380.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Laberianus and Publia Avita had this made for their most devoted son, Marcus Aurelius Avitus, aged 21.
EDCS-05500380.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Aurelius Avitus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-05500381.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Prisca placed this for her most devoted son, Lucius Aurelius Rufus, aged 26, buried here.
EDCS-05500381.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Aurelius Rufus, set up by his mother.

EDCS-05500382.translation: To Cilia, daughter of Cilius, a slave girl of Fla Flavi, and to her brother.
EDCS-05500382.summary: A funerary inscription for Cilia and her brother, likely set up by their owner or a relative.

EDCS-05500383.translation: To Charito, belonging to January, our emperor.
EDCS-05500383.summary: An inscription possibly dedicating a monument or item to Charito, associated with an emperor.

EDCS-05500384.summary: A self-funded funerary monument set up by Chrysis.

EDCS-05500385.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ocellia, mother, had this made for Felix, freedman of Boutia, and for Fortunatus, freedman of Felix.
EDCS-05500385.summary: A funerary monument for two freedmen, set up by their mother or patron.

EDCS-05500386.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Grata, the patroness, set this up for Gallia Praepusa, her freedwoman, aged 25.
EDCS-05500386.summary: A funerary monument for the freedwoman Gallia Praepusa, set up by her patroness.

EDCS-05500387.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Arquia Helena, the mother, had this made for her most dutiful children, Helenus, aged 33, Festiva, aged 18, and Augustina, aged 15.
EDCS-05500387.summary: A funerary monument for three children, set up by their mother.

EDCS-05500388.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Bovia Materna and Iulia Maxima had this made for their most devoted father, Gaius Iulius Maternus, aged 64, with Julius Dexter, his freedman, overseeing it for the merits of his patron.
EDCS-05500388.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Iulius Maternus, set up by his daughters and overseen by his freedman.

EDCS-05500389.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelia Romula, the mother, and the salutary collegium had this made for her most devoted son, Marcus Iulius Seranus, aged 32, who died and was buried while traveling to the city.
EDCS-05500389.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Iulius Seranus, set up by his mother and a collegium, noting his death during travel.

EDCS-05500390.translation: To Gaius Kadius, aged 36. Gaius Kadius Salvianus, his brother, and Albania Anchiale, his wife, arranged for this to be made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500390.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Kadius, arranged by his brother and wife.

EDCS-05500391.translation: To the departed spirits of Lobessa, aged 60. Aponia Iunia, her most dutiful mother, arranged for this to be made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500391.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lobessa, arranged by her mother.

EDCS-05500392.translation: To the departed spirits of Publius Lucanius Reburrinus, son of Publius, aged 33, buried in Rome. Publia Procula, his mother.
EDCS-05500392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Lucanius Reburrinus, set up by his mother.

EDCS-05500393.translation: To the departed spirits of Quintus Lucceius Rufinus, aged 25. Lucceius Severinus, his father, placed this.
EDCS-05500393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Lucceius Rufinus, set up by his father.

EDCS-05500394.translation: To the departed spirits. For Maela Boblaeni, aged 55. She arranged for this to be made for herself.
EDCS-05500394.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maela Boblaeni, who arranged for her own burial.

EDCS-05500395.translation: To the departed spirits of Materna, daughter of Caeno, aged 40. Maelia Martialis, her most dutiful mother, placed this.
EDCS-05500395.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Materna, arranged by her mother.

EDCS-05500396.translation: To the departed spirits of Pullae, daughter of Pollio, and Ulleae, daughter of Tancinus. Avita, her mother, and Niger Coelae, her freedman, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500396.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pullae and Ulleae, arranged by the mother of Pullae and a freedman of Ulleae.

EDCS-05500397.translation: To the departed spirits of Rufina, daughter of Rufus, aged 22. Aponia Lobessa, her grandmother, and Aponia Iunia, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-05500397.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, set up by her grandmother and mother.

EDCS-05500398.translation: To the departed spirits. Sulpicia Helene, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sulpicia Phoebe, her most dutiful daughter, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Helene, arranged by her daughter.

EDCS-05500399.translation: To the departed spirits of Sulpicia Maxsuma, aged 16. Sulpicia Amoena, her most dutiful granddaughter, arranged for this to be made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Maxsuma, arranged by her granddaughter.

EDCS-05500400.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sulpicia Gemmula had this made for her daughter Sulpicia Stercusia, aged 26.
EDCS-05500400.summary: A funerary dedication by Sulpicia Gemmula for her daughter, Sulpicia Stercusia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500401.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Avitus, son of Valerius Marinus, aged 30. His mother Valeria Fuscilla set this up for her dearest, most dutiful, and obedient son. I wish verses to be written in the title, and may Valerius Avitus have written them decently. Born in Conimbrica, death suddenly snatched him away. I lived thirty years without fault in my life. Live, you who will live; I warn you, death presses upon all.
EDCS-05500401.summary: A funerary monument for Valerius Avitus, erected by his mother Valeria Fuscilla, which includes a self-composed epitaph reflecting on his life and warning the living about mortality, likely from Roman Lusitania.

EDCS-05500402.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Rufus, aged 23. His parents Turanius Primitivus and Liberia Stercusia set this up for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500402.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Turanius Primitivus and Liberia Stercusia for their son Valerius Rufus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500403.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Vibianus, son of Vibius, aged 30. Severus and Ria, his heirs, and Aprilis, his freedman.
EDCS-05500403.summary: A funerary dedication for Vibianus by his heirs Severus, Ria, and his freedman Aprilis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500404.translation: Marcus Publicius Gentilis and his son Modes had this put up for his wife and mother. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05500404.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Publicius Gentilis and his son Modes for his wife and mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500405.translation: Gaius Flavius Baeticus, the heir, had this made according to the will of Manius Antistius Agrippa, son of Quirinus, known as Agrippinus.
EDCS-05500405.summary: A funerary dedication executed by the heir Gaius Flavius Baeticus according to the will of Manius Antistius Agrippa, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500406.translation: Sacred to Piety. Julia Modesta from her own estate, in honor of the family of Sextus Aponius Scaevus Flaccus, her husband, flamen of the province of Lusitania, and in honor of the family of her parents, the Julii.
EDCS-05500406.summary: A dedication to the personification of Piety by Julia Modesta, honoring her husband, a provincial flamen, and her parents' family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500407.translation: Julia Modesta, flaminica of the province of Lusitania, repaired and donated these gates and colonnades from her own estate.
EDCS-05500407.summary: A public works dedication by Julia Modesta, a provincial flaminica, who repaired and donated gates and colonnades to her city, likely from Roman Lusitania.

EDCS-05500409.translation: For Julia Flavia, daughter of Cnaeus, to her patron, Iulius Rufus.
EDCS-05500409.summary: A dedication to Julia Flavia, daughter of Cnaeus, by her patron Iulius Rufus, likely from Roman Lusitania.

EDCS-05500410.translation: From her will, for Manlia Probilla.
EDCS-05500410.summary: A funerary dedication for Manlia Probilla, to be carried out according to her will, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500411.translation: A temple to the Genius of the municipality, Caius Cantius Modestinus from his own patrimony.
EDCS-05500411.summary: This inscription records a temple dedicated to the Genius of the municipality by Caius Cantius Modestinus.

EDCS-05500412.translation: A temple to Victory, Caius Cantius Modestinus from his own patrimony.
EDCS-05500412.summary: This inscription records a temple dedicated to Victory by Caius Cantius Modestinus.

EDCS-05500414.translation: Laelius Severus placed this for the Lares, willingly.
EDCS-05500414.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Lares by Laelius Severus.

EDCS-05500415.translation: For Florus with Numaper, from the will.
EDCS-05500415.summary: This inscription records a dedication for Florus and Numaper, commissioned by a will.

EDCS-05500416.translation: His brother Flaccus saw to this for Fronton Pellius.
EDCS-05500416.summary: This inscription records a monument commissioned by Flaccus for his brother Fronton Pellius.

EDCS-05500417.translation: Here lies Lucanus, son of Supo and Camala.
EDCS-05500417.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucanus, son of Supo and Camala.

EDCS-05500418.translation: Taporus, son of Maelus Boutus, 60 years old, lies here; may the earth be light for you; his sons saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500418.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Taporus, son of Maelus Boutus, aged 60, and notes that his sons commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05500419.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: Areius and Rufina saw to the construction for their father Rufinus and their mother Rufina and their sister Rufina.
EDCS-05500419.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Areius and Rufina for their deceased parents and sister.

EDCS-05500420.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld: for Sunua, daughter of Bocchus, 11 years old, for her most dutiful mother Amoena and her son-in-law Albinianus saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500420.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Amoena and Albinianus for Sunua, daughter of Bocchus, who died at age 11.

EDCS-05500421.translation: For Valerius Reburrus, 17 years old, his father and mother saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500421.summary: This funerary inscription records the monument commissioned by the parents for their son Valerius Reburrus, aged 17.

EDCS-05500424.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. The mother had this made for Rufina, daughter of Rufus, aged 57.
EDCS-05500424.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, set up by her mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500425.translation: Tiro, son of Gallus, aged 13, lies here. As you pass by, I ask you, may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05500425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiro, a young boy, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500426.translation: Rufinus and Tiro wrote: To Veaminicus, son of C(o)ridon, Amugom, son of Lammatos, Crougeos, son of Magareus, Petranios, a pig for Jove, for Caeilobrigos.
EDCS-05500426.summary: This inscription, likely from a Celtic-speaking region under Roman influence, records a dedication of a pig, possibly as a votive offering, by Rufinus and Tiro.

EDCS-05500427.translation: Camlae, aged 3. Longeia, mother of Camalus, made this.
EDCS-05500427.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child named Camlae, set up by her mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500428.translation: Camala, daughter of Pisirus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05500428.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camala, a young woman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500429.translation: For Trofimenus, aged 17. Ursus had this made for himself and for his wife.
EDCS-05500429.summary: This funerary epitaph records Ursus commissioning a tomb for himself and his wife, with a mention of Trofimenus, likely their son or relative, who died at 17.

EDCS-05500430.translation: Reucalius, son of Turolus, paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-05500430.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Reucalius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500431.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Procilia, freedwoman of Rust, aged 50. [Dis Manibus] Prociliae P. A.
EDCS-05500431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Procilia, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500432.translation: For Rufo, son of Fuscus, aged 25. Fuscus, son of Albinus, [made this] for his son and for himself.
EDCS-05500432.summary: This funerary epitaph records Fuscus commissioning a tomb for his son Rufo and himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500433.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Rufo, aged 60, son of Lucius. For Amoena, aged 4, daughter of Severus. For Placidus, aged 30, son of Calvus. For Firmina, aged 40, daughter of Firmus. Lucius H., son of Rufus, had this made.
EDCS-05500433.summary: This is a large funerary epitaph commemorating five individuals: Rufo, Amoena, Placidas, Firmina, and likely Lucius himself, possibly a family tomb, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500435.translation: To the god Mercury, Aponius Sosumus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500435.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury by Aponius Sosumus.

EDCS-05500436.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed: Marcus, son of Marcinius, aged 60; his wife Cilea.
EDCS-05500436.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus and his wife Cilea.

EDCS-05500441.translation: Catuenus, son of Docquiricus, consecrated this to the Lares.
EDCS-05500441.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Lares by Catuenus, son of Docquiricus.

EDCS-05500442.translation: Tiberius Claudius Sanecius, a cavalryman of the 3rd Lusitanian Cohort, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the gods and goddesses of Conimbriga.
EDCS-05500442.summary: This is a votive inscription to the gods and goddesses of Conimbriga by Tiberius Claudius Sanecius, a soldier.

EDCS-05500443.translation: Modestus, son of Ambatus, aged 60, and Cornelia Gensulia, aged 50, are buried here; may the earth be light upon you. Caius Ammius Modestinus had this made for his father, Firmus Modesti, his freedman, and patron.
EDCS-05500443.summary: This is a funerary inscription and epitaph for Modestus and Cornelia Gensulia, commissioned by the freedman Caius Ammius Modestinus for his father.

EDCS-05500444.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Rufinus Limicus made this for himself and for his most dutiful children, Sulpicia Cilea, Sulpicius Rufus, and Sulpicia Rufina.
EDCS-05500444.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Sulpicius Rufinus Limicus for himself and his children.

EDCS-05500445.translation: To Iovis Chryseros, a freedman of the Igaeditani, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500445.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Iovis Chryseros by a freedman.

EDCS-05500446.translation: Cominius Manilius, son of Manilius, [dedicated this] to Mars.
EDCS-05500446.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Mars by Cominius Manilius.

EDCS-05500449.translation: Maurilla, freedwoman of Celer, had this made for her husband Arrenus, son of Crescentius, of Libia.
EDCS-05500449.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Maurilla for her husband Arrenus.

EDCS-05500451.translation: Pontius, brother [dedicated this] to Cilius, son of Pintamus.
EDCS-05500451.summary: This is an inscription set up by Pontius for his brother Cilius.

EDCS-05500452.translation: Curia Vitalis had this made for Gaius Curio Firmanus, son of Pullus, of the voting tribe Quirina, aged 63, and for herself.
EDCS-05500452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Curio Firmanus, commissioned by his wife Curia Vitalis.

EDCS-05500453.translation: Eurus, son of Cilus, aged 3, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Ialglius had this made for his son and himself.
EDCS-05500453.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Eurus, aged 3, and was commissioned by Ialglius.

EDCS-05500455.translation: Lucretia Avita, aged 3, and Marcus Lucretius Onesumus had this made for their daughter Aponia Fundana.
EDCS-05500455.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Marcus Lucretius Onesumus and Lucretia Avita commissioned a monument for their daughter Aponia Fundana.

EDCS-05500456.translation: Celerina, freedwoman of Marcia Paullina, aged 16, and Verecunda, freedwoman of Marcia Paullina, aged 32, and Tancina, freedwoman of Paulus, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you all.
EDCS-05500456.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for three freedwomen, Celerina, Verecunda, and Tancina.

EDCS-05500457.translation: Rufina, daughter of Rufius Tongetamus, had this made in accordance with the will of Modestina, daughter of Modestus, for her husband Modestus, son of Proculus, and for her mother.
EDCS-05500457.summary: This funerary epitaph, commissioned in accordance with a will, is for Modestus and his mother, dedicated by Rufina.

EDCS-05500458.translation: Reburrus son of Reburrus, aged 26, and Marcia, daughter of Marcus, had this made for their son and husband (Reburrus), and for themselves.
EDCS-05500458.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Reburrus, son of Reburrus, aged 26, and was commissioned by his parents.

EDCS-05500459.translation: Ippa, daughter of Rufus, and Avius, son of Rufus, for Rufus Tri[...]
EDCS-05500459.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph, possibly for Rufus Tri[---].

EDCS-05500460.translation: Reburrus had this made for his brother Tuatro Frontonis, of Clunia.
EDCS-05500460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Reburrus for his brother Tuatro Frontonis.

EDCS-05500463.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you. Taporus, from the village of Talabara, had this made for Maeilo, son of Camalus. Progela, daughter of Maeilo, and Duatius, son of Aranton, also contributed.
EDCS-05500463.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maeilo, son of Camalus, commissioned by Taporus, with contributions from Progela and Duatius.

EDCS-05500464.translation: Amminus, son of Andaitia, of Bandiarba, has willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Riaicus.
EDCS-05500464.summary: This is a votive inscription by Amminus to Riaicus.

EDCS-05500465.translation: Quintus, son of Modestus, aged 25, and Placidia, daughter of Modestus, aged 13, and Boudica, daughter of Flaccus, and Modestus, son of Celtiates, made this for their children, wife, and themselves.
EDCS-05500465.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deceased and those who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05500466.translation: Tertulla, mother, arranged for this to be made for Traianus Rufinus Niger.
EDCS-05500466.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother commissioning a monument for her son.

EDCS-05500467.translation: Rufus, son of Tanginus, fulfilled his vow to Victoria.
EDCS-05500467.summary: This votive inscription records Rufus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Victoria.

EDCS-05500468.translation: Proculus, son of Aebarus, Proculus, son of Meiduer, and Procula, daughter of Proculus, aged 15, lie here; may the earth be light upon you. Casa Bouti arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500468.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deceased and the person who arranged for the monument, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-05500469.translation: Montanus and companions arranged for this to be made for Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05500469.summary: This is a votive inscription indicating that a group of people commissioned a monument for Jupiter.

EDCS-05500470.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 28th time, consul for the 13th time, father of the country, the Augustan boundary marker between the people of Lancienses and the people of Oppidani and the people of Igaeditani.
EDCS-05500470.summary: This inscription records an Imperial boundary marker established by Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-05500471.translation: To the holy goddess Ataecina of the Turibrigi, and to Proserpina, Puitia [dedicated this].
EDCS-05500471.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddesses Ataecina and Proserpina.

EDCS-05500474.translation: To the springs, Tiberius Claudius Artemidorus, father [dedicated this].
EDCS-05500474.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Artemidorus dedicating something to springs.

EDCS-05500476.translation: To the springs, Iulia Lupa gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-05500476.summary: This votive inscription records Iulia Lupa gladly fulfilling a vow to the springs.

EDCS-05500477.translation: To Fortuna, Iatius Emetrius gladly dedicated this.
EDCS-05500477.summary: This votive inscription records Iatius Emetrius gladly dedicating something to Fortuna.

EDCS-05500478.translation: Sacred to Mars. Vettilla, daughter of Paculus.
EDCS-05500478.summary: A private dedication to the god Mars by Vettilla, daughter of Paculus.

EDCS-05500479.translation: To the Nymphs. Saturninus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500479.summary: A votive dedication to the Nymphs by Saturninus.

EDCS-05500480.translation: Lucius Cordius Symphorus, the physician, dedicated this to Venus Victorious, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500480.summary: A votive dedication to Venus Victorious by Lucius Cordius Symphorus, a physician.

EDCS-05500484.translation: Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, consul for the third time, holding tribunician power for the third time.
EDCS-05500484.summary: An inscription recording the offices held by Marcus Agrippa, consul and tribune, likely from the late 1st century BCE.

EDCS-05500485.translation: To Julia, daughter of [name missing], daughter of Caesar [name missing].
EDCS-05500485.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a woman named Julia, daughter of Caesar, with missing information.

EDCS-05500486.summary: An inscription possibly recording a dedication or a tombstone for Tiberius Claudius by Publius Lucretius.

EDCS-05500490.translation: To the Divine Antoninus Pius Augustus.
EDCS-05500490.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the deified emperor Antoninus Pius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500493.translation: Our lord Theodosius, perpetual Augustus, for the most fortunate day of the tenth anniversary, [Greek text illegible].
EDCS-05500493.summary: An inscription celebrating the tenth anniversary of the emperor Theodosius, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-05500499.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Domitius Pastor, a veteran of the Seventh Gemina legion, aged 86. Valeria Vernacla made this for her most devoted host. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500499.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran of the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by his host, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05500502.translation: The colony of Augusta Emerita made this. Seleucus and Anthus.
EDCS-05500502.summary: An inscription indicating that Seleucus and Anthus, likely officials or citizens of Augusta Emerita, commissioned a public work.

EDCS-05500506.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Silvanus, son of Aristaeus, aged 80, a pearl-dealer. Prepis, his freedwoman and heir, had this made for her well-deserving patron. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500506.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Silvanus, a pearl-dealer, erected by his freedwoman and heir, Prepis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500508.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gratus, brother and slave of Lucius Iulius Secundus, a money-changer, aged 25. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500508.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gratus, erected by his brother and slave, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500510.translation: To Gaius Allius Marci, freedman of Aeminium, for his most obedient father, from Valeria Vegeta.
EDCS-05500510.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Allius Marci, set up by his daughter Valeria Vegeta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500511.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aventinus, freedman of Maxima, from Ammaiensis, aged 70. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. Amabilis, freedwoman of Successa, made this for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-05500511.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aventinus, erected by his wife Amabilis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500512.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Scitianus, from Araus, aged 15. Here he lies.
EDCS-05500512.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Scitianus, a young boy, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500513.translation: Salvius Pacius Pai. Here he lies. 1152.
EDCS-05500513.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Salvius Pacius Pai, whose age is indicated by the numeral VTR DXII, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500516.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ianuarius, son of Venustus, from Emerita, aged 85. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Flavius Sexticius made this for his most deserving father.
EDCS-05500516.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, erected by his son Titus Flavius Sexticius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500517.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Iuventius Annianus, freedman of Urbicus, from Emerita, aged 14. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500517.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iuventius Annianus, a young boy, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500518.translation: Maria Gemina, from Emerita, aged more or less 50. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. She arranged for this to be made for herself during her lifetime, next to her husband.
EDCS-05500518.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maria Gemina, who arranged for her own burial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500525.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ammonika Maura, aged 18. Ammonicus Maurus made this for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-05500525.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ammonika Maura, erected by her father Ammonicus Maurus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500527.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Baebius Florus Galeria Pacensis, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publius Iulius Hermetion, father, and Iulia Daphne, mother, made this for their most dutiful and excellent son.
EDCS-05500527.summary: This is a tombstone set up by parents for their deceased son, Quintus Baebius Florus Galeria Pacensis, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05500528.translation: To Claudia Maria Pacensis, from Tiberius Claudius Artemidorus.
EDCS-05500528.summary: This is an inscription dedicating a monument or object to Claudia Maria Pacensis by Tiberius Claudius Artemidorus.

EDCS-05500529.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Licinius Fundanianus Salaciensis, aged 70, his wife Mummia Modestina made this for her most dutiful husband, under the care of Publius Albicianus Sal. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500529.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a wife for her deceased husband, Lucius Licinius Fundanianus Salaciensis, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05500532.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Flavia Flavina, mother, and Floria Severa, wife, saw to the making of this for Marcus Rufinus Albinus Taporus, aged 25.
EDCS-05500532.summary: This is a tombstone commissioned by the mother and wife for Marcus Rufinus Albinus Taporus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05500533.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Rubrius Flaccus Tuccitanus, aged 33, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Rubria Nais, mother, made this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500533.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a mother for her deceased son, Caius Rubrius Flaccus Tuccitanus, who died at the age of 33.

EDCS-05500534.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Pompeia Cloutiane Turdula, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500534.summary: This is a tombstone for Pompeia Cloutiane Turdula, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05500536.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Aelius Justus Lupatius, dearest son, who lived 1 year and 10 months, his most dutiful parents made this.
EDCS-05500536.summary: This is a tombstone set up by parents for their young son, Aelius Justus Lupatius, who died at the age of 1 year and 10 months.

EDCS-05500537.translation: Lucius Agilius Viator, son of Sextus, for Numisia.
EDCS-05500537.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Agilius Viator, son of Sextus, to someone named Numisia.

EDCS-05500538.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Alfidia Athenais, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Alfidii Athenodorus and Helpis made this for their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-05500538.summary: This is a tombstone erected by parents for their deceased daughter, Alfidia Athenais, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05500540.translation: Alfius Veto is buried here; 12 feet in front, 8 feet in the plot.
EDCS-05500540.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Alfius Veto and specifies the dimensions of his grave plot.

EDCS-05500541.translation: Amphonina Iulia Nymphe is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Her husband Hedyphnus made this for his excellent wife, and for his excellent mother-in-law, most dutifully.
EDCS-05500541.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amphonina Iulia Nymphe, set up by her husband and mother-in-law, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500542.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ancharius Septimianus, aged 65, and his most dutiful wife Aplania Lais made this for her most dutiful husband; he is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500542.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ancharius Septimianus, set up by his devoted wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500547.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Dorus had this made for his most dutiful wife Atilia Nicopolis, aged 45; she is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500547.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilia Nicopolis, whose husband Aurelius Dorus arranged for her burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500548.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attennia Helene, aged 75, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Her freedmen Attenii Thetis, Rusticilla, and Pamphilus made this.
EDCS-05500548.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attennia Helene, set up by her freedmen, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500549.translation: Quintus Attius Mo is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Ennia, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-05500549.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Attius Mo, with a posthumous addition or mention of Ennia, daughter of Caius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500551.translation: Caius Camerius Claranus, aged 11 months, is laid here; I ask you, passerby, as you go by, say 'may the earth lie lightly upon you'.
EDCS-05500551.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the infant Caius Camerius Claranus, appealing to passersby for a blessing, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500554.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Cassius Calicles, aged 20, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Iulius Ursianus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-05500554.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cassius Calicles, set up by his brother Caius Iulius Ursianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500556.translation: Clovatia Irena, freedwoman of Caius, the nurse, 12 feet in front and 8 feet in the field.
EDCS-05500556.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot belonging to Clovatia Irena, a freedwoman and nurse, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500558.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Marina, daughter of Lucius, aged 27, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500558.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Marina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500562.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Docquiricus Vitalio, aged 65, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Albania Sabina, your devoted wife, and dearest friends, with whom you lived for 38 years, your dutiful spirit, best soul.
EDCS-05500562.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Docquiricus Vitalio, set up by his devoted wife Albania Sabina and friends, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500567.translation: Gallaeca, wife of Cornelius Fuscus, lived 9 years, 3 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-05500567.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gallaeca, wife of Cornelius Fuscus.

EDCS-05500569.translation: Marcus Helvius Marsua, freedman of the woman, aged 60. Mallia Galla, freedwoman of the woman, his wife, aged 41. Here they lie. May the earth be light upon you. You who pass by on a hurried journey, traveler, halt your step, I ask, for the favor I seek is a small delay. I beg that as you pass, you say: 'May the earth be light upon you.'
EDCS-05500569.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Helvius Marsua and his wife Mallia Galla, requesting passersby to wish them a light earth.

EDCS-05500570.translation: To Marcus Helvius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, and to his brother, and to Quintus Helvius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, and to his brother Moderatus.
EDCS-05500570.summary: A dedication of a tomb or monument to brothers Marcus Helvius and Quintus Helvius Moderatus.

EDCS-05500575.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Iuliana made this for her incomparable husband, Iulius Optatus, aged 45. He lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500575.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Iulia Iuliana to her husband Iulius Optatus.

EDCS-05500576.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Ianuaria made this for her most devoted brother, Iulius Patroclus, aged 41. He lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500576.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Iulia Ianuaria to her brother Iulius Patroclus.

EDCS-05500577.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iulius Moderatus, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, aged [ ]. He lies here. May the earth be light upon you. Caius Iulius Probus had this made for his father.
EDCS-05500577.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Moderatus, for whom his father Caius Iulius Probus arranged the burial.

EDCS-05500578.translation: Caius Iulius Lydus, Lucius Vibius Gemellus, Vibia Admata. Here they lie. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05500578.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Lydus, Lucius Vibius Gemellus, and Vibia Admata.

EDCS-05500579.translation: His wife Iunia made this. Anatelontes lived 63 years.
EDCS-05500579.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Anatelontes, for whom his wife Iunia made the monument.

EDCS-05500582.translation: Marcus Iunius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, Caius Armillus, and Aurelia Quintilla. Here they lie. May the earth be light upon you. Quinta, daughter of IV V [Illegible].
EDCS-05500582.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius, Caius Armillus, Aurelia Quintilla, and Quinta.

EDCS-05500586.translation: Licinia Baeticata, freedwoman of Licinius, aged 35. Titus Manilius Quietus, aged [ ]. He arranged for this to be made at his own expense for himself and his wife.
EDCS-05500586.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Baeticata and Titus Manilius Quietus, with Titus Manilius Quietus arranging it for himself and his wife.

EDCS-05500589.translation: At the age of 8 years, Lucius Mar Ursianus and Vaissmo and had this made for themselves; he is buried here.
EDCS-05500589.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child who died at the age of 8, with the monument commissioned by Lucius Mar Ursianus and Vaissmo.

EDCS-05500590.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maxuma, aged 9 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500590.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Maxuma who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-05500598.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Pompeius Similis, also known as Titullus, lived 74 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500598.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Pompeius Similis, who died at the age of 74.

EDCS-05500601.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pusinca, aged 40 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Attius Priscus, dutiful, for one deserving.
EDCS-05500601.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pusinca, who died at the age of 40, with a dedication by Attius Priscus.

EDCS-05500602.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Reburrus Sexticius Serenianus, Caius Sexticius, Publius Sexticius, sons, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Sexticius Sulpicianus placed [this] for his own [family] and for Minicia Firmillae.
EDCS-05500602.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Reburrus Sexticius Serenianus and his sons Caius Sexticius and Publius Sexticius, with a dedication by Lucius Sexticius Sulpicianus for his family and Minicia Firmillae.

EDCS-05500603.translation: To Titi, daughter of Titi, aged 28 years, and to [his wife] aged 57 years.
EDCS-05500603.summary: This fragment of an inscription commemorates Titi, daughter of Titi, aged 28, and likely her mother.

EDCS-05500604.translation: Titus Sempronius for himself. To the gods.
EDCS-05500604.summary: This is a very short inscription, possibly the beginning of a dedication or funerary text, by Titus Sempronius for himself.

EDCS-05500605.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittius Fidus, aged 40 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500605.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sittius Fidus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-05500606.translation: Spathale, aged 70 years. Valeria made [this] for her most dutiful mother. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500606.summary: This funerary inscription records that Valeria commissioned a monument for her mother, Spathale, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05500608.translation: Caius Valerius Pacatus took care to have this made for his most wise father.
EDCS-05500608.summary: This inscription records that Caius Valerius Pacatus commissioned a monument for his father.

EDCS-05500609.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Vettia Rufina, daughter of Alfius Marcius.
EDCS-05500609.summary: A funerary dedication for Vettia Rufina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500610.translation: Titus Vibidius, freedman of Titus, and Antestia Quinti, freedwoman of Iucundus, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500610.summary: A tomb inscription for two freed individuals, Titus Vibidius and Antestia Quinti, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500611.translation: Caius Vibius Albanus, Quinta, daughter of Marcia and Marcius.
EDCS-05500611.summary: A funerary dedication, likely for Caius Vibius Albanus and Quinta, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500612.translation: To her husband, aged 50 years, Caius Viriacius Vetto and Lucius Hortavius Marus.
EDCS-05500612.summary: A funerary dedication for a husband, aged 50, possibly set up by Caius Viriacius Vetto and Lucius Hortavius Marus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500613.translation: He lies here, may the earth lie lightly upon you, Vitalis. His sister Procula had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-05500613.summary: A funerary inscription for Vitalis, paid for by his sister Procula, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500614.translation: To his daughter Luci, aged 15 years, C. Commodus made this for himself.
EDCS-05500614.summary: A funerary inscription for Luci, aged 15, likely made by C. Commodus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500616.translation: To the lady, Turibrigens Adaegina, her husband.
EDCS-05500616.summary: A funerary dedication for Adaegina, by her husband, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500617.translation: To Jupiter of Taurum, for the health and safe return of Lupus, son of Albonius, and of his brother Capinius Albonius, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500617.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter for the health and return of Lupus and Capinius Albonius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500618.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, pontiff, consul, prince of the youth.
EDCS-05500618.summary: An honorary inscription for Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, likely from the early Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500620.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, patron.
EDCS-05500620.summary: An honorary inscription for Drusus Caesar, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500621.translation: To Domitia Augusta, Quintus Licinius Saturninus and Lucius Mummius Pomponianus, duovirs.
EDCS-05500621.summary: This is a dedication to an empress by two local magistrates, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-05500623.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the name and age of a deceased woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500624.translation: Publius Modestus, aged 70, is buried here. Papia Capitolina, his freedwoman and wife, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500624.summary: This is an epitaph for Publius Modestus, commissioned by his freedwoman and wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500625.translation: Fecunda Herenia is buried here, 12 feet. Famelius saw to its dedication with a sum of silver.
EDCS-05500625.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Fecunda Herenia, funded by Famelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500630.translation: Sacred to Mars. Vettius Rufus, a shoemaker, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500630.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars, dedicated by a shoemaker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500631.translation: Ponceia, daughter of Bouti, willingly paid her vow to Liber Pater.
EDCS-05500631.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Liber Pater, dedicated by Ponceia, daughter of Bouti, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500632.translation: Publius Aelius Fuscus placed this willingly.
EDCS-05500632.summary: This is a dedication of a monument by Publius Aelius Fuscus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500633.translation: For his merits, Cornelia placed her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05500633.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cornelia, dedicated in recognition of merits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500634.translation: Amana, daughter of Cilus, aged 60. Camira, daughter of Ambatus, aged 50. They are buried here. Camira saw to the construction for herself and her mother.
EDCS-05500634.summary: This is a double epitaph for Amana and Camira, commissioned by Camira for herself and her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500637.translation: Boutia Visali, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Avitus.
EDCS-05500637.summary: This is an epitaph for Boutia Visali, commissioned by Avitus, including a common funerary formula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500639.translation: Fusca, daughter of Caudus, may the earth lie lightly upon you here; Cancila, daughter of Doctius, had this made at her expense by her will.
EDCS-05500639.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fusca, daughter of Caudus, commissioned by Cancila, daughter of Doctius.

EDCS-05500640.translation: Iulius is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. He had this made.
EDCS-05500640.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius, with the work commissioned by himself.

EDCS-05500642.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Tonceta, daughter of Proculus.

EDCS-05500645.translation: Modesta, daughter of Quintus, [aged --] is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Her father had this made.
EDCS-05500645.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modesta, daughter of Quintus, commissioned by her father.

EDCS-05500646.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Sabina, daughter of Sabinus.

EDCS-05500647.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Vettia, daughter of Donatus, with her age.

EDCS-05500648.translation: Vettia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500648.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Vettia Modesta, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-05500649.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Abulaius, son of Vibus Tertullus, a slave, aged 8, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother and father [had this made].
EDCS-05500649.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Abulaius, aged 8, son of Vibus Tertullus, a slave, commissioned by his parents.

EDCS-05500650.translation: Anius, son of Caturon, called Triteus.
EDCS-05500650.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Anius, son of Caturon, also known as Triteus.

EDCS-05500651.translation: Gaius Epius, aged 60, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500651.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Epius, aged 60, with the work commissioned by himself.

EDCS-05500652.translation: Caturo, son of Caturonis, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His sons saw to it that this was made for their father.
EDCS-05500652.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Caturo, with his sons as dedicators.

EDCS-05500654.translation: Sacred to the god Mercury. ERPAVLEG placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500654.summary: A dedication to the god Mercury, indicating the donor ERPAVLEG fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-05500655.translation: Q. (placed this) for Severus, son of L. L. I., aged thirteen.
EDCS-05500655.summary: A funerary inscription for Severus, a thirteen-year-old, likely erected by his father L. L. I.

EDCS-05500656.summary: A simple funerary epitaph identifying the deceased as Quintus Cercius.

EDCS-05500658.translation: Tancinus, son of Tancinus, aged fifty-seven, is here. His wife saw to it that this was made.
EDCS-05500658.summary: A funerary inscription for Tancinus, with his wife as the dedicator.

EDCS-05500659.translation: Rufa lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Caius Sestilius Severus saw to it that this was made for his wife.
EDCS-05500659.summary: A funerary inscription for Rufa, erected by her husband Caius Sestilius Severus.

EDCS-05500662.translation: Caecilia, daughter of Quitus Maior, aged sixty-two.
EDCS-05500662.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying Caecilia, daughter of Quitus Maior, and her age.

EDCS-05500665.summary: An identifying inscription for Apponia Prima, likely related to Cornelius Senecae.

EDCS-05500666.translation: Anianus, slave of Lucius Iulius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500666.summary: A funerary inscription for Anianus, a slave belonging to Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-05500667.translation: The bearers (of the burden/of the altar) willingly placed the altar for the priest Lucius Septumius.
EDCS-05500667.summary: An inscription recording the voluntary dedication of an altar by unknown individuals for the priest Lucius Septumius.

EDCS-05500668.translation: Vegeta, daughter of Sempronius Africanus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500668.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vegeta, a woman aged 25, indicating her burial place and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500670.translation: Maura, daughter of Mailo, aged 30, lies here. Her mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500670.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Maura, a woman aged 30, commissioned by her mother.

EDCS-05500671.translation: Lucius Alefius erected an altar to Jupiter Solutorius.
EDCS-05500671.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Lucius Alefius to the deity Jupiter Solutorius, likely for a sanctuary or public space.

EDCS-05500672.translation: Lucius Hermelius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, a veteran of the XX legion.
EDCS-05500672.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Lucius Hermelius, a veteran of Legio XX, indicating his name, parentage, tribe, and military service.

EDCS-05500673.translation: A lies here. Coela set this up.
EDCS-05500673.summary: This is a tombstone inscription where 'A' is likely an initial, with Coela identified as the person who erected the monument for the deceased.

EDCS-05500674.translation: Arco and Camira, daughter of Visalius, son of Tancinus, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both. Their son arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500674.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a couple, Arco and Camira, commissioned by their son, expressing a wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-05500677.translation: Lucius Norsius, son of C. F[---], of the --- tribe, [---].
EDCS-05500677.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that records the name Lucius Norsius and likely his lineage and tribal affiliation, with much missing information.

EDCS-05500678.translation: Quinta, daughter of Butrio, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Arco, her husband, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500678.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quinta, aged 35, commissioned by her husband Arco.

EDCS-05500679.translation: Valeria Modesta, daughter of Quintus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500679.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Valeria Modesta, aged 50, with a common wish for the deceased's peaceful rest.

EDCS-05500681.translation: Cilia, daughter of Arcon, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500681.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Cilia, daughter of Arcon, expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-05500682.translation: Helvia Tertia, daughter of Rufus, aged 10, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500682.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of a young girl named Helvia Tertia.

EDCS-05500683.translation: Quinta, daughter of Gemellus, aged 35, and Helvia Gemela, daughter of Gemellus, aged 5, lie here. May it lie lightly upon you. Lucius took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500683.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of two individuals, Quinta and Helvia Gemela, with a commissioner named Lucius.

EDCS-05500685.translation: Saturnina, daughter of Pellius, willingly paid her vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-05500685.summary: An inscription recording a vow paid to Jupiter by Saturnina.

EDCS-05500686.translation: Abrunus, son of Lucius Divinus, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500686.summary: An inscription recording a vow paid by Abrunus, son of Lucius Divinus.

EDCS-05500687.translation: Gaius Valerius Albanus, from Suscenna, [erected] the altar to Lucius Divinus and willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-05500687.summary: An inscription recording an altar erected to Lucius Divinus and a vow paid by Gaius Valerius Albanus.

EDCS-05500688.translation: Abrunus, son of Camalus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500688.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Abrunus, son of Camalus.

EDCS-05500690.translation: Lucius Goutius, son of Camalus, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500690.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Goutius, aged 60.

EDCS-05500691.translation: Bovalus, son of Tancinus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He lies here with his sons SEVE and Quadratus.
EDCS-05500691.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Bovalus, son of Tancinus, along with his sons SEVE and Quadratus.

EDCS-05500693.summary: An inscription identifying Gaius Valerius Albanus as being from Suscenna.

EDCS-05500695.translation: Reve, son of Afer Albini, from Turolus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Anabaraeco.
EDCS-05500695.summary: An inscription recording a vow paid to Anabaraeco by Reve, son of Afer Albini.

EDCS-05500696.translation: Gaius Caecilius, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500696.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Caecilius, aged 80, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500697.translation: Caius Iulius Pelliocus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500697.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Pelliocus, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500698.translation: Lucius Rutilius Munus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His son had it made.
EDCS-05500698.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Rutilius Munus, aged 55, whose son commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05500700.translation: Camalus, son of Clotus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500700.summary: A funerary epitaph for Camalus, son of Clotus, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500701.translation: Severus made [this monument]. He is buried here.
EDCS-05500701.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating that Severus was the maker of his own monument.

EDCS-05500707.translation: Marcus Accius Crescens, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Currius Privatus had it made at his own expense.
EDCS-05500707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Accius Crescens, aged 60, commissioned by Caius Currius Privatus.

EDCS-05500708.translation: Acilia Tertia, daughter of Caius, aged 23, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500708.summary: A funerary epitaph for Acilia Tertia, daughter of Caius, aged 23, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500709.translation: Albinus, son of Rufus, aged 16, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500709.summary: A funerary epitaph for Albinus, son of Rufus, aged 16, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500710.translation: Quintus Caecilius Avitus, son of Sextus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500710.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Avitus, son of Sextus, aged 35, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500711.translation: Flaccus, aged 41, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500711.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flaccus, aged 41, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500712.translation: Flamilia, a slave woman, aged 20, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500712.summary: This inscription records the arrangement for a funerary monument by a slave woman named Flamilia.

EDCS-05500713.translation: Lucius Gavius Scodrinus, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-05500713.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Gavius Scodrinus, identified by name and age.

EDCS-05500714.translation: Herennia Severa, daughter of Caius, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, Caius.
EDCS-05500714.summary: This inscription records the burial of Herennia Severa, daughter of Caius, and includes a wish for her repose, possibly addressing her father.

EDCS-05500715.translation: Lucius Iulius Crescens, son of Lucius, aged 35, Pupelia Nigella, daughter of Aulus, aged [---].
EDCS-05500715.summary: This inscription begins to record two individuals, Lucius Iulius Crescens and Pupelia Nigella, giving their names, parentage, and ages, but is incomplete.

EDCS-05500716.translation: Caius Iulius Rusocus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500716.summary: This inscription records the burial of Caius Iulius Rusocus, aged 60, and includes a standard wish for his repose.

EDCS-05500717.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Marcus Iunius Nonius, likely a tombstone inscription but incomplete.

EDCS-05500718.translation: Macrinus, son of Gaius, aged 20, Aurelius Caecilius, aged 25. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500718.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Macrinus and Aurelius Caecilius, by name, parentage (for Macrinus), and age, with a wish for their repose.

EDCS-05500719.translation: Lucius Magius Tiro, son of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-05500719.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Magius Tiro, identified by name and father's name.

EDCS-05500720.translation: Mantaus, son of Albus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500720.summary: This inscription records the burial of Mantaus, son of Albus.

EDCS-05500721.summary: This inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Norbana, likely indicating a child's death.

EDCS-05500722.translation: Quintus Norbanus Prunicus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Cornelia Antiocis, to her son-in-law.
EDCS-05500722.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Norbanus Prunicus, placed by his mother-in-law, Cornelia Antiocis.

EDCS-05500723.translation: To Lucius Norbanus Rufus, aged 24, from Labeo, his uncle.
EDCS-05500723.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Norbanus Rufus, dedicated by his uncle Labeo.

EDCS-05500724.translation: Quintus Teius Paternus, son of Caius, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500724.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Teius Paternus, aged 15.

EDCS-05500725.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Trebius, son of Caius, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-05500726.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Uricius, son of Capito, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-05500727.translation: Romana, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulius had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05500727.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Romana, aged 20, funded by Iulius.
