EDCS-05500729.translation: Papiria, aged 60, of the 20th Legion, ETVO A L [illegible details], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. And [his] wife Secunda.
EDCS-05500729.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papiria, aged 60, a member of the 20th Legion, and his wife Secunda.

EDCS-05500730.translation: Iulia Maura, daughter of Caius, aged 27, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulius Caecilius Cefalus had this made.
EDCS-05500730.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maura, aged 27, whose monument was commissioned by Iulius Caecilius Cefalus.

EDCS-05500731.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Germana, aged 6. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500731.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Germana, aged 6, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-05500732.translation: Veneria, slave of Severa, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500732.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veneria, a slave of Severa, aged 25.

EDCS-05500733.translation: Julius Sestus made this area for his wife, aged 7 years.
EDCS-05500733.summary: This inscription records the creation of a funerary area by Julius Sestus for his young wife.

EDCS-05500735.translation: Gnaeus Gestus, under the auspices of Idonius, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05500735.summary: This inscription seems to record a dedication made by Gnaeus Gestus, possibly under the supervision or with the approval of someone named Idonius.

EDCS-05500736.translation: Avita, daughter of Avitus, willingly fulfilled her vow to Hercules.
EDCS-05500736.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Avita, daughter of Avitus, fulfilling a vow to the god Hercules.

EDCS-05500737.translation: C. Vitalius willingly placed this for Hercules, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-05500737.summary: This is a votive inscription where C. Vitalius willingly dedicated something to Hercules.

EDCS-05500738.translation: Publius Cam[elius?], son of Co[nillus?], dedicated an altar to Jupiter the Solitude-Solver.
EDCS-05500738.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter Solutor by Publius Cam[elius?].

EDCS-05500739.translation: Rufus, son of Tongius, fulfilled his vow to the Lar Familiaris.
EDCS-05500739.summary: This is a votive inscription where Rufus, son of Tongius, fulfilled a vow to the household gods.

EDCS-05500740.translation: Monianus, son of Pelcinus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-05500740.summary: This is a votive inscription where Monianus, son of Pelcinus, fulfilled a vow to Mercury.

EDCS-05500741.translation: Maurus Caudi willingly and with a willing spirit fulfilled his vow to Sagae.
EDCS-05500741.summary: This is a votive inscription where Maurus Caudi fulfilled a vow to Sagae.

EDCS-05500742.translation: [---] dedicated this to Labarus, as a legate for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05500742.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a legate to a deity or person named Labarus.

EDCS-05500743.translation: Arenierus, son of Aiaetus, aged 70 years, Coria, daughter of Arenierus, aged 1 year, Arcissus, son of Arenierus, aged 20 years, are buried here; may the earth be light upon them. Duatius, freedman of Arenierus, arranged for this to be made at his own expense.
EDCS-05500743.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arenierus, his daughter Coria, and his son Arcissus, with the monument arranged by Arenierus' freedman Duatius.

EDCS-05500745.translation: Cilius is buried here at 25 years of age. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Secunda, daughter of Cilius, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05500745.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cilius, commissioned by his daughter Secunda.

EDCS-05500746.translation: To S. Agrum Saeuticius, son of F. F., and his children Etelori(?), and Tillecum, and their sons.
EDCS-05500746.summary: A dedication, possibly funerary, to S. Agrum Saeuticius and his family.

EDCS-05500747.translation: Amona Alluqi is buried here at 50 years of age. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Scana Arco Cilea (made this).
EDCS-05500747.summary: A funerary epitaph for Amona Alluqi, possibly commissioned by Scana Arco Cilea.

EDCS-05500748.translation: Amabatus wrote this for Carlae Praisom, Secias, Erba, Muite as, Arimo, Praesondo, Singeyeyo, Iniava, then Vea, then Aedagaron, Teucae, Com, then Nurim, Issude, Ecurse, Coampilva, then...
EDCS-05500748.summary: A difficult and fragmented text, possibly a dedication or a record of individuals, with a commissioner named Amabatus.

EDCS-05500749.translation: Loemina, then Enu Petanim, then Arimom Sintamo, then Teucom Sintamo.
EDCS-05500749.summary: A list of names, possibly related to a dedication or a group, with the initial name being Loemina.

EDCS-05500750.translation: Lucius Lupus Tancini's son willingly paid his vow to Bandiae Apuluseaeco.
EDCS-05500750.summary: A votive inscription recording Lucius Lupus Tancini fulfilling a vow to Bandiae Apuluseaeco.

EDCS-05500751.translation: Cilius, son of Caenonis, willingly paid his vow to Apuluseaeco, deservedly.
EDCS-05500751.summary: A votive inscription recording Cilius, son of Caenonis, fulfilling a vow to Apuluseaeco.

EDCS-05500754.translation: Tureus, son of Bouti, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Solutor.
EDCS-05500754.summary: A votive inscription recording Tureus, son of Bouti, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Solutor.

EDCS-05500755.translation: Camalus, son of Tureus, set up an altar to Jupiter Solutor. Tureus, brother of Tureus, willingly paid (his vow).
EDCS-05500755.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter Solutor by Camalus, with a vow paid by Tureus, his brother.

EDCS-05500758.translation: Capito, son of Avitus, 14 years old; Avita, daughter of Avitus, 10 years old; Avitus, son of Capito, 40 years old, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Fusca, daughter of Capito, saw to its construction for her children and her husband from her own means.
EDCS-05500758.summary: A funerary epitaph for Capito, Avita, and Avitus, commissioned by Fusca for her children and husband.

EDCS-05500759.translation: Maelo, son of Tongs, 18 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father Tongius had this made.
EDCS-05500759.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maelo, a boy aged 18, commissioned by his father.

EDCS-05500761.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aebarus, son of Vallocavdus, 24 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His son had this made.
EDCS-05500761.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aebarus, aged 24, commissioned by his son.

EDCS-05500762.translation: Vegetus, freedman of Api, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-05500762.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Vegetus, freedman of Api, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05500764.translation: A good deed. Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Venetiae, daughter of Albinus, 35 years old, and for Laora, daughter of Crispinus Fronto, 26 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500764.summary: A funerary epitaph for Venetiae and Laora, detailing their ages, likely commissioned by their family.

EDCS-05500765.translation: Aleba, daughter of Celtus, willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05500765.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aleba, daughter of Celtus, recording the willing fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05500766.translation: Boutius, son of Antubellus, willingly fulfilled his vow to the goddess Navia.
EDCS-05500766.summary: A dedication to the goddess Navia by Boutius, son of Antubellus, recording the willing fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05500767.translation: Camira, daughter of Tongs, 30 years old, and Tongius, son of Sulla, 50 years old, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cilea [dedicated this].
EDCS-05500767.summary: A funerary epitaph for Camira and Tongius, providing their ages and likely commissioned by Cilea.

EDCS-05500768.summary: A brief funerary inscription simply stating the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-05500769.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-05500769.summary: A formal titulary inscription for Emperor Trajan, listing his numerous honors and titles, likely part of a public monument.

EDCS-05500771.translation: Sacred to the Emperor Nerva Trajanus Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus. Lacer, renowned for his divine skill, who built the bridge, also dedicated new temples; indeed, offerings alone satisfy the gods. He made a bridge to last forever in the world, and with divine skill he established a temple with the gods of Rome. Fortunate is each sacred undertaking. Caius Julius Lacer, by decree of the decurions, made and dedicated this [monument] with his friend Curio Igeditanus.
EDCS-05500771.summary: A dedication by Caius Julius Lacer, likely a wealthy individual or official, celebrating the construction of a bridge and temples, possibly honoring Emperor Trajan, commissioned by the local council (decurions).

EDCS-05500773.translation: Bandu Vordeaeco, surnamed Claranus Caenici, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05500773.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Bandu Vordeaeco, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500777.translation: Iamius Favius Cauriensis, aged 50, and Tritius Casapi, his son...
EDCS-05500777.summary: This is a funerary inscription that begins to record the details of Iamius Favius Cauriensis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500779.translation: Boccus Grati, son of Gratius, had this made for his wife Rufina, freedwoman of Caurus, who was dearly loved.
EDCS-05500779.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufina, commissioned by her husband Boccus Grati, likely from the late 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500787.translation: Celsus Duati, son of Duatius, aged 50, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you, Duatius.
EDCS-05500787.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celsus Duati, also offering a wish for the deceased Duatius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500790.translation: ...us Fusci and Coria Catueni had this made for their son.
EDCS-05500790.summary: This is a funerary inscription, incomplete, recording the parents of the deceased, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500791.translation: Niger Loucini, aged 9, had this made for his son Tusca because of his merits; he lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500791.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young Tusca, commissioned by his father Niger Loucini, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500798.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription recording the name and age of Tureus Taerai, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500804.translation: Salama Leurius, son of Boutius, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05500804.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Salama Leurius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500807.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vibia Felicitas made this for her husband Saturius Basilius, with whom she lived for 11 years, a deserving man.
EDCS-05500807.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Saturius Basilius by his wife Vibia Felicitas, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500809.translation: Caius Alionius Severinus willingly made this for Father Liber, after seeing him in a vision.
EDCS-05500809.summary: This is a votive inscription recording an offering made by Caius Alionius Severinus to Father Liber, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05500815.translation: To the Mother of the Gods, Britta.
EDCS-05500815.summary: This is a dedication to the Mother of the Gods by Britta, likely a personal offering.

EDCS-05500816.translation: To Salus, by the neighborhood of Caperensis.
EDCS-05500816.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Salus (Health), made by the local community of Caperensis.

EDCS-05500817.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering dedicated to the unconquered sun god, Sol Invictus Augustus.

EDCS-05500818.translation: Quin. Chr. Fusc. happily fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05500818.summary: This text records the fulfillment of a vow by Quin. Chr. Fusc., indicating a personal religious observance.

EDCS-05500819.translation: Magilus, son of L. Goutius, and Ara, son of Angeitus, took care of its construction.
EDCS-05500819.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Magilus and Ara, who supervised the construction of a monument or work.

EDCS-05500820.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, and mother of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, the most distinguished council of the Caperenses, devoted to her divine majesty.
EDCS-05500820.summary: This is a dedication by the local council of Caperenses to Julia Augusta, honoring her titles as mother of the camps, wife of Emperor Severus, and mother of Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-05500821.translation: To Caesar, son of C. Emo(n)is.
EDCS-05500821.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar, possibly a deified emperor, by C. Emo(n)is.

EDCS-05500822.translation: To Marcus Sulpicius, son of Andaetus, a Camalus of Caperensis, an equestrian of the II Thracian cavalry, the first troop of Proculus Julius, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-05500822.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication to Marcus Sulpicius, an equestrian officer, made according to his will.

EDCS-05500823.translation: For Avita Moderata, his grandmother, on the occasion of her honor when she was received as a citizen in Caperae. Cocceia Severa, daughter of Celsus, from Norba, placed this with the care and expense of her grandmother Avita Moderata.
EDCS-05500823.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Cocceia Severa for her grandmother Avita Moderata, celebrating her reception as a citizen.

EDCS-05500824.translation: To Trebia Procula, mother. Cocceia Severa, daughter of Celsus, from Norba.
EDCS-05500824.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Cocceia Severa to her mother, Trebia Procula.

EDCS-05500825.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Publius Aufidius, son of Publius, who held all public offices, his wife Martia, daughter of Caius, had this made for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-05500825.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aufidius, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-05500826.translation: His colleagues, the caretakers of the public shrines, had this made for Cessea's freedman, aged 40.
EDCS-05500826.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedman of Cessea, commissioned by his colleagues as caretakers of public shrines.

EDCS-05500827.translation: Albini filius, aged 50, the caretakers of the public shrines had this set up; here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500827.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albini filius, erected by the caretakers of the public shrines.

EDCS-05500828.translation: Gaius Aelius Paternus, son of Segontius, of the Galeria tribe, from Clunia, aged 45, here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sister Aelia Aia, the heir according to the will, had this made.
EDCS-05500828.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Aelius Paternus, commissioned by his sister and heir.

EDCS-05500830.translation: Caelia Aunia, wife of Julius Fuscus Tuberianus, aged 50, from Clunia, here she lies. Her stepson Julius Avitus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05500830.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelia Aunia, commissioned by her stepson.

EDCS-05500831.translation: Gaius Caelius Paternus, of the Galeria tribe, from Clunia, aged 25, here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The neighborhood of Clunians had this made.
EDCS-05500831.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Caelius Paternus, commissioned by his neighbors from Clunia.

EDCS-05500832.translation: Sedatus, son of Agenais Allonius, from Clunia, aged 28, here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500832.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sedatus, son of Agenais Allonius.

EDCS-05500833.translation: Lucius Publicius Thiamus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, from Emerita, aged 27, here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caecilius Vetto made this cippus for his comrade at his own expense.
EDCS-05500833.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Publicius Thiamus, commissioned by his comrade Caecilius Vetto.

EDCS-05500834.translation: Valeria Salvia, daughter of Marcius, from Emerita, aged 50, here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Publicius Severus, her uncle.
EDCS-05500834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Salvia, with her uncle Lucius Publicius Severus mentioned.

EDCS-05500835.translation: Gaius Marcius Clarus, from Hispalis, aged 50, here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcia Procula had this made for her father at her own expense.
EDCS-05500835.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Marcius Clarus, commissioned by his daughter Marcia Procula.

EDCS-05500836.translation: Lovesi, son of Interamicus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Lucius.
EDCS-05500836.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lovesi, stating his age and location of burial, with a wish for his repose.

EDCS-05500837.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Secundus erected this for his uncle Quinto Limico, aged 40, for his merits. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500837.summary: A funerary dedication by Secundus to his uncle Quinto Limico, commemorating his merits and burial.

EDCS-05500838.translation: For Quinto Verrucio Gemelo, freedman of Quinto, of Norba, aged 62, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500838.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quinto Verrucio Gemelo, a freedman, stating his age and location of burial.

EDCS-05500839.translation: Lucius Domitius, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, aged [---], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Domitius Fortunatus erected this for his patron at his own expense.
EDCS-05500839.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Domitius, commissioned by his patron Domitius Fortunatus, with a wish for his repose.

EDCS-05500840.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Datius Parthicus of Antioch erected this for his brother Lucius Aelius Quintus, aged 22, his illustrious and most dutiful brother.
EDCS-05500840.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Aelius Datius Parthicus for his deceased brother Lucius Aelius Quintus, noting his age and virtues.

EDCS-05500841.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Herculanus, freedman and heir according to the will, arranged for this to be made for Lucius Aelius Fortunatus, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-05500841.summary: A funerary dedication arranged by the freedman and heir for Lucius Aelius Fortunatus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-05500842.translation: Marcus Attius Silvanus, father, and Caeria Severa, mother, arranged for this to be made for Attia Paternus.
EDCS-05500842.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Marcus Attius Silvanus and Caeria Severa for their daughter Attia Paternus.

EDCS-05500843.translation: Aunia Angeiti, daughter of [---], aged 30, lies here.
EDCS-05500843.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aunia Angeiti, stating her age and location of burial.

EDCS-05500844.translation: Marcus Fidius Macer arranged for this to be made in his will for Bolosea, daughter of Pelli, and for Fidio Macrus, son of [---].
EDCS-05500844.summary: A funerary dedication arranged in the will of Marcus Fidius Macer for two individuals, Bolosea and Fidio Macrus.

EDCS-05500845.translation: Marcus Fidius Macer ordered this to be erected for his wife Iulia Luperca, daughter of Lupercus.
EDCS-05500845.summary: A funerary dedication commissioned by Marcus Fidius Macer for his wife Iulia Luperca.

EDCS-05500846.translation: To Frontonius Minatus, Lucius Caecilius Firmus, his freedman, aged 20.
EDCS-05500846.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a freedman named Frontonius Minatus by Lucius Caecilius Firmus, likely his patron.

EDCS-05500847.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iunius Ti, aged 50, had this made for his most devoted husband; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500847.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iunius Ti, aged 50, and was set up by his devoted husband.

EDCS-05500848.translation: Maternus, son of Fuscus, aged 3, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Balbinus, son of Balbus, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05500848.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Maternus, aged 3, with the monument erected by Balbinus at his own expense.

EDCS-05500849.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. L.Q.F.L.P. lived 16 years. Iulius Maxentianus made this for his most devoted daughter; she lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500849.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a 16-year-old girl, set up by her father Iulius Maxentianus.

EDCS-05500851.translation: Primus, freedman of Chilo, aged 45, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500851.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primus, a freedman aged 45, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500854.translation: Samacia, freedwoman of Marcus Acilius Frontus, aged 50, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Capito had this made for his mother.
EDCS-05500854.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Samacia, a freedwoman, aged 50, with the monument erected by her son Capito.

EDCS-05500855.translation: Severus, son of Cadarus, aged 12, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. GEI Tuscus, son of Atius, his uncle, gave this at his own expense.
EDCS-05500855.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a boy named Severus, aged 12, with the monument erected by his paternal uncle.

EDCS-05500858.translation: Vitulus TA I AMA C AV CLIV lies here.
EDCS-05500858.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription with a personal name, Vitulus, and illegible text, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500859.translation: Voconius, son of Sempronius, lived [X] years. Sempronius, his father, had this made.
EDCS-05500859.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Voconius, son of Sempronius, with the monument erected by his father.

EDCS-05500860.translation: La, daughter of Fuscus, aged [X], lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. ia Materna had this made.
EDCS-05500860.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a woman named La, daughter of Fuscus, with the monument erected by ia Materna.

EDCS-05500862.translation: Fulvius Rufus, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fulvius Firmus had this made for his father.
EDCS-05500862.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fulvius Rufus, commissioned by his son Fulvius Firmus, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05500863.translation: To Arbura, daughter of Tancinus, aged 70, and Camira, daughter, aged 20. Ambatus Pelli had this made.
EDCS-05500863.summary: A funerary epitaph for Arbura and Camira, commissioned by Ambatus Pelli, dating probably to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500867.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, in his 28th year of tribunician power, 13th consul, father of the fatherland, boundary marker between the Mirobrigenses Valut[a] and the Salmanticenses Val[eria].
EDCS-05500867.summary: An official inscription from Emperor Augustus, dating to his 28th tribunician power (28-27 BC or 3 BC-AD 3), marking a boundary between two tribal territories.

EDCS-05500870.translation: To Jupiter, Silo's son willingly paid his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-05500870.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Silo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500871.translation: Silo, son of Corai, willingly paid his vow to Cantunaeco with a willing spirit.
EDCS-05500871.summary: A votive inscription to Cantunaeco, dedicated by Silo, son of Corai, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500875.translation: Aper, son of Maurus, aged 35, son of Magilo, had this made according to the will.
EDCS-05500875.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aper, son of Maurus, commissioned by Magilo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500876.translation: Here lies Flavina, an incomparable daughter, aged 23. Eligius Flavius, son of Flavini and Flaccilla, and Allia Avita, daughter of Sereni and Varila, parents. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500876.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavina, aged 23, with her parents Eligius Flavius and Allia Avita also mentioned, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500877.translation: To the departed spirits. Tabola, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly on you. To the departed spirits, sacred. Rusus Scancini, aged 65, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500877.summary: A funerary inscription for Tabola (aged 35) and Rusus Scancini (aged 65), dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500878.translation: To Gaius Valerius Reburinus. Avius lies here. Aviae Junius Tancinus, nine feet.
EDCS-05500878.summary: A funerary epitaph for Avius and Aviae Iunius Tancinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500879.translation: To the departed spirits. Valens, son of Flamini, aged 40, lies here, may the earth lie lightly on you. To the departed spirits. Accus, son of Acci, aged 50, lies here, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500879.summary: A funerary inscription for Valens (aged 40) and Accus (aged 50), dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500883.translation: Cloutius, in place of his brother Titus Milonius, had this made. He was 30 years old.
EDCS-05500883.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Cloutius commissioned a monument for his deceased brother, Titus Milonius, who was 30 years old.

EDCS-05500887.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Thetis had this made for her 21-year-old son, Gaius Julius Narcissus.
EDCS-05500887.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a mother, Iulia Thetis, commissioned a monument for her son, Gaius Julius Narcissus, who died at age 21.

EDCS-05500889.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of a person named Sabinus.

EDCS-05500890.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cassius Vegetus had this made for his devoted wife, Amma Festi filia, of Albocola, aged 23, and Celti Flaviensis.
EDCS-05500890.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Cassius Vegetus for his wife, Amma Festi filia, who died at age 23, and also likely for Celti Flaviensis, possibly another family member.

EDCS-05500891.translation: Bolosea, daughter of Brevus, lived 70 years and is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Reburrus, son of Taporius, for his mother.
EDCS-05500891.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bolosea, daughter of Brevus, who lived 70 years, with a dedication from her son Reburrus, a centurion.

EDCS-05500892.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Father Flavus and Mother Valentina had this made for their 20-year-old son, Valentinus.
EDCS-05500892.summary: This is a funerary inscription where parents, Flavus and Valentina, commissioned a monument for their son, Valentinus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-05500893.translation: Trebia Severa willingly fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs of Capera.
EDCS-05500893.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Trebia Severa fulfilling a vow to the local Nymphs.

EDCS-05500896.translation: Anua, daughter of Reburrus, willingly fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-05500896.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Anua, daughter of Reburrus, fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs.

EDCS-05500898.translation: Angeitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-05500898.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Angeitus fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs.

EDCS-05500901.translation: Amanius, of the Nymphs of Capera.
EDCS-05500901.summary: This is a votive inscription indicating Amanius' dedication or connection to the Nymphs of Capera.

EDCS-05500904.translation: Alla, freedwoman of Nereus, gladly paid her vow to the Nymphs, as deserved.
EDCS-05500904.summary: An inscription recording a votive offering by Alla to the Nymphs.

EDCS-05500905.translation: Domitia Proculina, daughter of Lucius, priestess of the province of Lusitania and the first and perpetual priestess of her own municipality.
EDCS-05500905.summary: An inscription detailing the prestigious priesthoods held by Domitia Proculina.

EDCS-05500912.translation: To Andreine, daughter of Sa. Lucretia.
EDCS-05500912.summary: A dedication to Andreine by Lucretia.

EDCS-05500913.translation: Aunia, daughter of Tanuginus, aged 45, is buried here.
EDCS-05500913.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aunia, who died at age 45.

EDCS-05500914.translation: Aquilus Albinus Mamaturus, son, for his father Aestivus Niger and his mother Annia Mustari.
EDCS-05500914.summary: A dedication by Aquilus Albinus Mamaturus to his parents.

EDCS-05500916.translation: To Pellieius, son of Dangetus, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly on you. His son Rufus had this made.
EDCS-05500916.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pellieius, aged 60, with a wish for a light burial, commissioned by his son Rufus.

EDCS-05500919.translation: To the divine shades of Fuscilla.
EDCS-05500919.summary: A dedication to the spirits of the deceased Fuscilla.

EDCS-05500920.translation: To the divine shades of Iulia, daughter of Marcus Sempronius.
EDCS-05500920.summary: A dedication to the spirits of the deceased Iulia, daughter of Marcus Sempronius.

EDCS-05500921.translation: To the divine shades of Lucius Valerius Primeus, son of Valerius Capito, aged 18. His mother had this made.
EDCS-05500921.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Primeus, aged 18, commissioned by his mother.

EDCS-05500922.translation: Marcus Laberius, son, had this made in accordance with his father's will, for Marcus Laberius, son of Malgenus.
EDCS-05500922.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a testamentary instruction to commission a monument for Marcus Laberius, son of Malgenus.

EDCS-05500923.translation: Licinia, mother, ordered by will that this be made for Gaius Licinius Fuscinus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-05500923.summary: This funerary epitaph records a mother's commission for a monument for her son, Gaius Licinius Fuscinus.

EDCS-05500925.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maura and Flavinus and Lucanus took care to have this made at their own expense. He is buried here.
EDCS-05500925.summary: This funerary inscription records that Maura, Flavinus, and Lucanus commissioned this burial monument for someone named Lucullus.

EDCS-05500926.summary: This inscription is a simple naming monument, likely a tombstone, for Gaius Magius Tongius.

EDCS-05500929.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Gnaeus Pompeius and others.

EDCS-05500932.translation: To father Lucius Tapilius Vegetus, aged 80, and to mother Magia, aged 45. May the earth be light on you both. Lucius Tapilius Vegetianus took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500932.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Tapilius Vegetus and his wife Magia, commissioned by their son Lucius Tapilius Vegetianus.

EDCS-05500933.translation: To Gaius Valerius Severus, to Titus Valerius Pacatus, to Liguria, to Abia, for my husband and son and for myself, the three Pacati took care to have this made.
EDCS-05500933.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument commissioned by three individuals (likely women named Pacata) for themselves and two men, Gaius Valerius Severus and Titus Valerius Pacatus.

EDCS-05500934.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Iuliana took care to have this made for her mother Antonia Urbica, aged ---.
EDCS-05500934.summary: This funerary inscription records that Valeria Iuliana commissioned a monument for her mother, Antonia Urbica.

EDCS-05500937.translation: Sacred to Victoria. Lucius Marci paid his vow.
EDCS-05500937.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Lucius Marci fulfilling a vow made to the goddess Victoria.

EDCS-05500940.translation: Helena Alaesii is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Modeii A.
EDCS-05500940.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Helena Alaesii, with a partial name possibly related to the commissioner or a second individual.

EDCS-05500941.translation: Fundanus Severi, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05500941.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Fundanus Severi.

EDCS-05500942.translation: Here lies Pio, a most devoted mother; [her son] had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500942.summary: This is a tombstone for a mother named Pio, with a prayer for the deceased and a note about who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05500946.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rufinus, aged 15, a most dutiful son, had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500946.summary: This is a tombstone for Rufinus, a 15-year-old son, commissioned by his parents, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500947.summary: This inscription records the name and age of a child, Sempronius Crispinus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-05500948.translation: Valeria Tagana, daughter of Duelon, wife of Lucius Vibius Quirinus Reburrus, ordered this to be set up in her will.
EDCS-05500948.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Valeria Tagana left instructions in her will for this monument to be erected.

EDCS-05500951.translation: Flavia Rufina of Augustobrigensis, aged 24, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Parents Honorina and Caius Rustenus Decianus made this for their daughter.
EDCS-05500951.summary: This is a tombstone for Flavia Rufina, a 24-year-old woman, commissioned by her parents, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500952.translation: For Caesius Tancinus, aged 70. Freedmen Agilius and Tancinus had this made for their most indulgent patron, in accordance with his will. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500952.summary: This is a tombstone for Caesius Tancinus, a patron, commissioned by his freedmen according to his will, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500953.translation: IIC Ul ecci Luculli, aged 52. Modestus, freedman of Cec i Luculli, had this made at his own expense. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500953.summary: This is a tombstone for a person identified by a partial name, commissioned by their freedman, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500959.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilius Euphrosynus, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500959.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilius Euphrosynus, a 55-year-old man, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500960.translation: Marcia S M RIC EF, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sons Albinus and Sextus Flavius Flaccus had this made for their mother. Albinus Taporus of ISANGLES had this made for her merits.
EDCS-05500960.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcia, a woman aged 60, commissioned by her sons and also by Albinus Taporus for her merits, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500978.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 53, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500978.summary: This is a tombstone for Ulpia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 53, with a prayer for the deceased.

EDCS-05500979.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Galla, daughter of Marcus, aged 12, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500979.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aurelia Galla, indicating her age and location of burial.

EDCS-05500991.translation: To Isis, our Mistress, in accordance with the will of Scandilia Campana, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-05500991.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Isis, made from the estate of Scandilia Campana.

EDCS-05501005.translation: Caecilius Cacalo, aged 60, is placed here.
EDCS-05501005.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Caecilius Cacalo, stating his age and that he is buried here.

EDCS-05501006.translation: Lucius Calpurnius, son of Publius, aged 80, is placed here.
EDCS-05501006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Calpurnius, indicating his age and burial.

EDCS-05501008.translation: Didia Severina, daughter of Lucius, had this made in accordance with her will; her husband Pompeius Priscus and her heir Didius Severinus took care of its construction.
EDCS-05501008.summary: This funerary inscription records that the monument for Didia Severina was commissioned by her husband and heir, Pompeius Priscus and Didius Severinus, according to her will.

EDCS-05501012.translation: Gaius Julius Pedo, son of Gaius, aged 60, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Pedo, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-05501014.translation: Publius Julius Suriacus, aged 60, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publius Julius Niger, aged 25, is placed here.
EDCS-05501014.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Publius Julius Suriacus and his son Publius Julius Niger, with their respective ages.

EDCS-05501016.summary: This is a brief inscription likely commemorating Lucius Norbanus Rufus, possibly a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-05501017.translation: Lucius Papius Rufus, aged 30, may the earth lie lightly upon you. His father took care of its construction.
EDCS-05501017.summary: This funerary epitaph for Lucius Papius Rufus states his age and that his father oversaw the construction of the monument.

EDCS-05501021.summary: This is a short inscription possibly indicating Saturnia's ownership or a dedication made by her during her lifetime.

EDCS-05501026.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufilius, most dutiful, to his most dutiful wife, Marcellino, aged 62, Norbana Martiniana. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Norbanus Severianus, aged 75, Norbanus Severus. Gaius Norbanus Severianus had this made at his own expense. Gaius Norbanus Severianus, aged 75, Norbanus Severus. Gaius Norbanus Severianus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05501026.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aufilius and Marcellino, with details about their ages and who paid for the monument.

EDCS-05501029.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Silus Cosmos, aged 50, and Gaius Silus Flavius, aged 19, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Fabia Trophimae had this made for her husband and son at her own expense.
EDCS-05501029.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Silus Cosmos and Gaius Silus Flavius, with their ages and their mother's contribution.

EDCS-05501030.translation: To Elia Hygeia, from Taporus, to his most excellent, sweetest, most loving mother.
EDCS-05501030.summary: This dedication is from Taporus to his mother, Elia Hygeia, expressing affection.

EDCS-05501031.translation: Lucius Julius Crescens, son of Titus, aged 19, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother had this erected for her son.
EDCS-05501031.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Julius Crescens, aged 19, and notes that his mother paid for the monument.

EDCS-05501032.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [---] of Julius Vocem. Iulius Allus, son of [---]us, had this made for his most reverent father.
EDCS-05501032.summary: This funerary epitaph records a son's dedication to his deceased father, with some text missing.

EDCS-05501390.translation: Manius Metilius Asiaticus, son of Ursilius, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-05501390.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Manius Metilius Asiaticus, son of Ursilius, with his age.

EDCS-05501760.translation: Appius, son of Claudius, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05501760.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Appius, son of Claudius.

EDCS-05501761.summary: This inscription records that Caius Valerius Catto erected the monument.

EDCS-05501824.translation: Anicius Arquus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501824.summary: This inscription records Anicius Arquus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05502478.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for Iulia Felicitas.

EDCS-05502584.translation: Titus Manius, son of Titus, fulfilled his vow to Diana.
EDCS-05502584.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Diana by Titus Manius.

EDCS-05600003.translation: To the Emperor Augustus, son of Mars and Iovius.
EDCS-05600003.summary: An inscription dedicating an honor to Emperor Augustus, possibly on a military monument.

EDCS-05600004.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar, father of the fatherland, 23 miles.
EDCS-05600004.summary: An inscription marking a distance of 23 miles dedicated to Emperor Constantius I.

EDCS-05600006.translation: To Severus, conqueror of Adiabene, restorer of the city.
EDCS-05600006.summary: An epithet or title for an emperor, likely Severus Alexander, celebrating his victories and city restoration.

EDCS-05600007.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power twice, consul, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-05600007.summary: A full imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Tacitus, dating to his second consulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-05600008.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Claudius Tacitus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power twice, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-05600008.summary: A slightly abbreviated imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Tacitus, likely from his second consulship.

EDCS-05600009.translation: He repaired [this], consul four times, proconsul.
EDCS-05600009.summary: An inscription recording the repair of a structure by an individual who held the office of consul four times and proconsul.

EDCS-05600010.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul three times.
EDCS-05600010.summary: An imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Tacitus, mentioning his third proconsulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-05600013.translation: To the Emperor Marcus Numerianus Augustus, pious, fortunate, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, 20.
EDCS-05600013.summary: An imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Numerian, possibly marking a distance of 20 miles or a regnal year.

EDCS-05600014.translation: To Caesar Constantinus Augustus, pious, most brave Caesar, son of the deified Constantius, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-05600014.summary: An inscription honoring Caesar Constantine, son of Constantius I, highlighting his virtues and lineage.

EDCS-05600015.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 10th time, consul for the 4th time, imperator for the 21st time, restored it for the 6th time.
EDCS-05600015.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by the emperor Claudius, likely a public building or monument, indicated by his multiple honorific titles and powers.

EDCS-05600016.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 6th time, father of the fatherland, designated consul for the 4th time.
EDCS-05600016.summary: This inscription lists the titles and powers of Emperor Claudius, likely as part of an official dedication or record of his status.

EDCS-05600021.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Severus Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul for the 52nd time.
EDCS-05600021.summary: This inscription records the official titles and powers of Emperor Caracalla, likely part of a dedication or milestone commemoration.

EDCS-05600022.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 27th time.
EDCS-05600022.summary: This inscription lists the key titles and powers of Emperor Tiberius, likely part of an official inscription related to his reign.

EDCS-05600030.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored it for the 103rd time.
EDCS-05600030.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by Emperor Hadrian, highlighting his imperial titles and the significant number of times he undertook such works.

EDCS-05600031.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, made it for the 103rd time.
EDCS-05600031.summary: This inscription records a dedication or construction by Emperor Severus Alexander, emphasizing his imperial titles and the repeated nature of his public works.

EDCS-05600033.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored it for the 112th time.
EDCS-05600033.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by Emperor Hadrian, noting his imperial titles and the considerable number of restorations he completed.

EDCS-05600034.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored it.
EDCS-05600034.summary: This inscription documents a restoration project undertaken by Emperor Hadrian, listing his significant imperial titles.

EDCS-05600036.translation: Emperor for the 4th time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-05600036.summary: This inscription is a very brief record of imperial titles, likely part of a larger inscription that is now lost.

EDCS-05600043.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul.
EDCS-05600043.summary: This inscription records the titles of Emperor Trajan, likely as part of a public monument or dedication.

EDCS-05600044.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the second time, restored [this].
EDCS-05600044.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure, likely in Rome, and dates to his reign.

EDCS-05600047.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, father of the soldiers, holding tribunician power for the 20th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, restored [this] in the 136th year.
EDCS-05600047.summary: This inscription details Emperor Caracalla's extensive lineage and titles, recording his restoration of a structure and dating to his 136th year (likely AD 211-212).

EDCS-05600048.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the first time, restored [this] in the 139th year.
EDCS-05600048.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure, likely in Rome, and dates to his 139th year (AD 115-116).

EDCS-05600049.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, made [this] in the 140th year.
EDCS-05600049.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's construction of a monument, likely in Rome, and dates to his 140th year (AD 116-117).

EDCS-05600050.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored [this] in the 142nd year.
EDCS-05600050.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure, likely in Rome, and dates to his 142nd year (AD 118-119).

EDCS-05600051.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored [this] in the 143rd year.
EDCS-05600051.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure, likely in Rome, and dates to his 143rd year (AD 119-120).

EDCS-05600054.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, emperor for the 4th time, father of the country, in the 168th year.
EDCS-05600054.summary: This inscription records the titles and regnal year of Emperor Nero, likely commemorating a public work or event.

EDCS-05600055.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored [this] in the 176th year.
EDCS-05600055.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure, likely in Rome, and dates to his 176th year (AD 132-133).

EDCS-05600056.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored [this], two thousand paces.
EDCS-05600056.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a road, indicated by 'milia passuum II' (two thousand paces), likely in Rome.

EDCS-05600154.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 7th time, father of the country, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, had the roads and bridges that had collapsed from age restored, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, a most distinguished man, legate of the emperors pro praetore.
EDCS-05600154.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son, Caesar Verus, under the legation of Quintus Decius, likely during Maximinus' reign (AD 235-238).

EDCS-05600157.translation: To our Lord Magnentius, Pious, Invincible, Ever-August, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-05600157.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Magnentius, likely on a public monument, dating to his reign (350-353 AD).

EDCS-05600159.translation: To Marcus Numerius Numerianus, most noble Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-05600159.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Marcus Numerius Numerianus, a Caesar (junior emperor), likely dating to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600326.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the first time.
EDCS-05600326.summary: This inscription records the acclamation of Marcus Claudius Tacitus as emperor, likely on a public monument, dating to his brief reign in 275-276 AD.

EDCS-05600327.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the eighth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul, [measuring] miles.
EDCS-05600327.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Maximian, likely marking a road with mileage, dating to his eighth period of tribunician power (296-297 AD).

EDCS-05600341.translation: To Julia Marcia Gemina, daughter of Tiberius, his best friend; Lucius Quintius Priscion, together with Callaea, daughter of Titus Severinus, and Quintia Avita, his daughter, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05600341.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Marcia Gemina by her friends and a father and daughter, commissioned by local officials, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600342.translation: Araco Aranto Niceo: Iulia Maxuma Auvi willingly fulfilled her vow with her soul.
EDCS-05600342.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulia Maxuma Auvi, likely dedicated to a deity, possibly in the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600343.translation: To Sabina Augusta, daughter of Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, granddaughter of divine Nerva, daughter of divine Trajan Dacicus Parthicus, by decree of the decurions; Felicitas Iulia Olisipo, through Marcus Gellius Rutilianus and Lucius Iulius Avitus, the two men.
EDCS-05600343.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Empress Sabina by the city of Olisipo, likely erected by local magistrates, dating to her lifetime (c. 130-137 AD).

EDCS-05600344.translation: To Matidia Augusta; Felicitas Iulia Olisipo, through Quintus Antonius Gallus and Titus Marcius Marcianus, the two men.
EDCS-05600344.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Empress Matidia, likely erected by local magistrates in Olisipo, dating to her lifetime (c. 119-121 AD).

EDCS-05600345.translation: To Julia Casiana, daughter of Decimus, a most distinguished lady of the Castrensis legion; Castrensis Florica Sabina and Iulia Casiana [erected this] to their most devoted mother, their most obedient daughters.
EDCS-05600345.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by two daughters for their mother, Julia Casiana, a woman associated with the Castrensis legion, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600346.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Maxsumus, of the Galeria tribe, aged twenty years.
EDCS-05600346.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Maxsumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600347.translation: Antistia Maela, mother of Quintus Caecilius Rufus, of the Galeria tribe, 27 years of age, had this made.
EDCS-05600347.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a mother for her son, Quintus Caecilius Rufus, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-05600348.translation: Quintus Caesius Fundanus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, 25 years of age, lies here.
EDCS-05600348.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Caesius Fundanus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05600349.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Annia Corinthia, to her devoted grandson, Marcus Cassius Marcianus, 22 years of age.
EDCS-05600349.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Annia Corinthia to her grandson, Marcus Cassius Marcianus, who died at 22.

EDCS-05600350.translation: Clodia Montana, daughter of Titus, lies here.
EDCS-05600350.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Clodia Montana.

EDCS-05600352.translation: Lucius Cornelius Avitus, 17 years of age, lies here, [erected by his] mother.
EDCS-05600352.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a mother for her son, Lucius Cornelius Avitus, who died at 17.

EDCS-05600353.translation: Iulia Ammaia, daughter of Publius, lies here.
EDCS-05600353.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Iulia Ammaia.

EDCS-05600354.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, 20 years of age.
EDCS-05600354.summary: This funerary inscription is for Lucius Iulius, son of Lucius, who died at 20.

EDCS-05600355.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Maria Ursa.

EDCS-05600356.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Rutilia, mother, had this made at her own expense for Lucius Rutilius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, 30 years of age.
EDCS-05600356.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Rutilia for her son, Lucius Rutilius Severus, who died at 30.

EDCS-05600358.translation: Silvius, son of Magister, of the Dar... tribe, a freedman of Magister, 54 years of age.
EDCS-05600358.summary: This inscription records the death of Silvius, a freedman, at the age of 54, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-05600359.translation: To Julia Severa, the most excellent daughter, and to her pious stepmother, Gaius Fabius Tuscus, son of Gaius, [set this up].
EDCS-05600359.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Fabius Tuscus to his daughter Julia Severa and her stepmother.

EDCS-05600360.translation: Here lies Antonia Amoena; Arethusa, her daughter, gave and dedicated [this monument].
EDCS-05600360.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a daughter for her mother, Antonia Amoena.

EDCS-05600361.translation: Iulia Avita, wife [of the deceased], gave [this] to Marcus Atilius Numeri, son [of Numerius].
EDCS-05600361.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, Marcus Atilius Numeri.

EDCS-05600362.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Lucius Bovius Iulianus, stating his name and age.

EDCS-05600363.translation: Here lies Cassia Amoena, daughter of Marcius.
EDCS-05600363.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cassia Amoena, daughter of Marcius.

EDCS-05600364.translation: Here lies Lucius Cassius Acutius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe. Lucius Cassius Felix, freedman of Lucius, saw to its construction according to the will.
EDCS-05600364.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Cassius Acutius, with the work commissioned by his freedman, Lucius Cassius Felix, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-05600365.translation: The female relative of Galcior, aged 18. Marcella, daughter of Marcius, aged 15.
EDCS-05600365.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages of two young individuals, one identified as related to Galcior and the other as Marcella.

EDCS-05600366.translation: The abbot Eugenius lives. Chr Sest.
EDCS-05600366.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a marker, mentioning an abbot named Eugenius.

EDCS-05600367.summary: A simple epitaph recording the name Faustus and his father, Quintus.

EDCS-05600368.translation: Here lies Iulia Amoena, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-05600368.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Amoena, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-05600369.translation: Iulia Secunda, daughter of Caius, rests here.
EDCS-05600369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Secunda, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-05600370.translation: Caius Julius Firmus, son of Caius, rests here, and Iulia Dubra, daughter of Caius, rests here.
EDCS-05600370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Julius Firmus and Iulia Dubra, both children of Caius.

EDCS-05600371.translation: To the departed spirits of Laberia Procula, aged 61. Iulia Procula, daughter (erected this).
EDCS-05600371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laberia Procula, erected by her daughter Iulia Procula.

EDCS-05600372.translation: To the departed spirits of Lucius Licinius Rufi, son of Lucius, aged 30. His mother made this.
EDCS-05600372.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Licinius Rufi, erected by his mother.

EDCS-05600373.translation: To the departed spirits of Marcus Materni, son of Marcus, aged 25. Caesia Avita, mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05600373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Materni, erected by his mother Caesia Avita.

EDCS-05600374.translation: Marcus Terentius Priscus, son of Publius.
EDCS-05600374.summary: This inscription likely records the name and parentage of Marcus Terentius Priscus, son of Publius.

EDCS-05600375.translation: To [---]ianus, son of [---], from [---]. His brother (made this).
EDCS-05600375.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely recording the name and familial relationship of the deceased.

EDCS-05600376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pirinus Midinus.

EDCS-05600377.translation: Sacred to Mars. Allia Amoena made this in honor of her son, Titus Aemilius Martianus.
EDCS-05600377.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars, made by Allia Amoena in honor of her son.

EDCS-05600378.translation: To the departed spirits of Ve[---]e[---].
EDCS-05600378.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for a person whose name begins with 'Ve' and ends with 'e'.

EDCS-05600381.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Lulius Latnastus.

EDCS-05600382.translation: Titus Iulius Gracilis, aged [---]; Gaius Iulius Successor, aged 25.
EDCS-05600382.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Titus Iulius Gracilis and Gaius Iulius Successor.

EDCS-05600420.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Afra, aged 35, is buried here.
EDCS-05600420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Afra, stating her age and burial.

EDCS-05600421.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Gaius Cassius Fundanus, a veteran, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05600421.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by the veteran Gaius Cassius Fundanus.

EDCS-05600422.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Iulius Terentianus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 32, and Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Sextus, aged 20, and Terentia, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05600422.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Caius Iulius Terentianus, Iulia Maxuma, and Terentia.

EDCS-05600452.summary: This is a dedication to an unspecified deity by Paccius Fronto.

EDCS-05600453.translation: Sacred to Holy Diana. Fonteius Philomusus gladly placed this altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05600453.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana by Fonteius Philomusus.

EDCS-05600455.translation: To Silvanus, Holy, Domestic. Peculiaris placed this vow and altar.
EDCS-05600455.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus by Peculiaris.

EDCS-05600456.translation: M. Lucanus gave this gift from his own money.
EDCS-05600456.summary: This inscription records a gift given by M. Lucanus using his own funds.

EDCS-05600457.translation: To Aurelius Ursinus, a most distinguished man, governor of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-05600457.summary: This is a dedication or inscription honoring Aurelius Ursinus, governor of Lusitania.

EDCS-05600461.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Attius Nisus lived 52 years, 1 month, and 5 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. This mausoleum is under the 'ascia'.
EDCS-05600461.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Attius Nisus, recording his lifespan and burial location, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600462.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Avintina lived 35 years. Patroclus placed this for his deserving [wife/loved one]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600462.summary: A funerary inscription for Avintina, placed by Patroclus, recording her lifespan and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600463.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calemera lived 29 years, 9 months, and 10 days. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600463.summary: A funerary inscription for Calemera, recording her lifespan and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600464.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Diodora lived 25 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600464.summary: A funerary inscription for Diodora, recording her lifespan and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600466.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Hiriniana lived 52 years, 2 months, and 4 days. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600466.summary: A funerary inscription for Hiriniana, recording her lifespan, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600467.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Troilus lived 30 years and 4 months. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600467.summary: A funerary inscription for Troilus, recording his lifespan and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600468.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Lupercilla, 40 years of age. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600468.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Lupercilla, recording her age and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600469.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Montanus lived 33 years, 2 months, and 7 days. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600469.summary: A funerary inscription for Montanus, recording his lifespan, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600470.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus N Eros lived more or less 55 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600470.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextus N Eros, recording his approximate lifespan and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600471.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Numisius Phaius, 60 years of age. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600471.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextus Numisius Phaius, recording his age and burial, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600472.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Daphine, aged 7, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600472.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of a young girl named Daphine.

EDCS-05600476.translation: Uldius lived for 3 years, 6 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-05600476.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of a person named Uldius.

EDCS-05600478.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Speratus, a financial administrator from Balsium, placed this willingly.
EDCS-05600478.summary: A dedication to the emperor Augustus by a financial administrator.

EDCS-05600479.translation: Gaius Licinius Badius donated a podium of the circus, one hundred feet long, at his own expense.
EDCS-05600479.summary: A dedication recording a donation of circus infrastructure by Gaius Licinius Badius.

EDCS-05600480.translation: Lucius Cassius Celer donated a podium of the circus, one hundred feet long, at his own expense.
EDCS-05600480.summary: A dedication recording a donation of circus infrastructure by Lucius Cassius Celer.

EDCS-05600482.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In honor of Aemilia Chari, who lived 27 years, 6 months, and 15 days. Caius Flavius Relatus placed this here for his wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600482.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Aemilia Chari, set up by her husband Caius Flavius Relatus.

EDCS-05600483.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Albia Nereis, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600483.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Albia Nereis.

EDCS-05600484.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Domitius Festus, aged...
EDCS-05600484.summary: A tombstone inscription for Domitius Festus, with the age incomplete.

EDCS-05600485.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Flavius Seranus, aged 12.
EDCS-05600485.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus Flavius Seranus, who died at age 12.

EDCS-05600486.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ploce, daughter of Servi, Catula, aged 40, placed this for her mother. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600486.summary: A tombstone inscription for Catula, erected by her son on behalf of her mother.

EDCS-05600487.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Granius.
EDCS-05600487.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits by Marcus Granius, likely commemorating himself or a deceased relative.

EDCS-05600488.translation: Quintia Palustri, aged 20, is buried here. Vibius Proclus and his wife Tuscilla erected this statue at their own expense.
EDCS-05600488.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintia Palustri, aged 20, and states that Vibius Proclus and his wife Tuscilla erected her statue.

EDCS-05600489.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Annius, son of Publius, and his brother, aged 85, are buried here.
EDCS-05600489.summary: This funerary dedication to the departed spirits records the burial of Lucius Annius and his brother, who were both 85 years old.

EDCS-05600491.summary: This is a name inscription, likely a tombstone or a dedication by Marcus Bruttius Marcellus.

EDCS-05600496.translation: Iunia Corinthia, aged 17, is buried here. May the earth be light on you, my daughter. Satulla [erected this].
EDCS-05600496.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iunia Corinthia, aged 17, and is a dedication from her mother, Satulla.

EDCS-05600497.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Bocchus, son of Caius, flamen of the province, tribune of the soldiers of the III Augusta legion.
EDCS-05600497.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph honoring Lucius Cornelius Bocchus, detailing his civic and military offices.

EDCS-05600498.translation: Iulia Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, of Ebora, aged 42, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Quintus Petronius Maternus erected this for his mother.
EDCS-05600498.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Quintilla, aged 42, and states that Quintus Petronius Maternus erected it for his mother.

EDCS-05600499.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caesius Caesianus, aged 60. Caesia Vernacla, his freedwoman, took care to have this erected. He is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600499.summary: This funerary dedication to the departed spirits records the burial of Lucius Caesius Caesianus, aged 60, and notes that his freedwoman Caesia Vernacla arranged for its erection.

EDCS-05600500.translation: Titus Calleus Marcianus, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Cassia Marcella had this made for her nephew, a rascal, and also for Nemesiacus and his friends, from stone, 2 sesterces.
EDCS-05600500.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Calleus Marcianus, aged 20, and details that Cassia Marcella had it made for her nephew and friends, including a monetary contribution.

EDCS-05600501.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clarino A BIMSS
EDCS-05600501.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Clarino, the rest of the text being illegible.

EDCS-05600502.translation: Lucius C. Gallio is buried here, aged 50. May the earth be light on you. His sister C. Vitalis and his nephew Marcus Fulvius Caecilianus saw to its construction.
EDCS-05600502.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius C. Gallio, with his sister and nephew acting as dedicators.

EDCS-05600503.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Fabius Helicon is buried here, aged 95. May the earth be light on you. Pompeia Pialeh saw to its construction for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-05600503.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Fabius Helicon, with his wife Pompeia Pialeh as the dedicant.

EDCS-05600505.translation: Iulia Maela, daughter of Lucius, is buried here, aged 55. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600505.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulia Maela, daughter of Lucius, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-05600506.translation: Manilia Tusca, daughter of Caius, is buried here. Her mother, Terentia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05600506.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Manilia Tusca, with her mother Terentia Tertulla as the dedicant.

EDCS-05600507.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Atilius Chresimus saw to its construction for his well-deserving Silvanus Atiliae Priscillae, who lived 40 years. He is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-05600507.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Silvanus Atiliae Priscillae, with Atilius Chresimus as the dedicant and honoring his well-deserving status.

EDCS-05600509.translation: A household slave dedicated it willingly.
EDCS-05600509.summary: This very brief inscription records a dedication made willingly by a household slave.

EDCS-05600510.translation: Antubellicus Priscus arranged for the altar to Endovellicus to be set up here, at the god's command, willingly.
EDCS-05600510.summary: This inscription records Antubellicus Priscus setting up an altar for the god Endovellicus by the god's command.

EDCS-05600511.translation: Vesidius willingly fulfilled a vow to Endovellicus on behalf of his son Gaius Vesidius Fuscus.
EDCS-05600511.summary: This inscription records Vesidius fulfilling a vow to the god Endovellicus on behalf of his son.

EDCS-05600512.translation: To the god Endovellicus. In honor of his daughter Iulia Marcella.
EDCS-05600512.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Endovellicus, made in honor of the dedicator's daughter, Iulia Marcella.

EDCS-05600514.translation: Sacred to the god Endovelecus. Titus Annius Aper willingly dedicated a vow.
EDCS-05600514.summary: This inscription records Titus Annius Aper willingly dedicating a vow to the god Endovelecus.

EDCS-05600515.translation: Marcus L. Nigellius dedicates this sacred offering to the god Endovellicus for the health of his daughter, L. Marciana, and freely paid his vow.
EDCS-05600515.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Endovellicus by Marcus L. Nigellius for his daughter's health.

EDCS-05600516.translation: Marcus Vibius Bassus and Marcus Vibius Avitus made this sacred offering to Endovellicus.
EDCS-05600516.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Endovellicus by Marcus Vibius Bassus and Marcus Vibius Avitus.

EDCS-05600519.translation: Corinthus, assistant to Servius Acilius Glabrio, chief clerk, freely paid his vow of two pounds of gold.
EDCS-05600519.summary: A dedication recording a payment of gold made by Corinthus, an assistant clerk, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600520.translation: Lucius Aurelius Flavus, son of Lucius, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father, Publius Aurelius Niger, aged 11, arranged for his burial from his own funds. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600520.summary: A double tomb inscription for Lucius Aurelius Flavus and his son Publius Aurelius Niger, who died at a young age, with the father arranging the burial of his son.

EDCS-05600521.translation: Gaius Julius Gallus, a veteran of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, aged 70 after completing his service, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Julia Prima, his freedwoman and wife, arranged for his burial at her own expense for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-05600521.summary: A tomb inscription for Gaius Julius Gallus, a veteran of the VII Gemina Felix Legion, erected by his freedwoman and wife, Julia Prima.

EDCS-05600522.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Clodius Julianus, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Titus Clodius Modestus and Blesidiena Marcella, father and mother, arranged for their most dutiful son to be buried.
EDCS-05600522.summary: A tomb inscription for Marcus Clodius Julianus, aged 22, erected by his parents, Titus Clodius Modestus and Blesidiena Marcella.

EDCS-05600523.translation: Cominia Avita, daughter of Marcus, aged 9, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you as you pass by. Marcus Cominius Clemens and Vibia Avita, daughter of Marcus, arranged for their daughter to be buried.
EDCS-05600523.summary: A tomb inscription for Cominia Avita, aged 9, erected by her parents, Marcus Cominius Clemens and Vibia Avita.

EDCS-05600525.translation: Cornelius Gallus, son of Papiria, aged 75, arranged for this burial. I [arranged] this burial.
EDCS-05600525.summary: A tomb inscription for Cornelius Gallus, aged 75, with the phrase 'ego Q VIII' possibly indicating a co-dedicant or a specific arrangement of burial.

EDCS-05600526.translation: Aleba, daughter of Arconis, arranged for this burial for Lucius Iulius Regulus.
EDCS-05600526.summary: A tomb inscription indicating that Aleba, daughter of Arconis, arranged the burial of Lucius Iulius Regulus.

EDCS-05600527.summary: A simple tomb inscription for Pompeius Florus.

EDCS-05600528.translation: Lucretia Severa, daughter of Lucius, is laid here.
EDCS-05600528.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucretia Severa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600529.translation: Lucius Terentius Furnus, her husband, and his son Lucius Terentius Rufus, saw to the making of this for Pompeia Epagathe.
EDCS-05600529.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a husband and son for Pompeia Epagathe, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600534.translation: Claudia saw to the making of this for Sextus Crescentius.
EDCS-05600534.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Claudia for Sextus Crescentius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600536.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Iulianus had this made for his most dutiful father, Caius A M, aged 70.
EDCS-05600536.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Caius A M by his son Claudius Iulianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600537.translation: For Caius Sulpicius Pelios, son of Celtus, a soldier of the cohort of Lusitanians, who died at Coluni, Cuna made this for him.
EDCS-05600537.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Sulpicius Pelios, a soldier of the Lusitanian cohort who died at Coluni, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600538.translation: In honor and for the growth of the state, born and beloved prince, our lord Flavius Julius Constantius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the citizenry of Aeminium.
EDCS-05600538.summary: A dedication by the city of Aeminium to Emperor Constantius II, likely dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-05600539.translation: ... Silvanus of Olisipo for his son, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-05600539.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription recording the death of a child named Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600540.translation: Allia Avita, mother, had this made for her son Cadio Cariano, aged 21. I ask you who pass by, may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05600540.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cadio Cariano, commissioned by his mother Allia Avita, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600541.translation: Lucius Iunius Rufus had this made for his mother, Iunia Peculiaris, aged 22.
