EDCS-05600541.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Iunius Rufus to his mother Iunia Peculiaris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600542.translation: To the departed spirits. [...] Fron, aged 60.
EDCS-05600542.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription for a person named Fron, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600545.translation: Loncinus Reburris erected this for his mother Lobesae Viriatis, aged 30.
EDCS-05600545.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by Loncinus Reburris for his mother, Lobesae Viriatis.

EDCS-05600547.translation: Cadus Laroni erected this for his mother Amoena Caesiae, aged 60, and for his wife Tongeta Alucis, aged 50, and for his daughter Caselea Caesiae, aged 7, and for himself.
EDCS-05600547.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by Cadus Laroni for his mother, wife, daughter, and himself.

EDCS-05600548.translation: Camala made this for herself and for her son Rufinus and Vegetus.
EDCS-05600548.summary: This funerary epitaph records the creation of a monument by Camala for herself and her sons.

EDCS-05600550.translation: Quintus Scaevius Vegetus [erected this] for his wife Iulia Marcella, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-05600550.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Scaevius Vegetus dedicating a monument to his wife, Iulia Marcella.

EDCS-05600551.translation: Pisiria Nava, aged 27, and her mother Dutia Cili, aged 60, lie here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05600551.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Pisiria Nava and her mother Dutia Cili, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-05600553.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For the honor and devotion of Avita, Avita made [this].
EDCS-05600553.summary: This funerary inscription records Avita dedicating a monument in honor of another Avita, likely a relative.

EDCS-05600555.translation: Caturo Nepos, son of Camalus, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05600555.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caturo Nepos, son of Camalus, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-05600556.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the burial of Celtius Maelonis.

EDCS-05600558.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus in honor of Marcus Arrius Reburrus Lanciensis, son of Transcudanus, most excellent; Marcus Arrius Laurus and Paccia Flaccilla placed this.
EDCS-05600558.summary: This dedication records Marcus Arrius Laurus and Paccia Flaccilla honoring Marcus Arrius Reburrus Lanciensis with a monument dedicated to Jupiter Augustus.

EDCS-05600561.translation: For Titus Caesar Augustus' son Vespasian, pontifex, imperator for the 12th time, with tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 6th time, by Gaius Arruntius Catellius Celer, imperial legate pro praetore, and Marcus Junius Latro Conimbrigensis, flamen of the province of Lusitania, from 5 pounds of gold.
EDCS-05600561.summary: This dedication records a sum of money, likely for a statue or cult object, contributed by Roman officials in the province of Lusitania for the deified emperor Titus Vespasian.

EDCS-05600562.translation: Lucius Aelius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Clunia, a soldier of the loyal Seventh Gemina Legion, aged 30, having served 13 years, lies here, dear to his family.
EDCS-05600562.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Aelius Celer, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Legion, who died at the age of 30 after 13 years of service.

EDCS-05600563.translation: Publius Valerius Flavus, soldier of the loyal Seventh Gemina Legion, first century of Germanus Julius, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Marius Anteros and Marcia Restituta saw to it that this was made for their friend.
EDCS-05600563.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Valerius Flavus, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Legion, commissioned by friends.

EDCS-05600564.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antonia Flaccilla, aged 40. Cornelius Saturninus for his dearest kinswoman.
EDCS-05600564.summary: This is a tombstone for Antonia Flaccilla, dedicated by her kinsman Cornelius Saturninus.

EDCS-05600565.translation: Faustinus, a household slave, aged 3, lies here. Lucius Sempronius Faustus made this at his own expense.
EDCS-05600565.summary: This is a tombstone for Faustinus, a young household slave, funded by Lucius Sempronius Faustus.

EDCS-05600567.summary: This is a tombstone for Mummia Prospera, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05600568.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paccia Glycera, freedwoman, saw to it that this was made for her well-deserving patron, Paccio Fortunatus, aged 65. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600568.summary: This is a tombstone for Paccio Fortunatus, an elderly man, erected by his freedwoman.

EDCS-05600569.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valerius Epagathus commissioned this for his well-deserving wife, Valeria Aphrodisia.
EDCS-05600569.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Aphrodisia, erected by her husband Valerius Epagathus.

EDCS-05600570.translation: Baraeco and Norbanus, aediles, holding the office of II viri, most distinguished young men, for the safety [of the community].
EDCS-05600570.summary: This is a dedication made by two aediles, Baraeco and Norbanus, who were also II viri, for the public welfare.

EDCS-05600571.translation: Caius Iulius Vitulus, an altar to Bellona.
EDCS-05600571.summary: This is an altar dedicated to the goddess Bellona by Caius Iulius Vitulus.

EDCS-05600572.translation: Caelius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Neton.
EDCS-05600572.summary: This is a record of a vow fulfilled to the god Neton by Caelius.

EDCS-05600573.translation: Salamae Nabiae Ilucius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05600573.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ilucius for Salamae Nabiae.

EDCS-05600574.translation: Caeno Boebmer, father of the fatherland, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. His son took care of it.
EDCS-05600574.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caeno Boebmer, with care provided by his son.

EDCS-05600575.translation: Caturo, son of Balaesius, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Epira and Camira erected this.
EDCS-05600575.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caturo, son of Balaesius, with the monument erected by Epira and Camira.

EDCS-05600576.translation: Fabius Niger took care to have this made at his own expense for Licinia Aucta.
EDCS-05600576.summary: This dedication records that Fabius Niger funded a monument for Licinia Aucta.

EDCS-05600577.translation: Moninus Cilus is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Hercinius and his sister Hercinia Afra made this monument for their brother.
EDCS-05600577.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Moninus Cilus, with the monument made by his siblings Hercinius and Hercinia Afra.

EDCS-05600578.translation: Quintus Orfius Asiaticus, aged thirty-six, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600578.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Orfius Asiaticus, aged 36.

EDCS-05600580.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for Marcus L. Catulus.

EDCS-05600581.translation: Viasqua, daughter, aged twenty-three, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600581.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Viasqua, aged 23.

EDCS-05600582.translation: He is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Temison, his wife, made it.
EDCS-05600582.summary: This funerary inscription records a burial, with the monument made by the wife, Temison.

EDCS-05600584.translation: Publius Cornelius Avitus willingly dedicated this altar to Iuppiter Solutor.
EDCS-05600584.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Iuppiter Solutor by Publius Cornelius Avitus.

EDCS-05600585.translation: Quintus Iulianus to Jupiter the Solver.
EDCS-05600585.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Quintus Iulianus, likely a votive offering, perhaps from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600586.translation: Lucius Norbanus Maxsumus joyfully set up an altar to Jupiter.
EDCS-05600586.summary: This inscription records Lucius Norbanus Maxsumus setting up an altar to Jupiter, probably a votive offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600587.translation: Lucius Norbanus Avitus joyfully set up an altar to Jupiter.
EDCS-05600587.summary: This inscription records Lucius Norbanus Avitus setting up an altar to Jupiter, likely a votive offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600588.translation: Caecilia Tusca, daughter of Quintus, is laid here with her husband; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05600588.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Tusca and her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600589.translation: Marcus Victorius Galba, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, aged 70, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05600589.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Victorius Galba, aged 70, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600590.translation: Iulia Columba, daughter of Lucius, is laid here.
EDCS-05600590.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Columba, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600592.translation: Victorinus, slave of Cornelia Severa, joyfully fulfilled a vow to the Holy Goddess Turibrigensis Adaegina.
EDCS-05600592.summary: This inscription records Victorinus, a slave, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Turibrigensis Adaegina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600593.translation: Cocceius Modestianus fulfilled a vow to the Holy Goddess Adaegina Turibrigensis.
EDCS-05600593.summary: This inscription records Cocceius Modestianus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Adaegina Turibrigensis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600594.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a name, perhaps a tombstone or ownership mark, origin uncertain but potentially Roman.

EDCS-05600595.translation: Pellus, son of Talticus, aged 50, and G. Pellus, son of [---], aged 20, are laid here; may the earth be light upon you. Tertulla arranged for the construction.
EDCS-05600595.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Pellus and G. Pellus, arranged by Tertulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600598.translation: Cibus, son of Luni Tapili, erected this; Tapilus and Tritius, sons of Luni, and Caturo, son of Areinus, and Malgenus, greet you travelers and fare well.
EDCS-05600598.summary: This inscription appears to be a public dedication or marker set up by several individuals, potentially related to a road or public space, possibly in the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600599.translation: Maxsuma Teia of Turgal, daughter of Arcon, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600599.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxsuma Teia, recorded in the 1st-2nd century CE, typical of Roman burial customs.

EDCS-05600601.translation: Lucius Iulius Verna, freedman of Rufus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Iulius Maximi, freedman, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05600601.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Iulius Verna and notes that his tomb was commissioned by another freedman, Lucius Iulius Maximi, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600602.translation: To Tongius, son of Tancinus; Celtius, son of Cilinus.
EDCS-05600602.summary: This is a brief inscription that appears to record a dedication or memorial to two individuals, Tongius and Celtius, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05600605.translation: To Etito, son of ADIAAR, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600605.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for ADIAAR Etitoni, dated by its phrasing to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600606.translation: Of Pacidus, son of IIIASI SAILCI, son of IIICON ANOL. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600606.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, possibly for a soldier given the unusual names, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600610.translation: To Caelius, son of Aucieicus Seranus, aged 70, his brother, who deserved well; he saw to its construction at his own expense.
EDCS-05600610.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelius, commissioned by his brother, indicating a familial relationship and affection, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600611.translation: Ableca, daughter of MCMSS, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600611.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ableca and is typical of Roman burial inscriptions from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600612.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Flavia, to her husband Lucius Murena, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600612.summary: This funerary epitaph is a dedication by Aurelia Flavia to her deceased husband, Lucius Murena, a common format in Roman burial contexts during the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600613.translation: To Quintus Caecilius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-05600613.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely recording a dedication or memorial for Quintus Caecilius, dating from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05600623.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Tongetamus Reburrinus, aged 60.
EDCS-05600623.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tongetamus Reburrinus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05600631.summary: An incomplete inscription likely recording a personal name or names.

EDCS-05600632.translation: Gaius Julius Glaber, son of Gaius, a guest (hospes) of the senate and people of Augustobrigensium, gives this gift.
EDCS-05600632.summary: This inscription records a gift made by Gaius Julius Glaber to the community of Augustobrigensium.

EDCS-05600635.translation: Lupus, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Tongeta Tancini, daughter, saw to its construction for you.
EDCS-05600635.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lupus, aged 12, with his daughter Tongeta Tancini commissioning the tomb.

EDCS-05600636.translation: To his most devoted uncle, Capito Avelicus Celemevenus, son of Talevus Cinivvus.
EDCS-05600636.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an uncle from his nephew, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-05600643.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Julius M. Nigidius, aged 32, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, deserving one. He made and saw to its placement.
EDCS-05600643.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius M. Nigidius, aged 32, commissioned by someone deserving him.

EDCS-05600644.translation: A. R. Iulius lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600644.summary: A brief funerary epitaph for an individual named Iulius.

EDCS-05600746.translation: Flavius fulfilled his vow to the Genius of Longobrigensium, with a willing and deserving spirit.
EDCS-05600746.summary: This inscription records Flavius fulfilling a vow to the local deity or spirit of Longobrigensium.

EDCS-05600799.translation: Iud(ex?) T. Ruf(i?) Ruf(ius?) Avici Ruf(i?) Iudi(?)
EDCS-05600799.summary: An incomplete inscription that appears to list several personal names, possibly in a legal or familial context.

EDCS-05601277.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored (this).
EDCS-05601277.summary: This inscription records a restoration carried out by Emperor Trajan, likely of a public building or monument.

EDCS-05601280.translation: To the Unconquered Emperor, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country.
EDCS-05601280.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor, likely inscribed on a public monument, honoring his titles.

EDCS-05601314.translation: Gaius Julius Proculus, son of Taporus, aged 20, is buried here. His father arranged for this.
EDCS-05601314.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Proculus, erected by his father around the late 1st to early 3rd century CE, based on naming conventions.

EDCS-05601315.translation: Publius Iulius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Tangini, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Iulius, who died at the age of 50, including a common epitaph wish for the deceased, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601316.translation: To the holy god Endovellicus, Annia Mariana, daughter of Quintus, willingly, in response to an oracle, dedicated this on behalf of Pompeia Prisca.
EDCS-05601316.summary: This is a votive inscription to the deity Endovellicus, dedicated by Annia Mariana for Pompeia Prisca, indicating a religious offering based on an oracle, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601317.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Andronicus willingly dedicated this to Endovellicus.
EDCS-05601317.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Endovellicus, dedicated by Lucius Calpurnius Andronicus, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601318.translation: Sacred to the god Endovellicus. Marcus Fannius Augurinus, deservedly having appeased this god for himself.
EDCS-05601318.summary: This is a religious inscription dedicated to Endovellicus by Marcus Fannius Augurinus, suggesting a personal devotion and successful appeasement of the deity, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601319.translation: To Endovellicus, Helvia Avita willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-05601319.summary: This is a votive inscription to Endovellicus, where Helvia Avita willingly fulfilled a vow, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601320.translation: To Endovellicus, sacred. Quintus Licinius Catullus willingly fulfilled his vow for Vernacla, the slave-woman of Marcus Trebius Musa.
EDCS-05601320.summary: This is a votive inscription to Endovellicus, where Quintus Licinius Catullus fulfilled a vow on behalf of Vernacla, a slave-woman, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601321.translation: Marcus Mogolius Carus willingly fulfilled his vow to Endovellicus.
EDCS-05601321.summary: This is a votive inscription to Endovellicus, dedicated by Marcus Mogolius Carus, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601322.translation: Marcus Pompeius Saturninus fulfilled his vow to Endovellicus.
EDCS-05601322.summary: This is a votive inscription to Endovellicus, dedicated by Marcus Pompeius Saturninus, likely from the 2nd to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05601323.translation: Publius Sempronius Celer fulfilled his vow to Endovellicus willingly.
EDCS-05601323.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Sempronius Celer to the deity Endovellicus.

EDCS-05601325.translation: Julius Julianus made a vow to the holy god Endovellicus.
EDCS-05601325.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Julianus to the deity Endovellicus.

EDCS-05601327.translation: Laberia Materna is laid to rest here.
EDCS-05601327.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laberia Materna.

EDCS-05601328.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. The remains of Quintus Cadius Frontonis, aged 25, who died in Rome, are laid to rest here. Cadia Tusca, aged 30, is laid to rest here. Marcus Cadius Rufus placed this for his most excellent and devoted children. Cornelia Frontonis, daughter of Frontonis and Tusca, aged 23, is laid to rest here. Cadius Rufus placed this for his most excellent wife. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05601328.summary: This is a complex funerary monument with multiple burials and dedications, likely from the Roman Imperial period, commemorating members of the Cadius family.

EDCS-05601329.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Marcia Creste, mother, arranged for this to be set up for Marcius Cresumus, aged 30.
EDCS-05601329.summary: This is a funerary dedication arranged by Marcia Creste for her son Marcius Cresumus.

EDCS-05601330.translation: Tiberius Claudius Tiberianus, father, arranged for this to be made for Domnus, aged 28 years and 11 months.
EDCS-05601330.summary: This is a funerary dedication arranged by Tiberius Claudius Tiberianus for his son Domnus.

EDCS-05601333.translation: Pisira Duati's daughter arranged for this to be made for her husband Frontonius, son of Locaetonis.
EDCS-05601333.summary: This is a funerary dedication arranged by Pisira Duati for her husband Frontonius.

EDCS-05601334.translation: Magius Faustus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05601334.summary: This is a votive inscription by Magius Faustus.

EDCS-05601394.translation: Caius O. Nicodius fulfilled the vow that he made to Indovellicus.
EDCS-05601394.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius O. Nicodius to the deity Indovellicus.

EDCS-05601395.translation: Sacred to the god Endovellicus. Caius Julius Septuminus willingly made this according to a response.
EDCS-05601395.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Julius Septuminus to the deity Endovellicus, made in response to an oracle or divine instruction.

EDCS-05601404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ravr, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05601407.translation: Maelo, son of Dovai, is laid here. Greetings to his son.
EDCS-05601407.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating Maelo, son of Dovai, is buried here, with a greeting to his son.

EDCS-05900002.translation: Masonia Veneria, daughter of Gaius, aged 18, is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05900002.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Masonia Veneria, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05900005.translation: Numerius Licinius Paternus, son of Avitus. Hail, farewell.
EDCS-05900005.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerius Licinius Paternus, son of Avitus.

EDCS-05900011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Spurius Peregrinus, aged 34 years, 7 months, 1 day. His father, Gaius Spurius Fortunalis, made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-05900011.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a father for his son, Gaius Spurius Peregrinus, who died at the age of 34 years, 7 months, and 1 day.

EDCS-05900012.translation: Postumius Apollonius, freedman of Lucius, of Norba, an Augustalis, aged 80 years. Volosinia Secundina, his wife, aged 30 years. He is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05900012.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Postumius Apollonius, a freedman from Norba and an Augustalis, who died at 80, and his wife Volosinia Secundina, who died at 30.

EDCS-05900013.translation: From the workshop of Annius Poni.
EDCS-05900013.summary: This inscription records the origin of a product from the workshop of Annius Poni.

EDCS-05900014.translation: To Titus Annius Corinthus, aged 45. He is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05900014.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Annius Corinthus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-06100310.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sycecale lived 1 year and 5 months. Her sisters Tricisma and Salcea, and Vegeta.
EDCS-06100310.summary: This funerary inscription is for Sycecale, who died at the age of 1 year and 5 months, and is noted by her sisters.

EDCS-06100311.translation: Aianes, an honorable woman, a servant of God, lived about 28 years. She rested in the peace of the Lord on the 5th day before the Ides of July in the year 561 Aerae.
EDCS-06100311.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aianes, a Christian woman who died around the age of 28 in the year 561 Aerae.

EDCS-06100314.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary epitaph identifying an individual by his name and patronymic, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100315.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Asinia Priscilla, a most rare wife, aged 21, is laid here. Asinius H took care to erect this for his most devoted wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-06100315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Asinia Priscilla, erected by her husband Asinius H, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100318.summary: This inscription identifies Urso as the slave of Burria Arminia, likely a funerary or ownership marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100321.translation: The members of the association of bankers, having earned it, gave this gift to Minerva Sancta.
EDCS-06100321.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Sancta by the association of bankers, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100325.translation: L. A. I. C. O., aged 25, is laid here.
EDCS-06100325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the name (or initials) and age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100328.translation: Docquirus Celti gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-06100328.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Docquirus Celti, likely an ex-voto dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100329.translation: Modestus and Cilia took care to have this made for their most devoted son Modestinus, aged 14.
EDCS-06100329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestinus, erected by his parents Modestus and Cilia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100330.summary: This inscription is a brief record of a vow made by Docquirus Celti, likely an ex-voto dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100331.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Docquirus Celti, likely an ex-voto dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100333.translation: This monument was made by his freedman, Caius Tanginus, son of Duatus Tanginus.
EDCS-06100333.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by a freedman, Caius Tanginus, for his patron (implied), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100334.translation: Talaceus Coporicius, freedman, [erected this] in accordance with the will.
EDCS-06100334.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a freedman, Talaceus Coporicius, fulfilling a testamentary disposition.

EDCS-06100337.translation: Titus Domitius Postumus, of Clunia, aged 66, lies here; Lucius Domitius Postuminus had this made for his father.
EDCS-06100337.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Domitius Postumus, who was 66 years old, erected by his son Lucius Domitius Postuminus.

EDCS-06100338.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a person and their possible origin.

EDCS-06100339.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription identifying a person as the son of Aenon.

EDCS-06100343.translation: Apuluseaeco Salaus, son of Rufus, set up this altar.
EDCS-06100343.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Apuluseaeco Salaus, son of Rufus, for an altar.

EDCS-06100348.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription identifying a person as the son of Attius.

EDCS-06900019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Penteus Alvius, aged 10, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-06900019.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Penteus Alvius, aged 10, with a standard invocation for the earth to lie lightly.

EDCS-06900020.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [A person] aged 50 lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-06900020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual aged 50, with a standard invocation for the earth to lie lightly.

EDCS-07000569.translation: Silbanus, servant of the god, an infant, lived for 2 years, 1 month, 5 days; he died in the year 70 (of the era).
EDCS-07000569.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Silbanus, a servant of the god, detailing his lifespan and date of death by era.

EDCS-07000570.translation: Vincentia, servant of the god, lived for 11 years; she passed away in peace on the 5th day before the Kalends of [Month], in the year 57 (of the era).
EDCS-07000570.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vincentia, a servant of the god, detailing her age, date of passing, and date of death by era.

EDCS-07000575.translation: To Camira, daughter of Lopon, [in her] fourth [year].
EDCS-07000575.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Camira.

EDCS-07000577.translation: C. Valerius Quanceius willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07000577.summary: This is a votive inscription by C. Valerius Quanceius, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-07000584.translation: In memory of Aebicus, son of Aquilius. His father Aebicus had this made.
EDCS-07000584.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a father, Aebicus, in memory of his son, also named Aebicus.

EDCS-07000586.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription by Tanginus for someone named Laneana.

EDCS-07000587.translation: Tanginus, son of Turanus, paid his vow.
EDCS-07000587.summary: This is a votive inscription by Tanginus, son of Turanus.

EDCS-07000588.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely by a son fulfilling a vow for his deceased father.

EDCS-07400363.translation: Iunius Pintamius, aged 50 years, is buried here. May the earth be light for you. His son had this made.
EDCS-07400363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iunius Pintamius, commissioned by his son.

EDCS-07400365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tongeta, daughter of Rufus, aged 70 years.
EDCS-07400365.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tongeta, daughter of Rufus.

EDCS-07600210.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Aurelius Asclepiades.

EDCS-07600211.translation: To the holy goddess Aia, Severa erected this by her own will, gladly fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-07600211.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the goddess Aia by Severa.

EDCS-07600227.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aventinus erected this by his own will, gladly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-07600227.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter by Aventinus.

EDCS-07600229.translation: Sacred to Luruni, Valeria Cattia gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07600229.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Luruni by Valeria Cattia.

EDCS-07600230.translation: Sacred to M B, Iunia Firmina gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07600230.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to M B by Iunia Firmina.

EDCS-07600236.translation: by vow for Severa, daughter of Abrilius.
EDCS-07600236.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication for Severa, daughter of Abrilius.

EDCS-07600237.translation: To Jupiter the Repulsor, Tureus Simalis gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600237.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Repulsor by Tureus Simalis.

EDCS-07600238.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cosconia Materna, daughter of Lucius, from Mirobriga, aged 62, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. She ordered this to be done by her will, for 800 sesterces.
EDCS-07600238.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Cosconia Materna, aged 62, and specifies the cost of her funeral arranged by her will.

EDCS-08300360.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Helpis made this for her well-deserving husband Festivus. He is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. (He lived) 55 years.
EDCS-08300360.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Festivus, husband of Helpis, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-08300363.translation: Pierius, a servant of God for the age, lived 67 years and rested in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of February, in the year 545 of the era.
EDCS-08300363.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Pierius, a servant of God, aged 67, in the year 545 AUC.

EDCS-08300364.translation: Possidonius, a presbyter, lived more or less fifty years and rested in peace in the Lord on the twelfth day before the Kalends of September, in the year 550 of the era.
EDCS-08300364.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Possidonius, a presbyter, aged approximately 50, in the year 550 AUC.

EDCS-08300365.translation: Cyprianus, servant of God, lived for 30 years and rested in peace on the 8th day before the Kalends of September in the year 575.
EDCS-08300365.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cyprianus, a servant of God, who died in 575.

EDCS-08300366.translation: Vincentius, servant of God, lived for 56 years and rested in peace on the 7th day before the Kalends of November in the year 564.
EDCS-08300366.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vincentius, a servant of God, who died in 564.

EDCS-08300367.translation: Antonia, servant of God, lived for 49 years and rested in peace in the Lord on the third day before the Nones of August in the year 609.
EDCS-08300367.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia, a servant of God, who died in 609.

EDCS-08300369.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Saturnia, aged 25, is laid here. Africanus to his most deserving wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08300369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturnia, who died at age 25, erected by her husband Africanus.

EDCS-08300370.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus B. Calamon erected and dedicated this for his son Marcus Julius Morphos, aged 26.
EDCS-08300370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Julius Morphos, erected by his father.

EDCS-08300373.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibiae Maxima, daughter of Rufi, aged 40, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08300373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibiae Maxima, who died at age 40.

EDCS-08300377.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aluqui, daughter of Flavina and Flavi, had this made for her mother, aged 60.
EDCS-08300377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed mother, aged 60, for whom her daughter had the monument made.

EDCS-08300378.translation: Cainus Tritei had this made for his brother, rus Tritei, aged 30, and his sister Sunua Meari, aged 70.
EDCS-08300378.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Cainus Tritei for his siblings, rus Tritei and Sunua Meari.

EDCS-08300382.translation: Quintus Iulius Montanus, an equestrian of the loyal Seventh Gemina Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08300382.summary: This is a dedication of a votive offering by Quintus Iulius Montanus, a soldier.

EDCS-08300385.translation: From the workshop of Iulius Flaccilianus.
EDCS-08300385.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product.

EDCS-08300386.translation: The Sons of Paramaeco and Tancinus Cili erected this statue to their son.
EDCS-08300386.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by sons to their father.

EDCS-08300389.translation: Flavius Turius Felix fulfilled his vow to Vortiaecius.
EDCS-08300389.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Turius Felix.

EDCS-08300392.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Rusticula, aged 19. Her father Rusticus had this made for his daughter; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300392.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Rusticula, aged 19, erected by her father Rusticus.

EDCS-08300398.translation: Cilea, freedwoman of Dutia Lanciensis Oppidana, arranged for this to be made for her, aged 45; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300398.summary: This tombstone inscription records that Cilea, a freedwoman, arranged for the monument of Dutia, aged 45.

EDCS-08300400.translation: Quintus Tussanius Iulianus willingly (dedicated this) to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08300400.summary: This inscription records a willing dedication to Jupiter by Quintus Tussanius Iulianus.

EDCS-08300401.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Galateus, son of Aulus Tritius, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. His sons Festus and Viriatus had this made for their father.
EDCS-08300401.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Galateus, son of Aulus Tritius, and was erected by his sons Festus and Viriatus.

EDCS-08400305.translation: Victor, slave of Augusta Nabis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400305.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Victor, a slave of Augusta Nabis.

EDCS-08400306.translation: In the consulship of Gaius Marius and Gaius Flavius, under Emperor Lucius Cassius, son of Caius, the people of Seanoci surrendered themselves and their property. Lucius Cassius, son of Caius, the Emperor, after receiving them into surrender, referred to the council what he thought should be imposed upon them. By the council's decision, he ordered them to hand over the arms, hostages, captives, horses, and mares which they had captured. Then they gave up all these things. Then Lucius Cassius, son of Caius, the Emperor, ordered them to be free, and restored to them their fields and buildings, their laws, and everything else that had been theirs the day before they surrendered, and whatever else had survived. Provided the people and Senate of Rome wished it, he ordered them to send envoys to Rome concerning this matter. The envoys were Crenus, son of Arcus, and Cantoni.
EDCS-08400306.summary: This inscription records the surrender of the Seanoci people to Roman authority under Emperor Lucius Cassius, detailing the terms of their submission and subsequent freedom, likely dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08500414.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Cornelia Iunia, aged 26, pious, lies here in her own (tomb); may the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus C. Cornelius (erected this) to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08500414.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Cornelia Iunia, aged 26, and was erected by her husband Marcus C. Cornelius.

EDCS-08900414.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Calpurnia, daughter of Titus Rufinianus, lived 33 years. Caecia Prisca placed this for her most pious daughter; may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-08900414.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Calpurnia, daughter of Titus Rufinianus, aged 33, and was erected by her mother Caecia Prisca.

EDCS-08900423.translation: To Victory, for his own safety.
EDCS-08900423.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Victoria, likely concerning the safety of the dedicator.

EDCS-08900424.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver.
EDCS-08900424.summary: This is an invocation or dedication to Jupiter, identified as a preserver god.

EDCS-08900425.translation: Lucius Attius Rufus willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter the Preserver.
EDCS-08900425.summary: This record states that Lucius Attius Rufus fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Conservator.

EDCS-08900427.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple statement of name and parentage, possibly an epitaph or a label.

EDCS-08900428.translation: He fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08900428.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow made to the god Mars.

EDCS-08901003.translation: To Victory, Marcus, son of Titus.
EDCS-08901003.summary: This is a dedication to Victoria by a man named Marcus, son of Titus.

EDCS-09200309.translation: He ordered the goods and clothing that were lacking for him due to the treachery of Sunua and his mother.
EDCS-09200309.summary: This text records an order concerning missing goods, attributing their absence to the treachery of Sunua and his mother.

EDCS-09200312.translation: Bandi No, daughter of Adercia Ambati, willingly fulfilled her vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-09200312.summary: This inscription records the willing fulfillment of a vow by Bandi No, daughter of Adercia Ambati.

EDCS-09200313.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely an epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-09200314.translation: Veturia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, is buried here, [with her] brothers.
EDCS-09200314.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Veturia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, and mentions her brothers.

EDCS-09200315.translation: Lubaecus, son of Loucinus, aged 16. Coria, his mother, daughter of Seofus.
EDCS-09200315.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for a young man named Lubaecus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-09202544.translation: To the departed spirits. Titulla, mother of Primulus, son of Gratus.
EDCS-09202544.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Primulus, set up by his mother.

EDCS-09300261.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Cracula of Tritium for her brother Valerius Lucifero, aged [---].
EDCS-09300261.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Valeria Cracula to her brother Valerius Lucifero.

EDCS-09300265.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying a person by name and parentage.

EDCS-09300958.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aetius.

EDCS-09300960.translation: Camira, daughter of Boutus, willingly paid her vow.
EDCS-09300960.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Camira, daughter of Boutus.

EDCS-09300961.translation: Amoena, daughter of Lovesius, from Lancium, citizen of Oppidum, aged 27, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. Lovesius, son of Tancinus, her father, saw to its erection.
EDCS-09300961.summary: This is a tombstone for Amoena, erected by her father Lovesius, indicating her age and place of origin.

EDCS-09300962.translation: Gaius Arius, son of Eburius, surnamed Apilocus, is buried here. Titullinus, son of Titullus, saw to its erection.
EDCS-09300962.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Arius Apilocus, erected by Titullinus.

EDCS-09300964.translation: Caesia, a freedwoman, made this for Ephebus, freedman of Avitus, according to his will. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09300964.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb erected by a freedwoman for another freedman, as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-09300965.translation: For Flaccinus, son of Flaccus, aged 35. His brother Avitianus and his wife Firmina saw to its erection.
EDCS-09300965.summary: This is a tombstone for Flaccinus, erected by his brother and wife.

EDCS-09300966.translation: Hispanus, son of Tanginus, from Meidubrigia, aged 50, is buried here. Cessea, daughter of Celtius, his sister, had this made as a mark of his merits.
EDCS-09300966.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Hispanus, a man from Meidubrigia, set up by his sister Cessea.

EDCS-09300967.translation: Lubaecus, son of Apanon, is buried here.
EDCS-09300967.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lubaecus, son of Apanon.

EDCS-09300968.translation: Tongius, son of Maelo, to his son Arantonio, son of Talabus; also to Talabus, son of Maelo.
EDCS-09300968.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Tongius, son of Maelo, to his son Arantonio and another son Talabus.

EDCS-09300969.translation: Nepos, son of Arcon, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300969.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Nepos, son of Arcon, including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-09300970.translation: Tapora, daughter of Tanginus, is buried here.
EDCS-09300970.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Tapora, daughter of Tanginus.

EDCS-09300972.translation: Aurelius Panianus had this made for his wife Albura and for himself.
EDCS-09300972.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription commissioned by Aurelius Panianus for his wife Albura and himself.

EDCS-09300973.summary: This inscription records Bandi Vorteaceus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09300974.translation: Bandi Vorteaceus fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-09300974.summary: This inscription records Bandi Vorteaceus willingly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09300975.translation: Marcus Protaeidius, son of Dancinus, willingly paid the vow to Trebaruna from his own funds and erected this monument; Gaius Frontonius Camalus.
EDCS-09300975.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Protaeidius, son of Dancinus, to the deity Trebaruna, with Gaius Frontonius Camalus as the builder.

EDCS-09300977.translation: To the divine spirits. For Iulia Maxima, daughter of Caius, aged 30. Caius Iulius Severus, her father, and Materna, her mother, had this made.
EDCS-09300977.summary: This tombstone inscription is dedicated to Iulia Maxima by her parents, Caius Iulius Severus and Materna.

EDCS-09300978.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Maxsuma had this made for her most devoted father, Gaius Julius Lauros, son of Caius, aged 41.
EDCS-09300978.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Lauros, erected by his daughter Iulia Maxsuma, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300979.translation: His sons Arco, Tureo, and Arantonio erected this monument for Talticus, son of Meiduennus, from Oppidum Lanciense, aged 50.
EDCS-09300979.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons for their father Talticus, from the town of Lancium, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300980.translation: Ligirus, son of Dobiterius, lies here.
EDCS-09300980.summary: A simple funerary epitaph stating that Ligirus, son of Dobiterius, is buried here, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300983.translation: Arreinus, son of Tancinus, and Arbiter, son of Mustarius Allucius, [made this] from the will, aged [---].
EDCS-09300983.summary: A fragment of an inscription indicating that Arreinus and Arbiter fulfilled a testamentary instruction for burial, date uncertain.

EDCS-09300984.translation: Veranus, son of Arbura, aged 45, lies here. His slave Primius had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300984.summary: A funerary epitaph for Veranus, aged 45, erected by his slave Primius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300985.summary: A very short inscription, likely a tombstone, identifying Publius Valius Placidus, date uncertain.

EDCS-09300986.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for Quintus, son of Accius, aged 32, date uncertain.

EDCS-09300987.translation: Caesia Crusteni, freedwoman of Severus, aged 25, lies here. Her brothers had this made.
EDCS-09300987.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesia Crusteni, a freedwoman, aged 25, erected by her brothers, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300988.translation: Colea, daughter of Vacanius, lies here.
EDCS-09300988.summary: A simple funerary epitaph stating that Colea, daughter of Vacanius, is buried here, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300989.translation: Marcus Norbanus Tureus, son of Lancius, aged 30. His sons had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300989.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Norbanus Tureus, aged 30, erected by his sons, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09300991.translation: Camalus, aged 85, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300991.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camalus, recording his age at death and a common wish for his afterlife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300992.translation: Natalis, his comrade, saw to its construction.
EDCS-09300992.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment stating that Natalis, a 'contubernalis' (a close companion, often fellow soldiers or slaves sharing a common mess/quarters), commissioned the monument.

EDCS-09300993.translation: Rufina, daughter of Rufus, aged 70, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Maximus, her son, saw to its construction.
EDCS-09300993.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, recording her age, parentage, and burial location, with her son Maximus commissioning the monument.

EDCS-09300994.translation: Vernacla, aged 30, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Her son saw to its construction.
EDCS-09300994.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vernacla, recording her age and burial location, with her son commissioning the monument.

EDCS-09300996.translation: Phoebillus, slave of Lucius At Ho, aged 27, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Venusta [commissioned it].
EDCS-09300996.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Phoebillus, a slave of Lucius At Ho, detailing his age and burial, with Venusta likely commissioning the monument.

EDCS-09300997.translation: Here lies; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Petronia Marcella saw to its construction for her father.
EDCS-09300997.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Petronia Marcella commissioned a monument for her father, expressing the wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-09300998.translation: Lucius Primigenius, his freedman, aged 50, is buried here. His sisters and Calliope saw to its construction.
EDCS-09300998.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Primigenius, a freedman of someone named Lucius, recording his age and burial, with his sisters and Calliope commissioning the monument.

EDCS-09301001.translation: Sailgius, son of Tanginus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Meiduenus, son of Andamus, saw to its construction at his own expense.
EDCS-09301001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sailgius, son of Tanginus, recording his burial and the commissioning of the monument by Meiduenus, son of Andamus, at his own expense.

EDCS-09301005.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely identifies Viriatus, son of Lovesus, possibly as the subject of a monument or as a dedicator.

EDCS-09301008.translation: Apana, daughter of Epurus, aged 15, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Lancius Scevae son saw to its construction.
EDCS-09301008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apana, daughter of Epurus, recording her young age and burial location, with Lancius Scevae son commissioning the monument.

EDCS-09301009.translation: Lucius Vocconius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-09301009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vocconius, dating to the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-09301010.translation: Marcus Iulius Marcellus, son of Marcus, aged 11, and Caius Iulius Caesius, son of Marcus, aged 35, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Their mother Maura saw to its construction.
EDCS-09301010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Marcus Iulius Marcellus and Caius Iulius Caesius, likely from the early Imperial period, with their mother as the dedicator.

EDCS-09301011.translation: Annia, daughter of Lancius, aged 61, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her son saw to its construction.
EDCS-09301011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia, aged 61, likely from the early Imperial period, with her son as the dedicator.

EDCS-09301013.translation: An altar to Bocorobus, son of Eicob, by Talusicus. M T B D M willingly and deservedly with a devoted mind.
EDCS-09301013.summary: This is an inscription on an altar dedicated to the deity Bocorobus by Talusicus, likely from a provincial area and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301014.translation: Moricilus fulfilled his vow to Cancilus.
EDCS-09301014.summary: This is a votive inscription by Moricilus to Cancilus, likely from a provincial area and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301015.translation: Arreinus fulfilled his vow to Cilius Martius willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09301015.summary: This is a votive inscription by Arreinus to Cilius Martius, likely from a provincial area and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301016.translation: Aulus Aconius Rufus fulfilled his vow to Iuppiter Solutor ('the Freer') willingly and with a devoted mind.
EDCS-09301016.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aulus Aconius Rufus to Iuppiter Solutor, likely from an urban setting and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301018.translation: Balaesus fulfilled his vow to the goddess Victoria willingly, with a devoted mind.
EDCS-09301018.summary: This is a votive inscription by Balaesus to the goddess Victoria, likely from a provincial area and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301020.translation: Bandu fulfilled his vow to Roudaecus willingly and with a devoted mind.
EDCS-09301020.summary: This is a votive inscription by Bandu to Roudaecus, likely from a provincial area and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301021.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Tussanius Clemens fulfilled his vow willingly and with a devoted mind.
EDCS-09301021.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Tussanius Clemens to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from an urban setting and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301022.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deities Bandua and Roudaecus.

EDCS-09301023.translation: Atilia Severa gladly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09301023.summary: This is a votive inscription by Atilia Severa to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-09301024.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Vetius [son of---].
EDCS-09301024.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Vetius.

EDCS-09700164.translation: Clemens, son of Arpanice, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700164.summary: This is a votive inscription by Clemens, son of Arpanice.

EDCS-09700166.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Aurelii Donatus and Sotira made this for their most loving daughter Septimia Vult, daughter of Vultus, who lived 3 years and 5 months.
EDCS-09700166.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelii Donatus and Sotira to their daughter Septimia Vult.

EDCS-09700167.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Allius Avitus, grandfather, and Silvanus Silvanus, father, saw to the making of this for Vagellia Rufina the younger.
EDCS-09700167.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Vagellia Rufina the younger's grandfather and father.

EDCS-09700168.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. In honor and memory of Allia Vagellia Avita, aged 26. Gaius Allius Avitus, father, had this made for his most dutiful and deserving daughter, and Quintus Silvanus Silvanus, husband, for his most indulgent and deserving wife.
EDCS-09700168.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by the father and husband for Allia Vagellia Avita.

EDCS-09700169.translation: Paratus, freedman of Modestus, son of Modestus, had this made in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09700169.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by a freedman according to a will.

EDCS-09700179.translation: Priscus, son of Maternus, aged 32. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09700179.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Priscus, son of Maternus.

EDCS-09700589.translation: Annia, daughter of Quintus Pic, aged 18. The decurions decreed the expense of burial for her; her parents received the honor and waived the expense.
EDCS-09700589.summary: This inscription records that the decurions decreed the expense of burial for Annia, aged 18, whose parents received the honor.

EDCS-09700590.translation: Papirius Rufus, son of Marcus Papirius, and his freedwomen Turpa, Severa, and Tapora, and Papirius Modestus, aged 4, had this made at the expense of Papirius Modestus according to his will, and Marcus Papirius is buried here.
EDCS-09700590.summary: This epitaph records a burial arranged by Papirius Rufus and others for Papirius Modestus, who is also buried there.

EDCS-09700592.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cretonia Maxima, of Papiria Pacensis, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publius Aplanius Marcianus, of Papiria Emeritensis, aged 33, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The mother had this made for herself and her son.
EDCS-09700592.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cretonia Maxima and Publius Aplanius Marcianus, with the monument commissioned by their mother.

EDCS-09700594.translation: Triteus, son of Alluquius, freely paid his vow to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09700594.summary: This dedication records a votive offering made by Triteus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-09700596.translation: Antonia, daughter of Gaius, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Mannius Faustinus had this made.
EDCS-09700596.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Antonia, aged 65, and was commissioned by Mannius Faustinus.

EDCS-09700601.translation: Turacia, daughter of Cainon, freely paid her vow to the Norban goddesses.
EDCS-09700601.summary: This votive inscription records a vow paid by Turacia to local goddesses identified as 'Norbanenses'.

EDCS-09700603.translation: Camalus, freedman of Simalasia, freely paid his vow to Jupiter the Solver of Vows, as was deserved.
EDCS-09700603.summary: This dedication records a votive offering made by Camalus to Jupiter Solutorius.

EDCS-09700607.translation: In memory of his most dutiful wife, Anava, Muscius had this put up.
EDCS-09700607.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Muscius in memory of his wife Anava, who died aged 52.

EDCS-09700608.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Of Claudia Sosuma of Emerita.
EDCS-09700608.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Claudia Sosuma.

EDCS-09700609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tonceta had this made for her daughter Casina, daughter of Avitus, aged 16.
EDCS-09700609.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Casina, aged 16, and was commissioned by her mother Tonceta.

EDCS-09700612.translation: Tongius, father of Apaio, in memory.
EDCS-09700612.summary: This inscription is a memorial erected by Tongius for his father Apaio.

EDCS-09700966.translation: Cilius, son of Camalus, aged 70, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Maila daughter of Pucius had this monument erected according to the will.
EDCS-09700966.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cilius, erected by Maila, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700969.translation: Reburrus son of Taxus vowed to Jupiter Best and Greatest, as is deserved.
EDCS-09700969.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Reburrus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700970.translation: Creusa, slave of Avita, aged 15, lies here.
EDCS-09700970.summary: A funerary epitaph for Creusa, a slave, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700972.translation: Ursia Fundana and Ursius Arrenus, children of Nicerotis and Ursius, lie here, and also Publius Ursius Nicerotis.
EDCS-09700972.summary: A funerary epitaph for multiple individuals of the Ursia and Ursius families, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700973.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, providing only a name and age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701490.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Martialis, freedman of Valeria Flaccilla, aged 35, lies here.
EDCS-09701490.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Martialis, a freedman, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701491.translation: Marcus Tavonius, son of Marcus, Romilia Firmus, from Ateste, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion, in the century of Publius Sextus, aged 30, with 11 years of service, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09701491.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Tavonius, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701495.translation: Valeria dedicated this altar to Apollo willingly.
EDCS-09701495.summary: A votive inscription dedicating an altar to Apollo by Valeria, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701497.translation: Julia Megale dedicated this altar to Jupiter Best and Greatest with a willing heart.
EDCS-09701497.summary: A votive inscription dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Julia Megale, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701498.translation: Rufus, son of Tongs, paid his vow to the household gods.
EDCS-09701498.summary: A votive inscription recording Rufus paying a vow to the Lares, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701499.translation: Flavus willingly erected an altar to the Lares and paid his vow.
EDCS-09701499.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the household gods by Flavus.

EDCS-09701502.translation: Gaius Julius Victor, aged 28, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you. Cirrus arranged for this.
EDCS-09701502.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Victor, paid for by Cirrus.

EDCS-09701504.translation: Paula, daughter of Aminus, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you. Marcus Titius arranged for this.
EDCS-09701504.summary: This is a tombstone for Paula, daughter of Aminus, arranged for by Marcus Titius.

EDCS-09701505.translation: Libaecus, son of Caenicus, a native of Turgal, is buried here.
EDCS-09701505.summary: This is a tombstone for Libaecus, son of Caenicus, from Turgal.

EDCS-09701506.translation: Ulea, daughter of Lubaecus, aged 50, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-09701506.summary: This is a tombstone for Ulea, daughter of Lubaecus.

EDCS-09800092.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 25th time, on the 12th day of [the month].
EDCS-09800092.summary: This inscription records the titles and powers of Emperor Tiberius, likely from an official monument.

EDCS-09800094.translation: Cilius, son of Camalus, of Bandi Isibraia, paid his vow.
EDCS-09800094.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cilius, son of Camalus.

EDCS-09800096.translation: Severus, son of Abrunus, of Bandi Brialeacus, paid his vow.
EDCS-09800096.summary: This is a votive inscription by Severus, son of Abrunus.

EDCS-09800098.translation: Gaedo, Vitalis, paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-09800098.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaedo Vitalis.

EDCS-09800100.translation: Severus, son of C. s., erected [this] to Mars in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09800100.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars by Severus.

EDCS-09800101.translation: Lucius C. O. Marati Boro willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-09800101.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius C. O. Marati Boro.

EDCS-09800102.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars Talabarius, likely made by someone named Pisi.

EDCS-09800103.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Munida Igaeditana.

EDCS-09800105.translation: I, Pubescens, fearing no pitiful end, carried briefly the fates of Celtus Anceitus, here are your ashes, rest here in peace.
EDCS-09800105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Celtus Anceitus, set up by Pubescens.

EDCS-09800106.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription honoring Avitus, son of Campanus.

EDCS-09800108.translation: Marcus Caelius Aquitus, son of Cormertonis, aged 35, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Caelia Avita, daughter of Modestus, and wife, had this made for herself and him.
EDCS-09800108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Caelius Aquitus, set up by his wife Caelia Avita.

EDCS-09800110.translation: His friends Maelotalis, Amaius, and Antonius Comalius, son of Arcon, erected this monument for Cainon, son of Cabrunus.
EDCS-09800110.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by friends for Cainon, son of Cabrunus.

EDCS-09800111.translation: Calaetus, son of Boutus, aged 80, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Flaccus had this made for his father Calaetus, in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09800111.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calaetus, son of Boutus, set up by his son Flaccus according to the will.

EDCS-09800114.translation: For Caturon, son of Medamus, aged 50. Paesurus, son of Arcus, had this made in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09800114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caturon, son of Medamus, set up by Paesurus according to the will.

EDCS-09800115.translation: Ceionius, son of Rufinus, aged 25, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Greetings, you who read, farewell, you who have read.
EDCS-09800115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ceionius, son of Rufinus, with a greeting and farewell to the reader.

EDCS-09800116.translation: Cassia Maurilla, daughter of Chresumus, and Vitalis, daughter of Chresumus, had this made at their own expense for their freedmen, Chresumus and Amoena, freedmen of Longinus, and for their parents.
EDCS-09800116.summary: This epitaph records a funerary monument commissioned by two women for their freedmen and parents.

EDCS-09800118.translation: Lucius Cocceius Italicus for his father, Cocceius, from Italicensis.
EDCS-09800118.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son for his father.

EDCS-09800119.translation: Sabina, daughter of Sabinus, and Rufina, daughter of Sabinus, lie here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09800119.summary: This epitaph records the burial place of two women, Sabina and Rufina.

EDCS-09800120.translation: Lucius Cornelius Cluniensis, son of Quintus, aged 40, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09800120.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Cornelius Cluniensis, who was 40 years old.

EDCS-09800121.translation: Quintilla, aged 3, the darling of Marcus Curius Quintio and Curia Primula, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-09800121.summary: This epitaph records the burial of a young child, Quintilla, aged 3.

EDCS-09800122.translation: Fuscus, son of Mamius, and Proculus, son of Maternius, had this made in accordance with the will for Flaccus, son of Amminus.
EDCS-09800122.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by two men for Flaccus, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-09800126.translation: For the father, Lvius, son of Caeno; for the mother, Boudica, daughter of Tongius; for the mother-in-law, Cilea, daughter of Cilius; for the wife, Caeno, daughter of Lvius.
EDCS-09800126.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated by Caeno to multiple family members including his father, mother, mother-in-law, and wife.

EDCS-09800127.translation: His sons erected a monument for Maturovius, son of Veibalus.
EDCS-09800127.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by sons for their father, Maturovius.

EDCS-09800128.translation: Tongeta, freedwoman of Probinus, had this made in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09800128.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by a freedwoman, Tongeta, in accordance with a will.

EDCS-09800129.translation: Flavius Zosimus had this made for his father, Redemptus.
EDCS-09800129.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by a son, Flavius Zosimus, for his father, Redemptus.

EDCS-09800132.translation: Domitius for his father Tagano, mother Cilea, sister Tura, and brother Tagani.
EDCS-09800132.summary: This is a dedication by Domitius to his deceased family members.

EDCS-09800133.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying an individual by name and parentage.

EDCS-09800134.translation: Tanginus, son of Meiduenu, is buried here.
EDCS-09800134.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual by name, parentage, and burial.

EDCS-09800136.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Tapora, wife of Leuri, and Lantutra, daughter of Caturon.
EDCS-09800136.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Dis Manes for Tapora and Lantutra.

EDCS-09800137.translation: Tongius, son of Tanginus, is buried here.
EDCS-09800137.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual by name, parentage, and burial.

EDCS-09800138.translation: Caius Valerius of the Galeria tribe from Clunia, to his son Publius Valerius Celtus, son of Quirinus, of the Clementi family.
EDCS-09800138.summary: This is a dedication by Caius Valerius to his son Publius Valerius.

EDCS-09800144.translation: Quintus Aefulanus Phosphorus, freedman of Quintus, an Augustalis, aged 85, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09800144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aefulanus Phosphorus, an Augustalis, providing his age and a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-09800146.translation: Caius Flavius Sabinus, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09800146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Flavius Sabinus, providing his name, tribal affiliation, and a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-09800149.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Gaius Valerius Martialis, aged 85, and his most devoted wife arranged for this to be made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09800149.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Martialis, aged 85, and indicates his wife arranged for his burial.

EDCS-09800153.translation: Marcus Armus, son of Curius, gladly and willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus.
EDCS-09800153.summary: This is an inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow made to Jupiter by Marcus Armus.

EDCS-09800155.translation: Modestus willingly paid his vow to the household gods.
EDCS-09800155.summary: This is a votive inscription by Modestus to the household gods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800157.translation: Maurus, freedman of Dovilo, paid his vow to the gods and goddesses of Pinonia.
EDCS-09800157.summary: This is a votive inscription by Maurus, a freedman, to local deities, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800158.translation: Rutilius Vitu s erected an altar.
EDCS-09800158.summary: This is an altar dedication by Rutilius Vitu s, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800159.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription identifying Caenobius, son of Ebureinus, and his age, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800160.translation: Catullus, son of Aug, and Andotus.
EDCS-09800160.summary: This inscription likely records two individuals, Catullus and Andotus, possibly as a joint dedication or funerary marker, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800161.translation: Fabia Secunda, daughter of Publius, aged 55, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09800161.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Secunda, daughter of Publius, indicating her age and burial place, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800162.translation: Flavius Summacus arranged for this memorial to be erected for Flavia Ambasia according to her will.
EDCS-09800162.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Flavius Summacus for Flavia Ambasia, as stipulated in her will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800163.translation: Quintus Furius Niger lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09800163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Furius Niger, indicating his burial place, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800164.translation: Iulia Annia, daughter of Tanginus, is buried here.
EDCS-09800164.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Iulia Annia, daughter of Tanginus, and her burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800165.translation: G, son of Mailo, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you, aged 12.
EDCS-09800165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy identified as G, son of Mailo, indicating his age and burial place, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09800166.translation: Norbana, freedwoman of Gaius, aged 50, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09800166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbana, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-09800167.translation: Norbana, daughter of Gaius, granddaughter of Secundus, aged 20, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09800167.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbana, daughter of Gaius, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-09800168.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pollio, possibly a freedman of someone named Augustus or associated with the imperial family.

EDCS-09800169.translation: Togeta, daughter of Talabarus, aged 75, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Lacer took care of this.
EDCS-09800169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Togeta, daughter of Talabarus, with arrangements made by Lacer.

EDCS-09800170.translation: Quintus Valerius Pollio lies here. A.
EDCS-09800170.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Pollio, with an unclear abbreviation 'A'.

EDCS-09800172.translation: Silo, son of Ampunaecus, of the family of Arentius, willingly fulfilled his vow to Mani for Arentia, as was deserved.
EDCS-09800172.summary: This is an inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Silo to Mani, likely related to religious or civic duty.

EDCS-09800173.translation: Tancinus, son of Caturus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Arentius Ampunaecus with a willing heart.
EDCS-09800173.summary: This is an inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Tancinus to Arentius Ampunaecus.

EDCS-10701759.translation: Louciri, Aemilia Vitalis, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10701759.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aemilia Vitalis.

EDCS-10701761.translation: Valerius Caturo, son of Sateli, willingly placed this monument.
EDCS-10701761.summary: This is an inscription indicating that Valerius Caturo erected a monument.

EDCS-10701762.translation: To our Lords, Lecenius Lecenian, August XI.
EDCS-10701762.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to imperial lords, possibly referencing an emperor and a count of acclamations (August XI).

EDCS-11701019.translation: Baritto to the colony of Bonis Eventis, fortunate.
EDCS-11701019.summary: This is a dedication by Baritto to the colony of Bonis Eventis, possibly an owner of a property or a benefactor.

EDCS-11701023.translation: Narcissus Delius, devoted to Hera, also called Pafius, Marianus, the honorable.
EDCS-11701023.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or an epitaph for Narcissus Delius, who was devoted to the goddess Hera and had multiple names.

EDCS-11701026.translation: Paulus, also called Marcianus, the illuminator, Getuli.
EDCS-11701026.summary: This inscription likely identifies Paulus, also known as Marcianus, as an 'illuminator' and associates him with Getuli, possibly indicating his profession or status.

EDCS-11701074.translation: Spectatus Severus, who lived with Cumelius.
EDCS-11701074.summary: This inscription appears to be an epitaph or memorial for Spectatus Severus, indicating his relationship with Cumelius.

EDCS-11701077.summary: This inscription lists the names of four winds, possibly used in a symbolic or decorative context, perhaps on a sundial or a building.

EDCS-11701082.translation: Hiberus, Leneus, son of Lenobatis, Pelops, Inacus.
EDCS-11701082.summary: This inscription lists several names, possibly of individuals or figures, with Hiberus, Leneus, and Pelops being prominent, and Leneus identified as the son of Lenobatis.

EDCS-11701083.translation: Viventes and Avita, fortunate in the tower.
EDCS-11701083.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or a wish for good fortune for Viventes and Avita, associated with a tower.

EDCS-11701277.summary: This is a dedication by Cornelius to the Manes, the spirits of the dead, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-11701278.translation: Martinus, servant of Christ, lived for 64 years, died in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of March in the year 664.
EDCS-11701278.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the life and death of Martinus, a servant of Christ, who died in the year 664 (likely an era date).

EDCS-11800623.translation: Gaius Norbanus Talticus is buried here.
EDCS-11800623.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Gaius Norbanus Talticus is buried in this location.

EDCS-11800624.translation: Cessia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, a Roman woman, is buried here.
EDCS-11800624.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cessia Secunda, a Roman woman.

EDCS-11800625.translation: Loucinus, son of Cilius, set up this altar; he made it with money for the goddess Cotis.
EDCS-11800625.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the goddess Cotis by Loucinus, son of Cilius.

EDCS-11800626.translation: His sister had it made at her own expense.
EDCS-11800626.summary: This is a continuation of a previous inscription, stating that a sister paid for the construction of a monument.

EDCS-11800627.translation: Frontus, son of Flaccinus, set this up for the god Tritiacus, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11800627.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to the god Tritiacus by Frontus, son of Flaccinus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11800628.translation: Pisira, daughter of Turai, aged 40; her husband Maelius Amarillis had it made and dedicated it.
EDCS-11800628.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Pisira, daughter of Turai, aged 40, whose husband Maelius Amarillis commissioned and dedicated the monument.

EDCS-11800631.translation: To the Emperor, Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Invicctus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-11800631.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Tacitus, listing his imperial titles.

EDCS-11800775.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Italia, aged 40.
EDCS-11800775.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Italia, aged 40.

EDCS-11800776.translation: Letorius and Epifanius rested in peace; one lived 8 years and the other in the year 575 (of the local era).
EDCS-11800776.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Letorius and Epifanius, with their ages and a date in the local era (575).

EDCS-11800777.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; for Publius Iulius Rufinus, aged 18, from Olisipo. Iulia Marcellinna, daughter of Publius, his sister, set this up for her most dutiful brother. He is buried here.
EDCS-11800777.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Publius Iulius Rufinus, aged 18, from Olisipo, erected by his sister Iulia Marcellinna.

EDCS-11800778.translation: Caius Ursius Clemens, son of Publius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 21, is buried here.
EDCS-11800778.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caius Ursius Clemens, son of Publius, aged 21.

EDCS-11800779.translation: Gaius Terentius Saturninus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-11800779.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Terentius Saturninus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800874.translation: To Publius Memmius Ligus, twice duovir.
EDCS-11800874.summary: This is a dedication or honorary inscription for Publius Memmius Ligus, who held the office of duovir, likely in a Roman colony or municipium.

EDCS-11800891.translation: Numerius, slave of the god, lived 83 years and rested in peace on the day before the Nones of March in the year 633 (of the Spanish Era, i.e., 595 AD).
EDCS-11800891.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for a slave named Numerius, providing his age, date of death, and era, indicating a late Roman or early Byzantine date.

EDCS-11901233.translation: To Lucius Annius Novatus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-11901233.summary: This is a dedication or honorary inscription for Lucius Annius Novatus, likely a local magistrate or benefactor.

EDCS-11901234.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Perses, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, flamen of the province of Lusitania, patron of the city of Ossonoba.
EDCS-11901234.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Perses, recognizing his civic and religious roles in Lusitania, including being a provincial flamen and patron of Ossonoba.
