EDCS-11901236.translation: Quintus Junius Avitianus, Lucius Aemilius Themison, Lucius Publicius Urbanus, Lucius Caecilius Plocamus, Lucius Licinius Fructus, Lucius Caecilius Nymphodotus, Marius Maximus, Marcus Fabius Myrtilus, Lucius Caecilius Symphorus, Marcus Julius Avitianus Geminius Callistus, [---] of the Ossonobenses, Lucius Livius Martialis, Lucius Licinius Calvus, Lucius Annius Lapillianus, Lucius Caecilius Liberalis, Lucius Herennius Cosconius, Lucius Aelius Superstes, Cnaeus Acilius Rufus, Quintus Junius Chrysanthus, Marcus Caecilius Urbanus, Lucius Licinius Opilio.
EDCS-11901236.summary: This inscription lists numerous names of citizens or notable individuals from the Ossonobenses community, likely related to a public monument or honorific dedication.

EDCS-11901238.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Acte, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901238.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Acte, providing her age and a common wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-11901239.translation: To Publius Aelius Surus Arches, VE.
EDCS-11901239.summary: This is a dedication or honorary inscription for Publius Aelius Surus Arches, with an unclear abbreviation 'VE'.

EDCS-11901240.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for a child named Castor, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11901242.translation: Daphnis, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901242.summary: This is a Roman funerary epitaph for Daphnis, providing her age and a common wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-11901249.translation: Aemilia Saturnina lived for 64 years, 6 months, and 17 days. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-11901249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Saturnina, recording her age and expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-11901250.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Amimetus lived for 23 years; he is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-11901250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amimetus, recording his age and that he is buried here, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-11901251.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Chrysantus lived for 22 years and 2 months; he is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-11901251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrysantus, recording his age and that he is buried here, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-11901252.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Dionysianus lived for 39 years. Go, traveler, go! May the underworld gods not envy you this place, this monument, this light earth; may good spirits willingly grant it to the departed. Maritima lived for 25 years. Go, traveler, go! May the underworld gods not envy you this place, this monument, this light earth; may good spirits willingly grant it to the departed.
EDCS-11901252.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph, likely commemorating two individuals, Dionysianus and Maritima, with elaborate warnings to travelers and wishes for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-11901253.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Patricia lived for 11 years and 4 days. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Patricius lived for 43 years, 3 months, and 14 days. [---]
EDCS-11901253.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of two individuals, Patricia and Patricius, likely siblings or family members.

EDCS-11901254.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Paullianus lived for 16 years and 5 months. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-11901254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Paullianus, recording his age and expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-11901255.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Protypus lived for 15 years, 7 months, and 8 days; he is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you, most deserving.
EDCS-11901255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Protypus, recording his age, burial location, and expressing a wish for a light burial, noting his deserving nature.

EDCS-11901256.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tyche lived for 20 years and 2 months; she is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Herennianus lived for 25 years and 3 months; he is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-11901256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages and burial locations of two individuals, Tyche and Herennianus.

EDCS-11901258.translation: To the sacred waters, Patulus fulfilled a vow by his father's will.
EDCS-11901258.summary: This inscription records Patulus fulfilling a vow to the sacred waters, as stipulated in his father's will.

EDCS-11901260.summary: This very brief inscription records a voluntary vow made by Urmicus.

EDCS-11901261.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Pompeia Exoce, grandmother, dedicated this for Cepio Semproniano, aged 5 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901261.summary: A tombstone dedicated by a grandmother for her grandson, Cepio Semproniano, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-11901262.translation: Gaius Julius Arenius is buried here. Arbura, daughter of Bolbius and wife of Arenius, is buried here. Iulia Amoena, daughter of Arenius, is buried here.
EDCS-11901262.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of three individuals: Gaius Julius Arenius, Arbura (his wife), and Iulia Amoena (his daughter).

EDCS-11901263.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lupa, aged 2 years, 10 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-11901263.summary: A tombstone commemorating Lupa, who died at a very young age (2 years, 10 months, 15 days).

EDCS-11901264.translation: He lived 41 years and 7 days. Publia Aemilia Catullina dedicated this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11901264.summary: A tombstone dedicated by a wife, Publia Aemilia Catullina, for her husband who lived 41 years and 7 days.

EDCS-11901271.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus S. Euprepes, aged 38 years.
EDCS-11901271.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Marcus S. Euprepes, who died at the age of 38.

EDCS-11901272.translation: This was made in honor of the sacred Ortygius. Magi [were] responsible for it.
EDCS-11901272.summary: An inscription recording the dedication or construction of something in honor of Ortygius, carried out by Magi.

EDCS-11901275.translation: Content with the honor, Lucius Helvius Flavianus waived the expense.
EDCS-11901275.summary: An inscription stating that Lucius Helvius Flavianus, satisfied with the honor, remitted the cost of something.

EDCS-11901276.translation: My patron, Gallius, the sixth age [of life] took me when I was fifty-five years old. The soil of Italy bore me, Spain sheltered me. A winter, unknown to all, took me away. I lay as a stranger in this place, when he who gave all these things to me also gave this tomb.
EDCS-11901276.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Julius Aptus, a freedman, detailing his patron Gallius's provision of his tomb after he died at age 55.

EDCS-11901277.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Accennia Herennia, aged 60 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901277.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Accennia Herennia, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-11901278.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annia Materna, aged 50 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901278.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Annia Materna, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-11901280.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cogitata, aged 5 years. Firmidius Peregrinus had this made for his daughter. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901280.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Cogitata, set up by her father Firmidius Peregrinus.

EDCS-11901281.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Lupiana lived 21 years. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Liburnius Victor.
EDCS-11901281.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Lupiana, whose monument was erected by Liburnius Victor.

EDCS-11901282.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Liburnius Galeria Maternus, aged 23 years. Lucius Liburnius Mus, his father, for his most dutiful son. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901282.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Liburnius Galeria Maternus, set up by his father Lucius Liburnius Mus.

EDCS-11901283.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Memmius Pabiccio.
EDCS-11901283.summary: A funerary epitaph mentioning Quintus Memmius Pabiccio.

EDCS-11901284.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publicia Lucina, aged 4 years. She lies here in the tomb. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901284.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publicia Lucina, a young child, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901285.translation: Rustica, daughter of Livi, lies here.
EDCS-11901285.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Rustica, daughter of Livi.

EDCS-11901286.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Euremon for his most deserving and most dutiful wife Sestia Aemerita, who lived 59 years, 3 months, and 9 days. He placed this. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901286.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sestia Aemerita, erected by her husband Aemilius Euremon, with detailed age information.

EDCS-11901287.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Tullius Donatus Faustinus, son, who lived 16 years. Tullius Vellicus and Porcia Materna, for their most dutiful son, placed this. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901287.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tullius Donatus Faustinus, set up by his parents Tullius Vellicus and Porcia Materna.

EDCS-11901288.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gemellae, aged 10 years.
EDCS-11901288.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gemellae, a child aged 10.

EDCS-11901291.translation: For Beryllus, freedman of Augustus, most diligent and most loving procurator, vicar of the rationales, an excellent and just man, restorer of the mines. The colonists of our lord Augustus of the Vipascensis mine willingly erected this statue with its base at his own expense, the following having subscribed; dedicated by Titus Iunius, dedicated to Gnaeus Claudius.
EDCS-11901291.summary: A dedication of a statue to Beryllus, an imperial freedman holding administrative positions in the Vipascensis mine, by the grateful colonists. The inscription dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901293.translation: Gaius Anvius Buanus, son of Gaius, lies here.
EDCS-11901293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Anvius Buanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Atilius Statullus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901295.translation: Bouta, daughter of Iulia, aged 15, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Bouta, a 15-year-old girl, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Amoena, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Materna, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901298.translation: Gaius Iulius Letondo, son of Boutius.
EDCS-11901298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Letondo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laberia Coimia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901300.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Licinius Fuscus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901301.translation: Lucius Lo[c]isicus Maxumus, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901301.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Lo[c]isicus Maxumus, aged 35, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901302.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modestianus, aged about 10, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publius Aelius Modestus for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-11901302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, a dedication by Publius Aelius Modestus to his son Modestianus, who died around the age of 10, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901303.translation: May the earth lie lightly on Cnaeus Naeudius Rufus.
EDCS-11901303.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cnaeus Naeudius Rufus, expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-11901304.translation: Of Marcus Postumius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-11901304.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name and filiation for a tombstone or a monument.

EDCS-11901305.translation: Agria Rufina arranged for this to be made for Marcus Valerius Rufus, her husband, for Marcus Valerius Marcellus, her son, and for Marcus Valerius Marcellus, her grandson; they are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you all.
EDCS-11901305.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman named Agria Rufina who commissioned a tomb for her husband and two descendants, expressing a wish for their peaceful rest.

EDCS-11901306.translation: To Viria Julia, daughter of M. Mu. Caius Iulius.
EDCS-11901306.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a funerary marker with names and potentially a relationship indicated.

EDCS-11901311.translation: To Venus, Caius Iulius Rufinus, master.
EDCS-11901311.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus by Caius Iulius Rufinus, who held the position of 'magister'.

EDCS-11901323.translation: For Gaius Agrius Rufus Silonis, admitted to the *adlectio* as an Italian, Marcus Castricius Lucanius and Gaius Valerius Paezon, to their excellent friend.
EDCS-11901323.summary: This is a dedication or memorial erected by Marcus Castricius Lucanius and Gaius Valerius Paezon to their esteemed friend, Gaius Agrius Rufus Silonis.

EDCS-11901324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Porcius Severus of Mirobriga, of the Celtic tribe, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901324.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Porcius Severus of Mirobriga, stating his age and tribal affiliation, and expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-11901326.translation: Modesta, daughter of Norbanus Modestus, arranged for this to be made for herself and her family.
EDCS-11901326.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Modesta, daughter of Norbanus Modestus, commissioned a burial place for herself and her relatives.

EDCS-11901328.translation: For the daughters of Lucius, in accordance with the will of Caius Valerius Vibianus, brother, curator; Caius Valerius Asbestus, freedman, heir, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-11901328.summary: This inscription details the arrangements made by Caius Valerius Asbestus, a freedman and heir, to fulfill the will of his brother and curator, Caius Valerius Vibianus, for his daughters.

EDCS-11901329.translation: Col. [son] of L. V. [---], aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-11901329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, identified by his tribal name and age, indicating he is buried here.

EDCS-11901351.summary: This inscription records the title of Lucius Cornelius, possibly dedicating a monument or office.

EDCS-11901352.summary: This inscription records the name Sextus Appuleius Priamus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-11901353.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Lupercilla, aged 28. Iulius Maximus had this made for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-11901353.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Flavia Lupercilla at age 28, commissioned by her son Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-11901354.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Marius.
EDCS-11901354.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Marius, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-11901355.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marius Badiolus, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901355.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Marius Badiolus at age 38, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-11901357.translation: Marcus Sulpicius Zographus, aged 80. Iunia Satulla, aged 70. Zographus and his wife are buried here.
EDCS-11901357.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Marcus Sulpicius Zographus and his wife Iunia Satulla.

EDCS-11901366.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Attius Helvianus, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901366.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Attius Helvianus at age 20, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-11901368.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Galla, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Hypnus had this made for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-11901368.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Galla at age 35, commissioned by her husband Hypnus.

EDCS-11901371.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marciana is buried here, aged 3. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901371.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Marciana at age 3, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-11901373.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For my most dutiful grandmother, Pacciae Lepidinae, aged 80. Quintus Licinius Felix Nepos placed this. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901373.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Pacciae Lepidinae at age 80, placed by her grandson Quintus Licinius Felix Nepos.

EDCS-11901374.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Servilius Claranus, aged 18, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-11901374.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Servilius Claranus, who died at 18.

EDCS-11901376.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius S La, aged 60, lies here.
EDCS-11901376.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius S La, who died at 60.

EDCS-11901390.translation: Here lies, may the earth be light on you, the most virtuous and religious woman of her time, aged 55, perpetual priestess of the citizenship of Mirietanum, a most dutiful mother and grandmother, placed here by her sons and grandsons.
EDCS-11901390.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 55-year-old woman who held the office of perpetual priestess and was a devoted mother and grandmother.

EDCS-11901391.translation: To our Juno Primogenea, and to Felix, by the slaves.
EDCS-11901391.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Juno Primogenea and to a person named Felix, possibly by slaves.

EDCS-11901394.translation: Lucius Marcius Pierus, content with the honor, remitted the expense. His friends, Lucius Marcius Pierus Pacensis, Augustalis of the colony of Pacensis and of the municipality of Eborensis, set up this monument through a collection of money for his merits.
EDCS-11901394.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by friends for Lucius Marcius Pierus Pacensis, who was an Augustalis, with the honoree himself declining to pay for it.

EDCS-11901397.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication related to Sextus Numisius Avi.

EDCS-11901398.translation: Gaius Julius Niger, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis, and Iulia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius, lie here; may the earth be light on them.
EDCS-11901398.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Niger, an Augustalis, and Iulia Prima, both freedpersons.

EDCS-11901399.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Cassius Vettonianus Pacensis, aged 26, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-11901399.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Cassius Vettonianus Pacensis, who died at 26.

EDCS-11901400.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agathon BIV
EDCS-11901400.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for someone named Agathon.

EDCS-11901401.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apollonius Molon, aged 40, placed [this]; Afrosa lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-11901401.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Apollonius Molon, aged 40, and Afrosa, placed by Apollonius.

EDCS-11901404.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Clodius Barbario lived 59 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Iulius Herennianus, his son, had this made for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-11901404.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius Barbario, set up by his son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901406.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cocceia Clarilla lived 80 years. She is buried here. Pompeius Communis placed this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11901406.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cocceia Clarilla, erected by her son Pompeius Communis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901407.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cornelia Agate, aged 3 years, is buried in this place. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901407.summary: A brief funerary epitaph for a young child, Cornelia Agate, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901409.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Oriculo Floricae Agatae, his most dutiful wife, for whom he lived 42 years and 1 month, placed this monument.
EDCS-11901409.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband for his deceased wife, detailing the length of their marriage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901410.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Cleopatra, aged 33 years. Her most dutiful husband, Herennius Priscus, placed her here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901410.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Cleopatra, erected by her husband Herennius Priscus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901411.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Chrysis, aged 23 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901411.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Chrysis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901413.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Lucceianus lived 35 years. His mother Iulia Vernacla placed him here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-11901413.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Lucceianus, set up by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901416.summary: A very brief funerary marker for Pompeianus, indicating his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901424.translation: Sacred to Apollo the Healer. Aemilia Aelia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11901424.summary: A dedication to the god Apollo, made by Aemilia Aelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901426.translation: For the health of our Gaius Atilius Cordus. Catulus, his slave, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901426.summary: A votive inscription to the god Salus (Health), dedicated by a slave named Catulus for his master, Gaius Atilius Cordus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901431.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabianus lived 26 years. Epolita his wife.
EDCS-11901431.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fabianus at age 26 and is set up by his wife Epolita.

EDCS-11901432.translation: Maximus [erected this] to Julia Maxima, daughter of Caius, his mother.
EDCS-11901432.summary: This funerary inscription records Maximus setting up a monument to his mother, Julia Maxima.

EDCS-11901435.translation: Decimus Iulius Navus, son of Decimus, of the Galeria tribe, and Corania, daughter of Titus, and Saturninus and Octavia [erected this] to Decimus Iulius, son of Decimus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-11901435.summary: This inscription records a family setting up a monument, likely funerary, for Decimus Iulius Navus.

EDCS-11901436.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius lived 65 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901436.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius at age 65 and includes a standard wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-11901437.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Laberia Caletyche lived 42 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901437.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Laberia Caletyche at age 42 and includes a standard wish for her afterlife.

EDCS-11901439.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. LVCV lived 60 years. [He/She] is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, my best husband.
EDCS-11901439.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of LVCV at age 60 and is set up by a spouse, possibly a wife, indicating a loving relationship.

EDCS-11901440.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lupus lived 30 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901440.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lupus at age 30 and includes a standard wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-11901442.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Optentia lived 21 years. Felicissimus her husband erected [this]. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-11901442.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Optentia at age 21 and was erected by her husband, Felicissimus.

EDCS-11901443.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Serviliana [lived] 33 years, a devoted mother.
EDCS-11901443.summary: This funerary inscription records Serviliana, described as a devoted mother, who lived 33 years.

EDCS-11901444.translation: Trebicia Badia lived 65 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901444.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Trebicia Badia at age 65 and includes a standard wish for her afterlife.

EDCS-11901446.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. My incomparable and most holy [daughter/wife/etc.], for thirteen years and eight months, Minoomnis, daughter of F.R., lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901446.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, likely erected by a parent, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901454.translation: Cornelia Avita, daughter of Lucius, aged twenty-eight years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Cornelius Rufinus, father, and Iulia Rufina, mother, had this made.
EDCS-11901454.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Avita, erected by her parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901455.translation: Quintus Iulius Macer to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-11901455.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Quintus Iulius Macer to his son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901457.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Philon, aged twenty-five years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Philon, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901458.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. [She/He] lived for fifteen years. AI, [his/her] mother, placed [this]. [He/She] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a person who died young, erected by their mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901460.translation: Titus's slave, Quintus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Quintus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901461.translation: Fulfilled her vow to Isis as commanded.
EDCS-11901461.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Isis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901463.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Anniola placed [this] for her most deserving patron Alcimioni. [He/She] lived [number] years. [He/She] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901463.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a patron, erected by his freedwoman or client, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901464.translation: Lucius Annius Fundanus for himself and his family lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901464.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Annius Fundanus and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901465.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Avitianus lived for thirteen years and fifteen days.
EDCS-11901465.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Avitianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901467.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Chariton, aged 26, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901467.summary: A funerary epitaph for Chariton, aged 26.

EDCS-11901468.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cocceia Victoria, aged...
EDCS-11901468.summary: A funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Cocceia Victoria.

EDCS-11901472.translation: Here lies Monia Levcina, freedwoman, aged 19. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901472.summary: A funerary epitaph for Monia Levcina, a freedwoman aged 19.

EDCS-11901478.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius.
EDCS-11901478.summary: A funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Tiberius.

EDCS-11901484.translation: Sacred to Saint Runeso Caecilius. Gaius Licinius Rinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901484.summary: A dedication to Saint Runeso Caecilius by Gaius Licinius Rinus.

EDCS-11901485.translation: For Salus, for the health of Acilius Rufinus. Canidius willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-11901485.summary: A vow fulfillment to the goddess Salus for the health of Acilius Rufinus by Canidius.

EDCS-11901486.translation: Agilis fulfilled her vow to Cornelia as she had promised.
EDCS-11901486.summary: A vow fulfillment by Agilis to Cornelia.

EDCS-11901488.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Norbana Emeritensis, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Fundanus had this made for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-11901488.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Norbana Emeritensis, aged 35, commissioned by her husband Fundanus.

EDCS-11901489.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Fundana, aged 21 years, 3 months, 1 day, is buried here.
EDCS-11901489.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Fundana, aged 21 years, 3 months, and 1 day.

EDCS-11901490.summary: A funerary epitaph, incomplete, identifying Marcus Appuleius as the son of Marcus.

EDCS-11901491.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Rufiniana, daughter of Titus, lived for 33 years. Cecia Prisca placed this for her most devoted daughter, buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901491.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Rufiniana, set up by her mother Cecia Prisca, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901492.translation: For Cornelia Maxuma, daughter of Lucius. Cornelius Valens, her husband, and Valeria Amoena, her sister, saw to its construction.
EDCS-11901492.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Maxuma, commissioned by her husband and sister, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901493.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Iulianus, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Marciana and Iulius Vernaculus, his heirs, saw to its construction.
EDCS-11901493.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Iulianus, erected by his heirs, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901494.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iunia Tyche, aged 54, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Calpurnius Marcianus constructed this for his most deserving wife.
EDCS-11901494.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Tyche, set up by her husband Calpurnius Marcianus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901495.translation: For Maecia, daughter of Lucius. Publius Maecius Pertica and Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Titus, constructed this for their daughter.
EDCS-11901495.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maecia, commissioned by her parents Publius Maecius Pertica and Iulia Maxuma, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901496.translation: Buried here is Mailo, son of Caenonis.
EDCS-11901496.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Mailo, son of Caenonis, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11901500.translation: Sacred to the Calanticenses. With a vow made, Talonti gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901500.summary: A votive inscription to the Calanticenses, a local deity or group, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901502.translation: For Quintus Iulius Decimus, son of Decimus. Galeria Cordo and Iunius Maricus, IIvir, [dedicate this].
EDCS-11901502.summary: An inscription possibly related to a dedication or funerary monument for Quintus Iulius Decimus, son of Decimus, mentioning Galeria Cordo and the magistrate Iunius Maricus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11901505.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Decembrina lived for 35 years. Sabinianus placed this. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901505.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decembrina, erected by Sabinianus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901506.translation: Erbeidus, son of Balai, is buried here.
EDCS-11901506.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Erbeidus, son of Balai, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11901507.translation: Fortunata is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901507.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, a simple tombstone wishing her a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-11901510.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Laberia Nigra, aged 61, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Gaius Aurelius Victorinus had this made for his aunt.
EDCS-11901510.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Laberia Nigra, with the monument erected by her nephew Gaius Aurelius Victorinus.

EDCS-11901511.translation: Marcus Menelaus lived for 50 years. Victorina placed this for her most deserving husband; he is laid in this tomb; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901511.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Marcus Menelaus, erected by his wife Victorina.

EDCS-11901514.translation: Sacred to Fontanus. Threptus, slave of Caius Appuleius Silo, willingly paid his vow for finding water.
EDCS-11901514.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Fontanus by Threptus, a slave, for the discovery of water.

EDCS-11901515.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Iulius Maximianus willingly placed this according to his own funds for the erection of the divinity of this monument.
EDCS-11901515.summary: This is a dedication to the Mother of the Gods, erected by Iulius Maximianus using his own personal funds.

EDCS-11901516.translation: Born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-11901516.summary: This is a short honorific inscription likely referring to a public official or soldier dedicated to the state.

EDCS-11901517.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Annia Eutichis, most dutiful mother, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901517.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Annia Eutichis, described as a dutiful mother.

EDCS-11901518.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Philetus, formerly aged 70, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901518.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Philetus, who was 70 years old.

EDCS-11901519.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Hegesistrate, aged 17; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901519.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Calpurnia Hegesistrate, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-11901520.translation: Lucius Caecilius, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, is placed here.
EDCS-11901520.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius, identifying him by his name, father, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-11901521.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Fabius Tryphon Argyrius, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Tryphon, his father, had this made for his most beloved son.
EDCS-11901521.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Fabius Tryphon Argyrius, erected by his father.

EDCS-11901522.translation: Iulia Avita, daughter of Avitus, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Turrania Maxsuma, her mother, and Iulius Maxsumus, her brother, had this made.
EDCS-11901522.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Avita, erected by her mother and brother.

EDCS-11901525.translation: Iulius Amoenus, son of Lucius, and Maila, daughter of Iulius Coutus, aged 40, lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Tancinus, son, had this set up.
EDCS-11901525.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Amoenus and Maila, erected by their son Tancinus.

EDCS-11901526.translation: Lucius Iulius Rufus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Colomba, his wife, had this made.
EDCS-11901526.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Rufus, erected by his wife.

EDCS-11901527.translation: Laberia Catulla, daughter of Gallus, aged 55, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901527.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laberia Catulla.

EDCS-11901529.translation: Lucius Licinius Catullus, aged 60, and his wife Stertinia Caesia, aged 50, lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Licinius Avitus, their son, had this set up in accordance with his father's will.
EDCS-11901529.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Licinius Catullus and his wife Stertinia Caesia, erected by their son according to the father's will.

EDCS-11901532.translation: Maximus Ocelaeci, aged 75, lies here. Marcus Iulius Gener, dutiful.
EDCS-11901532.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maximus Ocelaeci, with a concluding remark by Marcus Iulius Gener.

EDCS-11901533.translation: Maxumus, son of Severus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His father and mother had this made.
EDCS-11901533.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maxumus, erected by his parents.

EDCS-11901534.translation: Quintus Romanius Tuscus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 17. Baebia Boutia had this made for her son.
EDCS-11901534.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Romanius Tuscus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-11901536.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Maxumus, aged 80, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Arria Quintilla, his wife, and Avita, his daughter, had this made.
EDCS-11901536.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Maxumus, erected by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-11901540.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a 20-year-old boy.

EDCS-11901541.translation: Malgeinus took care to have this made.
EDCS-11901541.summary: This inscription records that Malgeinus commissioned the creation of a monument or object.

EDCS-11901544.translation: When Tiberius Caesar and Lucius Aelius Sejanus were consuls on the twelfth day before the Kalends of February, Quintus Stertinius Bassus, son of Quintus Stertinius, Quintus Stertinius Rufus, son of Quintus Stertinius, and Lucius Stertinius Rufinus, son of Quintus Stertinius, entered into a relationship of hospitality with Lucius Fulcinius Trio, legate of Tiberius Caesar, his children, and his descendants. Lucius Fulcinius Trio, legate of Tiberius Caesar, received Quintus Stertinius Bassus, son of Quintus Stertinius, Quintus Stertinius Rufus, son of Quintus Stertinius, and Lucius Stertinius Rufinus, son of Quintus Stertinius, their children, and their descendants, into his protection and patronage, and that of his children and descendants.
EDCS-11901544.summary: This inscription, dated to the consulship of Tiberius Caesar and Sejanus (AD 20), records a formal agreement of hospitality and patronage between the Stertinii family and the legate Lucius Fulcinius Trio.

EDCS-11901545.translation: Felicio Torritatus is worse than Cirdalus, having dismissed Felicio. Profitere, but you abandon your own affairs.
EDCS-11901545.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal message or a fragment of a legal dispute, possibly related to dismissals and accusations of abandonment, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901546.summary: This inscription lists the names of four wind gods, likely found on a public monument or a private dwelling.

EDCS-11901553.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hermes, a freedman of Publius, with an abbreviation possibly indicating a location or material.

EDCS-11901554.translation: Iulia Anus placed this willingly in spirit for Endovellicus, a legacy from her ancestors.
EDCS-11901554.summary: This inscription records that Iulia Anus willingly dedicated a monument in honor of Endovellicus, a family heirloom.

EDCS-11901555.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Endovellicus by Iulia Maxuma.

EDCS-11901556.translation: Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Publius, willingly fulfilled her vow to Endovellicus.
EDCS-11901556.summary: This inscription records Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Publius, fulfilling a vow to Endovellicus.

EDCS-11901557.translation: Iullia Modesta, daughter of Caius, willingly fulfilled her vow to Endovellicus.
EDCS-11901557.summary: This inscription records Iullia Modesta, daughter of Caius, fulfilling a vow to Endovellicus.

EDCS-11901558.translation: Gaius Julius Capito willingly set this up in honor of Endovellicus.
EDCS-11901558.summary: This inscription records a voluntary offering by Gaius Julius Capito to the deity Endovellicus.

EDCS-11901561.translation: Quintus Julius Pultarius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901561.summary: This inscription records Quintus Julius Pultarius fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901563.translation: Marcus Livius Severus willingly set this up in honor of Endovellicus, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-11901563.summary: This inscription records a voluntary offering by Marcus Livius Severus to Endovellicus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901564.translation: Petronia Albilla willingly made this sacred offering to the god Endovellicus according to her vow.
EDCS-11901564.summary: This inscription records Petronia Albilla's voluntary sacred offering to the god Endovellicus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901565.translation: Gaius S. C. made this small shrine in honor of the god Endovellicus, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-11901565.summary: This inscription records Gaius S. C. making a small shrine to the god Endovellicus as a votive offering.

EDCS-11901566.translation: S. Romula ENT made this sacred offering to Endovellicus.
EDCS-11901566.summary: This inscription records S. Romula ENT making a sacred offering to Endovellicus.

EDCS-11901567.translation: Lucius T M and T M willingly made this in accordance with a command from the infernal region.
EDCS-11901567.summary: This inscription records Lucius T M and T M making a voluntary offering, possibly related to a supernatural command.

EDCS-11901570.translation: Vitalis, son and slave of Messi Sympheron, willingly set this up.
EDCS-11901570.summary: This inscription records Vitalis, a freedman or possibly enslaved person, willingly setting up a monument.

EDCS-11901579.summary: This inscription records an individual named Anus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901594.translation: Quintus Em willingly fulfilled his vow to the holy goddess Burrulobrigensis.
EDCS-11901594.summary: This inscription records Quintus Em fulfilling a vow to the goddess Burrulobrigensis.

EDCS-11901596.translation: Marcus Coelius Celsus gladly to Mars.
EDCS-11901596.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating something to the god Mars by Marcus Coelius Celsus.

EDCS-11901597.translation: Rustrius gladly paid his vow to the goddess Proserpina.
EDCS-11901597.summary: This is a votive inscription by Rustrius to the goddess Proserpina.

EDCS-11901598.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Proserpina by Toncius.

EDCS-11901599.translation: Secundytius Victorinus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-11901599.summary: This is a votive inscription by Secundytius Victorinus.

EDCS-11901600.translation: Publius Anonius Silo, of the Quirina tribe, aged 61, is buried here; Aquilia, daughter of Camulus, arranged for this tomb to be made for herself and her husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901600.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Anonius Silo, arranged by his wife Aquilia, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11901601.translation: Quintus Attius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Numitoria Maxuma, daughter of Quintus, arranged for this tomb to be made.
EDCS-11901601.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Attius Rusticus, arranged by his wife Numitoria Maxuma, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11901602.translation: Blaesidiena Marcella, daughter of Gnaeus, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gnaeus Blaesidienus Marcellus and Valeria Tertulla arranged for this tomb to be made for their pious selves.
EDCS-11901602.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Blaesidiena Marcella, arranged by her father Gnaeus Blaesidienus Marcellus and sister Valeria Tertulla, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11901604.translation: Faleia Avita, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius T. Rufinus made and arranged for this tomb for his wife.
EDCS-11901604.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faleia Avita, arranged by her husband Lucius T. Rufinus, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11901607.translation: Cominia Maxsuma, wife of Gnaeus Marcius Nason, and her sons erected this monument.
EDCS-11901607.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Cominia Maxsuma and her sons for her husband Gnaeus Marcius Nason.

EDCS-11901608.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Marcius Tusculanus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His most devoted wife, Apronia Maxima, arranged for this to be made at her own expense.
EDCS-11901608.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Marcius Tusculanus, arranged by his wife Apronia Maxima, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11901609.translation: Maxsuma, daughter of Maxsumus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Modesta, daughter of Geminus, had this made.
EDCS-11901609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Maxsuma, who died at the age of 25, with her mother Modesta commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11901612.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Scribonia Maxima, daughter of Paternus, aged 15, and her mother made this for their most devoted daughter, most devotedly having it prepared.
EDCS-11901612.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication to the gods of the underworld by Scribonia Maxima and her mother for the deceased daughter/sister, aged 15.

EDCS-11901613.translation: Albanus, the son, had this set up for Sextus Socius Quartio and Catinia Maxsuma.
EDCS-11901613.summary: This inscription records that Albanus commissioned the erection of a monument for Sextus Socius Quartio and Catinia Maxsuma.

EDCS-11901615.translation: VS, son of Cai, and T. A. A. had this made for their most devoted son.
EDCS-11901615.summary: This funerary epitaph records the commissioning of a monument by parents for their devoted son, whose name is abbreviated.

EDCS-11901620.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the established town. Gaius Annius Valens gave this willingly.
EDCS-11901620.summary: This inscription records a voluntary dedication by Gaius Annius Valens to the Genius of the town.

EDCS-11901621.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Attia Maxima willingly fulfilled her vow with this inscription.
EDCS-11901621.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Attia Maxima to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-11901622.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Cateius Quietus.
EDCS-11901622.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Cateius Quietus.

EDCS-11901629.translation: RECIP, son of Avitus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901629.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by RECIP, son of Avitus.

EDCS-11901631.translation: Gaius Domitius Mustari, son of Mustarius, from Clunia, aged 33, lies here.
EDCS-11901631.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Domitius Mustari, aged 33, who was from Clunia.

EDCS-11901632.translation: From their own means, Lovesius, son of Anceitus, son of Ammaius.
EDCS-11901632.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or contribution made from personal funds by Lovesius, son of Anceitus, son of Ammaius.

EDCS-11901634.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Cilea, daughter of Cadarius.

EDCS-11901635.translation: Aranta, daughter of Viranus, had this made for Fusca, daughter of Dobiterius, aged 25, and for her daughter.
EDCS-11901635.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Aranta, daughter of Viranus, for Fusca, daughter of Dobiterius, and her daughter.

EDCS-11901637.translation: Severus, son of Avitus, aged 20, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you, father; he had this made.
EDCS-11901637.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severus, son of Avitus, erected by his father.

EDCS-11901638.translation: To the freedman of the Helviorum family, aged 3; he is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901638.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young freedman of the Helviorum family.

EDCS-11901639.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sontius, son of Severa, a child.

EDCS-11901640.translation: Tanginus, son of Lubaecus, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-11901640.summary: This inscription records Tanginus, son of Lubaecus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901641.translation: Aged 7; he is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you, father; he had this made.
EDCS-11901641.summary: This funerary epitaph for a 7-year-old was erected by his father.

EDCS-11901643.translation: Publius Carminius Macer, according to his vow.
EDCS-11901643.summary: This inscription records Publius Carminius Macer fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901644.translation: Celtius, son of Tongus, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter the Repulsor.
EDCS-11901644.summary: This is a votive inscription by Celtius, son of Tongus, to Jupiter Repulsor.

EDCS-11901645.summary: This inscription records Cirme fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11901649.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aricina Hypolitus, aged 3, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901649.summary: This is a tombstone for a child named Aricina Hypolitus, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-11901651.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fundanus [erected this] for his wife, Iulia Laeta, aged 21.
EDCS-11901651.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Fundanus to his wife, Iulia Laeta, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-11901652.translation: For Lucius Cassius Modestus, son of Lucius Cassius Flaccus, aged 12, lies here. Balbina MAI [erected this].
EDCS-11901652.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Cassius Modestus, son of Lucius Cassius Flaccus, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-11901654.translation: Gaius Julius Proculus, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His father took care to erect this.
EDCS-11901654.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Proculus, who died at the age of 35, erected by his father.

EDCS-11901656.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Montanianus, son of Cestus, lived for 20 years. His father erected this.
EDCS-11901656.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Montanianus' father to his son, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-11901657.summary: This is a dedication, likely to a deified emperor or a young man who died prematurely, from an unknown source.

EDCS-11901658.translation: [Dedicated to] Nicius, son of Gitius, aged I.
EDCS-11901658.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Nicius, son of Gitius, who died at the age of 1.

EDCS-11901664.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-11901664.summary: This is an inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius, detailing his titles and role as chief priest, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901665.translation: Imperator Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, in his third tribunician power, thrice consul, greatest German, greatest Dacian, greatest Sarmatian, Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, greatest German, greatest Dacian, imperator.
EDCS-11901665.summary: This inscription lists the titles and honors of Emperor Maximinus Thrax, including his victories and status as prince of youth, dating to his reign in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11901673.translation: To our lords, Flavius Julius Crispus Valerius Licinianus Licinius the Younger, and Flavius Claudius Constantine, most noble Caesars.
EDCS-11901673.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Crispus and Constantine the Younger, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-11901676.summary: This inscription is a formal titulature naming the Roman emperor Flavius Valerius Constans, likely part of a larger official inscription from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-11901677.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens, Augusti, in their first consulship.
EDCS-11901677.summary: This inscription records the consulship of the Roman emperors Valentinian and Valens, dating it to 365 CE.

EDCS-11901678.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Augustus
EDCS-11901678.summary: This inscription is a dedication or titulature to the Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius, probably from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13302459.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quinta Telci, freedwoman, had this monument prepared for her most devoted husband Frontonius, son of Laurus, aged 65.
EDCS-13302459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Frontonius, set up by his freedwoman wife Quinta Telci, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302461.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Proculinus, son of Proculus, had this prepared for himself and his most devoted wives Valeria and Amabilis, and for the nurse of my children.
EDCS-13302461.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Proculinus for himself, his wives Valeria and Amabilis, and a nurse, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302463.translation: Flavius, son of Albius, willingly set up his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-13302463.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302654.translation: Gaevus, son of Caturon, of Aviobrigensis, lies here, aged 28. Niger, his brother, had this prepared by will. ARIIT Raevo, Olisiponensis Exonatus.
EDCS-13302654.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaevus, with his brother Niger acting as executor of the will, and mentions associated individuals, likely from Roman Gaul.

EDCS-13302666.translation: To Lucius Cosconius Reburo, son of Lucius, a soldier of the 15th legion, aged 23.
EDCS-13302666.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lucius Cosconius Reburo, a soldier in the 15th legion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13302720.translation: Cassia Mermandice willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-13302720.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cassia Mermandice, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13400018.translation: Iulia Methe, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Manius Acilius Hymnus Augustalis prepared this for his wife.
EDCS-13400018.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iulia Methe, prepared by her husband Manius Acilius Hymnus Augustalis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13400019.translation: Marcus Iunius, son of Marcus, and Iunia Prima, freedwoman of Marcus, wife of Iunius, and Iunius Faustus, son of Iunius, and Euche, freedwoman of Lucius, wife of Iunius, wife of Lucius, had this made by will.
EDCS-13400019.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by will by Iunia Prima and others for Marcus Iunius and his family.

EDCS-13400020.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Iulius Rufinus, brother, arranged for this to be set up from his own money.
EDCS-13400020.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Quintus Iulius Rufinus for his brother, Lupi, who was from Aemina.

EDCS-13400022.translation: Paula Iulia Anta, 45 years old, is buried here. Caius Licinius Sabinus, a devoted husband, for his wife.
EDCS-13400022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Paula Iulia Anta, erected by her devoted husband Caius Licinius Sabinus.

EDCS-13400023.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Balbus, emperor, patron of the colony Norba Caesarina.
EDCS-13400023.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Cornelius Balbus, identified as emperor and patron of the colony Norba Caesarina.

EDCS-13400024.translation: Publius Antonius Protis gladly dedicated this to Hercules.
EDCS-13400024.summary: This is a votive offering dedicated to Hercules by Publius Antonius Protis.

EDCS-13400142.translation: Marcus Licinius Domesticus is buried here.
EDCS-13400142.summary: This is a simple tombstone recording the burial of Marcus Licinius Domesticus.

EDCS-13400144.translation: Aemilia Boutia, daughter of Caius, is buried here.
EDCS-13400144.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Aemilia Boutia.

EDCS-13400146.translation: Sextus Luccius Rufus, son of Sextus and Edonis, from the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-13400146.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Sextus Luccius Rufus.

EDCS-13400147.translation: Caudicus gladly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-13400147.summary: This is a record of a vow paid by Caudicus to Jupiter.

EDCS-13400148.translation: Gellius Up for his most dutiful mother, Grata, 37 years old.
EDCS-13400148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grata, dedicated by her son Gaius Iulius Up.

EDCS-13400232.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, pontifex, consul, imperator, prince of youth, by the community of the Igaeditani.
EDCS-13400232.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, by the community of the Igaeditani, likely dating to his lifetime.

EDCS-13400234.translation: To our Constantius Flavius Valerius, consul Caesar.
EDCS-13400234.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantius, a consul Caesar, likely dating to the Tetrarchic period.

EDCS-13400254.translation: Quatius Apianis, son of Dandus Tatibe, to Voctotolus.
EDCS-13400254.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Quatius Apianis to a deity or person named Voctotolus, possibly of local significance.

EDCS-13400260.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Juno, with a possibly local epithet Caburavis.

EDCS-13400261.translation: Bassus, son of Tanginus, willingly paid his vow to Mars.
EDCS-13400261.summary: This inscription records Bassus, son of Tanginus, willingly fulfilling a vow to the god Mars.

EDCS-13400262.translation: Rectus, son of Rufus, paid his vow to Revelanganus Daegui.
EDCS-13400262.summary: This inscription records Rectus, son of Rufus, fulfilling a vow to a deity or person named Revelanganus Daegui.

EDCS-13400263.translation: Lucanus, son of Adius, for the distinguished victim.
EDCS-13400263.summary: This inscription likely records Lucanus, son of Adius, associated with the selection of a sacrificial victim.

EDCS-13400265.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta, in honor of her daughter Rufina, daughter of Reburrinus; Severa, mother, for her daughter.
EDCS-13400265.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Venus Augusta, made by Severa for her daughter Rufina, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13400266.translation: …nius, son of Arcius and Vegetus, from Igaeda, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-13400266.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by a man from Igaeda, son of Arcius and Vegetus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-13400267.translation: To Lucius Marcius Fuscus, son of Quirinus, Avitus, prefect of the corps of engineers, prefect of the I Cohort of Suran Archers, tribune of soldiers of Legio X Fretensis, prefect of the I Ala of Roman citizen cavalry, honored with gifts; Marcius Maternus, an equestrian of the same ala, for his excellent prefect because of his merit.
EDCS-13400267.summary: This is a dedication by Marcius Maternus to his superior officer Lucius Marcius Fuscus, detailing his extensive military career and achievements, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13400269.translation: Amoena, daughter of Maelo, had this made according to the will of Albino, son of Tanginus, a townsman of Lancius.
EDCS-13400269.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument commissioned by Amoena for Albino, a townsman of Lancius.

EDCS-13400270.translation: Aternus, son of Aca, of Clunia, aged 20, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-13400270.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Aternus, a 20-year-old man from Clunia.

EDCS-13400272.translation: Reburrus, son of Malgeinus, willingly placed this votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, according to his vow.
EDCS-13400272.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Reburrus.

EDCS-13500173.translation: Crissus, son of Talaburus, of Aebosocus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Trebaruna.
EDCS-13500173.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Trebaruna by Crissus.

EDCS-13600137.translation: From the workshop of Asellus: may you use [it] happily, in God.
EDCS-13600137.summary: This inscription is a maker's mark or blessing from the workshop of Asellus.

EDCS-13600282.translation: Marcus Fulvius Fabianus willingly fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-13600282.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars by Marcus Fulvius Fabianus.

EDCS-13600283.translation: C. Putia, freedwoman of Caius, willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-13600283.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter by C. Putia.

EDCS-13700300.translation: Caius Sempronius Avitus willingly fulfilled his vow to Erbina.
EDCS-13700300.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Erbina by Caius Sempronius Avitus.

EDCS-13700302.translation: Toncus' son Saturninus willingly placed this dedication to Bellona.
EDCS-13700302.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication to Bellona by Saturninus.

EDCS-13700303.translation: Boutia, daughter of Latro, of Histinium, lies here.
EDCS-13700303.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Boutia, daughter of Latro, from Histinium.

EDCS-13700417.translation: Galeria Festianus gladly paid his vow to the divine Sigerius Stillifer with a willing spirit.
EDCS-13700417.summary: This is a votive inscription from Galeria Festianus to the divine Sigerius Stillifer, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700418.translation: Camalus, son of Saelgi, gladly paid his vow to Togae.
EDCS-13700418.summary: This is a votive inscription by Camalus to Togae, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800100.translation: To the Genius of Mars Depenorus.
EDCS-13800100.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of Mars Depenorus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800102.translation: Caius Caielianus Modestus gave this gift to the castellans of Araocelum.
EDCS-13800102.summary: This inscription records a gift from Caius Caielianus Modestus to the castellans of Araocelum, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800104.translation: Lucius Latrius Blaesus gladly paid his vow to the Bandae Velugo and Toiraeco.
EDCS-13800104.summary: This votive inscription is from Lucius Latrius Blaesus to the deities Bandae Velugo and Toiraeco, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800105.translation: Arcius Epeici Bracarus made this for the willing god Tueraeus.
EDCS-13800105.summary: This is a dedication made by Arcius Epeici Bracarus for the god Tueraeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900138.translation: Marcus Iulius Tyrannus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis, dedicated this sacred offering to Apollo.
EDCS-13900138.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Apollo by Marcus Iulius Tyrannus, a freedman and Augustalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900139.translation: To Aturrus, 22 years old, son of Biseus; Iunia Tusca, daughter of Titus, to her brother; Tusca, mother of Licia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-13900139.summary: This inscription appears to be a complex epitaph or dedication possibly commemorating Aturrus and possibly mentioning Iunia Tusca and Tusca as relatives, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900405.translation: Clodia Urbana, 40 years old, for half a month, made this for her incomparable and most loving husband, Sempronianus. You who read this from across the way, eat, drink, play, live!
EDCS-13900405.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Urbana, set up by her husband Sempronianus, with a cheerful exhortation to the living, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14700010.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Bocchus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Salaciensis, flamen of the province of Lusitania, prefect of engineers, tribune of the soldiers of the Augustan Legion VIII, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-14700010.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Lucius Cornelius Bocchus, detailing his various official positions including flamen of Lusitania and tribune, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14700012.translation: To Galecho, Quintus Iulius Ge to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-14700012.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Iulius Ge to his son, Galecho.

EDCS-14700013.translation: The burial of Tessodis on the sixteenth day before the Kalends of January in the year.
EDCS-14700013.summary: This inscription records the burial date of Tessodis.

EDCS-14700015.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Avita, mother, had this made for her most dutiful daughter Maxima, daughter of Naevidia, aged 34.
EDCS-14700015.summary: This tombstone was erected by Avita for her daughter Maxima, who died at age 34.

EDCS-14700017.translation: To the god Silvanus, Caius, son of S., paid his vow.
EDCS-14700017.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-14700020.translation: Quintus Vibius Fuscus, the piper, is buried here.
EDCS-14700020.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Quintus Vibius Fuscus, a piper.

EDCS-14700023.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Trebecia Arataea had this made for her most dutiful father Lucio Trebecio Corinto, aged 75.
EDCS-14700023.summary: This tombstone was erected by Trebecia Arataea for her father Lucio Trebecio Corinto.

EDCS-14700030.translation: Catuenus, son of Avelus, paid his vow to Bandi Vortecius willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-14700030.summary: This is a votive inscription by Catuenus to Bandi Vortecius.

EDCS-14700031.translation: Pintilisina, daughter of Arconicum, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-14700031.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Pintilisina, aged 60, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-14700299.translation: Equaesus, son of Pentavius, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-14700299.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Equaesus, aged 30, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-14700363.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-14700363.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Galerius Maximianus.

EDCS-15000041.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caburnianus, curator of the public affairs of the Coilarnenses, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15000041.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caburnianus, a local official, to Jupiter.

EDCS-15000044.translation: To Jupiter Assaeco, Marcus Caecilius Caeno gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15000044.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Caecilius Caeno to Jupiter Assaeco.

EDCS-15000045.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater, Gaius R T gladly fulfilled his vow to his grandfather.
EDCS-15000045.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius R T to Liber Pater, dedicated to his grandfather.

EDCS-15000046.summary: This is a simple dedication to Jupiter.

EDCS-15000059.translation: Sacred to the Cohortal Mercury, guardian.
EDCS-15000059.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury as a guardian deity.

EDCS-15000060.translation: Firmus, a freedman of Augustus, the steward of the twentieth tax on inheritances, gladly made this for himself.
EDCS-15000060.summary: This is a dedication by Firmus, an imperial freedman and steward, likely related to his service or position.

EDCS-15300354.translation: To Jupiter Solutorius, Camalus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15300354.summary: This is a votive inscription by Camalus to Jupiter Solutorius.

EDCS-15300358.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest Augustus, Lucius Valerius Vegetinus [made this] for himself.
EDCS-15300358.summary: This is a self-dedicated inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest Augustus by Lucius Valerius Vegetinus.

EDCS-15300359.translation: To our lord Magnentius, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-15300359.summary: This is an inscription honoring the Caesar Magnentius, likely a dedication or dedication of a building.

EDCS-15300361.translation: Melineum for dryness, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus; Psoricum for itching, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus; Stactum for clarity, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus; Nardinum for harshness, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus; Crocodes for roughness, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus; Thurinum for pustules, from Caius Caecilius Fortunatus.
EDCS-15300361.summary: This inscription lists various medicinal preparations or unguents for different ailments, all attributed to Caius Caecilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-15700063.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Vassedo to Vienna.

EDCS-15700064.translation: Arentia, daughter of Sunua, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15700064.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Arentia, daughter of Sunua.

EDCS-15700065.translation: Montanus set this up for Arentia and Arentius, son of Tangi.
EDCS-15700065.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Montanus to Arentia and Arentius.

EDCS-15700066.translation: Albinus, son of Moculus, willingly erected this altar for Arentius Croniensis, in accordance with a vow made by his grandfather Pisirius Boetelus.
EDCS-15700066.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar by Albinus for Arentius Croniensis, fulfilling a vow of his grandfather.

EDCS-16000017.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Maximino, twice a magistrate of the Eborobrittians, aged 52.
EDCS-16000017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maximino, who served as a magistrate of the Eborobrittians and died at the age of 52.

EDCS-16000517.translation: Voconus, son of Voconus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Trebaruna.
EDCS-16000517.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Voconus, son of Voconus, to the deity Trebaruna.

EDCS-16000518.translation: Valerius, alive, made this for Tridius, son of Modestus, and Seurra Transminiana, daughter of Modestus, aged 20, from the century of Serans.
EDCS-16000518.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Valerius for Tridius and Seurra Transminiana, both aged 20 and belonging to the century of Serans.

EDCS-16200958.translation: Flaviana, servant of God, rested in peace on the third day before the Kalends of May in the year 570.
EDCS-16200958.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Flaviana, a servant of God, on the third day before the Kalends of May in the year 570.

EDCS-16201029.translation: Iulia Rufina willingly sets this up for Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.
EDCS-16201029.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Iulia Rufina to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.

EDCS-16201030.translation: Tongeta, daughter of Aranton, mother, [set this up for] her son Titus, aged 25, and her daughter Camira, daughter of Titus, aged 16.
EDCS-16201030.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Tongeta, mother, for her son Titus and daughter Camira.

EDCS-16201222.translation: Dobiteina, daughter of Auxon, aged 15. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-16201222.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 15-year-old girl named Dobiteina.

EDCS-16201223.translation: Cloutius, son of Dobiter, aged 65. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-16201223.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 65-year-old man named Cloutius.

EDCS-16201224.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Tangino, son of Tritus.

EDCS-16201623.translation: Lucius K Syriaches willingly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-16201623.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Lucius K Syriaches to the Nymphs.

EDCS-16201625.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered One. Caius Curius Avitus, with his father Accius Hedychrus, Demetrios made this.
EDCS-16201625.summary: A dedication to an Unconquered God, possibly Mithras, made by Caius Curius Avitus, with his father, and executed by Demetrios.

EDCS-16201693.translation: Tiberius Fonteius Esoonei, son of Qasocus, places this altar to Jupiter at his own expense.
EDCS-16201693.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Tiberius Fonteius Esoonei, son of Qasocus.

EDCS-16201694.translation: Quintus Circinius, son of P V C S R I, fulfilled his vow in the 204th year of the colony.
EDCS-16201694.summary: A record of a vow fulfilled by Quintus Circinius in the 204th year of the colony, likely relating to a local calendar.

EDCS-16202014.translation: To Munidus, to Eberobrigae, daughter of Toudopalandaigae, Ammaia Boutea [dedicated this].
EDCS-16202014.summary: A dedication mentioning Munidus and Eberobrigae, daughter of Toudopalandaigae, by Ammaia Boutea, possibly funerary or votive.

EDCS-16300308.translation: Tongius, son of Boutus, fulfilled his vow to Vesta.
EDCS-16300308.summary: A record of a vow fulfilled by Tongius, son of Boutus, to the goddess Vesta.

EDCS-16300565.translation: Severus willingly fulfilled his vow to the Eternal God.
EDCS-16300565.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Severus to an Eternal God, possibly associated with imperial cult or mystery religions.

EDCS-16400068.translation: Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, consul for the third time, holder of tribunician power for the third time.
EDCS-16400068.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Marcus Agrippa, likely from a public monument or building, dating to around 27-25 BC.

EDCS-16400432.summary: This is a very short inscription, possibly a name or a derogatory term, with no further context provided.

EDCS-16400433.translation: Vetaliana, servant of God, lived 40 years, rested in peace on the ninth day before the Kalends of August, in the year 560 of the era.
EDCS-16400433.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vetaliana, a Christian, dating to 522 AD.

EDCS-16500131.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-16500131.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, likely from a temple or shrine.

EDCS-16700022.translation: Lucius P. S. willingly placed this to the Holy Goddess Lady Bellona.
EDCS-16700022.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Bellona by Lucius P. S., possibly marking a statue or altar.

EDCS-16800203.translation: Caturo Saluti of Bidienus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16800203.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caturo Saluti of Bidienus, likely placed at a shrine.

EDCS-16900012.translation: Lubaecus, son of Tanginus, of Apana, daughter of Leuri, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Tanginus, his father, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-16900012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lubaecus, with his father arranging the monument, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-16900157.translation: Sacred to Juno. Claudius Daphnus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16900157.summary: This is a votive inscription to Juno by Claudius Daphnus, likely dedicated at a temple or shrine.

EDCS-16900203.translation: Publius Caecilius Rufinus and Pompeia Chrysis made this for their son Publius Caecilius Rufinianus, aged 2 years and 8 months.
EDCS-16900203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Publius Caecilius Rufinianus, made by his parents.

EDCS-16900204.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Here lies Ursia Verana for her well-deserving husband Afranius Apollonius, aged 60 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-16900204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Afranius Apollonius, erected by his wife Ursia Verana.

EDCS-16900208.translation: Seranus dedicated this altar to Fontanus from his own funds.
EDCS-16900208.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the god Fontanus by Seranus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900210.translation: Gaius Aemilius Crispus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Termes, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Aemilius Grypus and Aemilia Urbica, his freedmen, saw to its construction at their own expense.
EDCS-16900210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Aemilius Crispus, set up by his freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900222.translation: Maxsomma, servant of God, lived 291 years, rested in peace on the 4th day of May in the year 603 (of the era). Dulcisus, servant of God, lived 93 years, rested in peace the day before the Kalends of August in the year 610 (of the era). Epundius.
EDCS-16900222.summary: This inscription records the ages and death dates of two individuals, Maxsomma and Dulcisus, who are identified as servants of God, likely in a Christian context, with dates referenced to a local era.

EDCS-16900291.translation: Norbanus, son of Publius Seneca, aged 35, is here.
EDCS-16900291.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Norbanus, son of Publius Seneca, indicating his age and that he is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900292.translation: Rufina, daughter of Rufus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Norbanus Celer made this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-16900292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufina, erected by her husband Lucius Norbanus Celer, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900295.translation: Marco Traio Samaci Dualio, aged 15, his bones [returned] to his mother. Rufinus Rufi Cadarus.
EDCS-16900295.summary: This inscription likely records the burial of Marco Traio Samaci Dualio, aged 15, with a reference to his mother, possibly erected by Rufinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900296.translation: Iulia Sulpicia, daughter of Cassius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. H FAB Gaius Gracus for his wife, 8 years.
EDCS-16900296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Sulpicia, erected by Gaius Gracus possibly as her husband, mentioning an unclear '8' possibly referring to years of marriage or age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100152.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100152.summary: This is a generic funerary epitaph for an individual aged 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100154.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Segia, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tanginus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100154.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Segia aged 40 and Tanginus aged 60, dating to the Roman Imperial period.
