EDCS-18100155.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Granus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Granus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100156.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Attia Aira, aged 55, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Esca Escivis lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100156.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of two women, Attia Aira and Esca Escivis, with standard epitaphs invoking peace for the deceased.

EDCS-18100336.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, identifying the deceased as Ederatus Cumelus.

EDCS-18100338.translation: Kaeso, son of Aulus, made the mosaic room.
EDCS-18100338.summary: This inscription records a building dedication, specifying that Kaeso, son of Aulus, commissioned a room with a mosaic floor.

EDCS-18100339.translation: Alfia Amoena, daughter of Lucius, lies here.
EDCS-18100339.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Alfia Amoena, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-18100385.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Numerianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-18100385.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Numerian, likely from a public monument or a place honoring the emperor.

EDCS-18100386.translation: Cinius, his son, arranged for the construction for Deibuerana, aged 50, and Angeta, aged 20.
EDCS-18100386.summary: This funerary inscription records that Cinius arranged the burial or monument for his daughters Deibuerana and Angeta.

EDCS-18100387.translation: Sara, daughter of Arreinius, erected this. Tanginus, son of Celtus.
EDCS-18100387.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Sara, daughter of Arreinius, with Tanginus, son of Celtus, possibly as a collaborator or witness.

EDCS-18100388.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the titles of an emperor, likely Emperor Domitian, whose full name was Titus Flavius Domitianus, and possibly a dedication or statement associated with him.

EDCS-18100390.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record a measurement or quantity (XXV, i.e., 25) and possibly a personal name or place (Tongeto Aranto).

EDCS-18100391.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph, where RVTA might be a name and 'F F' could stand for 'fecit fecerunt' (made) or 'filia filius' (daughter son).

EDCS-18100393.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ovina, daughter of Rufus, aged 50.
EDCS-18100393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ovina, daughter of Rufus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-18100395.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. His son Caius, with the care of Marcus, had this made for Gaius, son of Caius Rufus, aged 55. May the earth rest lightly upon you, Gaius.
EDCS-18100395.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication of a tomb by a son for his father, Gaius, son of Caius Rufus, aged 55.

EDCS-18100397.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Serenis by Aurelius.

EDCS-18100398.translation: To the Bandae Ocelenses. Celtius, son of Ulpinius, paid his vow to Camalus.
EDCS-18100398.summary: This inscription records Celtius, son of Ulpinius, fulfilling a vow to the deity Camalus for the Bandae Ocelenses.

EDCS-18100400.translation: To the Bandae Alabaraicae Sulenses. Avitus, of the cohort of Tiberius Claudius Modestus, paid his vow with a willing heart.
EDCS-18100400.summary: This inscription records Avitus, a soldier in the cohort of Tiberius Claudius Modestus, fulfilling a vow to the Bandae Alabaraicae Sulenses.

EDCS-18100401.translation: Amoena, daughter of Malgeinus, aged 20, is buried here. Malgeinus Bati, Caburius Magio, Acilis, and Trevoatus Allucquai had this made. Also Cintumunis, sister, aged 11, is buried here.
EDCS-18100401.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Amoena, aged 20, and her sister Cintumunis, aged 11, for whom relatives had the monument made.

EDCS-18100402.translation: M. Aelius, son of Cadius, Caius [dedicated this]...
EDCS-18100402.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication by M. Aelius, son of Cadius, possibly related to a water channel (aquaeductus).

EDCS-18100403.translation: Boundary of the Irucoutior water channel. Here is the boundary of the Searea water channel...
EDCS-18100403.summary: This inscription marks the boundary of two water channels, the Irucoutior and the Searea.

EDCS-18100406.translation: [His son] Max eina had this made for [his] most dutiful son...
EDCS-18100406.summary: This fragmentary inscription records that Max eina had a monument made for his son, indicating a familial relationship and possibly a funerary context.

EDCS-18100409.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible...
EDCS-18100409.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-18100410.translation: To Caesar Augustus, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-18100410.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Augustus, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-19100693.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apronia Rufina had this made for Sempronius Campanus of the Aravi, aged 40.
EDCS-19100693.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Apronia Rufina for her deceased husband, Sempronius Campanus, who was 40 years old.

EDCS-19100701.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, possibly identifying an individual named Nosndalus.

EDCS-19100702.translation: Claudian, son of Poire, Seke III.
EDCS-19100702.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription with personal names and possibly a numerical designation.

EDCS-19100714.translation: Boutius, a stonemason, had this made for his aged 15-year-old [son/daughter].
EDCS-19100714.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a stonemason for his young child.

EDCS-19100715.translation: Cuntirus, son of Angeitus, Madureus, had this made for his dearest brother.
EDCS-19100715.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Cuntirus for his brother.

EDCS-19100717.translation: To Trio, son of Tagani, from Esia Gracilis.
EDCS-19100717.summary: This is a dedication, possibly funerary, from Esia Gracilis to Trio.

EDCS-19100719.translation: To bonus, aged 100, a veteran from the optio of the Caurienses.
EDCS-19100719.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a 100-year-old veteran who served as an optio.

EDCS-19100728.translation: Light earth [to mark the grave]. Pinarea N Tanginus and N Mailo had this made according to [his/her] will.
EDCS-19100728.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that the burial was arranged in accordance with a will.

EDCS-19100732.translation: Tongius, son of Virotus, aged 80, a soldier, an equestrian, of the first cavalry wing [made this].
EDCS-19100732.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tongius, an 80-year-old soldier of the first cavalry wing, with his son Catuenus also mentioned.

EDCS-19100740.translation: Marcus Iunius, son of Marcus, rests here.
EDCS-19100740.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-19100741.translation: Marcus Iunius Veto, son of Marcus, rests here.
EDCS-19100741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Veto, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-19100946.translation: Ashes of Quintus, son of Quintus, Quintus, son of Quintus, Quintus, son of Quintus, Quintus, son of Quintus.
EDCS-19100946.summary: This inscription records the ashes of multiple individuals named Quintus, son of Quintus, suggesting a family burial.

EDCS-19100947.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julia Iuliana, a mother, arranged for this to be made for her most dutiful son, Marcus Iulius Avitus, aged 28.
EDCS-19100947.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother to her deceased son, Marcus Iulius Avitus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19100951.translation: Of Caius Cornelius Celtus, son of Quintus; of Numerius of Nerva; of Lucius Lucretius Celtus.
EDCS-19100951.summary: This inscription lists the names of several individuals, possibly related to property ownership or inheritance, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19200019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucretius Callaecus and Lucretia Maura arranged for this to be made for their most dutiful son, Lucretius Callaecio, slave, aged 23.
EDCS-19200019.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents for their deceased son, Lucretius Callaecio, who was a slave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19200021.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Suira lived 28 years. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-19200021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Suira, recording her age at death and a common wish for the afterlife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20000225.translation: Lucius Pomponius Celer, aged [...], rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Pomponia Celerina arranged for this to be made for her most loving brother.
EDCS-20000225.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where a sister, Pomponia Celerina, arranged for the burial of her brother, Lucius Pomponius Celer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301625.translation: Of the slaves of Oppius Optimi.
EDCS-20301625.summary: This inscription likely indicates the slaves belonging to a person named Oppius Optimi, possibly related to property or a tomb, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301756.translation: To Aloiosae, while alive, in ERMI.
EDCS-20301756.summary: This inscription is difficult to interpret due to its brevity and the unknown term 'ERMI', but it appears to be a dedication or marker related to a person named Aloiosae, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301758.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop belonging to Valerius Paternus.

EDCS-20301769.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple ownership mark or name, possibly for Atticus, son of Nummius Festus.

EDCS-20400145.translation: Malequius arranged for this to be made for Laenius, aged 30.
EDCS-20400145.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Malequius commissioned a monument for Laenius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-20400147.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Cicano at the age of 3.

EDCS-20400149.translation: To the Lady Turibrigensis Ataecina, Caesius Crescens willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400149.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a monument to the goddess Turibrigensis Ataecina by Caesius Crescens.

EDCS-20400150.translation: To the holy Goddess Lady Turibrigensis Ataecina, Lucius Norbanus Severus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400150.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a monument to the holy goddess Turibrigensis Ataecina by Lucius Norbanus Severus.

EDCS-20400151.translation: To the Lady Turibrigensis Ataecina the Holy, Lucius Pontius Severinus, for his father, a vow.
EDCS-20400151.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Pontius Severinus for his father, dedicating something to the Lady Turibrigensis Ataecina.

EDCS-20400152.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Turibrigensis Ataecina, though the text is partially garbled.

EDCS-20400153.translation: To the holy Goddess Lady Turibrigensis Ataecina, Annius Severus, for his son, a vow.
EDCS-20400153.summary: This is a votive inscription by Annius Severus for his son, dedicating something to the holy goddess Turibrigensis Ataecina.

EDCS-20400154.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Turibrigensis Ataecina by Secundus.

EDCS-20400158.translation: To the Holy Goddess, Lady Ataecina of Turibriga, Iulia Severa willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-20400158.summary: A vow fulfilled by Iulia Severa to the goddess Ataecina of Turibriga.

EDCS-20400159.translation: To the Goddess, Lady Ataecina of Turibriga, Flavia Patricia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-20400159.summary: A vow fulfilled by Flavia Patricia to the goddess Ataecina of Turibriga.

EDCS-20400161.translation: To the Lady, Holy Goddess Ataecina of Turibriga, Caius C. Severus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400161.summary: A vow fulfilled by Caius C. Severus to the goddess Ataecina of Turibriga.

EDCS-20400167.translation: Cilis, aged 60, and Boetela, daughter of Cilus, aged 51, are laid here; may the earth be light upon you. Flavus, a cavalryman, had this made for his father and mother.
EDCS-20400167.summary: A tombstone for a father and mother, commissioned by their son Flavus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400168.translation: Cilea, daughter of Flavia, aged 30, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you. Flavus had this made for his mother in accordance with her will.
EDCS-20400168.summary: A tombstone for Cilea, commissioned by her mother Flavus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400174.translation: Marcus Norbanus Vestinus willingly offered this to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-20400174.summary: An offering made by Marcus Norbanus Vestinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400176.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camalus, son of Tancinus. (Aged) V.
EDCS-20400176.summary: A dedication to the departed spirits, possibly a tombstone fragment, mentioning Camalus.

EDCS-20400177.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sineros, freedman of Vacaena, is laid here. Vacaena, daughter of Aluquius, his patroness, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-20400177.summary: A tombstone for a freedman Sineros, commissioned by his patroness Vacaena.

EDCS-20400178.translation: The villagers of Arcobriga set up this altar to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, willingly.
EDCS-20400178.summary: An altar dedicated by the villagers of Arcobriga to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-20400181.translation: Caucus set this up in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-20400181.summary: An object or dedication set up by Caucus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-20400185.translation: Pisti, the slave woman, took care to have this made.
EDCS-20400185.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a slave named Pisti.

EDCS-20400186.translation: Belicina, freedwoman of Gaia, is laid here.
EDCS-20400186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Belicina, a freedwoman.

EDCS-20400188.translation: Camira, daughter of Alluquus, is laid here.
EDCS-20400188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Camira, daughter of Alluquus.

EDCS-20400189.translation: Gaius P. N., aged 80 years, made this, his heir.
EDCS-20400189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius P. N., made by his heir.

EDCS-20400190.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gavios Priscus, aged 40 years. Ius X.
EDCS-20400190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gavios Priscus, who died at age 40.

EDCS-20400192.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sacra made this. Mero Pemai Svenius, son of Iusmin, C. T.
EDCS-20400192.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sacra, likely for Mero Pemai Svenius.

EDCS-20400193.translation: To Julius, son of Marcius. Caesius, son of Marcius. Superstes, son of Marcius. Capito.
EDCS-20400193.summary: This inscription lists the names of three individuals, possibly related or members of the same family.

EDCS-20400194.translation: Arruntia Avita, aged 50 years, is laid here. May the earth be light on you. Tvo Va M. is laid here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-20400194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arruntia Avita, with a wish for light earth and a second, partially preserved, name.

EDCS-20400322.translation: Anceitus, son of Tancinus, is laid here, aged 50 years.
EDCS-20400322.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anceitus, son of Tancinus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-20400441.translation: Of the Apidii, sons of Lucilius, aged...
EDCS-20400441.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription referring to the sons of Lucilius from the Apidii family.

EDCS-20400487.translation: Gaius Laberius Meducenus is buried here.
EDCS-20400487.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Laberius Meducenus.

EDCS-20400491.translation: Turanus erected a statue to his father, Langarus, son of Lucius Betacus.
EDCS-20400491.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue by Turanus to his father, Langarus, son of Lucius Betacus.

EDCS-20400501.translation: Silius, son of Avitus, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-20400501.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Silius, son of Avitus.

EDCS-20400502.translation: Andercius, son of Allucius, of Erbinia, willingly paid his vow to Iaedis Cantibidonis.
EDCS-20400502.summary: This is a votive inscription by Andercius, son of Allucius, of Erbinia, to Iaedis Cantibidonis.

EDCS-20400503.translation: Capiio, son of Pisirus, of Erbinia, willingly paid his vow to Iaedis Cantibidonis.
EDCS-20400503.summary: This is a votive inscription by Capiio, son of Pisirus, of Erbinia, to Iaedis Cantibidonis.

EDCS-20400504.summary: This is a dedication to Bandus, son of Vorteacius.

EDCS-20400507.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Tacitus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul.
EDCS-20400507.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Tacitus, likely from his reign (275-276 AD).

EDCS-20400509.translation: Caturo Aereini occupied the temple of Jesus the Lord, a space twenty-five feet wide, the day before the Kalends of June, with TH OLO VIA as consuls.
EDCS-20400509.summary: This inscription records the occupation of a temple space by Caturo Aereini, with a date indicated by the consuls.

EDCS-20400517.translation: Lucius Manlius Almus, son of Decimus, of the Aemilian tribe, from Peinticis; Gaius Plotus; Gaius Tureius.
EDCS-20400517.summary: This inscription lists several names, possibly associated with a legal or administrative record or a monument.

EDCS-20400528.translation: Farewell, Evenus and Antiochians, to your sweetest child Tatianus, who lived for one year and 20 days, in memory. Farewell.
EDCS-20400528.summary: This is a Greek funerary epigram for Tatianus, a child, dedicated by his father Evenus and the people of Antioch.

EDCS-20401567.translation: For the health of their master Marcus Iulius Claudius, they set this up.
EDCS-20401567.summary: This is a dedication by freedmen for the well-being of their patron.

EDCS-20401569.summary: This inscription records a boundary marker, possibly related to land ownership or a military post.

EDCS-20401571.translation: Malgeinus and Bovi's sons erected this.
EDCS-20401571.summary: This is a dedication or memorial erected by the sons of Malgeinus and Bovi.

EDCS-20401574.translation: Caesiana, slave of Norbanus, wife of Publius Maternus, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401574.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Caesiana, recording her age and family connections.

EDCS-20401575.translation: Iulia Rufa, daughter of Taurus, aged 3, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her father took care of the arrangements.
EDCS-20401575.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Rufa, with her father noted as responsible for the burial.

EDCS-20401576.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Parents arranged for the tomb of Quintilianus, son of Quintus, aged 15, to be made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401576.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Quintilianus, erected by his parents.

EDCS-20401577.translation: Servilus, son of Corcorus, aged 30, is buried here. Amoena had this made for her son.
EDCS-20401577.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servilus, erected by his mother Amoena.

EDCS-20401579.translation: Arescit, from the workshop of Valerian.
EDCS-20401579.summary: This inscription indicates the maker or origin of an object, possibly a mosaic or a crafted item.

EDCS-20401586.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [Aged] 1 year.
EDCS-20401586.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph for an infant.

EDCS-20401589.summary: This is an inscription identifying an individual, likely related to ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-20401590.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius, aged 60, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20401590.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavius, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-21300320.translation: Iustinus, son of Menander, of the Teretina tribe, Flavius Neopolitanus, aged 46, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sabina and Menander, with his sisters Recepta and Salvina, have set this up for their most devoted and deserving husband and father.
EDCS-21300320.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Sabina and her children to her husband Iustinus, who died at the age of 46.

EDCS-21700211.translation: Custinus had this made for his freedman Nova, aged 20. He is placed here.
EDCS-21700211.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Custinus commissioned a monument for his freedman Nova, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-21700212.summary: This funerary epitaph appears to be a dedication to a person named Serva, possibly with additional names or epithets. The exact meaning is unclear due to fragmentation.

EDCS-21700214.translation: Satria Silvana from Emerita, aged 55, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-21700214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Satria Silvana from Emerita, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-21700218.translation: Paulus, servant of God, lived 73 years, departed in peace on the 6th day before the Ides of May in the era 555.
EDCS-21700218.summary: This epitaph records the death of Paulus, a 'servant of God', who died in 555 AD.

EDCS-21700221.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Ummidius, who held the offices of duovir and flamen.

EDCS-21700227.translation: Philodamus, the magister, gives from his own money for our Aefulana.
EDCS-21700227.summary: This inscription records that Philodamus, in his role as magister, funded a gift for Aefulana.

EDCS-21700228.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Popillia to her son, Lucius Popillius Primus.

EDCS-21700229.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Ponticus, a client, to Gaius Sentius Macro.

EDCS-21700233.translation: Gregorius, a man of high standing, servant of God, lived 56 years and 5 months, rested in peace on the 16th day before the Kalends of November in the year 530 of the era. Perpetua, servant of God, lived 16 years, rested in peace on the 5th day before the Kalends of July in the year 620 of the era. Heleuterius, archdeacon of the Emerita church, servant of God, lived 33 years, rested in peace on the 4th day before the Kalends of January in the year 640 of the era.
EDCS-21700233.summary: This inscription is a multi-part epitaph recording the deaths and ages of Gregorius, Perpetua, and Heleuterius, with dates given according to an era, likely from the 6th to 7th centuries CE.

EDCS-21700237.translation: Avitus, aged 9, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. His household set this up.
EDCS-21700237.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Avitus, who died at age 9, with a conventional wish for his repose and a note about who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-21700248.translation: Lancius, son of Arcon, aged 50, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. His wife Pomponia had this made.
EDCS-21700248.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lancius, aged 50, and states that his wife Pomponia commissioned the monument.

EDCS-21700254.translation: Norbanus, aedile, duovir. Maecia, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-21700254.summary: This inscription lists civic and possibly familial titles for Norbanus and Maecia, likely from a public monument or honorary dedication.

EDCS-21700255.translation: Salaetius willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-21700255.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Salaetius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-21700256.summary: A simple epitaph stating that Paulla Ponila is buried here.

EDCS-21700259.translation: Aged [---] is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. His son made this.
EDCS-21700259.summary: An epitaph for an unnamed individual of unspecified age, with a standard wish for repose and attribution to a son.

EDCS-21700260.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Severus, aged 20, noting his mother, Iulia Secunda.

EDCS-21700261.translation: Attia Oli, daughter, Quieta, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21700261.summary: This tombstone inscription honors Attia Quieta, daughter of Oli, with a standard wish for her repose.

EDCS-21700263.translation: Gaius Norbanus set this up willingly for Liber and Libera.
EDCS-21700263.summary: This is a dedication of a shrine or offering to the gods Liber and Libera by Gaius Norbanus.

EDCS-21700264.translation: Marcus Coelius Silanus fulfilled his vow to Hercules willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-21700264.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Marcus Coelius Silanus fulfilling a vow to Hercules.

EDCS-21700266.translation: Floria, daughter of Placus, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-21700266.summary: This is a votive inscription by Floria, daughter of Placus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-21700270.translation: Quintus Sulpicius Rusticus [dedicated this] to his brother.
EDCS-21700270.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Quintus Sulpicius Rusticus to his brother.

EDCS-21700272.translation: Claudius Rusticus willingly dedicated this sacred object to Mars.
EDCS-21700272.summary: This is a dedication to Mars by Claudius Rusticus.

EDCS-21700277.translation: Vortiacio Avitus, son of Aviticus Toncus, willingly fulfilled his father's vow.
EDCS-21700277.summary: This votive inscription records Vortiacio Avitus fulfilling a vow made by his father.

EDCS-21700279.translation: Cornelius Avitus, aged 61, is buried here.
EDCS-21700279.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cornelius Avitus, aged 61.

EDCS-21700281.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Jupiter Depulsor.

EDCS-21700287.translation: Ursa, daughter of Tancinus, aged 35, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-21700287.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Ursa, daughter of Tancinus, aged 35, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-21700288.translation: Iucunda, slave of Caius Appuleius Speratus, aged 20, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-21700288.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iucunda, a slave of Caius Appuleius Speratus, aged 20, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-21700634.translation: Flavus, son of Flavini, erected this vow willingly.
EDCS-21700634.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavus, son of Flavini, erecting a monument.

EDCS-21700647.translation: Severa, daughter of Tongius, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-21700647.summary: This inscription records Severa, daughter of Tongius, willingly fulfilling a vow, likely on a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-21700650.translation: VI Neabo willingly set this up.
EDCS-21700650.summary: This inscription records a dedication by VI Neabo, likely on a small votive or funerary monument.

EDCS-21700655.translation: Arco, son of Mantius, willingly fulfilled his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-21700655.summary: This inscription records Arco, son of Mantius, fulfilling a vow deservedly, likely on a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-21700656.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or epitaph simply naming 'Liberalitas Iulia', possibly a person or an abstract concept.

EDCS-21700661.translation: Lucius Pomponius Blastus of Caesaraugusta, for himself.
EDCS-21700661.summary: This inscription records Lucius Pomponius Blastus of Caesaraugusta setting up a monument for himself, likely funerary.

EDCS-21700664.translation: Catulla is buried here; Helis Augustalis and Iulia Gadilla saw to it.
EDCS-21700664.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Catulla is buried here, with Helis Augustalis and Iulia Gadilla having arranged it.

EDCS-21700667.translation: Aedile, Duumvir, and priest of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-21700667.summary: This inscription lists official Roman titles held by an individual, likely part of a larger inscription or honorary monument.

EDCS-21700672.translation: Sacred to Mercury of the Emperors, Aguaeco. In honor of Magius Saturninus, his father Magius Reburrus and mother Victoria Victorilla dedicated this.
EDCS-21700672.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mercury, honoring Magius Saturninus, made by his parents Magius Reburrus and Victoria Victorilla.

EDCS-21900091.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Martialis Oclati's son, Cobelcus, aged 36, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His wife Petreia Marcella made this.
EDCS-21900091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cobelcus, son of Martialis Oclati, who died at 36, made by his wife Petreia Marcella.

EDCS-21900092.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Flavia Elpidu, who deserved well. Marcius Urbicus [dedicated this].
EDCS-21900092.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcius Urbicus to his well-deserving wife, Flavia Elpidu.

EDCS-21900094.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nereus, aged 16, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Hermes, his father, had this made for his most devoted son.
EDCS-21900094.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nereus, erected by his father Hermes, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900101.translation: Titus Baebius Cluniensis, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900101.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Baebius Cluniensis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900103.translation: Greetings, Lord Paul! Faustinus greets you and begs you, Lord, as you are accustomed to do, to collect that olive oil yourself, and take care that you compel those slaves under oath that they do not deceive you; collect those vats of rushes and the seals from your ring, and see those tiles that are fastened with a brooch as I sent them. Send Meriacius from Tiliata to come to help you, one who is being born, and one to our slave Mancio from Siriola. Pesitula, and that spirit at Na ris, direct for the holy day. May Christ protect you thus.
EDCS-21900103.summary: A personal letter, possibly inscribed on a wax tablet or other medium, from Faustinus to Paul, requesting assistance with business and legal matters, likely from the late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-21900109.translation: Pela, daughter of Tanginus, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her daughters had this made.
EDCS-21900109.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pela, erected by her daughters, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900110.translation: Sangenus, son of Conceltus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900110.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sangenus, son of Conceltus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900112.translation: O AIV Turracius, I have paid your father's vow willingly with all my heart.
EDCS-21900112.summary: A votive inscription where an individual named Turracius fulfills a vow made by his father, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900113.translation: L. Calatiius, son of [---], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900113.summary: A funerary epitaph for L. Calatiius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900116.translation: Lubacus, son of Boutius, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-21900116.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lubacus, son of Boutius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900117.translation: Equalis Pecuni made this marker for his son Caburus. Amana Clouti [daughter/wife] ITE.
EDCS-21900117.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caburus, erected by Equalis Pecuni, with an additional, possibly related, inscription by Amana Clouti, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900118.translation: Marcus Iulius Badius willingly paid his vow to Salus with all his heart.
EDCS-21900118.summary: A votive inscription where Marcus Iulius Badius dedicates a vow to the goddess Salus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900119.translation: Gaius Norbanus Gemellus, son of Gaius, 14 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21900119.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young boy named Gaius Norbanus Gemellus, indicating he died at the age of 14 and that his mother commissioned the monument.

EDCS-21900123.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Marcus Munatius Montanus, possibly on a monument or as part of a funerary record, though the age is incomplete.

EDCS-21900124.translation: Adorus, son of Oraisasus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-21900124.summary: This inscription records Adorus, son of Oraisasus, fulfilling a votive offering or promise.

EDCS-21900128.translation: Gemellus, son of Andetus, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus.
EDCS-21900128.summary: This inscription records Gemellus, son of Andetus, making a votive offering to the supreme god Jupiter.

EDCS-21900197.translation: He received 20 modii of wheat, 2 modii for Germano, 2 modii for Umo in the market of Monsius, 3 modii for Teudatus the rustic, 1 modius for Nonnus.
EDCS-21900197.summary: This inscription is an accounting record detailing quantities of wheat distributed to various individuals, likely for a market or distribution transaction.

EDCS-21900202.translation: Vincentius I, Bonifatius I, Bitorius I, Profidentius I, Ferbodus I, Perias [---].
EDCS-21900202.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely associated with a communal or official capacity, with Roman numeral notations that may indicate rank or quantity.

EDCS-21900211.summary: This inscription is a dedication or record attributed to the kings Cindasvintus and Recesvintus, likely of Visigothic origin.

EDCS-21900212.translation: [---] jar, one emina, two sextarii, two measures of grain from Paulus the vendor, one sestarius from Burila, two measures from Ade, one sestarius from Barosa, [---] Valentinam, [---] Freter.
EDCS-21900212.summary: This inscription appears to be an inventory or accounting of measures of grain or liquid, listing quantities attributed to specific individuals and locations.

EDCS-21900213.translation: Notitia raised seven cheeses, Maurelus one cheese, and Ioannes, freedman, one cheese.
EDCS-21900213.summary: This inscription records a list of contributions, specifically cheeses, provided by Notitia, Maurelus, and Ioannes, a freedman.

EDCS-21900228.translation: VI measures and Simplicius I modius, VI measures for Maseti, I modius for delicacies, and Sigerius and Iustina I modius, Precurasor III modii, to the god VIII modii, Ioannis in angarias LX modii, XII loads, and [---] adding [---] per Sigerius to modii from Lebaia, XVI modii of seme-turtles, II modii for Flascinus, VI measures with his freedwomen, I sestarius for Flaina, IIII measures for Maxima, I modius for Manno, III measures for Procula. Bonus and Flamnus and Nonnus the elder and Patricius took I modius. Ioannis for the horses XXXIII modii, Masetius II modii for Bodenecas III modii. [---] 478.
EDCS-21900228.summary: This extensive inscription details a complex distribution of goods, including grain and other items, to various individuals and for specific purposes like offerings to a god and provisions for horses, with a final count likely related to the total distributed amounts.

EDCS-21900280.translation: Sacred to Bandi Vorteaeus. He fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-21900280.summary: This votive inscription records a vow fulfilled for the deity Bandi Vorteaeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900282.translation: Eutychius willingly set up a votive offering to Endovellicus and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-21900282.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the deity Endovellicus, dedicated by Eutychius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900286.translation: V. Cia willingly set up a votive offering.
EDCS-21900286.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by V. Cia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900288.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Attius Ionicus is buried here.
EDCS-21900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Attius Ionicus, a 'Dis Manibus' dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900289.translation: Popillius willingly set up an offering to the Household Gods.
EDCS-21900289.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Household Gods (Laribus Vialibus) by Popillius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900290.summary: This is a dedication to the Household Gods (Laribus) by Popillius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200065.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Hegemon, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. His fellow freedmen made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-22200065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hegemon, aged 25, erected by his freedmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200066.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Fortunatus made this for his most dutiful mother, Mussia Proculina, aged 65. She is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-22200066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mussia Proculina, aged 65, erected by her son Iulius Fortunatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200067.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Iulius Maternus, a veteran, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Iulia Candida took care to have this made for her husband.
EDCS-22200067.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Maternus, a veteran, erected by his wife Iulia Candida, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200068.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Veneria, aged 62, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Hieria, aged 74, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Cornelius Calitycius took care to have this made for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-22200068.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription detailing the burials of Cornelia Veneria and Cornelia Hieria, both erected by Cornelius Calitycius for his wife and mother respectively, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200069.translation: Decimus Paccius Agathopus willingly fulfilled his vow for the health of Marcia Matrona.
EDCS-22200069.summary: This is a votive inscription from Decimus Paccius Agathopus dedicated to the health of Marcia Matrona.

EDCS-22200070.translation: Marcus Licinius Faustinus and Lucius Annius Agathopus are buried here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-22200070.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Faustinus and Lucius Annius Agathopus, requesting a light earth upon them.

EDCS-22200071.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Casia Iuliana, aged 23. Her most devoted father, Casius Iulianus.
EDCS-22200071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Casia Iuliana, set up by her father Casius Iulianus.

EDCS-22200072.summary: This is a very short funerary epitaph for a child named Lucius Cocceius, dedicated to his mother.

EDCS-22200074.translation: Alitius Aurilianus and Hisconia rest in peace. Amen.
EDCS-22200074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alitius Aurilianus and Hisconia, wishing them peace.

EDCS-22200102.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Severus Tongini, aged 21, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you, mother.
EDCS-22200102.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severus Tongini, aged 21, set up by his mother.

EDCS-22200105.translation: Pacula, daughter of Silvanus, aged 23, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you, brothers carried out the making.
EDCS-22200105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pacula, daughter of Silvanus, erected by her brothers.

EDCS-22200106.translation: Quintus, son of Dobiterius, from Bercia, aged 51, is buried here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-22200106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus, son of Dobiterius, from Bercia.

EDCS-22200111.translation: Doquirus set up this altar, sacred to Nabia.
EDCS-22200111.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the goddess Nabia by Doquirus.

EDCS-22200115.translation: Marcus S Sa willingly set up [this for] the Lares of Ostia.
EDCS-22200115.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods (Lares) of Ostia by Marcus S Sa.

EDCS-22200118.translation: Ecaus, son of Cali, at the age of 10.
EDCS-22200118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 10-year-old boy named Ecaus.

EDCS-22200121.translation: Aulus L V placed [this] for the Goddess Lady Health.
EDCS-22200121.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Salus by Aulus L V.

EDCS-22200122.translation: Palantico, son of Scanus Angeiti, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-22200122.summary: This is a votive inscription by Palantico, son of Scanus Angeiti.

EDCS-22200124.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple funerary epitaph.

EDCS-22200125.translation: Caburea, daughter of Hilarius Lanciesis, aged 90, is laid here; may the earth be light on you. A.
EDCS-22200125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caburea, daughter of Hilarius Lanciesis, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-22200127.translation: Suriaca, freedwoman of Norbana Quinti, aged 8, is laid here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-22200127.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Suriaca, an 8-year-old freedwoman.

EDCS-22200128.translation: [Name] aged 30 is laid here; may the earth be light on you; his wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-22200128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 30-year-old man, arranged by his wife.

EDCS-22200131.translation: His mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-22200131.summary: This is a phrase indicating that a mother commissioned a monument or work.

EDCS-22200132.translation: Aged 70, may you have light earth; [she/he] arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-22200132.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment for a 70-year-old person, indicating a commissioner.

EDCS-22200137.translation: Iulia Paula, daughter of Gaius, aged 70, is laid here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-22200137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Paula, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-22200138.translation: Titus Caecilius Rufus is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22200138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Caecilius Rufus, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-22200139.translation: Marcia Tertia, daughter of Severinus, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-22200139.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Tertia, daughter of Severinus, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-22200140.translation: Paulla Pupellia, freedwoman of Iulius, aged 100, is buried here.
EDCS-22200140.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paulla Pupellia, a freedwoman, noting her age at death.

EDCS-22200141.translation: Camillus Arrius, from Clunia, aged 70, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22200141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Camillus Arrius, a 70-year-old man from Clunia.

EDCS-22200142.translation: Fuscus paid his vow freely and deservedly to the Best and Greatest God.
EDCS-22200142.summary: An inscription recording Fuscus fulfilling a vow to an unnamed 'Best and Greatest God', likely Jupiter.

EDCS-22200144.translation: Eciricu Mailonus, son of Leurus, willingly set up this altar.
EDCS-22200144.summary: An inscription recording Eciricu Mailonus willingly dedicating an altar.

EDCS-22200145.translation: Tongetamus, son of Caunus, paid his vow freely with a willing spirit to Father Liber.
EDCS-22200145.summary: An inscription recording Tongetamus fulfilling a vow to Father Liber (Bacchus/Dionysus).

EDCS-22200150.translation: Iulius Erinicus paid his vow to the Free Goddess and to Liber.
EDCS-22200150.summary: An inscription recording Iulius Erinicus fulfilling a vow to Libera and Liber (Proserpina and Bacchus/Dionysus).

EDCS-22200249.translation: Iulius Proiectus ordered this to be set up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-22200249.summary: An inscription indicating that Iulius Proiectus commissioned a monument or offering in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22200323.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, from a vow, by Modestina.
EDCS-22200323.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest, dedicated by Modestina.

EDCS-22200336.summary: This is an epigrammatic dedication to Marciana Furonia, likely a woman of some standing, with no further context available.

EDCS-22200353.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. The patron set this up for his deserving freedman, Hilarius, aged 28. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a patron for his freedman, Hilarius, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-22200360.translation: Lucius Marcius Rufus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-22200360.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Marcius Rufus, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-22200363.translation: Reburrus, son of Talabius, paid his vow and set up this altar as deserved. Salquius.
EDCS-22200363.summary: This is a votive inscription by Reburrus, son of Talabius, who set up an altar.

EDCS-22200366.translation: Lupus Lanci paid his vow to Quangeius Tanngus.
EDCS-22200366.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lupus Lanci, paying a vow to Quangeius Tanngus.

EDCS-22200369.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Sextus set this up for his dearest mother Marcella, aged 53. Her bones lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sextus for his mother Marcella, who died at the age of 53.

EDCS-22400203.summary: This is a dedication or dedication, possibly part of a larger inscription, where Ateia made something for Vironus.

EDCS-22400208.translation: Rufinus arranged for this to be made for his father, aged 30.
EDCS-22400208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Rufinus arranged for a monument to be made for his father, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-22400209.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Licinius Nicon made this for his most devoted wife Munatia Eutycusa, aged 23.
EDCS-22400209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Licinius Nicon for his wife Munatia Eutycusa, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-22400210.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Cassiodora made this for her most devoted wife Eusintatus. She lies here.
EDCS-22400210.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Cassiodora set up a monument for her wife Eusintatus.

EDCS-22400211.translation: Gaius Julius Mandi Sangenus is buried here.
EDCS-22400211.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying the deceased as Gaius Julius Mandi Sangenus.

EDCS-22400212.translation: Publius Valerius Laetus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22400212.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Valerius Laetus, including a common funerary invocation.

EDCS-22400213.translation: Lucius Octavius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here. Octavius Ursus, son of Lucius, his grandson, is buried here.
EDCS-22400213.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Octavius and his grandson, Octavius Ursus.

EDCS-22400221.translation: Asteria lived 21 years, buried on the 8th day before the Ides of September.
EDCS-22400221.summary: This inscription records the age at death and burial date of Asteria.

EDCS-22400223.summary: This inscription notes the departure date of Eulali, likely a funerary record.

EDCS-22400226.translation: To the Holy Spirit, Sinplic, deserving well, who [died] at 35 years of age, received.
EDCS-22400226.summary: This inscription, likely Christian, records the name Sinplic, age at death, and recipient of divine grace.

EDCS-22400268.translation: servant of God, having completed a penance, received.
EDCS-22400268.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to a servant of God who underwent penance and was received, likely in a Christian context.

EDCS-22400313.translation: Maria, servant of God, lived [for] years
EDCS-22400313.summary: This incomplete inscription names Maria as a servant of God and begins to state her lifespan.

EDCS-22400314.translation: Maria, servant of God, lived 19 years, rested in peace on the 3rd day before the Nones of February, in the era 556.
EDCS-22400314.summary: This Christian inscription records the lifespan, date of rest, and era date (likely a local reckoning of the Spanish era) for Maria, a servant of God.

EDCS-22400315.translation: Decimus C. Vin Euca rests in peace with his wife.
EDCS-22400315.summary: This inscription records Decimus C. Vin Euca resting in peace, likely a funerary monument to a married individual.

EDCS-22400316.translation: Relicta Petrac, lying on the paved road, had a praiseworthy period of time; may she rest here worthily.
EDCS-22400316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Relicta Petrac, likely from late antiquity, indicating her burial place along a road.

EDCS-22400319.translation: of Menius, a boy, who lived in the name of Christ.
EDCS-22400319.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a boy named Menius, dating from the Christian period.

EDCS-22400334.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Vibius Camalus willingly fulfilled his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-22400334.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Lucius Vibius Camalus.

EDCS-22400335.translation: His son Clementis fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-22400335.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely funerary, where a son fulfilled a vow for his father Clementis.

EDCS-22400337.translation: Talabari's son set this up: Capito, son of Talab.
EDCS-22400337.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription set up by Capito, son of Talabari.

EDCS-22400340.translation: of Arpa, the spirited son, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22400340.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arpa, a 30-year-old man, with a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-22400551.translation: to the flamen of the province.
EDCS-22400551.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, likely a dedication or a title, mentioning a provincial flamen.

EDCS-22400552.translation: Gaius Valerius Iulianus of Seilia, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Antonius Iulianus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-22400552.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Iulianus, erected by his brother Marcus Antonius Iulianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800027.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Rufina the mother made this for her most devoted daughter Rufina, aged 18. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800027.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, where a mother honors her deceased daughter Rufina.

EDCS-22800029.translation: To the Holy Goddess, Sentius Marsus [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-22800029.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a 'Holy Goddess' by Sentius Marsus.

EDCS-22800109.translation: Macilia, daughter of Caius, aged 65, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800109.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Macilia at age 65, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800110.translation: Claudius Chilo, son of Vitulus, aged [---], is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Flaccilla saw to its construction at her own expense.
EDCS-22800110.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Claudius Chilo, with Flaccilla paying for the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800112.translation: Camalus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-22800112.summary: A votive inscription recording Camalus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800113.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Lucius Iulius at age 54, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800116.translation: Lucius Aemilius Proculus saw to its construction at his own expense for the villagers of Roudensium.
EDCS-22800116.summary: An inscription recording Lucius Aemilius Proculus funding a public work for the villagers of Roudensium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800118.translation: Gemellus paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-22800118.summary: A votive inscription recording Gemellus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800119.translation: To the spirits of the dead, Dartua Arre.
EDCS-22800119.summary: A funerary inscription honoring the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Dartua Arre, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800122.translation: Iulia Maior, daughter of Lucius, aged [---], is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800122.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Iulia Maior, daughter of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800123.translation: Quintus Iulius Aper, son of Lucius, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800123.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Quintus Iulius Aper, son of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800124.translation: Quintus Norbanus Saturninus willingly paid his vow to Mercury Coluau.
EDCS-22800124.summary: A votive inscription recording Quintus Norbanus Saturninus fulfilling a vow to the god Mercury Coluau, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800126.translation: To Liber and Libera, Quintus Caecilius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-22800126.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the gods Liber and Libera by Quintus Caecilius, likely on a private shrine or monument.

EDCS-22800129.translation: Proculus Cluniensis, aged 45, is buried here, [paid for] by Avinia Elc from her own money.
EDCS-22800129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Proculus Cluniensis, aged 45, with his burial paid for by Avinia Elc.

EDCS-22900007.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Celer Rufini, aged 75, was buried on the fifteenth day before the Kalends of February.
EDCS-22900007.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph recording the death and burial date of Celer Rufini, aged 75.

EDCS-22900016.translation: Marcus Ulpius Obiddus is buried here.
EDCS-22900016.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Marcus Ulpius Obiddus is buried at this location.

EDCS-22900025.translation: Norbanus Norbanus, Publius Norbanus, Marcus Norbanus, Quintus Norbanus, Norbanus Norbanus, Norbanus Norbanus, Norbanus Norbanus.
EDCS-22900025.summary: This inscription lists multiple individuals with the same given and family names, the purpose of which is unclear without further context, but may be related to a family or group.

EDCS-22900029.translation: Maurio, son of Marcus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Victoria.
EDCS-22900029.summary: This is a dedication by Maurio, son of Marcus, to the goddess Victoria, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22900030.translation: To the household gods of Aquitibus, Gaius Caecilius Rufus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-22900030.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfillment by Gaius Caecilius Rufus to the household gods, possibly of the region of Aquitibus.

EDCS-22900032.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Igius Lupus [dedicated this].
EDCS-22900032.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Igius Lupus, likely on a public or private temple.

EDCS-22900034.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Cai filia Maciaeca, aged 42, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22900034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Cai filia Maciaeca, aged 42, including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-22900035.translation: Tanginus, son of Lubaecus, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-22900035.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfillment by Tanginus, son of Lubaecus, to Jupiter.

EDCS-22900036.translation: Corvaeus Paternus erected this altar in fulfillment of his father's vow.
EDCS-22900036.summary: This is a votive inscription by Corvaeus Paternus, likely erected on an altar.

EDCS-22900037.translation: Her father arranged for this to be made for Coria and Peinuca, daughters of Tritus.
EDCS-22900037.summary: This text records a father's arrangement for a monument for his daughters, Coria and Peinuca.

EDCS-22900038.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible...
EDCS-22900038.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription honoring Emperor Probus.

EDCS-22900039.translation: Aponia Nicopolis, having received divine favor, gladly dedicated this to her own genius.
EDCS-22900039.summary: This is a dedication to a personal genius by Aponia Nicopolis, expressing gratitude.

EDCS-22900040.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Staius, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, priest of the deified Vespasian, aged thirty-four.
EDCS-22900040.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Staius, identified by tribe, priesthood, and age.

EDCS-22900044.translation: Marcus Apronius Avitianus, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-22900044.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely for an individual identified by father and tribe.

EDCS-22900046.translation: Duatius Avitus, aged 20, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. His mother Ullea Afuli and his father Avitus arranged for this to be made for their son.
EDCS-22900046.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Duatius Avitus, erected by his parents, including a common epitaph.

EDCS-22900047.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying the maker of an object as Pelcius, a stonemason.

EDCS-22900064.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Latro [dedicated this].
EDCS-22900064.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, likely by an individual named Latro.

EDCS-22900065.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Great Augustus, and to Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, the most noble Caesar, prince of youth.
EDCS-22900065.summary: This inscription honors the emperors Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus.

EDCS-23001966.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Optata as a freedwoman, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23001967.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely for a tombstone, identifying Maura by her gentilician and father's name.

EDCS-23001968.translation: Abascantius, freedman of Quintus Sulpicius, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-23001968.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Abascantius, a freedman, including his age and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-23001969.translation: Lucius Attius Acutus is buried here.
EDCS-23001969.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the burial of Lucius Attius Acutus.

EDCS-23001970.translation: Marcus Titinius Candidus is buried here, 8 feet deep, 12 feet in front.
EDCS-23001970.summary: This funerary epitaph specifies the burial location and dimensions of the plot for Marcus Titinius Candidus.

EDCS-23001971.translation: Quintus Camulius Elpomaus and Eugenia placed this, aged 49, is buried here.
EDCS-23001971.summary: This funerary epitaph names Quintus Camulius Elpomaus and Eugenia as associated with the burial, with Elpomaus being the deceased aged 49.

EDCS-23001972.translation: Hispana, daughter of Decimus, aged 27, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, 10 feet in the ground.
EDCS-23001972.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Hispana, daughter of Decimus, her age, and the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-23001973.translation: Publius Orbius Rusticus is buried here. His sister Orbia Restituta had this made at her own expense, 12 feet in front, 9 feet in the ground.
EDCS-23001973.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Orbius Rusticus, noting that his sister arranged and paid for the monument, and specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-23001974.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Proclus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Cominia Marcella [arranged this] from her own [resources].
EDCS-23001974.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Marcus Valerius Proclus, mentioning his age and that Cominia Marcella arranged for the monument, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001975.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Pontia Pergamis, 12 feet in front, 8 feet in the ground.
EDCS-23001975.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Pontia Pergamis and specifies the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-23001976.translation: Julius Longinus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Graphica, his wife, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-23001976.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Longinus, set up by his wife Graphica, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23001978.translation: Marcus Cornelius Urbanus, freedman of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-23001978.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Urbanus, a freedman, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23001982.translation: Cruseros is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-23001982.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cruseros, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23001984.translation: Lucius Norbanus, [legal status unknown], aged 5, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Norbanus Trocina [set this up].
EDCS-23001984.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Norbanus, set up by Norbanus Trocina, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23001990.summary: This is a dedication recording that Februarias made something, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002158.translation: Lucius Vitulus, son of Titus Avitus.
EDCS-23002158.summary: This is a dedication recording Lucius Vitulus, son of Titus Avitus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002176.translation: Quintus Farax, son of Quinti, aged 75, may the earth lie lightly on you. His son Quintus Farax Modestus, aged 25, had this made and is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-23002176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Quintus Farax and his son Quintus Farax Modestus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002177.translation: Titus Iulius Amminus willingly fulfilled his vow to Lacipaisia.
EDCS-23002177.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Titus Iulius Amminus fulfilling a vow, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002180.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary epitaph, mentioning Marcus Norbanus Arenus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002184.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary epitaph, mentioning Calpurnius Clemens, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23002188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Micus, a freedman, giving his age.

EDCS-23002190.translation: Lucius Cavius, son of Sextus, of the Balanus tribe, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-23002190.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cavius fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-23002398.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the second time, restored [this].
EDCS-23002398.summary: This is a dedication by Emperor Trajan, indicating the restoration of a public work during his reign, possibly dated by the number of paces indicated.

EDCS-23002437.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Julianus, likely a high-ranking official, possibly from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-23002473.translation: Priscus Andami Flavianus, aged 65, lies here.
EDCS-23002473.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Priscus Andami Flavianus, providing his age and burial location.

EDCS-23002477.translation: Publius Caecilius Niger, son of Marcius, lies here. Caecilia Aranta, daughter of Marcius, lies here.
EDCS-23002477.summary: This is a double funerary inscription for Publius Caecilius Niger and Caecilia Aranta, possibly siblings, indicating they lie here.

EDCS-23002480.summary: This is a very brief, possibly epitaphial or interrogative, inscription.

EDCS-23002484.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fla[vius?] Bracari, aged 40, lies here.
EDCS-23002484.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, for Fla[vius?] Bracari, providing his age and burial location.

EDCS-23101954.translation: Erythri, daughter of Zesaes, the metes of the mistress.
EDCS-23101954.summary: This inscription appears to be Greek, possibly referring to boundary stones or land ownership.

EDCS-23101965.summary: This is a brief Greek inscription, possibly a dedication or wish for a person named Palladi.

EDCS-23900196.translation: Marcus Torius Victor, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here. Iulia Amoena, daughter of Gaius, saw to its construction herself.
EDCS-23900196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Torius Victor, constructed by his wife Iulia Amoena.

EDCS-23900197.summary: This inscription is a very fragmentary epitaph, possibly for Gaius Fusus Rutilus.

EDCS-23900198.translation: Maxuma, daughter, saw to its construction.
EDCS-23900198.summary: This funerary inscription records that Maxuma saw to the construction of the monument.

EDCS-23900204.translation: Gaius Julius Capito is buried here. Maela Longinia is buried here. Rufus, son of Silon, is buried here.
EDCS-23900204.summary: This epitaph records the burial of at least three individuals: Gaius Julius Capito, Maela Longinia, and Rufus, son of Silon.

EDCS-23900205.translation: To the Divine Shades. Laberia Amoena, daughter of Marcus, aged 32, is buried here. Quintus Julius Nerva, her husband, saw to its construction.
EDCS-23900205.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Laberia Amoena, aged 32, and was constructed by her husband Quintus Julius Nerva.

EDCS-23900206.summary: This is a highly fragmentary epitaph that possibly names an individual named Vernac and mentions an age of 30.

EDCS-23900207.translation: Terentia, mother, saw to its construction. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-23900207.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Terentia, the mother, saw to the construction of the monument and includes a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-23900208.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cassia Iulia.
EDCS-23900208.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, mentioning Cassia Iulia.

EDCS-23900212.translation: Lucanus, son of Silon, aged 25. Atia, mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-23900212.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucanus, son of Silon, aged 25, and was constructed by his mother Atia.

EDCS-23900213.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a dedication or epitaph, to Vegetus Tuscus Calumedi.

EDCS-23900220.translation: Antirus erected this without fraud, by oracle.
EDCS-23900220.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Antirus, likely a private monument based on the mention of an oracle.

EDCS-23900221.summary: This is a votive inscription by Mantius Victor, indicating a dedication of a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-23900223.translation: Aetius, a servant of Christ, 39 years old, departed this life, laid to rest with his father, for all ages.
EDCS-23900223.summary: This Christian epitaph records the death of Aetius, a 39-year-old servant of Christ, and is dated to the Christian era.

EDCS-23900224.translation: A woman of twenty-eight years died in childbirth; the cognomen Aurelia Licinia Florida departed from this world in the name of Christ, faithful in Christ, happy in Christ.
EDCS-23900224.summary: This Christian epitaph records the death of Aurelia Licinia Florida, aged 28, who died in childbirth, and is dated to the Christian era.

EDCS-23900234.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely an owner's name or a simple dedication, potentially a tombstone or a property marker.

EDCS-23900235.translation: Macer son of Leuri of Aberciceae, his vow willingly and deservedly made.
EDCS-23900235.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Macer, son of Leuri of Aberciceae, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-24400071.translation: Iulia Tyche, freedwoman of Iulia Veneria, aged 70; Lucius Iulius Laurentius, freedman of Iulia Tyche, aged 50; Iulia Beronice, dutiful daughter, saw to it that this was made for her father. They are buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-24400071.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iulia Tyche and her freedman Lucius Iulius Laurentius, with their daughter Iulia Beronice commissioning the monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24400072.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Victoria Victulla, aged 36, and her nephews Terentius Novellus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-24400072.summary: This funerary inscription honors Victoria Victulla, aged 36, and was commissioned by her nephews, Terentius Novellus, and likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500821.translation: Sunua, daughter of Quintus, aged 25, is buried within; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-24500821.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sunua, daughter of Quintus, aged 25, and is likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500825.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary dedication or ownership mark, possibly from the Roman period.

EDCS-24500827.translation: Publius Ceacilius Maxsumus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-24500827.summary: This is a votive inscription from Publius Ceacilius Maxsumus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500832.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus Servilius Clemens, who died at the age of 35, possibly erected by someone named Servinus.

EDCS-24500833.translation: Severa Pomponia, daughter of Lucius, aged 9, her parents had this made. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-24500833.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Severa Pomponia, erected by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500834.translation: May the earth rest lightly upon you. His father had this made.
EDCS-24500834.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that a father commissioned this monument for his deceased son.

EDCS-24600003.translation: Avitus Fronto willingly paid his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-24600003.summary: This is a votive inscription to Apollo by Avitus Fronto, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24600004.translation: Burial of the infant Petra Lancie in the monastery. Lived for 8 years.
EDCS-24600004.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Petra Lancie, buried in a monastery and who lived for 8 years.

EDCS-24600007.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Caturo Celei, according to his vow.
EDCS-24600007.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caturo Celei, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24600008.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Augustus. Cloranus paid his vow, deservedly and willingly.
EDCS-24600008.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Augustus by Cloranus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24600015.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pe, aged 50.
EDCS-24600015.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Dis Manibus for a person named Pe, aged 50.

EDCS-24600016.translation: nus Crispi, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His son had this made. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Apius Pe, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His son had this made.
EDCS-24600016.summary: This inscription is a duplicate funerary epitaph for two individuals, Crispi and Apius Pe, both aged 55, whose sons commissioned the monuments.

EDCS-24600017.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Quarcadius, aged 20.
EDCS-24600017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Valerius Quarcadius, specifying his age.

EDCS-24600018.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the castellani (garrison soldiers) joyfully set up this votive offering.
EDCS-24600018.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by soldiers stationed at a fort, dedicated to Jupiter.

EDCS-24600618.translation: For Aurelius Fesestarius, one sextarius; Nuarius, one sextarius; for Elia, two sextarii and a half; Felix, one sextarius for Firminus; Benancius, two sextarii and a half; Iocus, his son, two sextarii; Ariolus, three sextarii and a half; Fla recondo, one sextarius; de fol reco.
EDCS-24600618.summary: This inscription details the distribution of a commodity, likely grain or wine, measured in sextarii, with a list of recipients and quantities.

EDCS-24600619.translation: Sign of the divine Savior, never again will you return. Whom the dictation of your waters after resolution came, holding back for a long time, neither hearing in dreams nor in the ears, sent back under retraction, you will see yourself to rejoice. Now in the abyss, and through Carcumiel Michael Raguel Gabriel Uriel and Raphael, against your enemies, and through the great holy father-in-law of the gods, to him saying, whoever in prayer, whoever to them, through the Son, O thunderbolt in all my [afflictions], if anyone has a woman, to him saying that.
EDCS-24600619.summary: This is a complex and partially damaged inscription that appears to be a magical or religious text, invoking divine names and protection.

EDCS-24600620.translation: Evir as witness, Paul as witness, Se as witness, Cro as witness. I, Aurasus, [set up] my [sign] and [my] grandfather's.
EDCS-24600620.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial, with the dedicator Aurasus attesting to its erection alongside witnesses and mentioning his grandfather.

EDCS-24600621.translation: Names: Marius, one half-hemina; Nonnus, one half-hemina; Marius Secundus, two sextarii; Tend [---]us, one half-hemina; Secundus Aurilius, at birth, one; Ben [---] Iocundus, at birth, one; Paulus, at birth, one; Nonnus, at birth, one; Redintus, at birth, one; Leoninus.
EDCS-24600621.summary: This inscription lists names along with quantities, possibly relating to a distribution of wine or oil, with some entries specifying 'at birth'.

EDCS-24600663.summary: This very short inscription consists of repeated affirmations of 'Amen', possibly used as a seal or invocation.

EDCS-25800700.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Pius Magnus, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, the most brave and fortunate prince. Ebora, 22 Roman miles.
EDCS-25800700.summary: This milestone inscription honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix and indicates a distance of 22 miles to the city of Ebora.

EDCS-25800701.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius.
EDCS-25800701.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an emperor, identifying him as the son of the deified Severus Pius.

EDCS-25800702.translation: Gaius Axonius, son of Quintus, of the Papirian tribe, from the colony of Firmum Picenum, made this monument to himself during his lifetime, and for his brother Quintus Axonius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-25800702.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Axonius for himself and his brother, specifying their names, parentage, tribe, and origin.

EDCS-26201109.summary: This inscription appears to be a repetition of a name, possibly a funerary marker or an ownership inscription.

EDCS-28300238.translation: Alucius Roscius, freedman of Caius, lies here; Acholi, a Jew, servant of Caius.
EDCS-28300238.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Alucius Roscius and Acholi, with familial and ethnic details, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300350.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product from the workshop of someone named Felix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300351.translation: From the workshop of Marcus Junius Amabilis.
EDCS-28300351.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product from the workshop of Marcus Junius Amabilis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300352.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Julius Proculus.
EDCS-28300352.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product from the workshop of Gaius Julius Proculus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300353.translation: From the workshop of Lucius M O.
EDCS-28300353.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product from the workshop of Lucius M O, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300361.translation: Marcus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-28300361.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300362.translation: The villagers of Munenses set up an altar to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-28300362.summary: This dedication records the villagers of Munenses setting up an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300479.translation: Lucius Munnius Marcianus, aged 75. Iulia Candida set up a statue to her most deserving husband.
EDCS-28300479.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Munnius Marcianus at age 75 and commemorates him with a statue erected by his wife, Iulia Candida, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300480.translation: Candida, aged over 70, set up an altar and a statue to me.
EDCS-28300480.summary: This funerary epitaph records Candida, aged over 70, setting up an altar and a statue, possibly for herself or a close relative, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300483.translation: To Mirobius, son of Abruius, from the Arqui, his slaves.
EDCS-28300483.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication to a deceased individual named Mirobius, with the dedicators being slaves of the Arqui family.

EDCS-28300488.translation: Dutia, daughter of Turanius, is placed here.
EDCS-28300488.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Dutia, daughter of Turanius.

EDCS-28400029.translation: Gaius Valerius Blandus from Ercavica is placed here.
EDCS-28400029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Valerius Blandus, who hailed from Ercavica.

EDCS-28400041.translation: From the workshop of Marcus I A.
EDCS-28400041.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Marcus, possibly an artisan or merchant.

EDCS-28400042.translation: From the workshop of Marcus I A.
EDCS-28400042.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Marcus, possibly an artisan or merchant.

EDCS-28400044.translation: From the workshop of Marcus I A.
EDCS-28400044.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Marcus, possibly an artisan or merchant.
