EDCS-28400046.translation: From the workshop of Gaius I A.
EDCS-28400046.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Gaius, possibly an artisan or merchant.

EDCS-28400047.translation: From the workshop of Gaius I P. From the workshop of Gaius I P.
EDCS-28400047.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Gaius, possibly an artisan or merchant, possibly indicating multiple items or a repeated marking.

EDCS-28400049.translation: From the workshop of Lucius M O. From the workshop of Lucius M O.
EDCS-28400049.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Lucius, possibly an artisan or merchant, possibly indicating multiple items or a repeated marking.

EDCS-28400050.translation: From the workshop of Lucius M O.
EDCS-28400050.summary: This inscription indicates an item originating from the workshop of a person named Lucius, possibly an artisan or merchant.

EDCS-28400059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caelia Calliste, aged 23, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Caelia Silvana made this for her deserving sister.
EDCS-28400059.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Caelia Calliste, with the monument erected by her sister Caelia Silvana.

EDCS-28400090.translation: Of Caius Valerius Labeo, son of Caius, and Eunia.
EDCS-28400090.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely records names, possibly for a funerary monument or a legal document.

EDCS-28400091.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Severa, daughter of Caecilius, aged 16 years, 6 months.
EDCS-28400091.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Severa, daughter of Caecilius, noting her age.

EDCS-28400094.summary: This inscription records a voluntary offering or monument placed by a person named Maxumus.

EDCS-28400095.translation: To our lord / Maximian / most noble / Caesar / aged 32.
EDCS-28400095.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Maximian, likely erected by a unit or official, possibly associated with a military or administrative context given the 'Caesar' title and age.

EDCS-28700468.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caburus, son of Secndio, aged 55, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700468.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Caburus, son of Secndio, noting his age.

EDCS-28700470.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Atius, son of Escius, aged 57. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700470.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Atius Esci, noting his age.

EDCS-28700472.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. G. R., aged 65.
EDCS-28700472.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of an individual identified by the initials G. R., noting their age.

EDCS-28700473.translation: Alaisus, son of Arrenus, aged 30, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700473.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Alaisus, son of Arrenus, noting his age.

EDCS-28700474.translation: Ambatuo to his son, Boutius, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700474.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Boutius, son of Ambatuo, with the monument erected by his father, noting the son's age.

EDCS-28700476.translation: Lucius Piv, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700476.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Piv, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-28700530.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Ambatus, son of Arquicus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-28700531.translation: Paullinus of Cauri, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Avita, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700531.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Paullinus of Cauri and Avita, both aged 50.

EDCS-28700537.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Medamus, son of Aron, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-28700544.translation: Arcoturus, son of Pisirus, aged 15 days, in memory of Arcon, son of Taginus. His father took care to have this made.
EDCS-28700544.summary: This tombstone commemorates the infant Arcoturus, son of Pisirus, placed by his father in memory of Arcon, son of Taginus.

EDCS-28700546.summary: This tombstone records Dovitena, aged 17, and Ama, aged 5.

EDCS-28700547.translation: Mentina, daughter of Mat, aged 62, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700547.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Mentina, daughter of Mat, who died at the age of 62.

EDCS-28700551.translation: Medugena, daughter of Cai, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700551.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Medugena, daughter of Cai, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-28700556.translation: Placidus' slave woman, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700556.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a slave woman of Placidus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-28700563.translation: [Name missing], aged 61, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-28700563.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an unnamed individual who died at the age of 61.

EDCS-28701568.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Talabus Tangini, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother took care of the burial.
EDCS-28701568.summary: A funerary epitaph for Talabus Tangini, aged 25, with his mother as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28701570.translation: R N. To N. F. I., aged 11, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Veienta, his wife, took care of the making and placed it for his mother.
EDCS-28701570.summary: A funerary epitaph for an 11-year-old, with the monument erected by his wife in memory of his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800654.translation: P. Pater P. P., father, took care of the making.
EDCS-28800654.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating a father commissioned the making of a monument for his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800672.translation: Claudia Tangina took care of the making for her Lubaecus, son of Antaeus, her avuncular [relative]; for her Binarea, daughter of Tritus, her paternal grandmother; for her Boutius, son of Lubaecus, her paternal uncle; and for her Cilia Caenonis, her paternal aunt. (Dedicated to) her own (family).
EDCS-28800672.summary: A family funerary monument commissioned by Claudia Tangina for several relatives, including her avuncular and paternal relatives, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800676.summary: A dedication by the villagers of Ocelonenses, likely to a deity or for a public purpose, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800677.translation: Iulia Eranigi, Ommia, Bandua, Horrico, freely paid their vow with willing heart.
EDCS-28800677.summary: A votive inscription from Iulia Eranigi, Ommia, Bandua, and Horrico, indicating the fulfillment of a religious promise, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800678.translation: Terentia, daughter of Caius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28800678.summary: A funerary epitaph for Terentia, daughter of Caius, with the standard 'sit tibi terra levis' formula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800679.translation: From the will of Rufus Mascellius Severus.
EDCS-28800679.summary: A testamentary inscription indicating that Rufus Mascellius Severus made provisions in his will for this monument or memorial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800680.translation: Aged 14, father and mother took care of the making for you, dear one. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28800680.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 14-year-old, with the monument commissioned by their parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800694.translation: Tulgan, son of Chintila, king. May the earth lie lightly upon you. May the gods protect you.
EDCS-28800694.summary: An inscription for Tulgan, son of Chintila, identified as a king, likely from a provincial or non-Romanized context within the Empire, possibly late antique or early medieval.

EDCS-29800061.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerius Numerianus Augustus, pious, fortunate, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, for the 20th time.
EDCS-29800061.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Numerian, likely inscribed on a public monument during his reign (late 3rd century CE).

EDCS-30000457.translation: Epaphra Felix to Faustus, his fellow slave.
EDCS-30000457.summary: This is a dedication from Epaphra Felix to his fellow slave Faustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000461.translation: Boutius, son of Pella, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you, from the people of the Aesuri.
EDCS-30000461.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Boutius, mentioning his tribal affiliation (Aesuri), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000463.translation: Sabinus, from the family of Bandus Vordaeus, willingly paid his vow with a free spirit to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-30000463.summary: This is a dedication recording a vow paid by Sabinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000464.translation: To the Lares, Placidus, son of Titus, [dedicated this].
EDCS-30000464.summary: This is a dedication to the Lares by Placidus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000478.translation: Marcus Clu[vius], of the Papirian tribe, by his will.
EDCS-30000478.summary: This inscription likely indicates a posthumous dedication or a memorial set up by Marcus Clu[vius] according to his will, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000479.translation: This monument, sacred to the Dis Manibus (Underworld deities), and this place... Lucius Cordius Philon, Cordia Avita, daughter, Cordia Lucana, wife, Lucius Cordius Hermes, freedman, [arranged] for their patrons and their comrade with whom he lived 55 years. They are buried here. May the earth be light upon you. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30000479.summary: This is a complex funerary monument set up by freedmen and family members for their patrons and a deceased comrade, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31200123.translation: To the Nymphs of Caprae, Aelius Epinicus willingly paid his vow with a free spirit.
EDCS-31200123.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs of Caprae by Aelius Epinicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31200124.translation: To the Fontanae Nymphs, Severinus, freedman of Roni(mius?), willingly paid his vow with a free spirit.
EDCS-31200124.summary: This is a dedication to the Fontanae Nymphs by Severinus, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31200125.translation: To Salus (Health), Valeria Privata willingly paid her vow with a free spirit.
EDCS-31200125.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Salus by Valeria Privata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300167.translation: To Lucius Roscio Aelianus Maecius Celer Manlius Postumus Mamilianus Vergilius Stabertianus, son of Lucius Roscilius Quirina, quaestor of Emperor Hadrian Augustus, member of the Augustales, peregrine praetor, legate of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, by decree of the provincial council.
EDCS-31300167.summary: This inscription records the career of Lucius Roscio Aelianus, a Roman official, likely dating to the reign of Hadrian (AD 117-138).

EDCS-31300206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata, his most faithful freedwoman and preserver, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Salvianus made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-31300206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunata, a freedwoman, dedicated by Salvianus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-31300308.translation: Toncius, son of Toncetamus, a standard-bearer (signifer) in the 2nd cohort of Lusitanians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Victoria. Ardunnus, son of Cominius, made this.
EDCS-31300308.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier named Toncius for the goddess Victoria, possibly dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-31300309.translation: Toncius, son of Toncetamus, a soldier from Igaedum, erected an altar to Trebaruna, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-31300309.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier named Toncius to the deity Trebaruna, likely from the Roman province of Lusitania.

EDCS-31600109.translation: For the health and victory of the vicus, Lucius Caecilius Viator willingly and with good spirit fulfilled his vow to Venus and Minerva.
EDCS-31600109.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Caecilius Viator for the welfare of his neighborhood (vicus) dedicated to Venus and Minerva.

EDCS-31600111.summary: This is a brief dedication for a monument erected by Fortunata.

EDCS-31600113.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Primus Axonius, son of Saro, willingly placed this.
EDCS-31600113.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Primus Axonius, likely a private religious offering.

EDCS-31600114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Stephano, son of Publius, aged 32, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-31600114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Stephano, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-31600115.translation: Ambatus, son of Malgeinus, lies here.
EDCS-31600115.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Ambatus.

EDCS-31600116.translation: Fuscus, son of Severus, from Limicus, aged 22, lies here in the fortress of Arguce. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father arranged for it to be made, under the supervision of Tauroco.
EDCS-31600116.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuscus, a soldier who died at age 22, with arrangements made by his father and supervised by Tauroco.

EDCS-31600117.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Antonius Mucnela, son of Albinus, took care to have this made for Apana, daughter of Reburrus, aged 19.
EDCS-31600117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apana, set up by her husband Antonius Mucnela.

EDCS-31600118.translation: Severus' son vowed willingly and deservedly to Jupiter.
EDCS-31600118.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by an unnamed son of Severus.

EDCS-31600119.translation: P. Reagui willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31600119.summary: This is a votive inscription by P. Reagui fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-31600120.summary: This is a dedication, likely to an emperor or high-ranking official named Flavius.

EDCS-31600121.summary: This is likely a name inscription, possibly from a tombstone or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-31600123.translation: Atilia Avita, daughter of Lucius, wife of Terentia Amoena.
EDCS-31600123.summary: This inscription records the names of Atilia Avita and Terentia Amoena, possibly a mother and daughter or related individuals.

EDCS-31600134.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pon, son of Iunius, aged 53.
EDCS-31600134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pon, son of Iunius.

EDCS-32601881.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Sucninus, son of Asedus.

EDCS-32601891.summary: This inscription likely refers to Marcus SVLVFEI Secundus, possibly in a dedicatory or ownership context.

EDCS-32601892.summary: This inscription refers to Gaius Aemilius Scribonius, likely a dedication or ownership marker.

EDCS-32601896.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Veranius Fronto, likely a personal name.

EDCS-32601930.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription for Venustus, identified by his patronymic.

EDCS-32601974.summary: This inscription identifies Clumene as a freedwoman of a patron named Julia.

EDCS-32602134.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal name, possibly indicating a non-Roman origin due to the name structure.

EDCS-32602136.summary: This is a builder's or dedicant's inscription stating that Gaius Valerius erected the monument or object.

EDCS-33000044.translation: Viriatus willingly arranged for this altar to be set up at his own expense
EDCS-33000044.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription where Viriatus paid for and arranged the erection of an altar.

EDCS-33600039.translation: Sestionis fulfilled her vow to the god Lord Ennovolicus
EDCS-33600039.summary: This is a votive inscription where Sestionis paid a vow to the deity Ennovolicus.

EDCS-33600040.translation: Turrecia, having willingly made this sacred offering to the god Endovellecus, arranged for it to be made
EDCS-33600040.summary: This is a votive inscription where Turrecia dedicated a sacred offering to the god Endovellecus and oversaw its construction.

EDCS-33600045.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Asbinus
EDCS-33600045.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from a private individual named Asbinus.

EDCS-33600049.translation: For Boutia, daughter of Tanginus, because of Palarus Quadratus and
EDCS-33600049.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or commemoration related to Boutia and possibly a reason involving Palarus Quadratus, but it is incomplete.

EDCS-33600052.translation: Procula, daughter of Albinius, from Eburobrices, willingly paid her vow to Arantia and Arantio.
EDCS-33600052.summary: This funerary or votive inscription records Procula paying a vow to the deities Arantia and Arantio.

EDCS-33600056.translation: Allius Agathas willingly paid his vow to the Holy Goddess.
EDCS-33600056.summary: This votive inscription records Allius Agathas paying a vow to a Holy Goddess.

EDCS-34300014.translation: Cocceia Amoena had this made for her husband, who was the freedman of Rufus Antutius, aged 48, and for herself.
EDCS-34300014.summary: This funerary epitaph records Cocceia Amoena commissioning a tomb for her husband and herself.

EDCS-34300015.translation: Tanginus Dautaionis, father, [dedicated this] to Samacia and Aurelia, and to his wife Tancia, daughter of Ancolus.
EDCS-34300015.summary: This funerary inscription records Tanginus Dautaionis dedicating a monument to his wife Tancia and possibly other female relatives or deities.

EDCS-34300016.translation: Rufina, daughter of [?], had this made as instructed by the will of her [father?], for her [son?], and for her uncle, and for my [grandmother?].
EDCS-34300016.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription indicates that Rufina commissioned a monument based on a will, for several family members.

EDCS-34300024.summary: This is a very short dedication, likely funerary, to a daughter named Madura.

EDCS-34300028.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Valerius lies here.

EDCS-34300031.translation: To Arcon, son of Duatus, lies here, aged 50; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300031.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Arcon, son of Duatus, and includes a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34300032.translation: Boutius, son of Camalus, aged 70; Amoena, daughter of Boutius, had this made for her father.
EDCS-34300032.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Boutius and that his daughter Amoena commissioned the monument.

EDCS-34300033.translation: Caerius Silvanus, son of Dauto, had this made for himself and for his wife Sila, daughter of Silon.
EDCS-34300033.summary: This funerary epitaph records Caerius Silvanus commissioning a tomb for himself and his wife.

EDCS-34300034.translation: To Gaius Valerius Flaccus, son of Quirina, Flaccinus.
EDCS-34300034.summary: This is a funerary dedication naming Gaius Valerius Flaccinus, son of Quirina.

EDCS-34300035.translation: To Gaius Valerius Frontonus, son of Rufi, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-34300035.summary: This is a funerary dedication naming Gaius Valerius Frontonus, son of Rufi.

EDCS-34300037.summary: This inscription likely records the name of an individual, possibly Camalus Entaramicus, son of Talontus.

EDCS-34300038.translation: Camalus, son of Brevus, is placed here.
EDCS-34300038.summary: This is a tombstone inscription stating that Camalus, son of Brevus, is buried here.

EDCS-34300039.translation: To Clementi, son of Lupeus, aged 16, and Lupo, son of Clementis, aged 60.
EDCS-34300039.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, Clementi (16) and Lupo (60), possibly commemorating their lives or deaths.

EDCS-34300046.translation: Iulia Severa, daughter of Nigri, had this made for Iulia Severina, daughter of Severus, and for herself, and for Iulia Avita, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-34300046.summary: This is a funerary dedication initiated by Iulia Severa for herself and two other women, Iulia Severina and Iulia Avita, likely family members.

EDCS-34300047.translation: Cilea, daughter of Cilius, had this made for her brother Laetus, son of Cilius, in accordance with the will.
EDCS-34300047.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Cilea for her brother Laetus, as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-34300048.translation: She had this made for Liguria, daughter of Liguris, and for Flavius Reburrus, son of Flavius, and for her brother Sabinus, son of Flavius, and for Procula, daughter of Boetelus, aged 25; she is buried here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-34300048.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by an unnamed woman for her relatives: mother Liguria, father Flavius Reburrus, brother Sabinus, and sister Procula, who died at 25.

EDCS-34300049.translation: Lucanus, son of Bovi, for himself and for his wife Sunua, daughter of Maturovus.
EDCS-34300049.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucanus, son of Bovi, and his wife Sunua.

EDCS-34300050.translation: Marcia had this made for her father Marcus, son of Arconis.
EDCS-34300050.summary: This is a funerary dedication commissioned by Marcia for her father, Marcus, son of Arconis.

EDCS-34300051.translation: To Marcus Coelius Silo, son of Malgeinus.
EDCS-34300051.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcus Coelius Silo, son of Malgeinus.

EDCS-34300052.translation: Memmia Flaccilla had this made for her mother, daughter of Junius Ulleus.
EDCS-34300052.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Memmia Flaccilla for her mother, the daughter of Junius Ulleus.

EDCS-34300053.translation: Niger, son of Tongus, lies here.
EDCS-34300053.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Niger, son of Tongus, is buried here.

EDCS-34300054.translation: Pintamus, son of Loucinus, lies here.
EDCS-34300054.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Pintamus, son of Loucinus, is buried here.

EDCS-34300055.translation: Rufus, son of Tritus, had this made for his sons Rufinus and Niger, for his daughter Vitalis, daughter of Prisca, and for his wife Camala, wife of Nigrus, daughter of Docquirus.
EDCS-34300055.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Rufus, son of Tritus, for his sons, daughter, and wife.

EDCS-34300056.translation: Seloca, freedwoman of Flaccilla, aged 58, lies here; Pacatus, son of Lucius Gutus, had this made.
EDCS-34300056.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Seloca, freedwoman of Flaccilla, aged 58, and was commissioned by Pacatus, son of Lucius Gutus.

EDCS-34300057.translation: To Severus, son of Pintamus; Severina, daughter of Severus, for her father.
EDCS-34300057.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Severina, daughter of Severus, to her father Severus, son of Pintamus.

EDCS-34300058.translation: Talabus, son of Salicus, lies here.
EDCS-34300058.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Talabus, son of Salicus, is buried here.

EDCS-34300059.translation: Tertula, daughter of Arcius, and Priscus, son of Maxillo, set up this monument for their mother Tuouta, daughter of Tongus; she lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300059.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tertula, daughter of Arcius, and Priscus, son of Maxillo, for their mother Tuouta, daughter of Tongus.

EDCS-34300060.translation: Urbana, freedwoman of Longinus, had this made for Tiberius Claudius Redemptus and for herself.
EDCS-34300060.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Urbana, freedwoman of Longinus, for Tiberius Claudius Redemptus and for herself.

EDCS-34300061.translation: Vitalis, daughter of Fuscus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300061.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vitalis, son of Fuscus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300062.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Trophimus [erected this] for his wife Cornelia Caliope. Cornelius Tranquillus [erected this]...
EDCS-34300062.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Cornelia Caliope by her husband Cornelius Trophimus and possibly another Cornelius Tranquillus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300063.translation: His mother had this made for her son Cumelius Talai, son of Cumelius, and grandson of Aleonis, and for her grandson Cilea.
EDCS-34300063.summary: A funerary monument erected by a mother for her son and grandson, likely dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300064.translation: ina, daughter of Cattyga, aged 75, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Camira, daughter of Tongatus, had this made for her mother.
EDCS-34300064.summary: A funerary epitaph for ina, daughter of Cattyga, with a dedication from her daughter Camira, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300065.translation: Celer, son of Bolsus, a builder, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cilea, daughter of Aebicus, his wife, had this made.
EDCS-34300065.summary: A funerary epitaph for Celer, a builder, with a dedication from his wife Cilea, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300066.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caelius had this made for his most devoted mother, Valeria Sextia, aged [..].
EDCS-34300066.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Sextia, erected by her son Lucius Caelius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300067.translation: His brother Reburrus had this made for Luatro, son of Fronto, from Clunia.
EDCS-34300067.summary: A funerary monument erected by Reburrus for his brother Luatro, likely dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300068.translation: To Gaius Curius Clemens, son of Gaius, of the voting tribe Quirina. Gaius Valerius Rufus [erected this]...
EDCS-34300068.summary: An inscription honoring Gaius Curius Clemens, son of Gaius, with a dedication from Gaius Valerius Rufus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300069.translation: To Lucius Coutius, son of Narsus. Caenanicus and Icatl [dedicated this].
EDCS-34300069.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Lucius Coutius, son of Narsus, by Caenanicus and Icatl, likely dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300070.translation: To Marcus Junius Crassus, son of Serenus, of the tribe Galeria. Marcus Junius [dedicated this].
EDCS-34300070.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Marcus Junius Crassus, son of Serenus, by Marcus Junius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300071.translation: Iulia Severa arranged for this to be made for Marcus Iulius Avitus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-34300071.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Severa to Marcus Iulius Avitus, likely a relative, for whom she arranged the monument's construction.

EDCS-34300074.translation: To Clara, daughter of Paullina, daughter of Aranton, [for her] mother.
EDCS-34300074.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Clara to her mother, who is identified by her paternal and maternal lineages.

EDCS-34300075.translation: Lucius Iulius Modestinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, [for his] son Lucius Iulius [---].
EDCS-34300075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Modestinus, son of Lucius, with the monument arranged for his son, Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-34300076.translation: Iulia, daughter of Iulius, his wife, arranged for this to be made for her freedman.
EDCS-34300076.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulia, wife of Iulius, arranged for the monument to be made for her freedman.

EDCS-34300077.translation: To Gaius Vibius, father, [from his] son Gaius Vibius.
EDCS-34300077.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a son, Gaius Vibius, to his father, Gaius Vibius.

EDCS-34300078.translation: Reburrus, son of Rufinus, arranged for this to be made for [his] mother.
EDCS-34300078.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Reburrus, son of Rufinus, arranged for the monument to be made for his mother.

EDCS-34300079.translation: Caelia, daughter of Accius, freedwoman, arranged for this to be made for [her] mother.
EDCS-34300079.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Caelia, daughter of Accius, a freedwoman, arranged for the monument to be made for her mother.

EDCS-34300080.translation: Rufinus arranged for this to be made according to the will.
EDCS-34300080.summary: This inscription records that Rufinus arranged for a monument to be constructed as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-34300081.translation: Docquirus, son of Cuntirus, lies here.
EDCS-34300081.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription stating that Docquirus, son of Cuntirus, is buried here.

EDCS-34300082.translation: Tongius, sixteen years old, from Brigium.
EDCS-34300082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tongius, who died at the age of sixteen and was from Brigium.

EDCS-34300083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attia Urania, identified by her name and status.

EDCS-34300084.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of Decia Vetusta.

EDCS-34300086.translation: Son of Lubaenus, to his father and sister.
EDCS-34300086.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a son to his father and sister.

EDCS-34300088.summary: This incomplete inscription likely refers to Flavius Nutro, identified as a soldier.

EDCS-34300091.translation: Cutaeca, freedwoman of Tuouta, aged 26. Aquilinus, son-in-law of Aquilius, and Junius, freedman of Tuouta and mother of Cutaeca, had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-34300091.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the son-in-law and freedman mother of Cutaeca, a freedwoman, who died at age 26.

EDCS-34300092.translation: To her son Concordiensis, aged 60. The daughter of Paternius had this made for her husband.
EDCS-34300092.summary: This funerary dedication was made by the daughter of Paternius for her husband, who died at age 60.

EDCS-34300093.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximianus Caesar.
EDCS-34300093.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus Caesar.

EDCS-34300096.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Titius Samia, possibly a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-34300575.translation: Annia Maganus, aged 75. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300575.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Maganus, aged 75, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34300578.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caburus, having deserved well of himself, here for himself.
EDCS-34300578.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits for Caburus, who apparently made arrangements for his own burial.

EDCS-34300580.translation: Dobiteina, daughter of Magilo, aged 20, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300580.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dobiteina, indicating her parentage and age at death.

EDCS-34300581.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Fuscus, son of Firmus, aged 45, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300581.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fuscus, son of Firmus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-34300582.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Cuntira, aged 75. Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Felicisima, aged 21.
EDCS-34300582.summary: This inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Cuntira and Felicisima, along with their ages.

EDCS-34300872.summary: This epitaph records the age of Melamanius, a freedman, at his death.

EDCS-34300875.translation: Cara, daughter of Tancinus, aged 25, is laid here. Flaccus, son of Lecebrus, had this put in place according to the will of his wife, paid for by Arreinus, son of Talabus.
EDCS-34300875.summary: This funerary epitaph describes Cara, daughter of Tancinus, and mentions a commission by Flaccus, son of Lecebrus, for his wife, with a payment arranger named Arreinus.

EDCS-34300952.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning Marcus Agrippa.

EDCS-34300961.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Emperor Trajan Hadrian Augustus, in honor of the divine house.
EDCS-34300961.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honoring Lucius Aelius Caesar, adopted son of Hadrian.

EDCS-34300976.summary: This inscription fragment names Caesar Quintus Iunius.

EDCS-34300982.summary: This inscription fragment appears to contain names, possibly of a woman and others.

EDCS-34300984.summary: This inscription fragment names Fulcinius Tuscini, son of Moloch.

EDCS-34300987.translation: Fabia, daughter of Caius, from the Fabii.
EDCS-34300987.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia, daughter of Caius, belonging to the gens Fabia.

EDCS-34300988.translation: To Lia, daughter of Quintus, from Tina Ilionis.
EDCS-34300988.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Lia, daughter of Quintus, possibly by Tina Ilionis.

EDCS-34301013.translation: Gaius Julius Nectar, freedman, erected this for the Augustobrigensian Spaniard.
EDCS-34301013.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Julius Nectar, a freedman, to a Spaniard from Augustobriga.

EDCS-34301015.translation: May you possess this house as yours by right, appeased martyr Eulalia, so that the enemy, recognizing this, may depart confused, and may this house, with its inhabitants, flourish through your favor. Amen.
EDCS-34301015.summary: This is a dedication or prayer invoking the martyr Eulalia for protection and prosperity of a household.

EDCS-34301046.translation: His son L. had it made for his most deserving and devoted patron.
EDCS-34301046.summary: This inscription records that a son had a monument made for his highly respected patron.

EDCS-34301049.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Iulia Silonis Secunda, daughter of Silon, aged four, had this made for her son Lucius Praesentius Rufinus. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34301049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Praesentius Rufinus, aged four, erected by his mother Iulia Silonis Secunda, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301050.translation: Lucius, son of Urcalon, aged fifty-five, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sons had this made.
EDCS-34301050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius, son of Urcalon, aged fifty-five, erected by his sons.

EDCS-34301051.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Maxsumus, son of Tancinus, aged sixteen, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father and mother had this made.
EDCS-34301051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxsumus, son of Tancinus, aged sixteen, erected by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34301052.translation: Aulus Publicius Viator is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34301052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Publicius Viator.

EDCS-34301053.translation: Publius Caecilius Niger, son of Quintus, aged fifty, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34301053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Caecilius Niger, son of Quintus, aged fifty.

EDCS-34301054.translation: Gisco from Gieni came to watch.
EDCS-34301054.summary: This brief inscription, possibly an epitaph or a milestone, records the arrival of Gisco from Gieni.

EDCS-34301055.translation: Augustinus, a slave, fulfilled his vow to Mars as ordered, deservedly.
EDCS-34301055.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars by a slave named Augustinus, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-34301057.translation: To Celtius, son of Andercus, and to Burrillus, son of Celtius, by the will of Burrillus, son of Celtius.
EDCS-34301057.summary: This inscription records a legacy or bequest from Burrillus, son of Celtius, to Celtius, son of Andercus, and Burrillus, son of Celtius.

EDCS-34301058.translation: Flaccus, from Magilancum in Mirobrigensis, aged 35, is buried here; may the earth be light on you, colleague; his colleagues had it made.
EDCS-34301058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flaccus, a 35-year-old man from Mirobrigensis, with his colleagues arranging for the monument.

EDCS-34301065.translation: Acira Turaesia willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-34301065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Acira Turaesia.

EDCS-34301067.translation: Caecilius Vetto of Cantisicom, from Segobrigensis.
EDCS-34301067.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple identification or funerary marker for Caecilius Vetto, indicating his origin.

EDCS-34500966.translation: Lucius Iulius Fuscus is buried here; Iulia Festa, his daughter, had it made.
EDCS-34500966.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Fuscus, with his daughter Iulia Festa arranging for the monument.

EDCS-34500967.translation: Sercus, slave of Sincera, is buried here; 14 feet in the front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-34500967.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a slave named Sercus and specifies the dimensions of his plot.

EDCS-34500970.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Aulus.
EDCS-34500970.summary: This is a maker's mark or signature from the workshop of Gaius Aulus.

EDCS-34500971.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus.
EDCS-34500971.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Tiberius Caesar.

EDCS-34500973.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 8th time, consul for the 4th time, emperor for the 8th time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-34500973.summary: This inscription is a titulary of Emperor Nero, listing his official titles and powers.

EDCS-34500974.translation: To the spirits of the departed: To Lebisinia Auges. Publius Cussius Phoebianus, procurator of Augustus, her husband, and Marcus Iulius Verianus, her son.
EDCS-34500974.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a husband and son for Lebisinia Auges.

EDCS-34900002.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Egma, father, by the vow of my family.
EDCS-34900002.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Egma, who identifies himself as a 'father'.

EDCS-34900003.translation: Arreini, son of Arreinius, gladly paid his vow to Jupiter the Solver.
EDCS-34900003.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Solutorius made by Arreinius.

EDCS-34900004.translation: Boutius, son of Ambatus, gladly paid his vow to Victory.
EDCS-34900004.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the goddess Victory made by Boutius.

EDCS-34900005.translation: Quadratus, for his Ilubeda, gladly paid his vow and it was deserved. For Ilubeda, sacred vow, gladly paid and deserved.
EDCS-34900005.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Quadratus for Ilubeda, possibly a relative or loved one.

EDCS-34900006.translation: Baebius Riburrinus gladly paid his vow and it was deserved to the sacred Celiborca.
EDCS-34900006.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the deity Celiborca made by Baebius Riburrinus.

EDCS-34900009.translation: Julius Achilles [made this] for Julius Eutychus, from the vow of his parents.
EDCS-34900009.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Julius Achilles for Julius Eutychus, likely a parent, fulfilling a parental vow.

EDCS-34900011.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Bouti Triti, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-34900012.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Dovitena, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-34900013.translation: To Sabinus, aged 21. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sabinus, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-34900015.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Marcus, son of Cilo, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900015.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Marcus, son of Cilo, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-34900017.translation: To Pintovius, son of Arcon, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He lived.
EDCS-34900017.summary: This funerary epitaph records Pintovius, son of Arcon, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34900025.translation: To Boutia, daughter of Cloutus, aged 40. May she lie here, and may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900025.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Boutia, daughter of Cloutus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-34900026.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aper, aged 12.
EDCS-34900026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aper, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-34900027.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Fausta, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Placidina, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900027.summary: This inscription contains two separate funerary epitaphs, one for Fausta and one for Placidina, both aged 60.

EDCS-34900028.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arria lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900028.summary: This funerary epitaph states that Arria lies here and wishes the earth lie lightly upon her.

EDCS-34900034.translation: To Boutius, son of Tancinus, aged about 90.
EDCS-34900034.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Boutius, son of Tancinus, who died at approximately 90 years of age.

EDCS-34900035.translation: To Cloutus, son of Ambinius, aged 20.
EDCS-34900035.summary: This inscription records Cloutus, son of Ambinius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34900036.translation: To Reburrina, daughter of Reburrus, aged 17. May the earth lie lightly upon you. To Firmanilla, daughter of Firmanus, aged 8. May she lie here, and may the earth lie lightly upon you. To Vegetina, daughter of Vegetus, aged 65. May she lie here, and may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900036.summary: This inscription contains three funerary epitaphs for Reburrina (17), Firmanilla (8), and Vegetina (65).

EDCS-34900037.translation: Rufina, daughter, aged [---]. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900037.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Rufina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900040.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Flaccus, son of Materni, aged [---].
EDCS-34900040.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Sempronius Flaccus, son of Materni, indicating his age is incomplete.

EDCS-34900041.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunia Vinusia, aged 31. Sacred to the departed spirits. Vacovia, aged 19.
EDCS-34900041.summary: This inscription records the burials of two women, Iunia Vinusia and Vacovia, with their ages, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-34900043.translation: Varilius Pontonis, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Varilius Pontonis, who died at age 70.

EDCS-34900044.translation: Audi Magilonis, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Audi Magilonis, who died at age 70.

EDCS-34900045.translation: [---] Vironicum, aged 50, may the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for someone named Vironicum, aged 50, with a lacuna at the beginning.

EDCS-34900046.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Tanginus as 80.

EDCS-34900047.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attia, aged 70, is buried here. May she lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attia, aged 70, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-34900049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clarus, son of Clarus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900049.summary: This funerary epitaph records Clarus, son of Clarus, aged 50, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-34900050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Oclatia, daughter of Quintus, aged 60, is buried here. May she lie light upon you.
EDCS-34900050.summary: This funerary epitaph records Oclatia, daughter of Quintus, aged 60, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-34900051.translation: To the infernal gods, Focimila, a woman.
EDCS-34900051.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the monument to the infernal gods for Focimila.

EDCS-34900052.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. F. E., aged 55.
EDCS-34900052.summary: This funerary inscription is sacred to the infernal gods for F. E., aged 55.

EDCS-34900053.translation: To the infernal gods. G. R., aged 65.
EDCS-34900053.summary: This funerary inscription is for G. R., aged 65, dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900054.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Iunius, son of Lucius, aged 35, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900054.summary: This funerary inscription records Iunius, son of Lucius, aged 35, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900055.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Moderatus, son of Econis, aged 65, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900055.summary: This funerary inscription records Moderatus, son of Econis, aged 65, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900056.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Prima, aged 61, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900056.summary: This funerary inscription records Prima, aged 61, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900057.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Reburrina, daughter of Reburrus, aged 45, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900057.summary: This funerary inscription records Reburrina, daughter of Reburrus, aged 45, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900058.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Vismiola, aged 5, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900058.summary: This funerary inscription records Vismiola, aged 5, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900059.translation: Sacred to the infernal gods. Vironus, son of Toutonus, aged 52, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900059.summary: This funerary inscription records Vironus, son of Toutonus, aged 52, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900061.translation: Adia, daughter of Aretiton, aged 30, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900061.summary: This funerary inscription records Adia, daughter of Aretiton, aged 30, and is dedicated to the infernal gods.

EDCS-34900062.translation: Alaesus, son of Tritus, of the Ubonici. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900062.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alaesus, son of Tritus, from the Ubonici tribe.

EDCS-34900064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arro, son of Caenon.

EDCS-34900066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aunia, daughter of Turai, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-34900068.translation: Cadaus, son of Magani, of the Coiliones, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cadaus, son of Magani, from the Coiliones, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-34900069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cadaus, son of Talavus, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-34900070.translation: Calisia, daughter of Ulirvus, beloved, lived 50 years. May she lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900070.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calisia, daughter of Ulirvus, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-34900071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Capito for his son, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-34900072.translation: Caurunius, son of Ambatus, of the Caurunici.
EDCS-34900072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caurunius, son of Ambatus, from the Caurunici tribe.

EDCS-34900073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cavelia, possibly the wife of Vironus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-34900074.translation: Domiteus, of the Buaci, son of Boutus.
EDCS-34900074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domiteus, from the Buaci tribe, son of Boutus.

EDCS-34900075.translation: Dobiterus son of Aronis, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-34900075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dobiterus, recording his name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-34900076.translation: Erguena daughter of Bouti, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900076.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Erguena, recording her name, parentage, age, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34900077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magana, recording her name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-34900078.translation: Magilo son of Elaesus, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly on you. He is buried here.
EDCS-34900078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magilo, recording his name, parentage, age, and a standard wish for the deceased, along with burial information.

EDCS-34900080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mentina, recording her name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-34900081.translation: Mentina daughter of Tritus, aged 70. May the earth lie lightly on you. She is buried here.
EDCS-34900081.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mentina, recording her name, parentage, age, and a standard wish for the deceased, along with burial information.

EDCS-34900082.translation: Placidus son of Elaesus, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-34900082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Placidus, recording his name, parentage, age, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34900083.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Pistrio, recording his name and parentage.

EDCS-34900084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Segontius, recording his name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-34900085.translation: Severus son of Ligirus, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-34900085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Severus, recording his name, parentage, age, and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34900086.translation: Talaus, son of Toncetamus, aged thirty, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Talaus, who died at the age of thirty.

EDCS-34900087.translation: Tritius, son of Ambinius, aged fifteen, may he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tritius, who died at the age of fifteen.

EDCS-34900088.translation: Tritius Bouti, aged twenty, may he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900088.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tritius Bouti, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-34900089.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Tritia, daughter of Magilo, possibly on a funerary monument or as part of a list.

EDCS-34900090.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Acinicinus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-34900091.translation: Ambatus, son of Burus, son of Turolus, aged fifty, may he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambatus, son of Burus, son of Turolus, who died at the age of fifty.

EDCS-34900093.translation: Pintovus, son of Caerus, aged sixty, may he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pintovus, son of Caerus, who died at the age of sixty.

EDCS-34900099.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored 136.
EDCS-34900099.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a structure or area, indicated by the number 136 (likely a measurement in paces or a numerical reference).

EDCS-34900100.translation: The Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, restored 137.
EDCS-34900100.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's restoration of a structure or area, indicated by the number 137 (likely a measurement in paces or a numerical reference).

EDCS-34900103.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, restored 157 Roman miles from Emerita Augusta.
EDCS-34900103.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan's restoration of a road or public work, measuring 157 Roman miles from Emerita Augusta.

EDCS-34900112.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased, likely erected by his son.

EDCS-34900113.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription stating the age of the deceased.

EDCS-34900116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Festus, brother, took care to have this made for Aurelius Rufus, a tabularius of the province of Lusitania in the patrimonial administration, who lived 34 years, 11 months, and 13 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Rufus, a provincial administrator, erected by his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900118.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Licinia Settiana and Licinius Lupidus, son of her son, took care to have this made for their most devoted father, Licinius Setianus, a veteran of the Quirina tribe from Cirtensis, aged 73. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinius Setianus, a veteran, erected by his wife and son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Iuniana and Antonius Saturninus of Madaura, aged 71, took care to have this made during my lifetime. If anyone takes away years from them after my death, as if...
EDCS-34900119.summary: This is a funerary inscription where the dedicator, Ulpia Iuniana, arranged for her own monument during her lifetime, with a curse against those who might disturb it.

EDCS-34900120.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Aemilius Nigrinus, dutiful, in his own place, aged 15. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, for eternal rest without care.
EDCS-34900120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Aemilius Nigrinus, a young boy, likely erected by his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900123.translation: Quintus Articuleius Avitus, son of Quintus, lived 5 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900123.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Quintus Articuleius Avitus, erected by his family.

EDCS-34900125.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Callirhoe, aged 40. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Helius took care to have this made for his deserving wife.
EDCS-34900125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Callirhoe, erected by her husband Helius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900126.translation: Aged 17. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly. Cordia Pompeiana took care to have this made for her father.
EDCS-34900126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed male aged 17, erected by his daughter Cordia Pompeiana.

EDCS-34900127.translation: Crescens, slave of Clementis, aged 7. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescens, a slave, recording his age and owner.

EDCS-34900129.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Publius Egnatius Florentinus, aged 74.
EDCS-34900129.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Publius Egnatius Florentinus, who died at the age of 74.

EDCS-34900133.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Quintus Iulius Avitus, Papiria Emerita, aged 48, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. By Synerusa.
EDCS-34900133.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Iulius Avitus and mentions Synerusa as the person who arranged it.

EDCS-34900135.translation: Iulia Felicissima, aged 26. Iulius Proculus and Iulia Crotale made this for their most loving daughter, well deserving. May the earth rest lightly upon her here. V I.
EDCS-34900135.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication made by parents for their deceased daughter, Iulia Felicissima.

EDCS-34900136.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Magia Maxima, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Aurelia Donata, daughter. Aurelius Successus.
EDCS-34900136.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Magia Maxima, aged 40, with contributions from Aurelia Donata and Aurelius Successus.

EDCS-34900138.translation: Philemon, freedman of Aulus Manlius Cornutus, 10 feet in front, 9 feet in the field.
EDCS-34900138.summary: This inscription likely records land boundaries or burial plot dimensions associated with Philemon, a freedman.

EDCS-34900151.translation: Caius Vibius Proculus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Mussania Maurilla, freedwoman of Marcus, arranged for this to be made at her own expense.
EDCS-34900151.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Vibius Proculus and the arrangement of his monument by Mussania Maurilla.

EDCS-34900152.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Irineus, son of Ambatus, and Botilla, daughter of Ami, arranged for this to be made for themselves and their husband. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-34900152.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Irineus and Botilla, who arranged for the monument for themselves and a husband.

EDCS-34900153.translation: Ammonicus willingly paid his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-34900153.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ammonicus to the Nymphs.

EDCS-34900154.translation: Miniatus willingly paid his vow to the Nymphs of Capua, as was fitting.
EDCS-34900154.summary: This is a votive inscription by Miniatus to the Nymphs of Capua.

EDCS-34900157.translation: Fulvius Rufius willingly paid his vow to Salus (Health) with a willing heart.
EDCS-34900157.summary: This is a votive inscription by Fulvius Rufius to the goddess Salus.

EDCS-34900225.translation: Preditus lived 75 years; his brother erected this.
EDCS-34900225.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Preditus and identifying his brother as the dedicator.

EDCS-34900227.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Modestia Primula, aged 47, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Modestius Corintus, her son, had this made for his most deserving and pious mother.
EDCS-34900227.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modestia Primula, detailing her age and burial, with her son Modestius Corintus as the commissioner.

EDCS-35001045.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aiminius Acita, aged 9, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Nigrinus had this made according to the will.
EDCS-35001045.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aiminius Acita, aged 9, with the executor Nigrinus commissioning the monument as per a will.

EDCS-35001048.translation: With tribunician power, he restored the consulship in the 157th year.
EDCS-35001048.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly referring to a political or administrative act, dating to the 157th year (of an unspecified era).

EDCS-35001052.translation: Quintius for himself and Arruntia; Arruntia is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-35001052.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating a shared burial plot for Quintius and Arruntia, with Arruntia already interred.

EDCS-35001054.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. P. Iulianus lived 16 years and 5 months. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-35001054.summary: A funerary epitaph for P. Iulianus, recording his age at death and wishing him a light earth.

EDCS-35200269.translation: By a divine scourge, Lux [my daughter] Siricia was snatched from me. Time has taken you and your mother away in an immense span. You had not yet reached your sixth year when you left dire wounds to your grieving father. Behold, with your tears you renewed the buried grief, and you yourself quickly joined your mother's tomb. Year 587.
EDCS-35200269.summary: A funerary epitaph in elegiac verse mourning the premature death of a young girl named Siricia, mentioning her mother's death and dating the inscription to 587 (likely AUC).

EDCS-35200345.translation: Lucius Licinius Fuscus is buried here.
EDCS-35200345.summary: A simple funerary inscription stating the burial of Lucius Licinius Fuscus.

EDCS-35200347.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording a name, possibly of a deceased person or a dedicator.

EDCS-35200351.translation: Avitus, son of Tongus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-35200351.summary: A funerary epitaph for Avitus, son of Tongus, stating his age and burial place.

EDCS-35200352.translation: Decumus, son of Placentia, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Placentia and Iulia had this made for their son.
EDCS-35200352.summary: A tombstone commemorating Decumus, a 12-year-old boy, commissioned by his parents.

EDCS-35200353.translation: Calventia Iuliana had this made for her most devoted husband, Lutatio Asper, aged 29.
EDCS-35200353.summary: A tombstone for Lutatio Asper, aged 29, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-35200354.translation: Lucius Lucretius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-35200354.summary: A tombstone identifying Lucius Lucretius Severus, a Roman citizen of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-35200355.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucritia Patricia, aged 39, erected this monument herself during her lifetime.
EDCS-35200355.summary: A tombstone set up by Lucritia Patricia, aged 39, for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-35200357.translation: Flavia Maxsima, mother, had this erected for her son Valerius Maximus, of the Quirina tribe, from Collipponensis, aged 20.
EDCS-35200357.summary: A tombstone for Valerius Maximus, a 20-year-old from Collipponensis, commissioned by his mother.

EDCS-35200360.translation: Avita, mother, had this made for Marcus Cassius Turrinus, son of Marcus, aged 5.
EDCS-35200360.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Cassius Turrinus, a 5-year-old boy, commissioned by his mother.

EDCS-35200361.translation: Calaito, son of Caeilus, lies here.
EDCS-35200361.summary: A tombstone identifying Calaito, son of Caeilus.

EDCS-35200363.translation: Manlia Probisa, according to her will.
EDCS-35200363.summary: An inscription indicating that Manlia Probisa made provisions for this monument in her will.

EDCS-35200364.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius RFLVIIS.
EDCS-35200364.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, bearing the name Aurelius RFLVIIS.

EDCS-35200365.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rufus EOIGI.
EDCS-35200365.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the departed spirits, bearing the name Rufus EOIGI.

EDCS-35200366.translation: Consecrated to the Divine Shades. Saturninus Cleme[ns].
EDCS-35200366.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades by Saturninus Cleme[ns].

EDCS-35200367.translation: Ulpius Sabinus, aged 1 year, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-35200367.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Sabinus, a child who died at the age of one.

EDCS-35200385.translation: The one who wrote this, Surianus, buggered Maevius.
EDCS-35200385.summary: This is a short, obscene inscription recording a personal insult.

EDCS-35200411.translation: Florentia, a virgin of Christ, lived 21 years and, with a short life, fulfilled many times, fell asleep in the peace of Jesus whom she loved, on the Kalends of April, in the year 626 [of the Spanish Era, i.e., 588 AD].
EDCS-35200411.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Florentia, a virgin, who died in 588 AD.

EDCS-35200412.translation: While I was enjoying a sweet life with my husband, Fortune immediately took me. I, Venantia by name, always a detriment to all, lived my life. I endured for twelve times four quiet years and at last paid the common debt of all. In this place I chose to rest, with my offspring whom the Lord had long ago called, purified by the washing of water. She rested in peace on the 11th day before the Kalends of February, in the year 581 [of the Spanish Era, i.e., 543 AD].
EDCS-35200412.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Venantia, who died in 543 AD and is buried with her children.

EDCS-35200429.translation: Ruinous old age had loosened the ancient masses; the work, fallen and broken by age, hung precariously, and the suspended way over the river had been lost, preventing passage across the bridge. Now, in the time of the powerful Getae king EurVicius, who ordered the lands surrendered to him to be cultivated, the magnanimous [king] strove to extend his name by his deeds and added his own to the ancient titles of Salla. For after he had renovated the city with excellent walls, he did not cease to achieve this greater miracle: he constructed arches, founded deep in the waters, and, imitating the wondrous work of the creator, he conquered the task. Furthermore, the love of Zenon, the chief priest, urged him to create such a defense for the fatherland. [This was done in the city of] Augusta Felix, destined to last for long ages, through the renewed zeal of the leader and pontiff, in the year 521 [of the Spanish Era, i.e., 483 AD].
EDCS-35200429.summary: This inscription records the public works, including bridges and walls, undertaken in Augusta Felix by King EurVicius and funded by Zenon, the chief priest, in 483 AD.

EDCS-35700023.translation: Boutia, daughter of Camalus, aged 30, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Camalus, son of Iu, from Arabriga, her father, saw to its construction.
EDCS-35700023.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Boutia, daughter of Camalus, with her father Camalus paying for the tomb.

EDCS-35700026.translation: Bodius and Amoena, daughter of Cilius, had it made according to the will.
EDCS-35700026.summary: This inscription states that Bodius and Amoena, daughter of Cilius, commissioned the monument as specified in a will.

EDCS-35700028.translation: Lucio Cerlo, aged 9 years. Fusca, his daughter, for his merits.
EDCS-35700028.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucio Cerlo, aged 9, and is set up by his daughter Fusca.

EDCS-35700030.translation: lies [someone] aged ... and a heart of [indecipherable] ... from Com[indecipherable].
EDCS-35700030.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription with missing names and details.

EDCS-36400179.translation: Roscia Caesia, daughter of Caius, aged 50, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-36400179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Roscia Caesia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-36400180.translation: Abundantius, servant of God, lived 12 years and rested in peace in the year 381.
EDCS-36400180.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Abundantius, dating to 381 CE.

EDCS-36400182.translation: To our lord Flavius Gratianus Pius Felix, victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, Octavius Clarus, a man of highest distinction, acting as his deputy prefect, dedicated this.
EDCS-36400182.summary: This is a dedication by Octavius Clarus, a high-ranking official, to Emperor Gratian, likely dating to Gratian's reign (367-383 CE).

EDCS-36400183.translation: Near the temple of Bellona, Marcius Va[---] Va[---] a dining room of stone.
EDCS-36400183.summary: This inscription possibly marks a dining area or structure near a temple of Bellona, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37000001.translation: Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, consul for the third time, with tribunician power for the third time.
EDCS-37000001.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Marcus Agrippa, likely dating to his consulships and periods of tribunician power between 31 and 27 BCE.

EDCS-37100074.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Agrippa and likely Augustus, dating to the Augustan period (late 1st century BCE).

EDCS-37100089.translation: to Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power
EDCS-37100089.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription referring to Augustus's titles, likely dating to the Augustan period (late 1st century BCE).

EDCS-37100096.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Aulus, son of [---]
EDCS-37100096.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a product from the workshop of Gaius Aulus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37100098.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a product from the workshop of Francius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37600155.translation: Rogata, servant of God, lived 55 years and departed in peace.
EDCS-37600155.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Rogata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-37600156.translation: Filex departed in peace on the twelfth day before the Kalends of July.
EDCS-37600156.summary: This inscription records the death of Filex, with the date of passing.

EDCS-37600163.translation: Adulteus, a cleric, lived for 10 years and rested in peace on the third day before the Ides of January in the year 767 (of the era).
EDCS-37600163.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Adulteus, a cleric, including his age and the date of his repose in 767.

EDCS-37600165.translation: Adiutor, a servant of God, rested in peace on the Ides of May.
EDCS-37600165.summary: This inscription marks the repose of Adiutor, identified as a servant of God, with a partial date.

EDCS-37600166.translation: Afranius, a presbyter, died in the peace of our Lord Jesus Christ on the fifth day before the Ides of February in the year 744 (of the era), for a good memory.
EDCS-37600166.summary: This epitaph records the death of Afranius, a presbyter, in the year 744, noting his peaceful passing and a memorial.

EDCS-37600167.translation: Amanda, a servant of Christ, lived for more or less 32 years and 5 months and rested in peace in the Lord on the seventh day before the Kalends of March in the year 582 (of the era).
EDCS-37600167.summary: This epitaph commemorates Amanda, a servant of Christ, detailing her lifespan of approximately 32 years and 5 months and her date of repose in 582.

EDCS-37600168.translation: Andreas, a servant of God and head cantor of the most holy church of Mertillianum, lived for 36 years and rested in peace on the third day before the Kalends of April in the year 563 (of the era).
EDCS-37600168.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Andreas, a servant of God and head cantor at the church of Mertillianum, who lived 36 years and died in 563.

EDCS-37600169.translation: Britto, a presbyter, lived for 65 years and rested in peace in the Lord on the Nones of August in the year 584 (of the era).
EDCS-37600169.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Britto, a presbyter, as 65 years, and his date of repose in 584.

EDCS-37600170.translation: Donata, a girl of Christ, lived for 22 years and rested in peace in the Lord on the third day before the Nones of July in the year 503 (of the era).
EDCS-37600170.summary: This epitaph records the life of Donata, a young follower of Christ, who died at 22 years old in the year 503.

EDCS-37600171.translation: Glandarius, a servant of God, lived for more or less 38 years and rested in peace in the Lord on the third day before the Kalends of June in the year 604 (of the era).
EDCS-37600171.summary: This epitaph records the life of Glandarius, a servant of God, who lived approximately 38 years and died in the year 604.

EDCS-37600172.translation: Hilarinus, a servant of God, lived for one year, 5 months, and 5 days, and rested in peace on the Nones of June in the year 604 (of the era).
EDCS-37600172.summary: This epitaph commemorates Hilarinus, a servant of God, noting his very young age (1 year, 5 months, 5 days) and his repose in 604.

EDCS-37600173.translation: Mannaria, servant of Christ, lived for nine years and four months. She rested in peace on the 16th day before the Kalends of April in the era 532.
EDCS-37600173.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Mannaria, a servant of Christ, who died in the era 532.

EDCS-37600174.translation: Orania, servant of God, lived for three years. She rested in peace on the Ides of November in the era 550.
EDCS-37600174.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Orania, a servant of God, who died in the era 550.

EDCS-37600175.translation: Romanus, priest and servant of God, lived for 75 years. He rested in peace in the Lord on the 15th day before the Kalends of [Month] in the era 560.
EDCS-37600175.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Romanus, a priest and servant of God, who died in the era 560.

EDCS-37600176.translation: Satyrius, priest, ministered in the presbyterate for 13 years. He departed in peace of our Lord Jesus Christ on the 6th day before the Nones of March in the era 527. His body rests.
EDCS-37600176.summary: This inscription records the ministry and death of Satyrius, a priest, who died in the era 527.

EDCS-37600177.translation: Simplicius, priest and servant of God, lived for 59 years. He rested in peace in the Lord on the 8th day before the Kalends of September in the era 575.
EDCS-37600177.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Simplicius, a priest and servant of God, who died in the era 575.

EDCS-37600178.translation: Tyberius, lector and servant of God, lived for approximately 14 years and five months. He rested in peace in the Lord on the 13th day before the Kalends of June in the era 604.
EDCS-37600178.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Tyberius, a lector and servant of God, who died in the era 604.

EDCS-37600179.translation: Here lies Zousimos, son of Polyknikos. Era [incomplete]
EDCS-37600179.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Zousimos, son of Polyknikos.

EDCS-37600180.translation: A servant of God lived for approximately 70 years. She rested in peace in the Lord on the day before the Kalends of February in the era 556.
EDCS-37600180.summary: This inscription records the life and death of an unnamed female servant of God who died in the era 556.

EDCS-37900047.translation: Artemas, servant of Claudius Martilinus, placed this votive offering to the holy goddess Ataecina Turibriga.
EDCS-37900047.summary: This inscription is a votive offering from Artemas to the goddess Ataecina Turibriga.

EDCS-37900048.translation: Lucius Iuventius Iulianus willingly fulfilled his vow to the holy goddess Turibrigensis.
EDCS-37900048.summary: This inscription records Lucius Iuventius Iulianus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Turibrigensis.

EDCS-37900052.summary: This is a dedication of a roof and columns by Avilius, likely a private benefactor, possibly in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900053.translation: Pompeius Front [built?] the work at his own expense.
EDCS-37900053.summary: This is a dedication of a work by Pompeius Front, funded by himself, possibly in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900059.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cae Beronice lived for 1 year, 6 months, and 12 days. Cae Victor, her father, made this for his devoted daughter. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-37900059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cae Beronice, who died young, set up by her devoted father, Cae Victor, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900060.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cal Calityche lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-37900060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cal Calityche, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900063.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Vitalis, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her most devoted mother made this for her dearest daughter.
EDCS-37900063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Vitalis, aged 35, set up by her devoted mother, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900067.translation: Flavinus, freedman, made this for his most devoted patroness.
EDCS-37900067.summary: This is a dedication by Flavinus, a freedman, to his patroness, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900068.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Iustina, aged 26. Titus Flavius Eutyches made this for his most deserving wife. She lies here.
EDCS-37900068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Iustina, aged 26, set up by her husband, Titus Flavius Eutyches, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900071.translation: To Medigenius, freedman and dearest friend of Iulius Ascanius.
EDCS-37900071.summary: This is a dedication to Medigenius, a freedman and friend of Iulius Ascanius, likely a funerary monument from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900074.translation: Aulus Ofilius Urbanus, aged 25. He lies here; 12 feet in front, 8 feet in the ground.
EDCS-37900074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and burial plot description for Aulus Ofilius Urbanus, aged 25, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900075.translation: Claudius Placidus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon him. Fautia Arethusa arranged for this to be made for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-37900075.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Placidus, aged 50, arranged by his wife Fautia Arethusa, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-37900078.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rutula, aged 9 years. Castricius Victorinus, her grandfather, made this. She is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-37900078.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Rutula, set up by her grandfather, Castricius Victorinus.

EDCS-37900159.translation: Felix Eugenia, servant of Christ, constructed a new gate for the entrance. The dedication of the barrier contains the vows of the virgins; the approaches to the thresholds will be revealed to the faithful. This virgin, mother of virgins, completed this sacred work with her servant, in the era 679.
EDCS-37900159.summary: An inscription commemorating the construction of a new gate and its dedication, possibly related to a convent or religious community, dated to the era 679.

EDCS-37900160.translation: Bonifatia, wife of Silvanus, lived 17 years. Received into peace on the 13th day before the Ides of February, in the name of Christ.
EDCS-37900160.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bonifatia, wife of Silvanus, stating her age and date of death.

EDCS-37900161.translation: Bracarius Felix lived 52 years. Departed on the Nones of April, in the era 419.
EDCS-37900161.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bracarius Felix, stating his age and date of death.

EDCS-37900162.translation: Cantonus, servant of God, lived 87 years. Rested in peace on the 11th day before the Kalends of January, in the era 555.
EDCS-37900162.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cantonus, a servant of God, stating his age and date of death.
