EDCS-37900163.translation: Whoever looks upon this burial work of Eolalius the cleric, the confessor, this place has been set aside. But if anyone truly wishes to disturb this monument of mine, may he be anathema, struck by the leprosy of Gehazi, and may he have a portion with Judas the betrayer, and be separated from the thresholds of the church and excluded from holy communion eternally.
EDCS-37900163.summary: A curse inscription protecting the tomb of Eolalius, a cleric and confessor, threatening severe spiritual and physical punishment for desecration.

EDCS-37900164.translation: Florentia, servant of God, lived 25 years, 5 months, 16 days. Rested in peace on the 13th day before the Ides of March, in the era 553. Marcella, servant of God, lived approximately 35 years. Rested in peace on the 4th day before the Kalends of July, in the era 570.
EDCS-37900164.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Florentia and Marcella, both servants of God, with their ages and dates of death, dated to the eras 553 and 570.

EDCS-37900165.translation: Fortuna, servant of God, lived 25 years. Rested in peace on the 14th day before the Kalends of March, in the era 639. Fortuna, servant of God, lived 25 years. Rested in peace on the 14th day before the Kalends of March, in the era 639.
EDCS-37900165.summary: A double funerary epitaph for two individuals named Fortuna, both servants of God, stating their identical ages and dates of death, dated to the era 639.

EDCS-37900166.translation: Lupercus the faithful, received into peace, lived 30 years.
EDCS-37900166.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lupercus, stating his age and that he was received into peace.

EDCS-37900169.translation: in the era 632. Usus, servant of God, lived 8 months, 10 days. Rested in peace.
EDCS-37900169.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Usus, a servant of God, mentioning his age in months and days and the era 632.

EDCS-37900170.translation: Reccaredus the doctor, having fulfilled his duty, rests in this tomb, to be resurrected; it is said he lived about [---]
EDCS-37900170.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Reccaredus, a doctor, indicating his death and hope for resurrection.

EDCS-37900171.translation: A handmaiden of God lived 10 years and 3 days, in peace, in the year 520 of the era.
EDCS-37900171.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a 'famula dei' (handmaiden of God) at the age of 10 years and 3 days, dating to the era year 520.

EDCS-37900182.translation: Chindasvintus the king, pious in his reign. Chindasvintus.
EDCS-37900182.summary: This inscription is a royal dedication or statement of power by King Chindasvintus.

EDCS-37900183.translation: Aeternalis, a servant of God, lived 46 years and rested in peace on the 8th day before the Kalends of September, in the year 648 of the Lord's era.
EDCS-37900183.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aeternalis, a servant of God, at age 46, dated to the era year 648.

EDCS-37900184.translation: Arestula, a handmaiden of God, lived 27 years and rested in peace on the 8th day before the Kalends of August, in the year 597 of the era.
EDCS-37900184.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Arestula, a handmaiden of God, at age 27, dated to the era year 597.

EDCS-37900186.translation: Asella, a handmaiden of God, lived 26 years and rested in peace on the 8th day of the Ides of September, in the year 560 of the era.
EDCS-37900186.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Asella, a handmaiden of God, at age 26, dated to the era year 560.

EDCS-37900187.translation: Aurilius, a servant of God, lived 34 years and rested in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of July, in the year [---] of the dupondius.
EDCS-37900187.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurilius, a servant of God, at age 34, with a partially legible date.

EDCS-37900188.translation: Barusus the priest, servant of God, lived 40 years and rested in peace on the 3rd day before the Nones of October, in the year 560 of the era.
EDCS-37900188.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Barusus, a priest and servant of God, at age 40, dated to the era year 560.

EDCS-37900189.translation: Bassilla, a handmaiden of God, lived 23 years and rested in peace on the 11th day before the Kalends of May, in the year 600 [or 605] of the era.
EDCS-37900189.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Bassilla, a handmaiden of God, at age 23, dated to the era year 600 or 605.

EDCS-37900190.translation: Benicnae, deserving well, was laid to rest in peace on the third day of the Ides of November; Symmacius made this for his most pious wife.
EDCS-37900190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Benicnae, made by her husband Symmacius, recording her burial date.

EDCS-37900191.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a formulaic dedication or ownership mark, mentioning Eraclius in relation to Jesus Christ.

EDCS-37900192.translation: Here rests the venerable bishop Fidelis
EDCS-37900192.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating the burial of Fidelis, a bishop.

EDCS-37900193.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating the burial of Gratianus.

EDCS-37900194.translation: Burial of Hippolitus the deacon on the 13th day before the Kalends of April in the year 546 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 508 AD)
EDCS-37900194.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the date of death of Hippolitus, a deacon.

EDCS-37900195.translation: Iohannes the penitent, servant of God, lived 32 years more or less, rested in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of August in the year 665 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 627 AD). Peace to whoever does not violate this tomb.
EDCS-37900195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iohannes, a penitent servant of God, specifying his lifespan and burial date, and including a curse against defacers of the tomb.

EDCS-37900196.translation: Maria, servant of God, lived 27 years, rested in peace on the day
EDCS-37900196.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Maria, a servant of God, noting her age at death and that she rested in peace.

EDCS-37900197.translation: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, in this tomb rests the servant of God Maurilius, lived in peace 60 years, was buried on the day of the Kalends of July. And whoever wishes to disturb this tomb or the small body, and whoever wishes to rest in this tomb when God commands him to be called from this world, may he pass without repentance and have as his share before the tribunal of our Lord Jesus Christ Judas Iscariot. His wife Orsitana will build this.
EDCS-37900197.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maurilius, a servant of God, specifying his lifespan and burial date, including a lengthy curse on those who disturb the tomb, and stating his wife Orsitana is the builder.

EDCS-37900198.translation: Octavia the devout lived 17 years and 10 months, received into peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of May in the year 480 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 442 AD)
EDCS-37900198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia, noting her age at death and her reception into peace on a specific date.

EDCS-37900199.translation: Orbanus the priest, servant of God, lived 75 years and six months, rested in peace on the seventh day before the Ides of August in the year 550 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 512 AD)
EDCS-37900199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Orbanus, a priest and servant of God, detailing his lifespan and burial date.

EDCS-37900201.translation: Proeictus erected this memorial for his wife Ursella, who lived with him for 12 years
EDCS-37900201.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Proeictus' wife, Ursella, and noting the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-37900202.translation: Lord Jesus Christ, forgive all the sins of your servant Quinigia, resting in this place; she lived 30 years and rested in peace on the 10th day before the Ides of March, in the year 700 of the era.
EDCS-37900202.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Quinigia, dated to 700 era.

EDCS-37900205.translation: Holy servant of God, lived 18 years and rested in peace on the 8th day before the Kalends of January, in the year 632 of the era.
EDCS-37900205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unidentified male, described as a servant of God, dated to 632 era.

EDCS-37900206.translation: Saturninus the penitent, servant of God, who lived a pure life in this world, lived more or less 68 years; having accepted penitence, he rested in peace on the 18th day before the Kalends of January, in the year 626 of the era.
EDCS-37900206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturninus, a penitent servant of God, dated to 626 era.

EDCS-37900207.translation: Stefanus the priest, servant of God, lived 3 years and rested in peace on the Kalends of August, in the year 590 of the era.
EDCS-37900207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Stefanus, a priest and servant of God, dated to 590 era.

EDCS-37900208.translation: Valentinus, servant of God, lived 37 years and rested in peace on the 2nd day before the Ides of July, in the year 552 of the era.
EDCS-37900208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valentinus, a servant of God, dated to 552 era.

EDCS-37900209.translation: Valeria, servant of God, lived 4 years and 9 months, and rested in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of February, in the year 550 of the era.
EDCS-37900209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria, a servant of God, dated to 550 era.

EDCS-37900218.translation: A long journey brought me forth, and her piety or the banquet brought me up to the heights; and he lived 16 lustra (80 years) of a long life and rested in peace on the 6th day before the Kalends of September, in the year 686 of the era.
EDCS-37900218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a young man who died after 16 lustra (80 years), dated to 686 era.

EDCS-37900224.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the origin of a product from the workshop of Necleltus.

EDCS-37900227.translation: Here lies Saturna, she lived in peace, she was entombed in the month of March on the 25th day, in the year 405 of the era.
EDCS-37900227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturna, dated to 405 era.

EDCS-37900242.translation: Blessed be the name of the Lord, who gives life and takes it away; may peace come and rest in your tomb. I, Jacob, son of Rabbi Senior, rest with a peaceful soul, bound in the bundle of life. Open the gates of peace, O angels of peace, and say to him: enter with peace. I lived 63 years, filled with wisdom, leading the art of artisans. I, Simeon, son of Rabbi Jacob, built this for me. May peace be upon [it].
EDCS-37900242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Simeon, son of Jacob, a skilled artisan, expressing a wish for peace in the afterlife and dated implicitly by the lifespans mentioned.

EDCS-38000007.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Otchim, likely from a tomb.

EDCS-38000008.translation: The servant of Christ Suinthiliuba rested in peace on the Nones of November in the year 704 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 666 AD).
EDCS-38000008.summary: This epitaph records the death of Suinthiliuba, a servant of Christ, with her date of death.

EDCS-38000009.translation: Servanda, servant of Christ, lived 42 years and rested in peace on the 9th day before the Kalends of July in the year 624 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 586 AD).
EDCS-38000009.summary: This epitaph records the age and date of death of Servanda, a servant of Christ.

EDCS-38000012.translation: The body of blessed Maurus rests here.
EDCS-38000012.summary: This is a tomb inscription identifying the resting place of blessed Maurus.

EDCS-38000013.translation: Be happy, Felix. Felix from the workshop of Avitus. Use [this] happily.
EDCS-38000013.summary: This is a personal inscription, likely on an object, wishing happiness to Felix and mentioning Avitus's workshop.

EDCS-38000017.translation: May Eusebia obtain complete restoration in age, healthy in flock and whole in law, or may she obtain in any place what they have increased according to the law of inheritance concerning the devoted, all envy of eyes and wrath of God, and hundred pounds of gold belonging to the kings, Damocenta, father, as decreed by VTR in time or reason on the Ides of March in the year 610 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 572 AD), happily.
EDCS-38000017.summary: This is a complex legal or devotional inscription, possibly related to inheritance and divine judgment, dated to the Ides of March in the year 610.

EDCS-38000143.translation: Iustus, a deacon and servant of God, lived 31 years and rested in peace on the Nones of May in the year 633 (of the Spanish era, i.e., 595 AD).
EDCS-38000143.summary: This epitaph records the age and date of death of Iustus, a deacon and servant of God.

EDCS-38000144.translation: Victoria, immaculate virgin and handmaid of Christ in the name of God, lived half a year and rested in peace on the seventh day before the Ides of November in the fourth month.
EDCS-38000144.summary: This epitaph records the brief life of Victoria, an immaculate virgin and handmaid of Christ.

EDCS-38000148.translation: God protected the life of Argimir.
EDCS-38000148.summary: This inscription is a statement of divine protection over the life of Argimir.

EDCS-38100236.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Caius Tettonius Suriacus.
EDCS-38100236.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods (Dis Manibus) for Caius Tettonius Suriacus, likely a funerary inscription.

EDCS-38100237.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius Proculus.
EDCS-38100237.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to the Divine Manes by Lucius Valerius Proculus.

EDCS-38100243.translation: Lucius Connus, aged [X], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100243.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Connus and includes a common epitaph wishing him a light repose.

EDCS-38100244.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia [---] of Iunius.
EDCS-38100244.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription is a dedication to the Divine Manes, likely for a woman named Iulia.

EDCS-38100247.translation: Pompeia Atia, wife of Pullius, is buried here.
EDCS-38100247.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Pompeia Atia, identifying her as the wife of Pullius.

EDCS-38100249.translation: Lucius Aemilius Vitulus dedicated this altar to Ceres in the Martial district, a donated place.
EDCS-38100249.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Ceres by Lucius Aemilius Vitulus in a specific district.

EDCS-38100251.translation: Norban(a?), daughter of Avelius, Secunda, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-38100251.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a woman named Secunda, daughter of Avelius, aged 40.

EDCS-38100253.translation: Gaius Iulius Montanus, aged 8, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100253.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of an 8-year-old boy named Gaius Iulius Montanus and includes a standard epitaph.

EDCS-38100256.translation: Norban(a?), Sequanda, daughter of Quintus, aged 4, is here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100256.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a young girl, Sequanda, daughter of Quintus, aged 4.

EDCS-38100257.translation: Lucius Norbanus Longinus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100257.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Norbanus Longinus and includes a common epitaph.

EDCS-38100259.translation: Quintus Norbanus Victor, son of Quintus, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100259.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Norbanus Victor, son of Quintus, aged 30.

EDCS-38100262.translation: Roscia Maxsuma, daughter of Caius, aged [---]
EDCS-38100262.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Roscia Maxsuma by name, patronymic, and age.

EDCS-38100266.translation: Fuscus, son of Dobiter, from Lancium, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuscus, identifying him by name, patronymic, origin, and age, and includes a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-38100267.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. M. Geminus, aged 50, is buried here. Accia Iusta [erected this] for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-38100267.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the burial of M. Geminus and erected by his wife, Accia Iusta.

EDCS-38100268.translation: Annia, daughter of Boutus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lancinus, son of Nilocus, made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-38100268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annia, identifying her by name and parentage, with a dedication from her husband Lancinus.

EDCS-38100269.translation: Fronto, son of Clemens, aged 23, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100269.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fronto, identifying him by name, patronymic, and age, and includes a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-38100270.translation: Procula, daughter of Tonginus, aged [---]. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-38100270.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Procula by name, parentage, and age, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-38100271.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Alluqius by name and age.

EDCS-38100273.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs, possibly recording a monetary offering.

EDCS-38100278.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Urocius Dovilus vowed [this] willingly with his spirit.
EDCS-38100278.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Urocius Dovilus, indicating a vow made willingly.

EDCS-38100279.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Iuliana had this made for her mother, Iulia Avita.
EDCS-38100279.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording that Claudia Iuliana commissioned a monument for her mother, Iulia Avita.

EDCS-38100286.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius L.
EDCS-38100286.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Iulius L. to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits).

EDCS-38700002.translation: Sinticius, a slave of God nicknamed Deidomum, following his paternal lineage of the Getae, entrusted his spirit to God in peace in this humble tomb, having completed 60 years of life in this age, on the 6th day before the Ides of August in the year 670 (of the era). May peace be granted to you by God.
EDCS-38700002.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Sinticius, a slave of God, who died in the year 670 (of the era).

EDCS-38700003.translation: Severus, a presbyter and servant of Christ, lived 55 years and rested in the peace of the Lord on the 11th day before the Kalends of November in the year 622 (of the era).
EDCS-38700003.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Severus, a presbyter and servant of Christ, in the year 622 (of the era).

EDCS-38700004.translation: In the name of the Lord, Solidonus lived 65 years and rested in peace on the day of the Kalends of June in the era...
EDCS-38700004.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Solidonus, who lived 65 years and died on the Kalends of June, with the era year partially missing.

EDCS-38700008.translation: Taumastus, a servant of God, lived 53 years and 5 months and rested in peace on the 17th day before the Kalends of January in the year 570 (of the era).
EDCS-38700008.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Taumastus, a servant of God, who died in the year 570 (of the era).

EDCS-38700009.translation: Burial of Paulus, a servant of God, lived 51 years and rested in peace on the 3rd day before the Ides of March in the year 582 (of the era).
EDCS-38700009.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Paulus, a servant of God, who died in the year 582 (of the era).

EDCS-38700010.translation: Optatus, a servant of God, lived 52 years and rested in peace on the 8th day of August in the year 551 (of the era).
EDCS-38700010.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Optatus, a servant of God, who died in the year 551 (of the era).

EDCS-38700015.translation: Florentius, a servant of God, lived seventy-five years and rested in peace in the year 604 (of the era). Florentia, a servant of God, lived 31 years and rested in peace in the year 604 (of the era).
EDCS-38700015.summary: This inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Florentius and Florentia, both servants of God, who died in the year 604 (of the era).

EDCS-38700016.translation: Vicentius, a servant of God, lived 64 years and rested in peace on the 3rd day before the Kalends of December in the year of the Lord 520.
EDCS-38700016.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Vicentius, a servant of God, who died in the year 520 (of the era).

EDCS-38700017.translation: Saturius, a servant of God, lived 71 years and 1 month and 6 days, and having accepted repentance, rested in peace on the 8th day before the Kalends of February in the year 623 (of the era).
EDCS-38700017.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Saturius, a servant of God, who died in the year 623 (of the era) after accepting repentance.

EDCS-38700018.translation: Litorius, a servant of God, lived for more or less 75 years and rested in peace on the 7th day before the Kalends of July in the era 548.
EDCS-38700018.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Litorius, a servant of God, with a specific date of repose.

EDCS-38700095.summary: This is a short inscription, likely a blessing or a wish for the deceased Aloiosae to live in Christ.

EDCS-38700097.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial, possibly linking Emanuel to Reccaredo.

EDCS-38700099.translation: Here lie the remains of Iulianus, the bishop, who died on the 11th day before the Kalends of April in the year 1024 of the era. Reader, I ask you to pray, do not refuse, so that you may have Christ the Lord as protector.
EDCS-38700099.summary: This epitaph records the death of Bishop Iulianus and includes a plea for prayers from the reader.

EDCS-38700100.summary: This is a maker's inscription, indicating Garsia Alfonsi as the creator of the object or monument.

EDCS-38700101.translation: The venerable brother, Lord Munius Monis, lies here in his resting place in the year 1072 of the era.
EDCS-38700101.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lord Munius Monis, a venerable brother, with a specific date.

EDCS-38700102.translation: Daniel leaves the fearful funeral rites of athletes and is rightly joined to the heavenly soldiers, who was excellent, generous to the people, and venerable. Behold here lie the purified members of the bishop Lisimathus, accepted by the heavenly citadel, a fisherman. He died, struck by a small star, he enjoys heavenly joy. He is led by the gaze of the Lord in the month of January in the year 1030 plus 24, Daniel the bishop.
EDCS-38700102.summary: This complex epitaph commemorates Bishop Daniel (also referred to as Lisimathus), celebrating his virtues, heavenly reception, and death in January of the era 1054.

EDCS-38700318.translation: Fuscinus, son of Fuscus, set this up willingly with a devoted spirit to the goddess Domina Nabia.
EDCS-38700318.summary: This is a votive inscription by Fuscinus to the goddess Domina Nabia.

EDCS-38700319.translation: Rufina, a religious servant of Christ, lived for more or less 45 years and rested in the peace of the Lord on the 5th day before the Kalends of October in the era 625.
EDCS-38700319.summary: This inscription records the life and peaceful repose of Rufina, a religious servant of Christ.

EDCS-38700320.summary: This is a dedication inscription, likely a funerary monument, from Marcius Rufus to Dipaencia.

EDCS-38800070.translation: Gunthoerta, servant of God, lived 35 years and rested in peace on the fourth day before the Kalends of May, in the year 656 of the era.
EDCS-38800070.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and repose of Gunthoerta, a servant of God, dated to the 7th century AD (era DCLVI).

EDCS-38800071.translation: Allius Bebius, servant of God, 27 years; Cunde Marcianus, to a servant of God, 21 years.
EDCS-38800071.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages of two individuals, Allius Bebius and Cunde Marcianus, both identified as servants of God.

EDCS-38800072.translation: Martialis, the first, 25 years, servant of God, extinguished by the sorrow of the people.
EDCS-38800072.summary: A funerary inscription lamenting the death of Martialis, a 25-year-old servant of God, who was taken by the people's sorrow.

EDCS-38800293.summary: An inscription indicating the origin of an object, stating it was made in the workshop of Lucanus.

EDCS-38800295.translation: Caius Oppius Restitutus, a slave, made this.
EDCS-38800295.summary: An inscription recording that Caius Oppius Restitutus, a slave, was the maker of the object.

EDCS-38900210.summary: A short inscription or epitaph wishing Aelias a life in Christ.

EDCS-38900217.translation: The statue of Romulius and Norbanus.
EDCS-38900217.summary: An inscription identifying a statue dedicated to or depicting Romulius and Norbanus.

EDCS-38900223.translation: I, Licia, on the sixth day before the Kalends of June, in the year 658 of the era.
EDCS-38900223.summary: A personal inscription by Licia, recording a specific date in the era of 658 (7th century AD).

EDCS-38900235.translation: Talassa, servant of God, lived 44 years and rested in peace on the 15th day before the Kalends of September, in the year 682 of the era.
EDCS-38900235.summary: A funerary inscription detailing the lifespan and repose of Talassa, a servant of God, dated to the 7th century AD (era DLXXXII).

EDCS-38900236.translation: All of you, surround me and weep with me, because it is not a new thing to die. Mourn with me, all you whom the cause of grief and the affliction of death greatly touches. Promise yourselves to lamentation and worthy actions. Maura was my beautiful cousin, exceedingly lovely to behold and fair of face, whom her mother bore chaste and the earth received as a virgin and restored to the tomb without wrongdoing. Woe is me, miserable one, who lost such a flower of youth from my 15th year. Woe is me, abandoned, who, just when I began to rejoice, will then be abandoned and tormented by many griefs for you. I, Calandronius, pray God that he may grant you eternal rest. She rested in the peace of the Lord on the fourth day before the Kalends of August, in the era 703.
EDCS-38900236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 703 (445/446 AD) by Calandronius mourning his cousin Maura, whom he describes as chaste and beautiful.

EDCS-38900270.translation: Here lies Eutychus, son of Zosimus, who lived in Libystinis for the year 148 of the era.
EDCS-38900270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph in Greek from the era 148 (c. 150 AD) for Eutychus, son of Zosimus, who lived in Libystinis.

EDCS-38900271.translation: Faustianus departed in peace on the seventh day before the Ides of November in the era 508.
EDCS-38900271.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 508 (450/451 AD) for Faustianus, who died and rested in peace.

EDCS-38900272.translation: Fistellus, an honorable man, lived for 70 years and rested in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of December in the era 548.
EDCS-38900272.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 548 (490/491 AD) for Fistellus, an honorable man, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-38900273.translation: Auriola, an honorable woman, lived for 27 years and rested in peace on the fourth day of the Ides of May in the era 548.
EDCS-38900273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 548 (490/491 AD) for Auriola, an honorable woman, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-38900274.translation: Exuperius, doorkeeper, servant of God, lived for 70 years and rested in peace on the seventh day before the Ides of July in the era 566.
EDCS-38900274.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 566 (508/509 AD) for Exuperius, a doorkeeper and servant of God, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-38900275.translation: Abundantius, servant of God, lived for 27 years and rested in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of February in the era 567.
EDCS-38900275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 567 (509/510 AD) for Abundantius, a servant of God, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-38900276.translation: Veranianus, servant of God, lived for [---] years and rested in peace on the fourth day before the Kalends of June in the era 569.
EDCS-38900276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 569 (511/512 AD) for Veranianus, a servant of God, whose age at death is not specified.

EDCS-38900277.translation: Fortunata, servant of God, lived for 22 years and rested in peace on the fifth day before the Kalends of January in the era 565.
EDCS-38900277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 565 (507/508 AD) for Fortunata, a servant of God, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-38900278.translation: Savinianus, servant of God, lived for 18 years and rested in peace on the fifteenth day before the Kalends of August in the era 581.
EDCS-38900278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the era 581 (523/524 AD) for Savinianus, a servant of God, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-38900279.translation: Senatrex, servant of God, lived 18 years and rested in the peace of the Lord on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of March in the year 604.
EDCS-38900279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Senatrex, a female servant of God, dated to 604 AD.

EDCS-38900281.translation: Flavianus, servant of God, lived and rested.
EDCS-38900281.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Flavianus, a servant of God.

EDCS-38900283.translation: Sabinus, an honorable man, lived 75 years and rested in peace on the third day before the Ides of March in the year 555.
EDCS-38900283.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sabinus, an honorable man, dated to 555 AD.

EDCS-39000177.translation: Caera, daughter of Sailcus, 40 years old, is placed here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caera, daughter of Sailcus, indicating her burial place and a wish for peace.

EDCS-39000178.translation: Celsius, son of Caius, is placed here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000178.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celsius, son of Caius, indicating his burial place and a wish for peace.

EDCS-39000179.translation: Clemens, son of Arronis, part of Carvia.
EDCS-39000179.summary: This inscription likely records the name Clemens and his familial or geographical connection to Arronis and Carvia.

EDCS-39000180.summary: This inscription records the name Cloutia and her parentage, likely as a dedication or identification.

EDCS-39000181.translation: Dobiteina, daughter of Aulon, 15 years old, is placed here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dobiteina, daughter of Aulon, indicating her burial place and a wish for peace.

EDCS-39000182.translation: Domitio, son of Bassinus, 60 years old, is placed here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitio, son of Bassinus, indicating his burial place and a wish for peace.

EDCS-39000184.summary: This inscription records the name Dovitena and her parentage, likely as a dedication or identification.

EDCS-39000185.translation: Fronto, son of Silon, aged 20, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fronto, recording his name, parentage, and age, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-39000189.translation: Laboina, daughter of Alais, aged 55, lies here; may the earth be light on you. Aurelius Lucius, aged 50, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burials and ages of two individuals, Laboina and Aurelius Lucius, with a common epitaphic formula.

EDCS-39000190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lapoena, recording her name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-39000191.translation: Lapona, daughter of Lucius, aged 12, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lapona, recording her name, parentage, age, and a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-39000192.translation: Lucius Accius Caeno, aged 50, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Accius Caeno, recording his name, age, and a common epitaphic formula.

EDCS-39000193.translation: Maela, daughter of Severus, aged 25, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maela, recording her name, parentage, age, and a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-39000194.translation: Medusinus, son of Adri, aged 20, is here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Medusinus, recording his name, parentage, age, and a standard epitaphic formula.

EDCS-39000195.translation: Modestinus, son of Modestus, aged 45, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000195.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestinus, recording his name, parentage, age, and a common epitaphic formula.

EDCS-39000196.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Mun Lupercus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-39000197.translation: Quinta, aged 65, lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-39000197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quinta, recording her age and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-39000199.summary: This epitaph records the age of Rusus, son of Albus.

EDCS-39000200.translation: [...]us, son of Julius, aged 40, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000200.summary: This epitaph records the death of a man identified as a son of Julius, aged 40.

EDCS-39000203.translation: Placidus, aged 60. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-39000203.summary: This epitaph records the death of Placidus, aged 60, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000205.translation: [...]cio, son of Tancinus, aged 40, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000205.summary: This epitaph records the death of a man identified as a son of Tancinus, aged 40.

EDCS-39000207.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avita Op, aged 45, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000207.summary: This epitaph records the death of Avita Op, aged 45, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caburanus, aged 15, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000208.summary: This epitaph records the death of Caburanus, aged 15, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000210.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Estius Seapi, aged 5, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000210.summary: This epitaph records the death of Estius Seapi, aged 5, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia, daughter of Flavius, aged 25, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000211.summary: This epitaph records the death of Flavia, daughter of Flavius, aged 25, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000212.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunius, son of Avitus, aged 3, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39000212.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iunius, son of Avitus, aged 3, and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000213.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lea.
EDCS-39000213.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lea and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-39000214.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Modestinus, aged 50.
EDCS-39000214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestinus, aged 50.

EDCS-39000215.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Placidus, son of Reburrus, aged 7. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Reburrina, daughter of Reburrus, aged 5. Here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Placidus and Reburrina, both children.

EDCS-39000217.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius, aged 20.
EDCS-39000217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius, aged 20.

EDCS-39000222.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ianua, aged [X]. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000222.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianua, with a wish for the earth to be light upon her.

EDCS-39000223.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Modius, son of Modius.
EDCS-39000223.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modius, son of Modius.

EDCS-39000230.translation: Aisus, son of Semelis, aged 60. Here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000230.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aisus, son of Semelis, aged 60.

EDCS-39000231.translation: Allia Paterna, aged 35. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Allia Paterna, aged 35.

EDCS-39000232.translation: Apana, daughter of Boutis, aged 20. Placed here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000232.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apana, daughter of Boutis, aged 20.

EDCS-39000234.translation: Caburus, son of Rufus, aged 60. Placed here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-39000234.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caburus, son of Rufus, aged 60.

EDCS-39700026.translation: To Lucius Vocontius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Paullus, for his faithful and constant defense of public interests before the most distinguished council, served as aedile, quaestor twice, duumvir twice, flamen of the divine and Augustan emperors in Rome, prefect of the I Cohort of Lusitanians and the I Cohort of Vettones, centurion of the III Italian Legion, and undertook an unpaid legation in Rome for his state; Liberalitas Julia Ebora, in the forum.
EDCS-39700026.summary: This is a dedication honoring Lucius Vocontius Paullus for his extensive public service in Ebora, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-39700048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aquilia Rufina had this erected for her most devoted father, Marcus Aquilius Aquilo of Interamna, aged 57.
EDCS-39700048.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aquilius Aquilo, erected by his daughter Aquilia Rufina.

EDCS-39700050.summary: A very brief epitaph, possibly a good luck charm or a dedication.

EDCS-39700071.summary: A short epitaph recording the name and age of Publius Lupus Bassus.

EDCS-39700076.translation: To our Emperor Caesar Germanicus Pius Felix Augustus, father of his country, proconsul, the 107th year [of the era].
EDCS-39700076.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Germanicus, likely commemorative, dated to the 107th year of an unspecified era.

EDCS-39700079.summary: A dedication to the emperors Flavius Julius, likely part of a longer inscription.

EDCS-39700081.translation: T. Meiduenius Lucinus gladly fulfilled his vow to the Divine Lucina.
EDCS-39700081.summary: A votive inscription by T. Meiduenius Lucinus to the goddess Lucina.

EDCS-39800472.translation: For the Lares, for the safety and security of the household. The neighbours, together with their companions, the games and the feast donated by its master Q. Sertorius, his domestic staff, his freedmen Q. Sertor Hermes, Q. Sertor Cepalus, and Q. Sertor Anteros.
EDCS-39800472.summary: A dedication to the Lares by neighbours and freedmen for the safety of a household belonging to Q. Sertorius, likely including a record of festival celebrations.

EDCS-39800473.translation: To our eternal Lords, the Augusti, Emperors Aurelius Valerius Diocletian and M. Aurelius Valerius Maximian, pious, happy, always Augusti. The boundary stones between Pacens and Eborens, by care of P. Datianus, vice-president, most devoted to our Lords. The people of Pacens.
EDCS-39800473.summary: A boundary stone inscription marking the territory between Pacens and Eborens, dedicated to Emperors Diocletian and Maximian during the prefecture of P. Datianus.

EDCS-39800483.translation: Lucius Coutius Bouti gladly fulfilled his vow to Asitrita.
EDCS-39800483.summary: A votive inscription by Lucius Coutius Bouti to the deity Asitrita.

EDCS-39900611.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Accia Peregrina, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 80.
EDCS-39900611.summary: A funerary epitaph for Accia Peregrina, erected by her own means or by a benefactor.

EDCS-39900612.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone identifying Orio as a slave of Macia.

EDCS-39900618.summary: This fragment appears to be the beginning of a tomb inscription, identifying Melamnius, son of Cancilius.

EDCS-39900619.translation: Cosillus, son of Tuapio, M. C....
EDCS-39900619.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely names Cosillus, son of Tuapio.

EDCS-39900621.translation: Verruciius, son of Ve..., willingly paid his vow to Belona.
EDCS-39900621.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made willingly by Verruciius to the goddess Belona.

EDCS-39900623.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Crastena Vitalis set this up willingly in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-39900623.summary: This is a votive dedication to the divine spirits by Crastena Vitalis.

EDCS-39900629.translation: Norbana, daughter of Quintus, Secunda, aged 1 year, [died]...
EDCS-39900629.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription for Norbana Secunda, daughter of Quintus, who died at age one.

EDCS-39900635.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Iulius Lascivi Ibarra, aged 33, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, father; the son had this made for his father Lascivi.
EDCS-39900635.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Lucius Iulius Lascivi Ibarra, aged 33, and was commissioned by his son.

EDCS-39900636.translation: Gaius Norbanus Gracilis, son of Gaius, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39900636.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Gaius Norbanus Gracilis, aged 20.

EDCS-39900637.translation: Iulia Boutia, daughter of Gaius, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Alburus had this made.
EDCS-39900637.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Iulia Boutia, aged 80, and was commissioned by Alburus.

EDCS-39900639.translation: Iulia Turpilla, daughter of Talonis, aged 55. Norbanus Saturninus had this made for his mother. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-39900639.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Iulia Turpilla, aged 55, and was commissioned by her son, Norbanus Saturninus.

EDCS-39900641.translation: Gaius Norbanus Rufus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His wife saw to the making of this.
EDCS-39900641.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Norbanus Rufus, aged 40, whose wife commissioned the monument.

EDCS-39900642.translation: Caecilia Coemea, daughter of Quintus, aged 90, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-39900642.summary: A tombstone for Caecilia Coemea, aged 90.

EDCS-39900646.translation: Macrio, son of Pompeia, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-39900646.summary: A tombstone for Macrio, son of Pompeia, aged 75.

EDCS-42700162.translation: Primigenia, slave of Lucius Mussidius, aged 8, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700162.summary: A tombstone for Primigenia, slave of Lucius Mussidius, aged 8.

EDCS-42700163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Priscilla, aged 12, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Laberia Amabilis, her mother, made this.
EDCS-42700163.summary: A tombstone for Priscilla, aged 12, made by her mother Laberia Amabilis.

EDCS-42700164.translation: Puccia Xanthippe, freedwoman of Publius, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700164.summary: A tombstone for Puccia Xanthippe, freedwoman of Publius, aged 25.

EDCS-42700165.translation: Roucia Maxima, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700165.summary: A tombstone for Roucia Maxima, aged 50.

EDCS-42700166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sentonia Lucrosa had this made for her son Sentonius Sacomius, aged 9, most dutifully. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700166.summary: A tombstone for Sentonius Sacomius, aged 9, made by his mother Sentonia Lucrosa.

EDCS-42700167.translation: Servilia Fausta, freedwoman of Marcus, made this for Marcus Servilius, son of Marcus, and for Pompeia Helena, freedwoman of Lucius, and for Marcus Servilius Pollio, son of Spurius.
EDCS-42700167.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Servilia Fausta for several family members, including Marcus Servilius and Marcus Servilius Pollio.

EDCS-42700168.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Satrianio made this for his son Compse, aged 30.
EDCS-42700168.summary: A funerary monument made by Satrianio for his son Compse, aged 30.

EDCS-42700169.translation: Tursidia, daughter of Lucius, lies buried here.
EDCS-42700169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tursidia, daughter of Lucius, stating she is buried here.

EDCS-42700170.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valerius Onesiphorus, aged 17, lies buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Onesumus made this for his deserving freedman.
EDCS-42700170.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Onesiphorus, aged 17, with his freedman Onesumus as the dedicator.

EDCS-42700172.translation: Publius Vettius Felix, freedman of Publius, lies buried here; 12 feet in front and 8 feet in depth.
EDCS-42700172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Vettius Felix, specifying the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-42700174.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vibius Crusidus, aged 20. Accius Hyncan lies buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vibius Crusidus, aged 20, with Accius Hyncan as the dedicator.

EDCS-42700175.translation: Lucius Vibius Faustus, father; Coronia, his freedwoman.
EDCS-42700175.summary: This inscription likely relates to a familial or social relationship, possibly a dedication or property record, involving Lucius Vibius Faustus and his freedwoman Coronia.

EDCS-42700177.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vitalis lived 20 years, 5 months, and [some] days.
EDCS-42700177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitalis, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-42700180.translation: Aequania Maura, freedwoman of Quintus, lies buried here.
EDCS-42700180.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aequania Maura, freedwoman of Quintus.

EDCS-42700181.summary: This inscription likely records the death of Severianus, aged 53, possibly erected by Severia Marcia.

EDCS-42700184.translation: She took care to have this made for her mother who deserved well of her. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700184.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed mother, erected by her daughter.

EDCS-42700185.translation: Omius, son of [---], aged 11 months, lies here. Fatis, aged 8 years. As you pass by, say: May the earth lie lightly on you. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-42700185.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two children, Omius (11 months) and Fatis (8 years), with a standard epitaph and a wish for peace for both.

EDCS-42700186.translation: Here lies Macer Modestus, son of Galeria, of the census, of the tribe Galeria. Paculla and Seranus, his parents, made this.
EDCS-42700186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Macer Modestus, set up by his parents, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700187.translation: Camira for herself and for Rustica, aged 30, daughter of Papiria. Rufo.
EDCS-42700187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Camira and Rustica, mentioning a dedication by Rufo, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700188.translation: Attennius, father, had this made for his most dutiful daughter. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you. She lived 10 years. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700188.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a daughter, set up by her father, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700189.translation: Patricia lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Tutilius Taporio had this made for his incomparable wife Nnia Prisca. Atrius.
EDCS-42700189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Patricia, set up by her husband Tutilius Taporio, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700193.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Badio, aged 45, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700193.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Badio, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700195.translation: Herennuleia and Herennuleius, aged 10, lie here.
EDCS-42700195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herennuleia and Herennuleius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700199.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. H. s. for his most dutiful [son/relative], aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700199.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a dutiful individual, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700200.translation: Vic Valerius, a slave, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vic Valerius, a slave, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700202.translation: A father for his most dutiful son. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a son, set up by his father, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700203.translation: Aemilius Feletus and Rufria Felicia had this made for their daughter. Julius Marcus had this made for his dearest wife. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a daughter and a wife, set up by Aemilius Feletus, Rufria Felicia, and Julius Marcus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-42700205.translation: For us [or For Lucius, with restoration] of 20 years.
EDCS-42700205.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for a person named Lucius who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-42700206.translation: Auri (daughter) of Publius, from her own property, took care to have this made.
EDCS-42700206.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription where Auri, daughter of Publius, funded the construction of the monument.

EDCS-42700207.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Itia, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 14 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Itia, a freedwoman of Marcus, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-42700208.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iucunus, aged 10 years, (buried according to the) rites of Vernaclus.
EDCS-42700208.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Iucunus who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-42700210.translation: T. [Lentulus?], son of T. [Lentulus?], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual named T. Lentulus, likely a Roman citizen, indicating he is buried in this location.

EDCS-42700214.translation: Marcus, son of Caius, of the tribe Aemilia.
EDCS-42700214.summary: This is likely an excerpt from a longer inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary text, identifying Marcus, son of Caius, and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-42700216.translation: Pomponius Farin, husband, made the monument. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700216.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Pomponius Farin erected a monument for his wife.

EDCS-42700220.translation: r and Lucius, by his will, made (this) for 26 and 22 years.
EDCS-42700220.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly mentioning Lucius and another individual, with ages of death indicated as 26 and 22, likely set up by Lucius's will.

EDCS-42700221.translation: Lucius, son of Pa . . ., Julia L. Felginas (and) their heir will hold this monument.
EDCS-42700221.summary: This inscription concerns the ownership or inheritance of a monument, possibly a tomb, belonging to the family of Lucius and Julia.

EDCS-42700227.translation: us and Anio with their own [people/property]...
EDCS-42700227.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, likely a dedication or funerary text, mentioning individuals named 'us' (a suffix) and Anio.

EDCS-42700229.translation: May the earth lie lightly upon you, alumna, who deserved well.
EDCS-42700229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a deserving alumna, possibly a student, expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-42700230.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pu Va.
EDCS-42700230.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) by someone named Pu Va.

EDCS-42700233.summary: This is a dedication to Valsteius, likely erected by his brother.

EDCS-42700237.translation: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife for the deceased.

EDCS-42700241.translation: Gaius Mattius, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-42700241.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Gaius Mattius, aged 40.

EDCS-42700246.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. A uro.
EDCS-42700246.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) by someone named A uro.

EDCS-42700249.translation: M. D[---] is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of M. D[---] and expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-42700250.translation: Had it made for [her] most indulgent and most dutiful son.
EDCS-42700250.summary: This is a funerary dedication, likely by a mother, for her exceptionally kind and devoted son.

EDCS-42700263.summary: This inscription likely records that someone named Draco[---] arranged for the erection of the monument or funerary rites.

EDCS-42700266.summary: This is a dedication by Valeria, freedwoman of Caius, likely for a religious or funerary purpose.

EDCS-42700271.summary: This inscription is a fragment likely naming an individual from Emerita.

EDCS-42700297.translation: To Gaius Julius Macro, son of Gaius, from Interamna(?).
EDCS-42700297.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Julius Macro, son of Gaius, possibly from Interamna.

EDCS-42700300.translation: Urban, father, legate of the emperor...
EDCS-42700300.summary: This inscription names Urban, described as a father and legate of the emperor.

EDCS-42700310.summary: This inscription names Aurelius, son of Decimus.

EDCS-42700314.summary: This inscription names Publius, son of Publius.

EDCS-42700325.summary: This inscription is a fragmented name, possibly starting with Quintus.

EDCS-42700342.summary: This inscription is a fragmented name, possibly starting with Julius.

EDCS-42700343.summary: This inscription names Marcus Frutuosus, son of Crispus.

EDCS-42700372.summary: This inscription fragment appears to name Marcus Aemilius Lepidus.

EDCS-42700384.translation: of the cohort of the legion...
EDCS-42700384.summary: This inscription fragment refers to a unit within a Roman legion.

EDCS-42700385.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary dedication or funerary text, with only names and possible filiation preserved.

EDCS-42700398.translation: To Svellia, daughter of Lucius, Vi...
EDCS-42700398.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication or funerary monument for Svellia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-42700402.translation: The son of Manius, a military tribune under the propraetor, a Lusitanian spoil, was slain here; may the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-42700402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a military tribune serving under a propraetor, who was killed during a campaign in Lusitania, likely dating to the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-42700404.translation: For the safety of Quintus C. P. Fontus.
EDCS-42700404.summary: This is an inscription recording a vow or dedication made for the well-being of Quintus C. P. Fontus.

EDCS-42700406.translation: Rufus gladly paid his vow to all the gods.
EDCS-42700406.summary: This is a votive inscription by Rufus, who willingly paid a vow to all the gods.

EDCS-42700410.translation: Trophimus paid his vow to Edigenius Domninicus.
EDCS-42700410.summary: This is a votive inscription by Trophimus, dedicating a vow to Edigenius Domninicus.

EDCS-42700411.translation: Marcus Antistius Marcellianus paid his vow gladly.
EDCS-42700411.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Antistius Marcellianus, who willingly paid his vow.

EDCS-42700412.translation: For the safety of Lucius Mitonius Frontonis, Dionisius paid his vow gladly.
EDCS-42700412.summary: This is a votive inscription by Dionisius, who willingly paid a vow for the safety of Lucius Mitonius Frontonis.

EDCS-42700414.translation: To Coronia, daughter of Publius, Procula, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-42700414.summary: This inscription is likely a public dedication or honorific monument to Coronia Procula, enacted by the local town council.

EDCS-42700416.translation: ...nio, son of Caius, Galba, as *duumvir*, of Iulia Augusta...
EDCS-42700416.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a magistrate, possibly a *duumvir* or other official, associated with Iulia Augusta, and might date to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-42700417.translation: Sergius Modestus, son of Marcus, priest of the deified Augustus, duovir, prefect of engineers, priest of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-42700417.summary: This is an epitaph for Sergius Modestus, listing his various priesthoods and civic offices, likely from Roman Lusitania.

EDCS-42700418.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to name Aulus Papirius, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-42700420.summary: This fragment likely names a praetorian guard named Niger, part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-42700421.translation: Aurelia Cantinia Severa, daughter of Lucius, wife of Rufo, a veteran of Legio X Gemina, and of Modestinus, and of Aurelius Lucius, made this in his own property.
EDCS-42700421.summary: This epitaph records Aurelia Cantinia Severa dedicating a tomb to her husband Lucius, a veteran of Legio X Gemina, and possibly her father-in-law Modestinus and father Aurelius Lucius.

EDCS-42700422.translation: Marcus Cornelius Pollio, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, and Marcus Cornelius Urbano, and Marcus Cornelius Celer, and Cornelia Iucunda, freedwoman of Marcus. May Fortune never allow you to know grief other than that which has passed. How pious you were, O youth, to be given to the flames. It will be a grief to know this now. He himself asks, whoever you are who attends, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you.' Here they are buried.
EDCS-42700422.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Cornelius Pollio and other Cornelius family members, lamenting the death of a young man and expressing wishes for his repose.

EDCS-42700423.translation: Iulia Annula, daughter of Caius, is buried here, snatched away by a wicked fate, whom death took in a short time, who had already completed nearly eighteen years. As you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you.' Candilla, freedwoman of Caius, 24 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. And again, 'May the earth lie lightly on you.' Lucius Iulius Felix, freedman of Teucer, took care to have this made at his own expense, 9 feet in depth, 12 feet in frontage.
EDCS-42700423.summary: This is a double epitaph for Iulia Annula and Candilla, with a final clause by Lucius Iulius Felix who paid for the tomb, indicating it was a burial plot with specific dimensions.

EDCS-42700424.translation: Iuventia Urbica, aged 21, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iuventia Primitiva made this for her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-42700424.summary: This is an epitaph for Iuventia Urbica, erected by her mother, Iuventia Primitiva.

EDCS-42700425.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Titus Vettius Pomponianus, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Caesia Felicissima made this for her most devoted husband while she was still alive.
EDCS-42700425.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Vettius Pomponianus, paid for by his wife Caesia Felicissima during her lifetime.

EDCS-42700429.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Crescens, from Emerita, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700429.summary: This is an epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Crescens, a 60-year-old man from Emerita.

EDCS-42700431.translation: Publius Antistius Niger, son of Publius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Lucceius Arogus, freedman of Titus, from Italica.
EDCS-42700431.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Antistius Niger and Titus Lucceius Arogus, one of whom may have paid for the tomb.

EDCS-42700432.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Norbana Severa of Lancium, aged 55, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Iulia Procula had this made at her own expense for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-42700432.summary: This is a tombstone for Norbana Severa, with her daughter Iulia Procula commissioning the monument.

EDCS-42700433.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rutilia, freedwoman, of Ossetana, aged 15, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Iulius Ulus made this for her as one deserving well.
EDCS-42700433.summary: This is a tombstone for the freedwoman Rutilia, commissioned by Iulius Ulus.

EDCS-42700434.translation: He of Romulensis is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700434.summary: This is a tombstone for an unidentified individual from Romulensis.

EDCS-42700436.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilia Secunda, daughter of Gaius, of Tingis, aged 67, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Helvia Rusticilla had this made.
EDCS-42700436.summary: This is a tombstone for Servilia Secunda, with Helvia Rusticilla commissioning the monument.

EDCS-42700437.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aelius Maximus, aged 35, and Octavia Rhodine, aged 30, are placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Pecio made this for his well-deserving patrons at his own expense.
EDCS-42700437.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Aelius Maximus and Octavia Rhodine, commissioned by Pecio.

EDCS-42700438.translation: Cnaeus Aemilius Fundanus, freedman of Amanda, is placed here.
EDCS-42700438.summary: This is a tombstone for Cnaeus Aemilius Fundanus, a freedman.

EDCS-42700439.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aesyria Epithymete, aged 45, is placed here. Caecilius Asclepiacus and Irinaeus made this for her as one deserving. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700439.summary: This is a tombstone for Aesyria Epithymete, commissioned by Caecilius Asclepiacus and Irinaeus.

EDCS-42700441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Afinius Deucalio, aged 18. Afinia Trepte, his most dutiful mother, made this for her son. He is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700441.summary: This is a tombstone for Afinius Deucalio, commissioned by his mother Afinia Trepte.

EDCS-42700443.translation: Lucius Allius Firmo [son?]. Lucius Allius Nereus. Decimus Iulius Fuscus. Libertas. Lucius Allius Crescens. Allia Corinthia.
EDCS-42700443.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly members of a family or household, with some names possibly indicating familial relationships or freed status.

EDCS-42700444.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Annius Polytimus, aged 60. Alexandria, his wife, made this for her well-deserving husband. He is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700444.summary: This is a tombstone for Annius Polytimus, commissioned by his wife Alexandria.

EDCS-42700445.translation: Antonia Cruseis, companion of Antoni Ursianus, aged 45, Iunius Petrius made this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-42700445.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Iunius Petrius for his wife Antonia Cruseis, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-42700447.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Asuria Epithumete, aged 50, for her most deserving Publi Caecilius Threpiarion. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Asuria Epithumete for her husband Publi Caecilius Threpiarion, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-42700448.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caelia Gaionilla, aged 26. Lucius Caelius Victor, son, and Lucius Caelius Lucifer, husband, made this for their most dutiful wife. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the son and husband for Caelia Gaionilla, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-42700449.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Augustinus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Calpurnius Augustinus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-42700451.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Cornelia, aged [..]. Marcus Cornelius [made this] for his wife, dutiful.
EDCS-42700451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia, whose age and the name of her husband Marcus Cornelius are partially preserved.

EDCS-42700453.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Syra, wife, for her most dutiful husband Crescens, aged 51. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-42700453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Syra for her husband Crescens, who died at the age of 51.

EDCS-42700455.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tutilius Avitianus for his incomparable wife Fabia Veneria, aged 32. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700455.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Tutilius Avitianus for his wife Fabia Veneria, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-42700457.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Flora, aged 8 years, 8 months, and 16 days. Nicephorus, father, made this for his most dutiful daughter. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Nicephorus for his daughter Flora, who died at the age of 8 years, 8 months, and 16 days.

EDCS-42700459.translation: Gaius Fuficius Clemens, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. In front, [..] feet.
EDCS-42700459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Fuficius Clemens, specifying his tribal affiliation and including a measurement of the plot.

EDCS-42700460.translation: Gaiolus, aged 5, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. The father placed [this] from his own property.
EDCS-42700460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaiolus, who died at the age of 5, with the father noted as the dedicator.

EDCS-42700462.translation: Iulia Lubea, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-42700462.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Lubea, a freedwoman, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-42700463.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Vitula, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700463.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Iulia Vitula, aged 30, with a common funerary formula.

EDCS-42700464.translation: To Marcus Iulius Balbus, son of Maxilo.
EDCS-42700464.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Iulius Balbus, son of Maxilo, likely for a monument or tomb.

EDCS-42700465.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Emeritus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, aged 42, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Gaius Iulius Modestinus had this made for his brother.
EDCS-42700465.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gaius Iulius Emeritus, aged 42, with the monument erected by his brother, Gaius Iulius Modestinus.

EDCS-42700466.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulio Eugenitor, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Nereis made this for her most devoted father.
EDCS-42700466.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Iulio Eugenitor, aged 70, with the monument erected by his devoted daughter, Iulia Nereis.

EDCS-42700467.translation: Lucius Iulius Urbicus, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. [This was made by] Iulius Colonus for his most devoted son.
EDCS-42700467.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Iulius Urbicus, aged 22, with the monument erected by his devoted father, Iulius Colonus.

EDCS-42700468.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunia Agatha, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iunius Agatonicus made this for his freedwoman.
EDCS-42700468.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Iunia Agatha, aged 25, with the monument erected by Iunius Agatonicus for his freedwoman.

EDCS-42700470.translation: The bones of Caius Iunius Pinus, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-42700470.summary: This inscription indicates the location of the bones of Caius Iunius Pinus, a freedman.

EDCS-42700471.translation: Publius Licinius Crescens and Licinia Tituria, aged 37, are buried here.
EDCS-42700471.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Licinius Crescens and Licinia Tituria, both aged 37.

EDCS-42700473.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilius.
EDCS-42700473.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the departed spirits, likely marking a tomb for someone named Manilius.

EDCS-42700475.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Myrinus, a slave, aged 12, Claudia Marciana arranged for this to be made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700475.summary: This is a tombstone for Myrinus, a 12-year-old slave, arranged by Claudia Marciana.

EDCS-42700476.translation: Narcissus, slave of Lucius Iulius Metellus Bassus, aged 3, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700476.summary: This is a tombstone for Narcissus, a 3-year-old slave of Lucius Iulius Metellus Bassus.

EDCS-42700477.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Norbanus Narcissus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Vibius Norbanus arranged for this to be made for his father.
EDCS-42700477.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Norbanus Narcissus, aged 60, arranged by his son Vibius Norbanus.

EDCS-42700478.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Vestalis.
EDCS-42700478.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Vestalis.

EDCS-42700479.translation: Petronia Agilis is buried here. Aponia Serana provided the burial place and this monument for her companion.
EDCS-42700479.summary: This tombstone records that Aponia Serana provided a burial place and monument for Petronia Agilis, her companion.

EDCS-42700480.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Petrusidius Crescenti, who died at 24 months old.

EDCS-42700481.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Pompeianus, most dutiful son, aged 20 years, 3 months, 23 days. Simplicius and his wife Pompeia, his parents, arranged for this to be made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700481.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by parents Simplicius and Pompeia to their son Pompeianus, who died at age 20 years, 3 months, 23 days.

EDCS-42700482.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Pompeia Attica, aged 37, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her devoted mother, Pompeia Clara, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-42700482.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Pompeia Clara to her devoted daughter Pompeia Attica, aged 37.

EDCS-42700483.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Pompeius Aelianus made this for his freedman Pompeius Colonus, aged 25. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-42700483.summary: This tombstone records that Pompeius Aelianus made it for his freedman Pompeius Colonus, aged 25.

EDCS-42700484.translation: Lucius Pompeius Diadumenus, freedman of Lucius Pompeius, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Hedylus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-42700484.summary: This tombstone records that Hedylus arranged the burial for Lucius Pompeius Diadumenus, freedman of Lucius Pompeius, aged 30.

EDCS-43200003.translation: To Trebiae Vegetae, daughter of Severa Norbensis, her aunt.
EDCS-43200003.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Severa Norbensis to her aunt Trebiae Vegetae.

EDCS-43200004.translation: Regina, daughter of Tancinus, is laid here.
EDCS-43200004.summary: This is a tombstone for Regina, daughter of Tancinus.

EDCS-43200005.summary: This is a tombstone for Aivica, daughter of Anterus.

EDCS-43200007.translation: Felix and Briseis, pious parents, made this for Celsus, aged 71. May the earth lie lightly on you. Made at his own expense.
EDCS-43200007.summary: This is a tombstone for Celsus, erected by his parents Felix and Briseis.

EDCS-43200008.translation: Turolius, son of Virotus, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Pintamus made this for his father.
EDCS-43200008.summary: This is a tombstone for Turolius, son of Virotus, erected by his son Pintamus.

EDCS-43200009.translation: Severa, daughter of Irius, aged 5, is laid here. Tiberius Fabianus, her husband, took care of the making of this.
EDCS-43200009.summary: This is a tombstone for Severa, daughter of Irius, erected by her husband Tiberius Fabianus.

EDCS-43200011.translation: Gaius Julius Lacer erected this altar so that he might perform sacred rites to the gods.
EDCS-43200011.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Gaius Julius Lacer for religious purposes.

EDCS-43200012.translation: Gaius Julius Lacer is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-43200012.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Lacer.

EDCS-43200013.translation: Gaius Julius Lacer is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-43200013.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Lacer.

EDCS-43200014.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Aelius, son of Amilcar.

EDCS-43200015.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Coceius Lusitanus, son of Publius Italicus Coceius, a legionary veteran of the Lancians, a townsman of Pu$	extit{g}$ (probably for P$	extit{i}$a $	extit{U}$rsiana $	extit{g}$allorum), is buried here. His mother, Anais, made this for him, her freeborn son.
EDCS-43200015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Coceius Lusitanus, a veteran of a Roman legion, set up by his mother Anais.

EDCS-43200016.translation: Aemilia Severina erected this for her brother Aemilius Severinus, son of Calpurnianus, aged fifteen.
EDCS-43200016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Severinus, erected by his sister Aemilia Severina.

EDCS-43200017.translation: Marcus, son of Lougus the Clunian, aged thirty, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus, son of Lougus, a citizen of Lugdunum (modern Lyon), with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-43200019.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Ammius Rufus, son of Lucius Ammius Ligustinus.
EDCS-43200019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Ammius Ligustinus, set up by his son Lucius Ammius Rufus.

EDCS-43200020.translation: Cabura, daughter of Domitius, aged sixteen. Her mother Casina [made this].
EDCS-43200020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cabura, daughter of Domitius, erected by her mother Casina.

EDCS-43200021.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Cilea, daughter of Tancinus, aged fifty, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Ambatus and Boutia had this made for their mother, their sons.
EDCS-43200021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cilea, daughter of Tancinus, erected by her sons Ambatus and Boutia.

EDCS-43200029.translation: Apicius Satullus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Nymphs of Capera.
EDCS-43200029.summary: This is a votive inscription by Apicius Satullus to the Nymphs of Capera, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-43200030.translation: Vitia Ammira of Lamis willingly paid her vow with a willing spirit to the Nymphs of Capera.
EDCS-43200030.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vitia Ammira of Lamis to the Nymphs of Capera.

EDCS-43200031.translation: Prebeve willingly paid his vow with a willing spirit to the Nymphs of Capera.
EDCS-43200031.summary: This is a votive inscription by Prebeve to the Nymphs of Capera.

EDCS-43200032.translation: Avita, his own, placed this for Norbenus, with the care and expense of Avitus Moderatus.
EDCS-43200032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Norbenus, placed by Avita, with the support of Avitus Moderatus.

EDCS-43200034.translation: To the divine spirits. Gnaeus Marcius Stertinus, most beloved husband. Belcontus Sertor and Ocubus Eumelius Marcia, his unhappy daughter and son, placed this.
EDCS-43200034.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gnaeus Marcius Stertinus, set up by his wife and children.

EDCS-43200036.translation: Terentia, aged 23, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-43200036.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Terentia, aged 23.

EDCS-43200037.translation: Coria, daughter of Ambatus, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Mantaus Angeti and his son Casina Anderenus erected this.
EDCS-43200037.summary: A tombstone for Coria, aged 30, erected by Mantaus Angeti and his son Casina Anderenus.

EDCS-43200038.translation: Marcus Flavius Celsus placed this altar, which he dedicated, to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-43200038.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Flavius Celsus.

EDCS-43200039.translation: Caius Licinius Firminus, aged 15, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-43200039.summary: A tombstone for Caius Licinius Firminus, aged 15.

EDCS-43200043.translation: Secunda Helvia, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-43200043.summary: A tombstone for Secunda Helvia, aged 20.

EDCS-43200044.summary: A tombstone for Sextus Clodius Urbanus.

EDCS-43200045.translation: Tritius, son of Cloutus. His brothers placed this cippus for Caturo and Vir, aged 9, who lies here.
EDCS-43200045.summary: A tombstone for Vir, aged 9, erected by his brothers for him and Caturo.

EDCS-43200049.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Caius Pompeius Seranus and Terentia Vitalis, parents, made this for their four children.
EDCS-43200049.summary: A funerary dedication set up by parents for their four children.

EDCS-43200051.translation: To the divine spirits. Traveler, whom you see, you would think him to be the ashes of Iberus. You are mistaken; you see L. Cominius Camers, brave in war. Nor do I boast falsely, who was killed under Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus' son for liberty. Not Hercules, whom Gades worships, nor Bellona, whom the Camertes adore, nor all the Roman gods could snatch me from death when I fell.
