My soldiers, recognizing me not by my wounds, placed me here for a righteous cause. Farewell.
EDCS-43200051.summary: A soldier's epitaph, likely from the Civil Wars, detailing his bravery and death in service under Pompey's son, and his desire for remembrance despite his wounds.

EDCS-43200052.translation: How varied are the fates of men! Born among the Marsi, Domitius Thoranius, I reached the farthest lands, having followed unhappy arms under Gnaeus Pompeius; I fell here, struck by a wound. O happy Astigitanus, neither gods nor a better cause! Pitiful me, scarcely 20 years old, torn from death! Finally, Lucius Thoranius, born at Tusculum, a sudden levy and fire cremated me. And for three months [---], they erected this tombstone so far from my homeland.
EDCS-43200052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitius Thoranius, who died in battle far from his homeland, erected by his relative Lucius Thoranius.

EDCS-43200053.translation: I, Lucius, with my mother Vocuntia, who followed me four years and four days later, on the 4th day before the Nones of August, died. And I, who always protected my mother while alive, now I beg all mortals to allow her ashes to be touched. We lived innocently. This Gnaeus Pompeius followed, whom she had nursed with milk, and I fostered Gnaeus and other better persons.
EDCS-43200053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a freedman named Lucius, who died shortly after his mother Vocuntia and requests that her grave be respected, with Gnaeus Pompeius mentioned as a follower and foster-child.

EDCS-43200054.translation: To Fabio Metellus, son of Marcus, who fell with his fatherland when the Roman Republic was collapsing, Publius Servilius, son of Publius, willingly erected this tombstone for his friend, who chose death rather than see the hated light, as all things were heading towards the loss of Roman liberty.
EDCS-43200054.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Servilius for his friend Fabio Metellus, who died during the collapse of the Roman Republic.

EDCS-43200055.translation: To the Manes. Boutia, daughter of Boutus Dovilus, a Vestal virgin, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a Vestal virgin named Boutia, daughter of Boutus Dovilus.

EDCS-43200056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modesta, daughter of Septumius, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-43200058.translation: Aetura, daughter of Alucius, is buried here, aged 25. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aetura, daughter of Alucius, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-43200059.translation: To Libera. Caius Norbanus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-43200059.summary: This dedication is made by Caius Norbanus to the goddess Libera, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-43200060.translation: Lucius, aged 5, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His mother took care to have this made.
EDCS-43200060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius, who died at the age of 5, erected by his mother.

EDCS-43200061.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Titullus.
EDCS-43200061.summary: This is a dedication to the Divine Manes by Titullus.

EDCS-43200063.translation: Caecilia Aranta, daughter of Muntanus, is here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Aged 20.
EDCS-43200063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Aranta, daughter of Muntanus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-43200064.translation: Sacred to the holy deities. Claudius Samalis, son of Marcus, dedicated this altar with his own money.
EDCS-43200064.summary: An altar dedicated by Claudius Samalis, son of Marcus, to holy deities, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200065.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Quintus Caecilius Celtius.
EDCS-43200065.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintus Caecilius Celtius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200066.translation: May the earth be light on you. Quintia, slave of Maxuma, aged 3, is laid here.
EDCS-43200066.summary: A tombstone for Quintia, a 3-year-old slave of Maxuma, with a common funerary wish, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200068.translation: Gaius Norbanus Caturus, aged 50. Quintus Norbanus Niger took care to have this made for his brother.
EDCS-43200068.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Quintus Norbanus Niger for his brother Gaius Norbanus Caturus, aged 50, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200069.translation: This is the monument of Caburea, daughter of Aenimarus.
EDCS-43200069.summary: An inscription identifying a monument belonging to Caburea, daughter of Aenimarus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200070.translation: Norbania Maxuma, daughter of Ceabsus, aged 50, [died here].
EDCS-43200070.summary: A funerary inscription for Norbania Maxuma, daughter of Ceabsus, aged 50, likely dating to the Imperial period, with a lacuna.

EDCS-43200073.translation: Manlianus happily and willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-43200073.summary: A record of a vow fulfilled by Manlianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200075.translation: Norbana Anta, daughter of Quintus, aged 20. Her husband Caeno took care to have this made. She is laid here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-43200075.summary: A tombstone for Norbana Anta, daughter of Quintus, aged 20, commissioned by her husband Caeno, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200076.translation: Abitus, son of Caius, aged 20, is laid here. May it be...
EDCS-43200076.summary: A funerary inscription for Abitus, son of Caius, aged 20, with a common funerary wish, likely dating to the Imperial period, with a lacuna.

EDCS-43200077.translation: Norbanus Coutius, aged 60, and Norbanus Tanginus, aged 15, are laid here. May the earth be light on you. Tagana took care to have this made for her father and brother from her own means.
EDCS-43200077.summary: A funerary monument for Norbanus Coutius and Norbanus Tanginus, commissioned by Tagana for her father and brother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200078.summary: This is a votive inscription by an individual named Julius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-43200080.translation: Caius Noarcus Macer erected this sacred offering to Bellona at his own expense.
EDCS-43200080.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Bellona by Caius Noarcus Macer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200083.translation: Suriacus, son of Turanus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200083.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Suriacus, son of Turanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200084.translation: Norbana Victorina, daughter of Quintus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200084.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Norbana Victorina, daughter of Quintus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200085.translation: Iulia Fortunata for her daughter Partenis, slave of Accia Marciana, aged 40.
EDCS-43200085.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Fortunata for her daughter Partenis, who was a slave and has an associated age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200089.translation: Sacred to the Inferi, for Romana, wife of Flavius Tauri, an excellent woman.
EDCS-43200089.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed (Dis Manibus) for Romana, wife of Flavius Tauri, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200090.translation: Julius Saronis, aged 41, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His grandsons Marcus Cuntius Rufus and Fusigius saw to its erection.
EDCS-43200090.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Julius Saronis, aged 41, whose grandsons Marcus Cuntius Rufus and Fusigius commissioned the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200091.translation: Caesia, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200091.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caesia, aged 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200093.translation: Aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her husband Tiro saw to its erection.
EDCS-43200093.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an unnamed woman aged 25, commissioned by her husband Tiro, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200094.translation: Sacred to the Inferi. Anteros commissioned this monument to be erected for his tent-mate Sosumus Clementinus.
EDCS-43200094.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed (Dis Manibus) by Anteros for his contubernalis (tent-mate or partner) Sosumus Clementinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200095.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, his son Lucius Petronius Severus fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-43200095.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Lucius Petronius Severus for his son.

EDCS-43200096.translation: Tancinus, son of [---], aged 30, lies here. May the earth be light on you. His son made this.
EDCS-43200096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tancinus, erected by his son.

EDCS-43200098.translation: Restus, son of Peliqolus, aged 30, lies here. May the earth be light on you. F[---] P[---] V[---] R[---].
EDCS-43200098.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Restus, son of Peliqolus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-43200099.translation: Boutius, aged 10, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Caecilia, daughter of Coemeus, made this for her father.
EDCS-43200099.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Boutius, erected by his daughter Caecilia.

EDCS-43200100.translation: Ac [---] tulus lies here. His son erected this for his father.
EDCS-43200100.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ac [---] tulus, erected by his son.

EDCS-43200101.translation: Iulia Anula, daughter of Iulius, aged 65, lies here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-43200101.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Anula, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-43200102.translation: Norbana Anulla, aged 60, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Publius Caelius Tacitus, her son.
EDCS-43200102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbana Anulla, erected by her son Publius Caelius Tacitus.

EDCS-43200103.translation: She fulfilled her vow to Belona with willing spirit.
EDCS-43200103.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Belona.

EDCS-43200104.translation: Lucius Norbanus Rufus, with willing spirit, [built] an altar to Jupiter.
EDCS-43200104.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lucius Norbanus Rufus.

EDCS-43200105.translation: Soconia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, aged 9, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Caius Iulius, her father, made this.
EDCS-43200105.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Soconia Secunda, erected by her father Caius Iulius.

EDCS-43200106.translation: Amoena, daughter of Ancetius, aged 55, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amoena, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200107.translation: Gaius Norbanus Clemens Macandici lies here.
EDCS-43200107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Norbanus Clemens Macandici, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200108.translation: Gaius Norbanus Tancinus Ablicus lies here.
EDCS-43200108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Norbanus Tancinus Ablicus, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200110.translation: Severus, son of Titus, aged 14, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Severus, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200111.translation: Publius Iu V s ES Publius I SN Publius A IS
EDCS-43200111.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a meaningful translation or summary.

EDCS-43200112.translation: Publius Iulius Tapilus, son of Publius, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father took care to have this made.
EDCS-43200112.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Iulius Tapilus, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43200113.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a meaningful translation or summary.

EDCS-43200114.translation: Norbana Clara, freedwoman of Victor, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43200114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbana Clara, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300004.translation: Maro, aged 44, took care to have this made at his own expense.
EDCS-43300004.summary: This funerary inscription records Maro, who provided for his own burial, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300006.translation: Norbana Rufina, daughter of Rufinus, aged 45, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her sons took care to have this made.
EDCS-43300006.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbana Rufina, dated by its formula to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300007.translation: Surus, freedman of Nabias Tancinus, willingly dedicated this altar and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-43300007.summary: A freedman named Surus dedicates an altar and fulfills a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-43300008.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Lucius Caecilius Crescens.

EDCS-43300010.summary: An incomplete dedication or funerary inscription mentioning Lucius Valerius Crescent(---).

EDCS-43300011.translation: Severa, daughter of Turaesus, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Her brother Turcaudus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-43300011.summary: A funerary epitaph for Severa, commissioned by her brother, requesting a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300012.translation: Publius Calpurnius Rusticus, freedman of Lucius, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300012.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Calpurnius Rusticus, requesting a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300014.translation: Peloris, servant of Caius Albicius Fuscinius, aged 3, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300014.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Peloris, a slave, requesting a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300017.translation: Here lies Verunus, [son?] of C. Tancinius Tercie(?); may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300017.summary: A funerary epitaph for Verunus, with a missing parentage, requesting a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300018.translation: Quadratus, son of Alabus, aged 80, is laid here.
EDCS-43300018.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Quadratus.

EDCS-43300023.translation: Marcus Blaesius Vegetus is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300023.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Blaesius Vegetus, requesting a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-43300024.translation: Celtius had this made for his father Coutius.
EDCS-43300024.summary: A funerary or commemorative inscription where Celtius honors his father Coutius.

EDCS-43300025.translation: Surus, son of Tancinus, lies here. His friends set up this monument with due rite.
EDCS-43300025.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Surus, son of Tancinus, placed by his friends.

EDCS-43300026.translation: Rufa, daughter of Titus, Modesta, aged 10.
EDCS-43300026.summary: This inscription records the death of Rufa Modesta, daughter of Titus, at the age of 10.

EDCS-43300027.summary: This is the name inscription for Quintus Porcius Modestinus.

EDCS-43300028.translation: Titus Modestus, aged 10, saw to its construction.
EDCS-43300028.summary: This epitaph records that Titus Modestus, aged 10, commissioned its erection.

EDCS-43300030.translation: [---] LALIMI, aged 32, lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-43300030.summary: This epitaph records the burial of a person identified partially as LALIMI, aged 32.

EDCS-43300032.translation: Victrix, the slave-woman of Caecilius Bassus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-43300032.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Victrix, slave-woman of Caecilius Bassus, at the age of 50.

EDCS-43300037.translation: Attonius Flavo, son of Acconis, from Clunium, aged 65, lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-43300037.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Attonius Flavo, son of Acconis, from Clunium, at the age of 65.

EDCS-43300039.summary: This inscription records a dedication or donation made by the son of Alufi.

EDCS-43300040.translation: Cupiena, daughter of Camalus, aged 25, lies here. Camalus, son of Avelius, and Duenia, daughter of Anmon, arranged for its construction for their daughter.
EDCS-43300040.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Cupiena, daughter of Camalus, aged 25, erected by her parents Camalus and Duenia.

EDCS-43300041.translation: To Amviaronus, son of Abmatius, Attius Arreinnicus gives and dedicates.
EDCS-43300041.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Attius Arreinnicus to Amviaronus, son of Abmatius.

EDCS-43300043.translation: To Paternia, daughter of Sempronia Firmus, of Clunium, aged 6 years. Edicrocus, her brother, [wishes] that the earth may be light for you.
EDCS-43300043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Paternia, erected by her brother, dating to antiquity.

EDCS-43300044.translation: Modestus Merius Limicus made this tomb for his brother, aged 25 years. The heir [did so] by will.
EDCS-43300044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestus Merius Limicus, who built a tomb for his brother, commissioned by will.

EDCS-43300045.translation: Victoria Celtiberia, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 20 years, is buried here. May the earth be light for you. (Q. I. M. - Quod Iubet Maximus?)
EDCS-43300045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victoria Celtiberia, a freedwoman, indicating she is buried here and wishing her peace.

EDCS-43300046.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph indicating a person identified only by an initial and possibly a cognomen is buried here.

EDCS-43300051.summary: This is a titulature inscription identifying Emperor Caesar Augustus and mentioning his age.

EDCS-43300053.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power V, imperator IIII, [aged] 62.
EDCS-43300053.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, listing his honors and age.

EDCS-43300055.summary: This is a titulature inscription identifying Emperor Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-43300058.translation: Emperor Caesar Severus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, restored [this] in the year 200.
EDCS-43300058.summary: This is an inscription recording the restoration of something by Emperor Severus Pius Felix Augustus, dating to 200 AD.

EDCS-43300062.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus, the Unconquered Augustus, [in his] 90th year.
EDCS-43300062.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Flavius Valerius Severus, likely referring to a building or monument, possibly indicating a significant year or honor.

EDCS-43300063.translation: Here lies Maximiana, daughter of Nicolaus. He rested in the month of November, the 15th day, on the preparation day, in the 23rd hour.
EDCS-43300063.summary: This is a funerary inscription in Greek for Maximiana, daughter of Nicolaus, providing her burial date and time.

EDCS-43300066.translation: Amena, daughter of Pidernus, gladly fulfilled her vow to Arcon.
EDCS-43300066.summary: This is a votive inscription by Amena, honoring Arcon, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300068.translation: Catuenus, son of Aelandus, lies here.
EDCS-43300068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Catuenus, son of Aelandus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300069.translation: Here lies [the daughter] of Caius Norbanus Tongus.
EDCS-43300069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, mentioning the daughter of Caius Norbanus Tongus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300073.translation: Pedanius Ergasius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Graphicus, son of Ogisa.
EDCS-43300073.summary: This is a votive inscription by Pedanius Ergasius, honoring Graphicus, son of Ogisa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300075.translation: Peidurtia, and Cabura, and Cilura arranged for this to be made for their son Caburus, son of Cilurus, from Caurium.
EDCS-43300075.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Peidurtia, Cabura, and Cilura for their son Caburus, from Caurium, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300076.translation: Talabus, son of Lamungus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-43300076.summary: This is a votive inscription by Talabus, son of Lamungus, to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300083.translation: Here lies [a child] of 2 years. May the earth lie lightly on you. Ceia, the freedwoman, made [this] and set it up while alive.
EDCS-43300083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 2-year-old, set up by the freedwoman Ceia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300084.translation: Here lies [a person] of 40 years. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fuscilla arranged for this to be made for her brother.
EDCS-43300084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 40-year-old, arranged by Fuscilla for her brother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300085.translation: Here lies [a person]. May the earth lie lightly on you. The son arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-43300085.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with an unknown recipient, arranged by a son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300087.translation: Maxsuma, daughter of Talabarus, aged 35, lies here.
EDCS-43300087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxsuma, daughter of Talabarus, aged 35, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300088.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Maila, daughter of Cilus, aged 13, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, father and mother had this made.
EDCS-43300088.summary: A tombstone for Maila, a 13-year-old girl, whose parents commissioned the monument.

EDCS-43300091.translation: Cisa, freedwoman of Tancinus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Quintus Melvius [commissioned this].
EDCS-43300091.summary: A tombstone for Cisa, a freedwoman, commissioned by Quintus Melvius.

EDCS-43300092.translation: Coriana, daughter of Titus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Cilenmie had this made.
EDCS-43300092.summary: A tombstone for Coriana, a 25-year-old woman, commissioned by Cilenmie.

EDCS-43300093.translation: Quadratus, son of Talabus, aged 20, and Amana, daughter of Eburius, aged 20, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300093.summary: A tombstone for two individuals, Quadratus and Amana, both aged 20, likely a couple.

EDCS-43300094.translation: Emuria, daughter of Pintanus, is buried here.
EDCS-43300094.summary: A tombstone for Emuria, daughter of Pintanus.

EDCS-43300095.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-43300095.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, with his age partially preserved.

EDCS-43300097.translation: Celerius, son of Maurus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Rufus, his brother, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-43300097.summary: A tombstone for Celerius, aged 35, commissioned by his brother Rufus.

EDCS-43300098.translation: Titus Caepasius Maximus is buried here.
EDCS-43300098.summary: A tombstone for Titus Caepasius Maximus.

EDCS-43300099.translation: Gaius Julius Maximus is buried here.
EDCS-43300099.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Maximus.

EDCS-43300100.translation: Publius Norbanus Sergia Flaccinus, aedile, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-43300100.summary: A tombstone for Publius Norbanus Sergia Flaccinus, identified as an aedile, aged 30.

EDCS-43300101.translation: Rufa, daughter of Ursotus, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Assantius to his wife.
EDCS-43300101.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rufa, with her husband Assantius dedicating it to her.

EDCS-43300104.translation: Severa, daughter of Alveus, aged 11, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300104.summary: A funerary epitaph for Severa, a young girl, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300107.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Assantius, son of Cacalo.

EDCS-43300108.translation: Tiberius Vettius Clemens, aged 50, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300108.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Vettius Clemens, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300110.translation: Aelaenus and his family are laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300110.summary: A funerary epitaph for a family group, Aelaenus and others, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300111.translation: Vettius Niger is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vettius Niger, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300112.translation: Capito, son of Matucenus, and Avita, daughter of Iulus, aged 16, and Casia, daughter of Vironus, and Aquilus, son of Annienus, are laid here. Tagana Caenonis had the work done for her father and for Avita, daughter of Lucius, her mother, and for herself. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-43300112.summary: A complex funerary monument commissioned by Tagana Caenonis for her father, mother, and herself, also commemorating several other individuals, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300113.translation: Duelius, son of Titus, Camalius, aged 25, is laid here. Camalius, son of Avelus, and Duelia, daughter of Armonius, had it made for their daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300113.summary: A funerary epitaph for Duelius, son of Titus Camalius, commissioned by his parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300114.translation: Gaius Arconius, son of Arconius, aged 60, is laid here. Made from the inheritance. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-43300114.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Arconius, son of Arconius, noting it was funded from his inheritance.

EDCS-43300118.translation: Mebues PECA A PR voluntarily paid his vow.
EDCS-43300118.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Mebues PECA A PR, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43300121.translation: To my daughter, 23 years of age.
EDCS-43300121.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a deceased daughter, recorded in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300123.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Ingenuus Firmus, my son, 4 years of age.
EDCS-43300123.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young son named Lucius Caecilius Ingenuus Firmus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300124.translation: Norbanus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, Severus, 10 years of age, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-43300124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Norbanus Severus, dated to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300125.translation: 9 years of age, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Julius saw to its construction.
EDCS-43300125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed child, indicating Julius was responsible for the monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300126.translation: Lucius Norbanus Tancinus, son of Aidanius, built an altar well for Salus.
EDCS-43300126.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the goddess Salus by Lucius Norbanus Tancinus, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300127.translation: Titus Norbanus Quintus, son of Titus, set up this altar to Ataecina in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-43300127.summary: This is a votive dedication of an altar to the goddess Ataecina by Titus Norbanus Quintus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300128.translation: Norbana Ospita, daughter of Turacius, aged IC [99?], and Norbana Senicia, daughter of Titus, aged 11 years, lie here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-43300128.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Norbana Ospita and Norbana Senicia, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300130.translation: To the Divine Manes, Amoena, aged 35 years.
EDCS-43300130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amoena, dedicated to the spirits of the departed, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300131.translation: Cilea, aged 50 years, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Caturo, aged 20 years, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-43300131.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Cilea and Caturo, dated to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300132.translation: To Memor/ia, Atanus, Clavicus, Amonicus, brothers; Tancinus, son of Cauricus, saw to its construction at his own expense for (his) dearest ones.
EDCS-43300132.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tancinus for his brothers Memor/ia, Atanus, Clavicus, and Amonicus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-43300136.translation: Lucius, son of Titus, and his father Lucius had this made.
EDCS-43300136.summary: This is a dedication inscription for a monument, likely commissioned by a father and son duo.

EDCS-43300138.translation: Tertia Norbana Dota is buried here.
EDCS-43300138.summary: This is a tombstone inscription indicating the burial place of Tertia Norbana Dota.

EDCS-43300139.translation: Vivius Rufus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-43300139.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription recording Vivius Rufus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-43300140.translation: Eniracillus, son of Voncon, willingly placed this vow according to his intention.
EDCS-43300140.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription recording Eniracillus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-43400347.translation: The Emperor Caesar, divine descendant, Pontifex Maximus, Consul for the twelfth time, Tribune for the tenth time, Imperator for the eighth time, having pacified the world by sea and land, with the temple of Janus closed and the Roman people restored to their best laws and most sacred institutions, improved the upper road, begun by consuls and interrupted in many places, promoting it to be wider and longer as far as Gades, for the dignity of the empire.
EDCS-43400347.summary: This is an imperial rescript inscription detailing the completion of a major road construction project under an emperor, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE, to improve imperial infrastructure.

EDCS-43400348.translation: Maximian Augustus, unconquered, Pontifex Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Gothic Maximus, Imperator for the ninth time, Consul for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, repaired the [structure] for the second time. [Worked on for] 60 [days/units].
EDCS-43400348.summary: This is an imperial inscription recording the repair of a structure by Emperor Maximian, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-43400349.translation: The Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune for the second time, Imperator for the seventh time, Consul for the third time, designated for the fourth time, Father of the Fatherland, restored the road from the city of Capera to Emerita at his own expense. [Worked on for] 58 [days/units].
EDCS-43400349.summary: This is an imperial inscription from Vespasian's reign (69-79 CE) recording the restoration of a road at imperial expense.

EDCS-43400350.translation: The Emperor Caesar Domitian Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Germanicus, ordered the work, left unfinished by the negligence of the publicans due to that wicked tax-farming group, and deprived of all future public duty, to be completed. [Worked on for] 81 [days/units].
EDCS-43400350.summary: This is an imperial inscription from Domitian's reign (81-96 CE) concerning the completion of public works previously neglected by tax collectors.

EDCS-43400351.translation: Emperor Caesar Gallienus Augustus, Clement, Pious, having restored the city building, converted to good use, Gallienus Hispanius, ordered this road, interrupted in various places through the provinces, to be repaired. [Worked on for] 98 [days/units].
EDCS-43400351.summary: This is an imperial inscription from the reign of Gallienus (253-268 CE) recording the repair of provincial roads.

EDCS-43400352.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gratian, the pious, fortunate, greatest, victorious, and triumphant, always Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, conqueror of the Germans, Alamanni, Franks, and Goths, Tribune of the Plebs for the 7th time, acclaimed Imperator for the 6th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Country.
EDCS-43400352.summary: This is a titulary inscription honoring Emperor Gratian, listing his titles and acclamations, likely from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-43400353.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Constantine Augustus, promoter of peace and justice, founder of public tranquility, author of religion and faith, having remitted tribute everywhere, restored 113 border provinces.
EDCS-43400353.summary: This is a panegyric inscription praising Emperor Constantine for promoting peace, justice, and religion, and for restoring border provinces, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-43400552.translation: Lancius, son of Cilius, erected this altar to Jupiter the Solace-Giver.
EDCS-43400552.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lancius, son of Cilius, likely a private religious offering.

EDCS-43400553.translation: Pompeia Inventa, aged 55, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Pompeia. Pompeia C had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-43400553.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pompeia Inventa, recording her age and that it was paid for by Pompeia C.

EDCS-43400557.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Alfius S. willingly and deservedly placed this altar.
EDCS-43400557.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Lucius Alfius S., a personal religious offering.

EDCS-43400558.translation: Lupus, son of Marinus, lies here, and Lobesa, his wife.
EDCS-43400558.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lupus, son of Marinus, and his wife Lobesa.

EDCS-43400559.translation: Titus, son of Mantai, aged 50, is here. His son had this monument made.
EDCS-43400559.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus, son of Mantai, recording his age and that his son commissioned the monument.

EDCS-43400561.translation: Severina, aged 5, [may] the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-43400561.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Severina, aged 5, with a wish for a light earth, likely commemorating a young child.

EDCS-43400562.translation: To the gods of the underworld. [---] VCA NAF, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His son had this made.
EDCS-43400562.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an individual identified partially as VCA NAF, aged 60, and indicates his son commissioned the monument.

EDCS-43400563.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Caesia Osius daughter Secunda, aged 75, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, daughter, mother.
EDCS-43400563.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caesia Secunda, daughter of Osius, aged 75, who was also a daughter and mother, likely commissioned by her child.

EDCS-43400564.translation: May Pisira V L lie here and may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-43400564.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely for a woman named Pisira, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-43400566.translation: To L. S. V. Tancinus, son of E., a freedwoman.
EDCS-43400566.summary: A funerary epitaph for L. S. V. Tancinus, son of E., a freedwoman, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100010.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, from the most generous donatives, D II Caecilius Ur, soldier of the Third Legion Augusta Pia Fidelis, has given and to give 2000 sesterces to each for a bench.
EDCS-44100010.summary: A military donation decree from a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta Pia Fidelis to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, likely dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucina lived 45 years. Priscinus and Crysero, her most devoted brothers, erected this. May the earth be light upon you here.
EDCS-44100053.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucina, erected by her brothers Priscinus and Crysero, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100054.translation: Sentia Laurilla, daughter of Tanginus, aged 85, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. Sentius Sadala and Sentia Repentina, her heirs, saw to its construction according to her will.
EDCS-44100054.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sentia Laurilla, erected by her heirs Sentius Sadala and Sentia Repentina, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100056.translation: To Liber Pater Augustus, Felicitas Iulia of Olisipo [dedicates this] through Sextus Julius Avitus Cassius, one of the two magistrates.
EDCS-44100056.summary: A dedication to Liber Pater Augustus by Felicitas Iulia of Olisipo, likely dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100057.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Sextus Pompeius Postumus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100062.translation: The freedman of Marcus Sulpicianus, content with the honor, [erected this] from his own money for his wife.
EDCS-44100062.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed wife, erected by her freedman husband, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100064.translation: Torquatus, son of Cilampurus, Calva Bonco, paid a vow. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-44100064.summary: A votive inscription for Torquatus, son of Cilampurus, by Calva Bonco, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100065.translation: Dudamus, son of Duatus, and Bolosea, daughter of Simatis, lie here.
EDCS-44100065.summary: A funerary epitaph for Dudamus and Bolosea, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100066.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Vegetinus, son of Vegetus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44100066.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Vegetinus.

EDCS-44100071.translation: To the deified genius, Marcus Aurelius, willingly (fulfilled a vow).
EDCS-44100071.summary: This is a dedication to a deified genius by Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-44100073.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming an individual.

EDCS-44100075.translation: Lucius Baberius Aescinus and Acris, daughter of Aescinus, are buried here.
EDCS-44100075.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Baberius Aescinus and Acris.

EDCS-44100076.translation: Caius Caecilius Maximus, son of Caius, 52 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100076.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Caecilius Maximus, including his age and a common epitaph.

EDCS-44100077.translation: Caelia Lepida is buried here, 12 feet in front and [some measurement] feet in the field.
EDCS-44100077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Lepida, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-44100078.translation: Elvia Paulla, 21 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Equa, her mother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-44100078.summary: This funerary inscription for Elvia Paulla, aged 21, was commissioned by her mother.

EDCS-44100079.summary: This inscription likely names an individual, Caius Iulius Aristaeus, possibly with a place of origin.

EDCS-44100080.translation: Licinia Vitalis, daughter of Caius, 16 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caius Licinius Faustus (dedicated this) for his daughter, 12 feet in front and [some measurement] feet in the field.
EDCS-44100080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Vitalis, aged 16, with plot dimensions specified by her father.

EDCS-44100081.translation: Gaius Upilius Asclepius, freedman of Gaius, from Olisipo, 12 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Ulpius Aq[---]
EDCS-44100081.summary: This funerary inscription names Gaius Upilius Asclepius, a freedman from Olisipo, and includes a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-44100082.translation: Prudens, the slave of Lucius Cornelius Placidus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He was 40 years old.
EDCS-44100082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Prudens, a slave, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100083.translation: Modica, his wife, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modica, the wife of the deceased, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100084.translation: Papiria Rustica, a Roman equestrian, (erected this) for her mother and for Fabia, daughter of Marcus, 45 years old. May she be favorable to us.
EDCS-44100084.summary: This funerary monument, likely a tombstone, was dedicated by Papiria Rustica to her mother and a Fabia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100085.translation: Titus Etrilius, son of Caius, Papiria tribe, is buried here, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-44100085.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Etrilius, indicating his burial arrangements were made by will, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100091.translation: Quintus Apuleius Victor, 55 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100091.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Quintus Apuleius Victor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100092.translation: Valeria Laeta, daughter of Lucius, 80 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100092.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Valeria Laeta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100093.translation: Gaius Iulius Maximus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-44100093.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name and filiation from a tombstone or public record, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100094.translation: Lucius Norbanus Calus, son of Quintus, [died at] years old.
EDCS-44100094.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph that records the name and filiation of Lucius Norbanus Calus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100095.translation: Albinus, son of Tiberius, willingly paid his vow to Liber Pater.
EDCS-44100095.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Albinus fulfilling a vow to the god Liber Pater, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44500121.translation: Rufinus, father of Marcus Gurtio Quirina Cassianus, who held all offices in the republic of Collippon and was 33 years old, with Cassianus his grandson, arranged for this to be erected at his own expense.
EDCS-44500121.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Rufinus and his grandson Cassianus for Marcus Gurtio Quirina Cassianus, a local magistrate of Collippon, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44500122.translation: To Helvia Maxsuma, Valerius Severus, her client.
EDCS-44500122.summary: This is a dedication to Helvia Maxsuma by her client Valerius Severus, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-44500124.translation: May the earth lie lightly on Firmus, buried here.
EDCS-44500124.summary: This is a funerary inscription expressing a wish for the deceased, Firmus.

EDCS-44500127.translation: Rufinus, for his daughter Colona, and Claudius Cassianus, for their grandmother, arranged for this to be set up to Maxsuma.
EDCS-44500127.summary: This monument was erected by Rufinus and Claudius Cassianus in honor of Maxsuma, their grandmother, and Colona, Rufinus's daughter.

EDCS-44500128.translation: Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, arranged for this to be set up to her brother, Quintus Crastinus, who was [so many] years old.
EDCS-44500128.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Quintilla for her brother, Quintus Crastinus.

EDCS-44500130.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Fortunatus, buried here. He died during the siege of Collipp[o?]. And Q. Cassius Longus, his kinsman, prefect of the tomb of Sextus Fortunatus, his brother, gave orders for [his burial]. Here he was buried. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-44500130.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Sextus Fortunatus, who died during a siege, and notes that his kinsman Q. Cassius Longus arranged for his burial.

EDCS-44800412.translation: To Gaius Aefulanius Magnet, Augustalis, and to Aefulana Venusta, freedwoman of Magnet.
EDCS-44800412.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Aefulanius Magnet, an Augustalis, and his freedwoman Venusta.

EDCS-44800443.translation: Valeria Restituta, freedwoman of the Emperor.
EDCS-44800443.summary: This inscription identifies Valeria Restituta as a freedwoman of the emperor.

EDCS-44800444.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Valerius Vegetinus, aged 5 years.
EDCS-44800444.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Gaius Valerius Vegetinus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-44800446.translation: Vibia, daughter of Caius, aged 5 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-44800446.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Vibia, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-44800447.translation: Albinus, son of Lubaecus, aged 50 years, and Adernia, daughter of Maelo, aged 40 years, are buried here. Albinus, son of Lubaecus, ordered this to be made for himself and his wife in his will.
EDCS-44800447.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Albinus, son of Lubaecus, for himself and his wife Adernia, daughter of Maelo, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-44800448.translation: Turanius Rufus, son of Meiduenus, to his wife, aged 22.
EDCS-44800448.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a wife, identified by her age and patronymic.

EDCS-44800450.translation: To Arconius, son of Frontonius, aged 30, from Cluniensis, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-44800450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arconius, set up by someone from Cluniensis, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-44800494.translation: Iulia Huiguia, aged 18, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-44800494.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Huiguia, an 18-year-old woman, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-44800569.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Novatus, son of Lucius Annius, of the voting tribe Quirina, to his friends.
EDCS-44800569.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) by Novatus for his friends.

EDCS-44800580.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely identifying a person named Fuscus.

EDCS-44800585.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Lucius Tutilius and Tutilia.

EDCS-44800586.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed: Ti. Aurelius [---].
EDCS-44800586.summary: This is a funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the Manes.

EDCS-44800587.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed: Ianuaria Eupli, freedwoman, to her well-deserving husband, Quintus Fabius Pardala.
EDCS-44800587.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Ianuaria Eupli to her husband, Quintus Fabius Pardala.

EDCS-44800588.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Titus Publicius Modestus.

EDCS-45000064.translation: Ama, aged 30, may the earth lie lightly on her here.
EDCS-45000064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ama, aged 30, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-45000121.translation: Gaius Ammius Avitus made this for his father's mother, Camira, daughter of Apanon.
EDCS-45000121.summary: This is a funerary dedication from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, commemorating Camira.

EDCS-45000122.translation: Sunua, daughter of Pisirus, had this made at her own expense for her daughter Cilia, daughter of Lubaecus, and for her granddaughter Sunua, daughter of Elavus.
EDCS-45000122.summary: This is a funerary dedication from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, set up by Sunua for her daughter and granddaughter.

EDCS-45000123.translation: Maratus, called Vernaculus, for his freedwoman Cilia.
EDCS-45000123.summary: This is a funerary dedication from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, for the freedwoman Cilia.

EDCS-45000124.translation: Avita, daughter of Vitalis, willingly fulfilled her vow to Arantio Tanginiciaeco.
EDCS-45000124.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow to a local deity.

EDCS-45000128.translation: Talavius, son of Caburius, gave this at his own expense to Juno Linteia.
EDCS-45000128.summary: This is a votive dedication from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, to the goddess Juno Linteia.

EDCS-45000129.summary: This is a dedication from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, by Turea.

EDCS-45000130.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Elointus.

EDCS-45000131.translation: Celtienus, son of Canapius, willingly fulfilled his vow to the household gods of Cairus.
EDCS-45000131.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, dedicated to local Lares.

EDCS-45000132.translation: He willingly fulfilled his vow to Mercury Esibraeus.
EDCS-45000132.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, to Mercury Esibraeus.

EDCS-45000133.translation: Reve, son of Veius, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45000133.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown location, likely Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Reve.

EDCS-45000134.translation: He willingly fulfilled his vow to the god Reve Langanidaeco.
EDCS-45000134.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the deity Reve Langanidaeco, fulfilled willingly by an unnamed dedicator.

EDCS-45000135.translation: Clara, the freedwoman, willingly fulfilled her vow to Victoria Aphrodisia.
EDCS-45000135.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Clara, a freedwoman, to the deity Victoria Aphrodisia.

EDCS-45000137.translation: Procula, daughter of Silvanus, erected this for her father Proculus, son of Silvanus.
EDCS-45000137.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Procula to her father Proculus, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-45000138.translation: Claudius Severus erected this for his father Gaius, son of Caenonis, and his mother Claudia Casina.
EDCS-45000138.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Claudius Severus for his parents, Gaius and Claudia Casina.

EDCS-45000139.translation: Titulus Rusticani, aged 75, placed this for the departed spirits; his wife Pacatiana.
EDCS-45000139.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Titulus Rusticanus at age 75, with his wife Pacatiana as the erector.

EDCS-45000140.translation: Quintus Iulius Marianus erected this for his freedwoman mother Auniae Aveliae and his wife Iulia Felicula, and for himself.
EDCS-45000140.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Iulius Marianus erecting a monument for his freedwoman mother and his wife.

EDCS-45000141.translation: Marcus erected this for his father Reburrus, son of Treucatus, and his mother Cilea, daughter of Crassus.
EDCS-45000141.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus for his parents, Reburrus and Cilea.

EDCS-45000142.translation: Sila, daughter of Silo, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-45000142.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sila, daughter of Silo, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-45000144.translation: Turacia, daughter of Saelgi, is here.
EDCS-45000144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turacia, daughter of Saelgi.

EDCS-45000145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Attura Usispo erected this for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-45000145.summary: This funerary inscription records Attura Usispo erecting a monument for her husband and herself, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-45000146.translation: Flaccus, son of Cai, for himself and Casa Ara.
EDCS-45000146.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Flaccus for himself and Casa Ara.

EDCS-45000147.translation: Aulius, son of Paternius, for Turea, daughter of Talabarius.
EDCS-45000147.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Aulius for Turea.

EDCS-45000148.translation: Cutaeca, freedwoman of Bovius, aged 26, to Aquilius, her freedman and son-in-law, and to Nota. The mother had it made at her own expense.
EDCS-45000148.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a mother for her freedwoman daughter, her freedman and son-in-law, and Nota.

EDCS-45100003.translation: Laepo, Sunua, and Maela set this up with good will.
EDCS-45100003.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Laepo, Sunua, and Maela.

EDCS-45100004.translation: Hail Augustus Caesar, chief priest, son of a god...
EDCS-45100004.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription honoring Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-45100060.translation: Statue of Corronata, daughter of Cirrisus, aged 85.
EDCS-45100060.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue for Corronata.

EDCS-45100061.translation: His mother erected statues in her own name for her sons, sons of Alli, son of Arreinus, and of Mocosa, daughter of Lubaecus.
EDCS-45100061.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother's dedication of statues for her sons.

EDCS-45100064.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, by order of Gaius Calpurnius Flaccus, Gaius Calpurnius Fronto and Orbia Flaccilla, his parents, had it made.
EDCS-45100064.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus, commissioned by the parents of Gaius Calpurnius Flaccus.

EDCS-45200004.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar, 166.
EDCS-45200004.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus.

EDCS-45200017.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, restored the road, 140.
EDCS-45200017.summary: This inscription records the road restoration by Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-45200018.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored 137.
EDCS-45200018.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an object or structure by Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-45200019.summary: This brief inscription likely refers to a restoration effort and its date.

EDCS-45200020.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, restored 136.
EDCS-45200020.summary: This inscription states that Emperor Trajan restored something.

EDCS-45200024.translation: To our Emperors and Lords Severus and Maximinus, noble Caesars.
EDCS-45200024.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Severus and Maximinus.

EDCS-45200025.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored 132.
EDCS-45200025.summary: This inscription records a restoration by Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-45200075.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, with tribunician power, consul, chief priest, father of the country, proconsul, restored 27 Roman miles.
EDCS-45200075.summary: This inscription records a road improvement or construction by Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-45200117.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 2nd time, father of the country, chief priest, consul 133.
EDCS-45200117.summary: This inscription lists the titles and honors of Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-45200133.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored 101.
EDCS-45200133.summary: This inscription records a restoration by Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-45200136.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Crispus, most noble Caesar 110.
EDCS-45200136.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Julius Crispus.

EDCS-45400127.translation: Placed by Eutychis, wife of Estaminos, age 28.
EDCS-45400127.summary: This Greek inscription is a funerary marker for a woman named Eutychis.

EDCS-46400855.translation: Pintilus, son of Arconicus, aged 60, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-46400855.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pintilus, son of Arconicus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-46600178.translation: Anius Aetus set this up willingly from his heart.
EDCS-46600178.summary: This is a dedication made willingly by Anius Aetus.

EDCS-46600180.translation: Iulia Tertia, daughter of Lucius, fulfilled her vow willingly from her heart.
EDCS-46600180.summary: This inscription records Iulia Tertia fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-46600181.translation: Vordiacus Celtius willingly fulfilled his vow to Tongus.
EDCS-46600181.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vordiacus Celtius to Tongus.

EDCS-46600182.translation: Vicina, pious and fortunate, had this built at her own expense for Petronius Capitolinus and his father Severus, father and son; may the earth lie lightly on you. This was done with the care of her wife Procula.
EDCS-46600182.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Vicina for Petronius Capitolinus and his father Severus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600184.summary: This is a simple funerary marker for Arreinus Viro.

EDCS-46600185.translation: Rnnna, aged 11, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Veienta had this made for her mother.
EDCS-46600185.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rnnna, aged 11, with the monument made by her mother Veienta.

EDCS-46600186.translation: Marcus Iulius Modestus, aged 13, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-46600186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Modestus, who died at age 13.

EDCS-46600187.translation: Augenda, daughter of Pellius, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-46600187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Augenda, daughter of Pellius.

EDCS-46600189.translation: Ilurbedae Capito, son of Coelus, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-46600189.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ilurbedae Capito, son of Coelus, who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-47200001.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Daes Appinus.

EDCS-48700031.translation: Son of Cabruiamus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-48700031.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by the son of Cabruiamus.

EDCS-48700033.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Tongeta, daughter of Pitinna.

EDCS-48700034.translation: To the Divine Shades, Proclus, son of Fuscus, had this made for his most dutiful wife Camala, daughter of Maxumus.
EDCS-48700034.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Proclus for his wife Camala.

EDCS-48700051.translation: Marcus Clodius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 25, rests here.
EDCS-48700051.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Clodius Avitus, aged 25.

EDCS-48700052.translation: To the Divine Shades, Cornelia Gamice, aged 25, and Lucius Cornelius Victorinus, aged 30, brother and sister, rest here.
EDCS-48700052.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cornelia Gamice and Lucius Cornelius Victorinus, brother and sister.

EDCS-48700054.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, likely naming Gaius Ernius, son of Marcus, possibly on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-48700055.translation: Quintus, son of Marius, and Iulia Aranta, daughter of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, Tanginus, rest here.
EDCS-48700055.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Marius and Iulia Aranta.

EDCS-48700159.translation: Albanius, son of Albanius, of the Galeria tribe, a soldier in the Legio X Gemina, in the 3rd cohort, 1st century of Iulius Severus, ordered this to be made by his will; he rests here.
EDCS-48700159.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Albanius, a soldier of Legio X Gemina, whose monument was erected according to his will.

EDCS-48700160.translation: Sextus Curius Silvanus and Sextus Curius, son of Sextus.
EDCS-48700160.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, possibly funerary, naming two individuals named Sextus Curius.

EDCS-48700161.translation: Minucia Avita, daughter of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-48700161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Minucia Avita.

EDCS-48700162.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Licinius Afer, aged 25, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Licinius Licinianus, aged 50.
EDCS-48700162.summary: This inscription, likely from a tomb, records the ages of Caius Licinius Afer and Marcus Licinius Licinianus, possibly related individuals.

EDCS-48700164.translation: To Licinius, son of [Caius?], from Galeria Fusco Amoena.
EDCS-48700164.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial, possibly on a tomb, from Galeria Fusco Amoena to Licinius.

EDCS-48700165.translation: Valerius Avitus of the Galeria tribe lies here.
EDCS-48700165.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Avitus.

EDCS-48700166.translation: Marcus Minucius Severus, freedman of Minucia Aranta, freedwoman.
EDCS-48700166.summary: This inscription records the relationship between Marcus Minucius Severus and Minucia Aranta, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-48700167.translation: Marcus Valerius Reburrinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, [Valerius] CIVLAN, lies here.
EDCS-48700167.summary: This funerary epitaph identifies Marcus Valerius Reburrinus, son of Lucius, and includes an additional, possibly cognomen or gentile name.

EDCS-48700168.translation: Lucius Iulius Avitus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 25, lies here.
EDCS-48700168.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Avitus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-48700170.translation: Iulia Procula, daughter of Caius, and Licinia Tusca, daughter of Lucius, lie here, and Albanius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 13, lies here.
EDCS-48700170.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of three individuals: Iulia Procula, Licinia Tusca, and Albanius.

EDCS-48700171.translation: [Caius?] Fundanus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-48700171.summary: This incomplete inscription likely records the name and tribal affiliation of an individual, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-48700172.translation: [---]ia, daughter of Marcus, Amoena, aged 9, lies here.
EDCS-48700172.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of a young girl named Amoena, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-49300087.translation: Lenus Aper, son of Publius, of the Papiria tribe, a veteran of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-49300087.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lenus Aper, a veteran of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-49300088.translation: Quintus Sulpicius, brother of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, made this.
EDCS-49300088.summary: This inscription records an action by Quintus Sulpicius, identified as a 'brother' of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, likely a dedication or building project.

EDCS-49300116.translation: Titus Aelius Clemens willingly fulfilled his vow to the Lares.
EDCS-49300116.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Aelius Clemens, dedicated to the household gods (Lares), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-49300117.translation: Caius Teius Proclus, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-49300117.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Teius Proclus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-49300118.translation: N. Doni la is buried here.
EDCS-49300118.summary: This is a tombstone inscription, unfortunately fragmentary, for an individual identified as N. Doni la, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-49300120.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment of a funerary monument or dedication, identifying Helvius Surus as the son of Quintus.

EDCS-49300121.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, for Sila, a freedwoman of Caesus.

EDCS-49300124.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed: Sextia.
EDCS-49300124.summary: This is a funerary inscription, indicating that the monument is dedicated to the underworld deities on behalf of Sextia.

EDCS-49300126.translation: [F]C I N V S O Aro willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-49300126.summary: This is a votive inscription, although partially illegible, recording that Aro willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-49300128.translation: Reburrus, son of Avitus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-49300128.summary: This is a votive inscription by Reburrus, son of Avitus, who fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-49300133.translation: Commertonus, son of Celti, of the tribe Ataecina, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-49300133.summary: This is a votive inscription by Commertonus, son of Celti, of the tribe Ataecina.

EDCS-49300139.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Petronia Secundina, aged 25, is buried here; Calpurnius Flaccinus had this made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-49300139.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Secundina, set up by her husband Calpurnius Flaccinus.

EDCS-49300140.translation: She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-49300140.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a woman, with a common Latin phrase wishing rest to the deceased.

EDCS-49300144.translation: Iulius lived 80 years. May the earth lie upon you.
EDCS-49300144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius, recording his age as 80 years.

EDCS-49300145.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely an epitaph, recording an age of 63 years.

EDCS-49300148.translation: To the Lady Tuiribrigensis Atecina, Norbanus [dedicated this].
EDCS-49300148.summary: This is a dedication to a local deity, Tuiribrigensis Atecina, by a dedicator named Norbanus.

EDCS-49300149.translation: Norbanus Severinus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sons had this made.
EDCS-49300149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Norbanus Severinus, aged 35, erected by his sons.

EDCS-49300150.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely an epitaph, recording an age of 70 years.

EDCS-49300154.translation: Brio, son of Marcus, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-49300154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Brio, son of Marcus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-49300155.translation: Amona, daughter of Talavus, aged 60. Her son made this. She is buried here.
EDCS-49300155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amona, daughter of Talavus, aged 60, erected by her son.

EDCS-49300495.summary: This inscription is a fragment, likely commemorating Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-49300497.translation: Of his own free will for Aroius, his own spirit
EDCS-49300497.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication or votive offering made willingly by an individual.

EDCS-49300503.translation: To Bovanna, daughter of Marinus, Placidus, son of Clisius, to his mother, from the will of Avelea Mania, daughter of [---], aged XI years. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-49300503.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Avelea Mania, a young girl, set up by her mother's will.

EDCS-49300504.translation: To Julius Severus, son of Avitus, a most dutiful man; to Quintus Antonius Rectus, nephew of Marcus Julius, son of Avitus, Iulia Severa, daughter of Nigrus, saw to its construction.
EDCS-49300504.summary: This funerary inscription honors multiple male relatives and was commissioned by Iulia Severa.

EDCS-49300505.translation: To Maelus, aged XXII years; Severus, Camira, Aran, for their relatives, saw to its construction.
EDCS-49300505.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Maelus and was commissioned by Severus, Camira, and Aran for their relatives.

EDCS-49300506.translation: [---]nsis, aged XXXV years, is buried here. May the earth be light for you. Paternus saw to its construction for his brother.
EDCS-49300506.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual aged 35, commissioned by his brother Paternus.

EDCS-49300515.summary: This fragmented inscription records the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-49800408.summary: This inscription likely records the ages (25 and 8) of two individuals, Marciana and Sabina, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-50900001.translation: To Lucanus, aged XXX years; Amoena, aged XVI years; Maxima, aged XIII years. Cilius, son of Boutus, the father, and Sunua, daughter of Apanon, the mother.
EDCS-50900001.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of three individuals (Lucanus, Amoena, and Maxima) and identifies their parents, Cilius and Sunua.

EDCS-50900002.translation: lo, son of Caenon, is buried here
EDCS-50900002.summary: This funerary inscription records that an individual named lo, son of Caenon, is buried here.

EDCS-50900003.translation: Claudia Marcea had this made for her grandmother Avita, daughter of Celsus, and for Sabina, daughter of Sabinus, her grandmother.
EDCS-50900003.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Claudia Marcea for her grandmother Avita and great-grandmother Sabina.

EDCS-50900005.summary: This is a very short funerary inscription identifying Tanginus, son of Docquirus.

EDCS-50900006.translation: For Annia Vegetae, daughter of Annius Valentis Lanciensis, in accordance with the will of her mother, Anniae Rufinae.
EDCS-50900006.summary: This funerary inscription records the provision for Annia Vegetae, made by her mother's will.

EDCS-50900007.translation: Auniae Arantoni, son of Celtiaticus, and Cocceia Silonis, daughter of Oppidana, and Avita Naevia Silonis, daughter of Clara, had this made for their mother.
EDCS-50900007.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Auniae Arantoni and two women, Cocceia Silonis and Avita Naevia Silonis, for their mother.

EDCS-50900008.translation: To Avitus, son of Aper. Coelea, daughter of Manius, mother.
EDCS-50900008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Avitus, son of Aper, dedicated by his mother Coelea.

EDCS-50900009.translation: Bassus for Maturovus and Boudica, daughter of Sempronius, for themselves and for Bassinus, aged 30.
EDCS-50900009.summary: This funerary inscription records Bassus, Maturovus, Boudica, and Bassinus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-50900010.translation: Gaius Fabius Vernus, son of Gaius, from Clunium, and Bassae, freedwoman of Fabia, wife of Fabius, had this made in accordance with the will of Bassae. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-50900010.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Gaius Fabius Vernus and Bassae, a freedwoman, according to Bassae's will.

EDCS-50900011.translation: Gaius Lucretius of Aetaniensis erected this monument for his well-deserving wife Merina, who lived 31 years, and for himself; he will not be followed by an heir.
EDCS-50900011.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Lucretius for his wife Merina and himself, stating that he will not have a surviving heir.

EDCS-50900012.translation: Toutonus, son of Arconius, had this made for his wife Cileae, daughter of Cilius, and for their daughter Camira.
EDCS-50900012.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Toutonus Arconii for his wife Cileae and daughter Camira.

EDCS-50900013.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Arrius Egitaniensis, son of Lucius, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-50900013.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Arrius Egitaniensis, son of Lucius, indicating he is buried here.

EDCS-50900014.translation: Lucius Cocceius Lycius, aged 100; Caius Furius Lycius of Emerita, aged 50; Caius Furius Eutyches of Emerita, aged 20.
EDCS-50900014.summary: This inscription lists the names and ages of three individuals, likely from a tomb.

EDCS-50900015.translation: Silo, son of Legirus; Coelea, daughter of Maelonis; Amoena, daughter of Maelonis. Here they lie. May the earth be light upon you. Sila, daughter of Silonis, saw to it that this be made for her father, mother, and aunt.
EDCS-50900015.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Silo, Coelea, and Amoena, with Sila commissioning the monument for her relatives.

EDCS-50900016.translation: To Vegetinus, freedman of Amoena, aged 26. Amoena, freedwoman of Eutychia, saw to it that this be made for her son and herself.
EDCS-50900016.summary: This epitaph records a funerary monument commissioned by Amoena for her son, Vegetinus.

EDCS-50900017.translation: Flaccus and Longinus had this made for their parents, Virius, son of Tanginus, and Sunua, daughter of Vigani. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-50900017.summary: This funerary inscription records Flaccus and Longinus commissioning a monument for their parents.

EDCS-50900018.translation: To Iulia Varilla, daughter of Celer. Lucius Iulius Modestus of the Quirina tribe had a statue with a base made for his wife. Iulia Amoena, daughter of Sabinus, her mother, gilded it.
EDCS-50900018.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue and base for Iulia Varilla by her husband, Lucius Iulius Modestus, with her mother, Iulia Amoena, gilding it.

EDCS-50900019.translation: Lucius Graecinius, freedman of Lucius, aged 41. Here he lies. May the earth be light upon you. Graecinia Avelea had this made for her husband and herself.
EDCS-50900019.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Graecinius and the commissioning of the monument by his wife, Graecinia Avelea.

EDCS-50900020.translation: To Camalus, son, father; to Medama, daughter, mother; to Firminus, son, brother; to Firminus, son.
EDCS-50900020.summary: This inscription seems to be a list of familial relationships, possibly related to a dedication or a tomb, although the exact context is unclear.

EDCS-50900021.translation: To Flavius(?), freedman of Lucrius. Gemellus Carus, his heirs, according to the will, saw to it that this be made.
EDCS-50900021.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by the heirs of Gemellus Carus for their freedman, possibly named Flavius.

EDCS-50900022.translation: Dovvus, son of Aranton, and Dure Fuscus. Repa IXIIII. Saw to it that this be made.
EDCS-50900022.summary: This inscription appears to record the names of two individuals, Dovvus and Dure Fuscus, and a numerical notation possibly indicating a date or measurement, with one of them commissioning the work.

EDCS-50900024.translation: Silo Tangius, son of Silonis. Avita, daughter of Taci, his mother, saw to it that this be made.
EDCS-50900024.summary: This funerary inscription records that Avita, mother of Silo Tangius, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-50900025.translation: Tanginus Flavinius, son of Arantionius, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-50900025.summary: This inscription records a private dedication, likely of a funerary monument, funded by Tanginus Flavinius.

EDCS-50900027.translation: Sila, freedwoman of Silas, aged 11, had this made for herself.
EDCS-50900027.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by a young freedwoman, Sila.

EDCS-50900028.translation: Anius, son of Celtius, from Taporus of Careo.
EDCS-50900028.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying an individual, Anius, and his origins.

EDCS-50900029.translation: Gaius Curius, son of Caius, from Igeditanus, is buried here.
EDCS-50900029.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying Gaius Curius and his place of origin.

EDCS-50900030.translation: Lus, son of Pisirius, aged 15, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-50900030.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lus, son of Pisirius, who died at age 15.

EDCS-50900031.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to family members related to a burial or inheritance.

EDCS-50900032.summary: This inscription records Severa, identified as a mother, commissioning a monument, likely funerary.

EDCS-50900033.translation: son of Toni for his wife.
EDCS-50900033.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating a dedication made by a son for his wife.

EDCS-50900035.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying an individual as the son of Allucquus.

EDCS-50900044.translation: Maelus, son of Cilius, gave this boundary stone to his living son Boutius, son of Petobius, and made it with his own money.
EDCS-50900044.summary: This inscription records Maelus, son of Cilius, donating a boundary stone (cippus) to his son Boutius, funded by Maelus himself.

EDCS-50900046.translation: Mancus, son of Gracilis, a Celt, arranged for this to be made at his own expense for a friend.
EDCS-50900046.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by Mancus for his friend, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-50900047.translation: Caudicus, son of Amminus, for himself and his wife Cassina Catuenia.
EDCS-50900047.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caudicus for himself and his wife, probably from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-50900048.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Atti's son, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51000127.translation: For the Unconquered God, for [my/his] health, Firmus.
EDCS-51000127.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Unconquered God (Deo Invito) for health, likely dedicated by Firmus, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51400900.translation: This pastoral offering [was made] for the flock of Haras, 10 sheep for Broeneia Haraca, 10 sheep for Reve Augustus, a bull for Harucui, [son of] Bandi, [and] Munitia Carla, [and] Apinus Vendicus Eriacainus, augur, for the worship of the gods, by the decree of the elders.
EDCS-51400900.summary: This complex inscription appears to record a series of sacrificial offerings to various deities and individuals, possibly related to a communal religious event or contract, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51400902.translation: Deiba Borigo, Deibo Borigo, Vissaiego Borigo, Albinus, son of Chaereas, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-51400902.summary: This is a votive inscription by Albinus, son of Chaereas, paying a vow to the deities named Borigo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51400903.translation: Bandae, son of Amminus, willingly paid his vow to Luguus with a willing heart.
EDCS-51400903.summary: This is a votive inscription by Bandae, son of Amminus, dedicating a vow to the god Luguus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-52100133.translation: In the year 12, during the consulship of Titus Iulius Claudius Severus Italianus.
EDCS-52100133.summary: This is a dating formula from a Greek inscription, referencing the consulship of Titus Iulius Claudius Severus Italianus, likely in the late 2nd century CE.
