EDCS-52900116.translation: Clemens, son of Scitus, aged 50, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-52900116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clemens, son of Scitus, who died at the age of 50, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-52900118.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Capitolinus, Allia Camira willingly paid her vow and erected this altar.
EDCS-52900118.summary: This is a votive inscription by Allia Camira to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicating an altar and fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-52900119.translation: Gaius Canidius Fundanus, son of Galeria, is buried here.
EDCS-52900119.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Canidius Fundanus.

EDCS-53200215.translation: He had this made for his brother. As you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly on you, and on yourself.'
EDCS-53200215.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by an unknown person for his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53300010.translation: Marcus Galus Atius willingly offered a vow to the household gods for a safe return.
EDCS-53300010.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Marcus Galus Atius to the household gods.

EDCS-53300011.translation: Severus, son of Longinus, had this made for his father, Longinus, son of Cileas.
EDCS-53300011.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a monument by Severus for his father, Longinus.

EDCS-53300015.translation: Decuminus, son of Decumus, made this according to the will of Apinus, son of Arbus.
EDCS-53300015.summary: This inscription states that Decuminus, son of Decumus, fulfilled the will of Apinus, son of Arbus, by constructing this monument.

EDCS-53300016.translation: Aquilinus, son of Aquilus, from Bandi Vordiaigevi of Iacenium.
EDCS-53300016.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Aquilinus, son of Aquilus, with possible origins from Iacenium.

EDCS-53300017.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Caburia Caturonia, possibly with a place of origin from Deiba.

EDCS-53300018.translation: They established it. Tanginus Elarci made it.
EDCS-53300018.summary: This inscription records that Tanginus Elarci made a monument that was established by others.

EDCS-53300019.translation: Sepulchral altar for Abilenigus, son of Tali, of the Caraici.
EDCS-53300019.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a sepulchral altar for Abilenigus, son of Tali, belonging to the Caraici people.

EDCS-53300020.translation: Valeria Prisca had this made for her husband Modestinus, son of Modestus, and for Modestus, son of Modestinus, her brother-in-law.
EDCS-53300020.summary: This funerary inscription records that Valeria Prisca commissioned a monument for her husband Modestinus and his brother Modestus.

EDCS-53300292.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that is too damaged to determine its purpose or date.

EDCS-53300293.translation: Atiusa arranged for this to be made at her own expense for her patroness.
EDCS-53300293.summary: This inscription records an act of devotion or gratitude by Atiusa towards her patroness, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-54000023.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly recording individuals involved in a dedication or a burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400579.translation: Publius Popilius Avitus, son of Publius, by the indulgence of the pontiff of the Igaeditani, received a burial place before the temple of the great goddess Cybele, whom I felt was angry with me through death.
EDCS-55400579.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Popilius Avitus receiving a burial plot through the favor of local priests, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400977.translation: Sacred to Bellona. Marcus Petronius Severus.
EDCS-55400977.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Bellona by Marcus Petronius Severus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400978.translation: Lucius Norbanus Maternus, son of Lucius...
EDCS-55400978.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name and parentage of an individual, likely a funerary text from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400982.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Flavius, aged thirty-two years. Both father and mother are buried here.
EDCS-55400982.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Flavius, aged 32, and also his parents, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400983.translation: Lucretia Doqira, daughter of Quintus Lucretius Tanginus, is buried here.
EDCS-55400983.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucretia Doqira, daughter of Quintus Lucretius Tanginus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400984.translation: To Coelia, daughter of Turobius, aged twenty years.
EDCS-55400984.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Coelia, daughter of Turobius, at the age of twenty, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55400988.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to determine its exact nature, but it likely records the name of a person, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701668.translation: Fuscus, son of Severus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701668.summary: This is a dedication by Fuscus, son of Severus, fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-55701669.translation: Casina fulfilled her vow to the god Nituceus.
EDCS-55701669.summary: This is a dedication by Casina to the god Nituceus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-55701670.translation: For the tribal solidarity of the Ntobii, Avelius Anderci willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Arantius Niaetrius.
EDCS-55701670.summary: This is a dedication by Avelius Anderci, related to the tribal solidarity of the Ntobii, with a vow made to Arantius Niaetrius.

EDCS-55701671.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide any meaningful information.

EDCS-55701672.translation: Areina, daughter of Dobiterus, of Arcaicum.
EDCS-55701672.summary: This inscription identifies Areina, daughter of Dobiterus, and associates her with Arcaicum.

EDCS-55701673.translation: Verecundus, aged 50, lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-55701673.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Verecundus, aged 50, with a common epitaph wishing him peace in death.

EDCS-55701674.translation: Annius Priscianus, aged 25, at his own expense; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-55701674.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Annius Priscianus, aged 25, paid for by himself, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-55701675.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Alli, aged 60, lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-55701675.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Alli, aged 60, dedicated to the Manes, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-55701676.translation: Flavinus, son of Flavius, aged 55, lies here; may the earth rest lightly.
EDCS-55701676.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Flavinus, son of Flavius, aged 55, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-55701677.translation: Boutia, daughter of Medugeni, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-55701677.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Boutia, daughter of Medugeni, aged 50.

EDCS-55701678.translation: Balaesus, son of Turai, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-55701678.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Balaesus, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-55701679.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Urbana, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caleco, aged 22. May the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-55701679.summary: This funerary inscription records the burials of Urbana and Caleco, with epitaphs wishing them well in the afterlife.

EDCS-55701680.translation: Condisa, daughter of Carus, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-55701680.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Condisa, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-55701681.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flaccilla, aged 70.
EDCS-55701681.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flaccilla, possibly dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-55701682.translation: Legiri, son of Magilo, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-55701682.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Legiri, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-55701684.translation: Titus Duatius Bovi, son of Raternum, freely and deservedly paid his vow to Caesariciae.
EDCS-55701684.summary: This votive inscription records Titus Duatius Bovi fulfilling a vow to the deity Caesariciae.

EDCS-55701702.translation: Ser Atai, with a willing and happy spirit, freely and deservedly paid his vow to the god Mentoviacus.
EDCS-55701702.summary: This votive inscription records Ser Atai fulfilling a vow to the god Mentoviacus.

EDCS-55702049.summary: This is a dedication inscription, likely for a monument or object, made by Tagus Ocus.

EDCS-55702050.translation: Polturi Caenonis, with a willing spirit, freely paid his vow to Asidia, of the gentile Polturicii.
EDCS-55702050.summary: This votive inscription records Polturi Caenonis fulfilling a vow to the goddess Asidia.

EDCS-55702051.translation: Lucius Attius Vegetus freely paid his vow to Asidia Poltucea.
EDCS-55702051.summary: This votive inscription records Lucius Attius Vegetus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Asidia Poltucea.

EDCS-55702058.translation: To the goddess Ataecina, Tur [erected this].
EDCS-55702058.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Ataecina, likely erected by a person named Tur.

EDCS-55702060.translation: Severa, daughter of Rufus, aged 12, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-55702060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severa, recording her age and her father's name, and includes a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-55702061.translation: Reburrus, son of Dobiterus, and Amoena, daughter of Calus.
EDCS-55702061.summary: This inscription appears to list names, possibly of family members or individuals associated with a monument, with patronymics.

EDCS-58800001.summary: This inscription seems to be a fragmented name, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-58800007.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a freedman named Novellus, indicating his former owner's name is partially preserved.

EDCS-58800020.translation: For the safety and return of Gaius Silius Celsus, Arruntia Clara fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-58800020.summary: This votive inscription records Arruntia Clara fulfilling a vow to Jupiter for the well-being and safe return of Gaius Silius Celsus.

EDCS-58800022.summary: This inscription records a woman's name and her father's name (patronymic).

EDCS-58800026.translation: Sulla, son of Proculus, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-58800026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulla, recording his age and father's name, with a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-58800029.translation: Valutius to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-58800029.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary dedication from a father, Valutius, to his son.

EDCS-59500349.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Silvanus Mantaius, possibly to a female deity or person named Eburia.

EDCS-59500353.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of a person, Marcus Livius Cilus, possibly on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-59500357.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Anna erected this for her most devoted parents, Claudius Gracchus, soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, and Julia Alba, his mother. He lies here.
EDCS-59500357.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Aurelia Anna for her parents, Claudius Gracchus, a soldier, and Julia Alba.

EDCS-60300050.summary: This inscription simply records the name Quintus Metellus, likely as an owner or a person associated with a monument.

EDCS-60300065.translation: Cinilius Mamus Cienus placed this from his own funds, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-60300065.summary: This dedication records Cinilius Mamus Cienus fulfilling a vow with his own resources for an unspecified monument.

EDCS-60300067.summary: This inscription appears to record two personal names, Bandi and Brialeaco, possibly as dedicators or honorees.

EDCS-60300072.translation: Vernaculus made [this] with roof tiles for Abelterius at the shrine of Castor, 2950 [paces/units].
EDCS-60300072.summary: This inscription records the construction of something using roof tiles by Vernaculus for Abelterius at the shrine of Castor, with a measurement indicated.

EDCS-60300073.translation: Caburius, son of Tanginus, donated the token/badge from his own funds to the people who gather at Artica Caput.
EDCS-60300073.summary: This inscription records a donation by Caburius, son of Tanginus, of a token to a group associated with Artica Caput.

EDCS-60300074.translation: To Marcus Junius Gallus, twice chief magistrate, Turrania Cilea [dedicated this] for her son-in-law.
EDCS-60300074.summary: This dedication records Turrania Cilea honoring her son-in-law, Marcus Junius Gallus, who held the office of chief magistrate.

EDCS-60300276.translation: Cloutius, son of Celtius, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-60300276.summary: This inscription records Cloutius, son of Celtius, willingly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-60300277.translation: Maximinus, son of Maximus, aged 60, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. His sons arranged for this.
EDCS-60300277.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Maximinus, son of Maximus, aged 60, and notes that his sons arranged for his burial.

EDCS-60300278.translation: The Amallobricenses give this marker to Pecunus, son of Ael.
EDCS-60300278.summary: This is a dedication by the Amallobricenses to a person named Pecunus, likely a local deity or important figure.

EDCS-60700039.translation: To Caenon, son of Pintilus, aged 80, from Maila.
EDCS-60700039.summary: This is a funerary monument for Caenon, son of Pintilus, with a dedication by Maila.

EDCS-60800089.translation: Potitus, son of Reburrus, willingly paid his vow for Lumbis.
EDCS-60800089.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription by Potitus, son of Reburrus, fulfilling a vow to Lumbis, likely a deity.

EDCS-61000048.translation: Flavius Theodosius, perpetual Augustus, our lord.
EDCS-61000048.summary: This is an imperial inscription dating to the reign of Theodosius I (379-395 CE).

EDCS-62900005.translation: He is laid here, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly upon you; his son arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-62900005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man aged 60, with the inscription arranged by his son.

EDCS-63000002.translation: Tancinus' son, aged 60, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you; his son arranged this.
EDCS-63000002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the son of Tancinus, aged 60, with the inscription arranged by his son.

EDCS-63000005.translation: Doquirus' son, with a willing heart, [fulfilled] his vow to Roudaeco.
EDCS-63000005.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription by Doquirus' son to Roudaeco, likely a local deity.

EDCS-63000006.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription mentioning a person named Nurus, who paid a vow.

EDCS-63000007.translation: Velsinia, daughter of Lucius C., with a willing heart paid her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-63000007.summary: This is an ex-voto inscription by Velsinia, daughter of Lucius C., dedicating her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-63000009.translation: Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Anius, is laid here.
EDCS-63000009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maxuma, daughter of Anius.

EDCS-63000010.translation: Gaius Julius Corcorus, freedman of Gaius, is buried here.
EDCS-63000010.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Corcorus, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000016.translation: Lucius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-63000016.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Julius Rufus, recording his age and a common funerary wish, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63000017.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Fabius Omullus, likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-63000018.translation: Munatia Mansueta gladly fulfilled her vow to Bellona.
EDCS-63000018.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Bellona by Munatia Mansueta.

EDCS-63000019.translation: Aninia Paula, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Her mother, Cavia, saw to the construction.
EDCS-63000019.summary: This is a tombstone for Aninia Paula, recording her age and stating that her mother, Cavia, arranged the burial.

EDCS-63000020.translation: Caeno, son of Caucirus, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Valeria, his daughter, saw to the construction.
EDCS-63000020.summary: This is a tombstone for Caeno, son of Caucirus, with his daughter Valeria arranging the burial and a funerary wish.

EDCS-63000140.summary: This inscription records the name Casianus, son of Papirius, likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-63000141.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucretia.
EDCS-63000141.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus by Lucretia.

EDCS-63000143.translation: His daughter saw to the construction. He is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Cilina, his daughter, saw to the construction.
EDCS-63000143.summary: This tombstone indicates that the deceased's daughter arranged the burial, with a repetition of the phrase 'his daughter saw to the construction' and a funerary wish.

EDCS-63000157.translation: Sunua, daughter of Lubaecus, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Her sons saw to the construction.
EDCS-63000157.summary: This is a tombstone for Sunua, daughter of Lubaecus, recording her age and stating that her sons arranged the burial, along with a funerary wish.

EDCS-63000158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Materna, aged one year, is buried here.
EDCS-63000158.summary: This is a tombstone for a one-year-old girl named Iulia Materna.

EDCS-63000159.translation: Iulia Fania, aged seventy years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her son M. had this monument made.
EDCS-63000159.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Iulia Fania, aged seventy, and notes that her son commissioned the monument.

EDCS-63000160.summary: This inscription records Ianuarius fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-63000161.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tinus Ensis, a freedman.

EDCS-63000162.summary: This inscription identifies Myrtale as the freedwoman of Clovatius Marci.

EDCS-63000163.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, likely for a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-63000164.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Manius Ulpinius.

EDCS-63000165.translation: Here lies [---], aged 80 years. May the earth lie lightly on you. Macana had this monument made.
EDCS-63000165.summary: This tombstone records an eighty-year-old individual, with Macana commissioning the monument.

EDCS-63000168.translation: Quintus Cornelius Montanus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, aged sixty-one, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-63000168.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Cornelius Montanus, son of Quintus, a member of the Papiria tribe, aged sixty-one.

EDCS-63000169.summary: This is a naming inscription, likely for a tombstone or dedication, for Loreia Laeta.

EDCS-63000170.translation: Cancilius Modestus gave this from his own property.
EDCS-63000170.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Cancilius Modestus, likely for a public building or project.

EDCS-63000171.translation: To C. Mtar Rufus, Lucius Norbanus.
EDCS-63000171.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a dedication made by Lucius Norbanus to C. Mtar Rufus.

EDCS-63100017.translation: Caenicus, son of Toncus, aged 90, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you, Anua.
EDCS-63100017.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caenicus, son of Toncus, who died at the age of 90, with a wish for a light earth and a mention of Anua, likely the wife or mother.

EDCS-63100021.translation: Visalus Primus and his wife Opilia are here.
EDCS-63100021.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Visalus Primus and his wife Opilia.

EDCS-63100025.translation: Tanginus, son of Dovonis, aged 6, was killed; he lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you; his mother saw to its construction.
EDCS-63100025.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Tanginus, son of Dovonis, at the age of 6, with a wish for a light earth and the mention that his mother commissioned the monument.

EDCS-63600346.translation: Quintus Caecilius Maternus, son of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-63600346.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Caecilius Maternus, son of Caius.

EDCS-63600349.translation: To the departed spirits, for Marcella, his mother.
EDCS-63600349.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) for Marcella, identified as the mother of the dedicator.

EDCS-63600350.translation: Marcus Porcius Cato, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus.
EDCS-63600350.summary: This inscription records the name and ancestry of Marcus Porcius Cato, likely for a funerary or commemorative purpose.

EDCS-63600356.translation: To the departed spirits. For M. Varro, quaestor, who after his thirty-third birthday, when he placed burning coals on a hanging brazier, by fate's command, killed himself, and for himself. His son L. Varro and Fulvia Aelia to their most dutiful son and for themselves made and set up this monument.
EDCS-63600356.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for M. Varro, a quaestor, who committed suicide after his 33rd birthday, commissioned by his son and wife.

EDCS-63600357.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quinti and Lucretia, daughters of Quintus and Lucius respectively, had this made for their son Galeria Nepos, son of Lucius Lucretius.
EDCS-63600357.summary: Funerary monument commissioned by parents for their deceased son, Lucius Lucretius Nepos.

EDCS-63600359.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucretia Patricia, aged 39, placed this tombstone herself while alive.
EDCS-63600359.summary: Funerary epitaph of Lucretia Patricia, who dedicated her own tombstone.

EDCS-63600360.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Licinia Helene, aged 40, lies here.

EDCS-63600361.translation: Gaius Asinius Marcellus, son of Marcus, consul.
EDCS-63600361.summary: Fragmentary inscription identifying Gaius Asinius Marcellus, son of Marcus, as consul.

EDCS-63600362.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Min[---]
EDCS-63600362.summary: Fragmentary funerary inscription, likely beginning the name of the deceased.

EDCS-63600363.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Terentia Iulia had this made for her most devoted father, Gaius Julius Primitivus, aged 60. He lies here.
EDCS-63600363.summary: Funerary monument commissioned by a daughter for her deceased father, Gaius Julius Primitivus.

EDCS-63600364.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Paterna, mother, placed this altar for her most devoted daughter, Iulia, daughter of Marcus, aged 27. She lies here.
EDCS-63600364.summary: Funerary monument with an altar dedicated by a mother for her deceased daughter, Iulia.

EDCS-64100255.translation: Corneliana, mother, had this made for IN [---], aged 19. He lies here.
EDCS-64100255.summary: Funerary monument commissioned by a mother for her deceased son, identified by initials.

EDCS-64100256.summary: Fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Cornelius Cornelianus.

EDCS-64100257.summary: Funerary epitaph for Cassianus Casi Maelo.

EDCS-64600329.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lutatia Tertia, daughter [of Lutatius], is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-64600329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lutatia Tertia, a woman whose father might have been Lutatius.

EDCS-64700304.summary: This fragment appears to be the beginning of a funerary epitaph, likely for a man named Gaius Iulius Bro[ntus?].

EDCS-64900650.translation: [He/She] paid willingly from a good heart.
EDCS-64900650.summary: This fragment records a willing payment, possibly related to a vow or debt.

EDCS-64900655.translation: Lancius Tancinus, called Boucarus, son of Duatus, [erected this] from the will of Lancius.
EDCS-64900655.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Lancius Tancinus, nicknamed Boucarus, was commemorated according to the will of Lancius.

EDCS-65700888.translation: Boutia, daughter of Acastus. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-65700888.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Boutia, daughter of Acastus.

EDCS-65700889.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, naming a woman named Bocea, daughter of Madui.

EDCS-65700891.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Successor, son of Marcus, indicating his age is missing.

EDCS-66000008.translation: [Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus, son of Pompeius Magnus, and Sextus Pompeius, son of Magnus, grandson of Pompeius Magnus, grandson of Pompeius]
EDCS-66000008.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely related to the Pompey family, detailing lineage.

EDCS-66000020.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aemilius Statutus of Ambirodacus. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Statutus from Ambirodacus.

EDCS-66000022.translation: Alla, daughter of M. Arcon, aged 57, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Maternus and Ocia had this made.
EDCS-66000022.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Alla, daughter of M. Arcon, who was 57 years old, and was commissioned by Maternus and Ocia.

EDCS-66000029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Annius Placidus had this made for Iulius, the veteran.
EDCS-66000029.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius, a veteran, by Lucius Annius Placidus.

EDCS-66000031.translation: At the age of 6 years, 15 months. To my sweetest son.
EDCS-66000031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, recording their age at death.

EDCS-66000038.translation: Ausus, [of the] ala, is buried here.
EDCS-66000038.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Ausus, who served in an ala (cavalry unit).

EDCS-66300066.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mercury by Norbana Turina.

EDCS-66300068.translation: Sacred to the Good Gods. Cinteri and Muno; Gaius P. Marcinus with his son Gaius P. Marcellus Crescens.
EDCS-66300068.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Good Gods made by Cinteri, Muno, Gaius P. Marcinus, and his son Gaius P. Marcellus Crescens.

EDCS-66800070.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Petrus as a bishop.

EDCS-66800074.translation: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, I, your bishop Hildefonsus.
EDCS-66800074.summary: This is an introductory phrase by Hildefonsus, identifying himself as a bishop, likely at the beginning of a larger text or document.

EDCS-66800081.translation: If [it] was made holy by Ortygi, the father [of the] father, attending...
EDCS-66800081.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly related to a religious or familial dedication, mentioning Ortygi and a father figure.

EDCS-67200146.translation: To Dionysus the tanner. Eurus, Zephyrus, Boreas, Notus.
EDCS-67200146.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Dionysus, the tanner, and lists the names of the four winds.

EDCS-67400734.translation: To Bellona. Uru willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-67400734.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Bellona, by Uru.

EDCS-67400738.translation: Tritene, daughter of Licinus, of the Avonorum tribe, aged 10, is buried here. Her brothers Paterninus and Veniatus saw to its construction. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-67400738.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Tritene, dated by the context of other inscriptions from the region, with her brothers acting as dedicator.

EDCS-67400744.translation: Calia Caesi willingly fulfilled her vow to Liber.
EDCS-67400744.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Liber by Calia Caesi.

EDCS-67400747.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lutatia Tertia is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-67400747.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lutatia Tertia, dedicated to the Dis Manes.

EDCS-67400749.translation: Gaius, son of Celtius, expanded the boundaries for Tritiaecius.
EDCS-67400749.summary: This is a public or private inscription recording an expansion of boundaries by Gaius for Tritiaecius.

EDCS-67400750.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500071.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500073.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to 'our lords' Flavius Julius, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-67500084.translation: Marcus Valerius Ianuarius of the Quirina tribe, aged 60, from the expenses of his funeral, the neighborhood saw to its construction.
EDCS-67500084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Ianuarius, whose funeral expenses were covered by his neighborhood, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67500085.translation: Arantonius Tancinus, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-67500085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arantonius Tancinus.

EDCS-68300185.translation: Atilia Maxuma, daughter of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-68300185.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atilia Maxuma.

EDCS-68300186.translation: Licinia Boutia, daughter of Lucius, aged 25, is placed here.
EDCS-68300186.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Boutia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68300187.translation: Gaius Licinius Cid, son of Caius.
EDCS-68300187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Licinius Cid, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68300188.translation: Atilius Aper, son of Lucius, aged 15, is placed here.
EDCS-68300188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilius Aper, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68300189.translation: Marcus Statius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, is placed here.
EDCS-68300189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Statius Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68300250.translation: Gaius Terentius Celer and his mother Deccia Ulana have seen to it that this was made at their own expense for Quintus Terentius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Tanginus.
EDCS-68300250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a dedicatory clause, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68600113.translation: Threption, his most dutiful and most missed wife.
EDCS-68600113.summary: This is a funerary inscription praising a deceased wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68600127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Talavus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68700214.translation: Caepo Firmus, freedman of Sabina, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-68700214.summary: This is a votive inscription by a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68700215.translation: Allonius of Clunia, aged 70, is placed here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-68700215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a common Latin phrase wishing peace to the deceased, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68700217.translation: Amoena, daughter of Publius, aged 70, is placed here. May the earth be light upon you. Gaius Vibius Catullus, son of Lucius, arranged for this to be made for his mother.
EDCS-68700217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, indicating the son arranged for his mother's burial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69100246.translation: To Iasius, aged 15. Calliope, his mother, had this made for her most devoted son.
EDCS-69100246.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating a 15-year-old boy named Iasius, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-69100254.translation: Nothus sets this up to the All-Good, All-Great God, in fulfillment of a vow to Aranton.
EDCS-69100254.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Nothus, possibly honoring Aranton.

EDCS-69100256.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Atellius Clemens (dedicated this to) Tangina.
EDCS-69100256.summary: A funerary dedication to the underworld deities, possibly by Atellius Clemens for Tangina.

EDCS-69100257.translation: The burial of Bardascius, who lived 20 years, departed in peace on the 17th day before the Kalends of September in the year 522 (of the local era).
EDCS-69100257.summary: A Christian funerary epitaph recording the death of Bardascius in 522 AD (local era).

EDCS-69100258.translation: Redemptus, servant of God, lived 11 years, rested in the Lord on the 3rd Ides of June in the year 604 (of the local era).
EDCS-69100258.summary: A Christian funerary epitaph recording the death of Redemptus in 604 AD (local era).

EDCS-69100260.translation: Here lies Filus. May the earth be light upon you. Filus had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-69100260.summary: A tombstone inscription for Filus, who arranged for his own burial monument.

EDCS-69100266.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly indicating ownership or a maker's mark for bricks.

EDCS-69100268.translation: Gemelus, son of Gemelinus, aged 30, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. His mother had this made for her best son.
EDCS-69100268.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 30-year-old man named Gemelus, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-69200100.translation: Arantonius Pitalni, son of Tedaicis, gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Arentia and Arentius.
EDCS-69200100.summary: A votive inscription by Arantonius Pitalni, fulfilling a vow made to Arentia and Arentius.

EDCS-69400013.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Propertius Hierio, aged 23, Propertia Maura, aged 11, and Propertius Africanus had this made for their freedpeople.
EDCS-69400013.summary: A funerary dedication to the underworld deities by Propertius Hierio, Propertia Maura, and Propertius Africanus for their freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-69500483.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Bellona.

EDCS-69500484.translation: Gaius Alfius Maxumus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Alfia Clara, his sister, saw to its construction.
EDCS-69500484.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Alfius Maxumus, with his sister Alfia Clara as the commissioner.

EDCS-69500487.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you. His son saw to its construction.
EDCS-69500487.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely a fragment, stating a son commissioned the monument.

EDCS-69500488.translation: Tancinus, son of Caerus, aged 53, is buried here. Maliquius and Maternus, his heirs, saw to its construction.
EDCS-69500488.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tancinus, son of Caerus, with his heirs Maliquius and Maternus as commissioners.

EDCS-69800175.translation: To Phoebus and Diana, the people of Olisipo, for the safety and eternity of the Roman Empire, for the life and happiness of the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, Octavianus, pious, fortunate, victorious, Germanicus, Dacicus, Alexandrinus, Cestus Accidius, his permanent legatus pro praetore of the province of Lusitania, dedicated. With the decurions of Olisipo standing before them, the communities that aided this work, namely the municipality of Olisipo, the municipality of Salacia, the town of Mirobriga, the town of Tubucci, the town of Eburobrittium, erected a statue before the doors of the temple for the people of Olisipo, father of the fatherland, for benefits to the municipality, and the flamens dedicated it.
EDCS-69800175.summary: A monumental dedication by the legatus pro praetore Cestus Accidius at Olisipo to Apollo and Diana, celebrating the Roman Empire and Emperor Augustus, with inscriptions listing contributing municipalities and towns.

EDCS-70200027.translation: Annianus Peregrinus, honorable overseer of two synagogues, lived 45 years, of whom his citizens and his friends bear good testimony. Oh, that you did not enjoy your age! May your spirit rest well.
EDCS-70200027.summary: A funerary epitaph for Annianus Peregrinus, an overseer of synagogues, whose good character is attested by his community.

EDCS-70200097.translation: Lucius N Metellus is buried here, aged [---]. May the earth lie lightly on you. His son saw to its construction.
EDCS-70200097.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius N. Metellus, with his son as the commissioner.

EDCS-70200099.translation: Flavia Tertia, daughter of Quintus, aged 20.
EDCS-70200099.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment for Flavia Tertia, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-70200185.translation: Licinia Lupa, aged 27, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-70200185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Lupa, aged 27.

EDCS-70600095.translation: Musa paid [her vow] to Ataecina Turebriga and Proserpina.
EDCS-70600095.summary: A votive offering by Musa to the goddesses Ataecina Turebriga and Proserpina.

EDCS-70800032.translation: To the divine spirits. Atilia Maxima, aged 20. Her mother, Lucius Curtius Atilianus, and Servatus had this made for their dear daughter.
EDCS-70800032.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages and names of a deceased woman, Atilia Maxima, and the individuals responsible for her monument, likely her mother and another relative.

EDCS-70800037.translation: Pisira, daughter of Caturonis, lies here.
EDCS-70800037.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription identifying the deceased as Pisira, daughter of Caturonis.

EDCS-70800038.translation: Mancus willingly paid his vow to Oniaece Riva.
EDCS-70800038.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Mancus to a deity or individual named Oniaece Riva.

EDCS-70900412.translation: Attia, daughter of Lucius, had this made for Turrania Placida, daughter of Marci, from Eburobrittes.
EDCS-70900412.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Attia commissioned a monument for Turrania Placida, who was from Eburobrittes.

EDCS-70900416.summary: This is a tomb inscription identifying the deceased as Quintus Caecilius Segontius.

EDCS-71100011.translation: Ambata Ventoria, daughter of Lucius, and Gaius Aius Sempronius had this made for their mother.
EDCS-71100011.summary: This funerary inscription records that Ambata Ventoria and Gaius Aius Sempronius commissioned a monument for their mother.

EDCS-71300074.translation: Boucatus paid for the monument for Rufo, son of Equipes, from Limico castle, belonging to Elapiiocelos.
EDCS-71300074.summary: This inscription records that Boucatus paid for a monument for Rufo, identifying his parentage and place of origin associated with Elapiiocelos.

EDCS-72500145.summary: This is a brief inscription stating that Ma Ferrus was the maker of the object or monument.

EDCS-72500146.summary: This inscription likely refers to a person named Placidi Canrucatus, possibly as the deceased or the dedicator.

EDCS-72500160.summary: This inscription names Primigeni Albus, possibly as the subject of the inscription (deceased or dedicator).

EDCS-72500180.summary: This is a personal name, likely a dedication or the subject of a tombstone.

EDCS-72500303.summary: This is a personal name, likely a dedication or the subject of a tombstone.

EDCS-72500305.translation: Decumus and Caecilia [placed here] for Eighty-five years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-72500305.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a person who lived to be 85, set up by Decumus and Caecilia.

EDCS-72500314.translation: Cornuia, daughter of Caius, thirty-five years old, buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her mother arranged for this to be made for the deceased.
EDCS-72500314.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornuia, aged 35, whose mother arranged for the monument.

EDCS-72600207.summary: This is a personal name, likely part of a dedication or tombstone inscription.

EDCS-72600415.translation: To ODA riae who lived for twenty-one years, two months, and eleven days. Postumus [erected this] for his most deserving wife, the first in beauty and excellence of youth; after her, a girl died who was like her. [She is] buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-72600415.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of ODA riae at age 21, set up by her husband Postumus.

EDCS-72700058.summary: This is a personal name, likely part of a dedication or tombstone inscription.

EDCS-72800135.translation: Nico, servant of the god, who lived for more or less 65 years, rested in peace in the year 566 of the era. Acelleus, servant of the god, who lived for 32 years, rested in peace in the year 542 of the era.
EDCS-72800135.summary: This inscription records the death of two servants of a god, Nico (aged c. 65) and Acelleus (aged 32), with dates in the era.

EDCS-72900240.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Remes, whose age is partially lost.

EDCS-72900241.translation: Rutilius fulfilled his vow for Marci Aproniani.
EDCS-72900241.summary: This inscription records Rutilius fulfilling a vow made on behalf of Marci Aproniani.

EDCS-73300086.translation: Maelo, son of Boutus, made this for Coracila, daughter of Boutinus.
EDCS-73300086.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Maelo for Coracila, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74000103.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Aurelius Maxi...
EDCS-74000103.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100210.translation: Apana, daughter of Caius, aged 70, and Tritea, daughter of Lubenus, aged...
EDCS-74100210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two women, Apana and Tritea, with their ages recorded, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100220.summary: This is an inscription beginning with the name Lucius Vinicius, its purpose is unclear due to incompleteness.

EDCS-74200158.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius and Ternio Ater, from Terr Mandva, are set up here. Mandva's son, who lived 22 years, is buried here.
EDCS-74200158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius and Ternio Ater, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200310.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vibia Thisbe made this for her dearest and most devoted son, Gaius Silus Traius, aged 11 years and 3 months, who deserved well.
EDCS-74200310.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Vibia Thisbe for her son, Gaius Silus Traius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200314.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Antonius.
EDCS-74200314.summary: This is a mark from the workshop of Gaius Antonius, likely related to a craft or trade during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200331.summary: This inscription records 'Aquae' and the name Aulus Plautius Decianus, possibly indicating a location or ownership, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200332.translation: Macer Obisoqum, son of Ambatus, from Toletum, aged 100, is buried here.
EDCS-74200332.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Macer Obisoqum, from Toletum, who lived to be 100 years old, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200341.translation: He fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-74200341.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Mars, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200350.translation: Celtius, son of Mitani, gladly paid his vow to Fontus.
EDCS-74200350.summary: This is a votive inscription by Celtius, son of Mitani, dedicating a vow to the deity Fontus.

EDCS-74200354.translation: Lucius Valerius Flavinus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-74200354.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Valerius Flavinus dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimo Maximo.

EDCS-74200359.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Limicus, son of Arcuicis, possibly on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-74200361.translation: Doquirus, son of Celtus, made a vow.
EDCS-74200361.summary: This is a votive inscription by Doquirus, son of Celtus, stating that he made a vow.

EDCS-74200364.translation: Firminus, son of Firmanus, aged 16, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-74200364.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Firminus, son of Firmanus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-74200370.translation: Flaus, freedman of Sura, gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-74200370.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flaus, a freedman of Sura, dedicating a vow.

EDCS-74200372.translation: Lucius Marcius Maternus, a veteran, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Father Liber and Libera.
EDCS-74200372.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Marcius Maternus, a veteran, dedicating a vow to Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-74200374.translation: Ruburrus, son of Tongetami, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-74200374.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ruburrus, son of Tongetami, dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimo Maximo.

EDCS-74200378.translation: The deputy (II vir), an Augustalis priest, erected this gift at his own expense and saw to its construction; he is 40 years old and is buried here.
EDCS-74200378.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 40-year-old official (II vir flamen Augustalis) who paid for and oversaw the construction of his own monument.

EDCS-74200379.translation: Titus Caecilius Gallus is laid here.
EDCS-74200379.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Titus Caecilius Gallus is buried here.

EDCS-74200396.translation: Norbanus, son of Lucius Epaseaus, aged 10, lies here. May the earth be light.
EDCS-74200396.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Norbanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200397.translation: Rufus, son of Peicanus, and Arantia Ocelaeca for Arantius Ocelaecus. With good will.
EDCS-74200397.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication from Rufus and Arantia Ocelaeca for Arantius Ocelaecus.

EDCS-74200398.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to a person named Bandeius.

EDCS-74200399.translation: Ilurbedae Avitianus, son of Avitus, willingly paid [his vow].
EDCS-74200399.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ilurbedae Avitianus, son of Avitus, who willingly paid a vow.

EDCS-74200400.translation: Gaius Claudius Maxsumus [paid] his vow to Issibaeus.
EDCS-74200400.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Claudius Maxsumus to a deity or person named Issibaeus.

EDCS-74200402.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tomb or object, possibly funerary.

EDCS-74200407.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Sunua, freedwoman, aged 55, the daughter, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-74200407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Iulia Sunua, a freedwoman, arranged for the monument, likely for herself or a relative.

EDCS-74200408.translation: Annia Maxsuma, daughter of Marcus, lies here.
EDCS-74200408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Maxsuma, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-74200424.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Her husband arranged for this to be made for Iulia Tongeta, aged 40.
EDCS-74200424.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where a husband commissioned the monument for his wife, Iulia Tongeta.

EDCS-74200425.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Her husband arranged for this to be made for Quinti Ocarii, aged 20.
EDCS-74200425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where a husband commissioned the monument for Quinti Ocarii, likely his wife.

EDCS-74200429.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Proculeia Elavia, aged 52. Proculeius Flav[ius?]
EDCS-74200429.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Proculeia Elavia, aged 52, and is likely set up by Proculeius Flavius.

EDCS-74200445.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Marcus Allius Balbus, aged 30. Avita, daughter of Marcus, his mother, took care to have this made. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-74200445.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Allius Balbus, aged 30, and was set up by his mother, Avita.

EDCS-74200458.translation: Marcus Clodius Avitus, son of Marcus, aged 25, lies buried here.
EDCS-74200458.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Clodius Avitus, aged 25.

EDCS-74200461.translation: Calaito, son of Cavelius, lies here.
EDCS-74200461.summary: This very brief inscription records the burial of Calaito, son of Cavelius.

EDCS-74200468.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Varinia Serena made this according to her will. Lucretia, aged 32, her most excellent mother, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-74200468.summary: This funerary inscription states that Varinia Serena made the monument according to her will, and records the death of her mother, Lucretia, aged 32.

EDCS-74200527.translation: For Gaius Julius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-74200527.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Julius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-74200622.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Ori, son of Spurius, [---] O O, lies buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-74200622.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Ori, son of Spurius.

EDCS-74200627.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark, for Quintus, son of Frontonis Arginus.

EDCS-74200639.translation: Aecandus, son of Bovi[us?], lies buried here.
EDCS-74200639.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Aecandus, son of Bovi[us?].

EDCS-74200641.translation: Albicia Helena, freedwoman of Caius, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74200641.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Helena, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-74300005.translation: In this tomb rests the body of Lucrosus, deceased, who lived 78 years and rested in peace on the Ides of August, 201 CE; his wife Germanella, aged 60, died in peace on the third day before the Kalends of February, 207 CE.
EDCS-74300005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Lucrosus and Germanella, with precise dates given in both consular and era dating, placing it in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-74300020.translation: To the spirits of the Manes for Deuter Eleutherus, aged 22, most dutiful son, and for himself, he took care to have this made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74300020.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by a father for his deceased son, Deuter Eleutherus, and possibly for himself as well, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-74300023.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a personal dedication or a playful message, with unclear context but suggesting a personal connection to Alypius.

EDCS-74300024.summary: This is a simple epitaph or ownership mark identifying Cesea as the daughter of Celtus.

EDCS-74300027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the Manes, for Iulia Rufina, aged 2.
EDCS-74300027.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the Manes for a young child named Iulia Rufina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-74300030.translation: To the spirits of the Manes. Licinia Amanda, mother, here lie (with) her son Maurinus.
EDCS-74300030.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the spirits of the Manes and records that Licinia Amanda, the mother, is buried with her son Maurinus.

EDCS-74300034.translation: Atia Severa, daughter of Caius, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Herennius Elianus placed this for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-74300034.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Atia Severa, aged 35, and was erected by her husband Marcus Herennius Elianus.

EDCS-74300036.translation: Silo, son of Angeitus, of Maguacum, the best, took care to have this made at his own expense. Marcus Valerius Silo, first duumvir.
EDCS-74300036.summary: This inscription records a public work or dedication by Silo, son of Angeitus, possibly related to his office as duumvir, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-74300038.summary: This is a maker's mark or business sign indicating the workshop of Sabinus.

EDCS-74300039.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cadius Carus and Valeria Rufina, parents, had this made for their excellent son, Lucius Cadio Cellae, aged 27.
EDCS-74300039.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Cadio Cellae, erected by his parents, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300042.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Celea Flavina, aged 70. A devoted daughter set this up for her mother, pious.
EDCS-74300042.summary: A funerary dedication for Celea Flavina, erected by her devoted daughter, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300043.translation: Claudius T. P. fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-74300043.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Claudius T. P., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300045.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabius Capitonis, aged 70, is buried here. Capito and Paterna, most devoted sons, had this made.
EDCS-74300045.summary: A funerary dedication for Fabius Capitonis, erected by his devoted sons, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300046.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeius Maternianus, aged 20, is buried here. Pompeius Albura had this made for his son.
EDCS-74300046.summary: A funerary dedication for Pompeius Maternianus, erected by his father, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300048.translation: Proculus set up [this monument] for Corunius, aged 85.
EDCS-74300048.summary: A funerary dedication for Corunius, aged 85, erected by Proculus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300376.translation: Celerinus, aged 18, and Celerina, aged 9. Celerinus the father and Avita the mother had this set up and made.
EDCS-74300376.summary: A funerary dedication for Celerinus (18) and Celerina (9), erected by their parents, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300385.translation: Honoria, daughter of Marci, is buried here.
EDCS-74300385.summary: A funerary dedication for Honoria, daughter of Marci, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300389.translation: [This monument] was made for son A.
EDCS-74300389.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication for an individual identified as 'son A', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300393.translation: is buried here, aged 24. Mother and brothers.
EDCS-74300393.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication for an individual aged 24, indicating erection by their mother and brothers, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74300428.translation: Celsus, son of Saerus, a priest.
EDCS-74300428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celsus, son of Saerus, who held the title of priest.

EDCS-74300429.translation: Tito Boutius, son of Segontius, of Clunia, aged 11, is buried here.
EDCS-74300429.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tito Boutius, an 11-year-old boy from Clunia.

EDCS-74600171.translation: Vonconer, son of Proculus, of Eniraces.
EDCS-74600171.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Vonconer, son of Proculus, and his origin from Eniraces.

EDCS-74600174.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Gaius Longinus Aegyptius, from Aegyptus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-74600174.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Gaius Longinus Aegyptius, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-75200370.translation: Norbanus Seneco, son of Quintus, aged 35, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-75200370.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Norbanus Seneco, aged 35, with a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-75300078.translation: Orina, daughter of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-75300078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Orina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-75900037.translation: Avitus fulfilled his vow to Madurus Cusus Erulancus.
EDCS-75900037.summary: This is a votive inscription where Avitus fulfilled a vow to Madurus Cusus Erulancus.

EDCS-75900452.translation: I, Maternianus, vowed to the holy Reo.
EDCS-75900452.summary: This is a votive inscription where Maternianus declares he made a vow to a deity named Reo.

EDCS-75900458.summary: This inscription indicates that an object or place originated from the workshop of Sabina.

EDCS-75900459.summary: This inscription indicates that an object or place originated from the workshop of Sabina.

EDCS-75900468.translation: Reve Vadumico, son of Nigrinus, son of Nigri, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-75900468.summary: This is a votive inscription by Reve Vadumico, son of Nigrinus, son of Nigri, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-76600234.translation: Ata, daughter of Quinti, aged 25 years, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-76600234.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Ata, daughter of Quinti, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-77100182.translation: Formica, servant of the deity, lived 45 years and rested in peace on the Kalends of May in the 603rd year of the era.
EDCS-77100182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Formica, a servant of a deity, who died on May 1st in the year 603 of the era (likely AUC).

EDCS-77400117.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Procilla, daughter of Camulus, fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-77400117.summary: This is a votive inscription by Procilla, daughter of Camulus, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-77700122.translation: Tanginus sent a gift to Barbario Parthenopaeus, fully from Baucis.
EDCS-77700122.summary: This inscription records a gift sent by Tanginus to Barbario Parthenopaeus, originating from Baucis.

EDCS-77800127.translation: To the goddess Ulia, O M, Chylia Gemina.
EDCS-77800127.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Ulia by Chylia Gemina, likely for the well-being of herself or others (O M likely stands for Optimus Maximus or similar).

EDCS-78200079.translation: Vegetus, son of Mundio, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Solutor.
EDCS-78200079.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vegetus, son of Mundio, dedicated to Jupiter Solutor.

EDCS-78200085.summary: This inscription, possibly a tombstone or memorial, records Terentius Pito and mentions 'friends' and the number 85 (possibly an age or date).

EDCS-78200089.translation: A token for Atanius Dentoni, son.
EDCS-78200089.summary: This inscription appears to be a token or dedication for Atanius Dentoni, son of someone.

EDCS-79700004.translation: Albonius, son of Angeitus, is laid here.
EDCS-79700004.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Albonius, son of Angeitus, is buried here.

EDCS-80100012.translation: Arenus Live willingly placed this altar.
EDCS-80100012.summary: This dedication was placed by Arenus Live, likely a private individual, on an altar.

EDCS-80200124.summary: This inscription identifies Tiro, son of F., as a pontifex, possibly part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-80300112.translation: To the departed spirits. Fabricius Tuscus the Second set this up for his mother Iunia Marcella, the best of mothers.
EDCS-80300112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Fabricius Tuscus II for his mother, Iunia Marcella, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-80600001.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Quirinius Rufus, a soldier of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, lived 42 years. Antonius placed this monument for him, most dear; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-80600001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Quirinius Rufus, a soldier who served under Marcus Aurelius, erected by Antonius, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-80700042.translation: He who asserted the victory in the Celtiberian War, in honor of his own name, fortified the city of Ebora with strong troops and saw to it that water from different sources was brought together into one conduit and led to it.
EDCS-80700042.summary: This honorary inscription commemorates a military leader who fortified the city of Ebora and improved its water supply, likely dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-80700058.translation: Cassius Tuscus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-80700058.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying the deceased as Cassius Tuscus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80900072.translation: Here lies Theodoros, son of Megas, from the ship of Agathon.
EDCS-80900072.summary: This Greek epitaph records the burial of Theodoros, son of Megas, identified as being from the ship of Agathon, likely a sailor or traveler.

EDCS-80900074.summary: This inscription identifies Coreta as the slave of Virgiliani, likely part of a funerary monument or ownership marker.

EDCS-80900079.summary: This is a brief dedication or maker's mark, indicating that 'Senes' created the object or monument.

EDCS-81000045.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Julius Avitus and Annia Reburrina, father and mother, saw to the construction for Julius Vegetus, aged 5 years, and Quintus Julius Avitus, aged 25 years.
EDCS-81000045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by parents for their two sons, one a child and the other a young adult, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81200028.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars by Lucius Cocceius Vi, likely on a monument or altar.

EDCS-81200029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Dioscorus erected the monument.

EDCS-81200033.translation: To Avitana Uricum, he willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god.
EDCS-81200033.summary: This is a votive inscription to the deity Avitana Uricum, expressing the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-81200132.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, he willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-81200132.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-81200140.translation: To the Divine Manes, AVI A M VRIC VM had this made at his own expense; he lived 50 years.
EDCS-81200140.summary: This is a funerary inscription for AVI A M VRIC VM, who commissioned his own tomb and lived for 50 years.

EDCS-81200155.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Iullius.
EDCS-81200155.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, honoring Iullius.

EDCS-81500106.translation: Lancea, daughter of Tai, aged 20; Cato Apri, son of Aprius, aged 50; Cilea, daughter of Triteius, had this made in life for themselves or their family.
EDCS-81500106.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages and familial relationships of three individuals who commissioned their own burial monument.

EDCS-81600014.translation: Virius Agricola, procurator of the Emperors, with his wife Statilia Iuliane, daughter of Caius, and their children, [dedicated this] to Sol and Oceanus.
EDCS-81600014.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Sol and Oceanus made by Virius Agricola, an imperial procurator, along with his wife and children.

EDCS-81600017.translation: Lucius Aelius Paulus, an outstanding man, procurator of the province of Lusitania, [dedicated this] to the Unconquered Sun for the safety of himself and his family.
EDCS-81600017.summary: This is a dedication to the Unconquered Sun by Lucius Aelius Paulus, an outstanding man and procurator of Lusitania, for the welfare of his family.

EDCS-81600018.translation: To Sol Occiduus and Oceanus the Father.
EDCS-81600018.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Sol Occiduus and Oceanus Pater.

EDCS-81600024.translation: Lupus Casius from Arcobriga gladly fulfilled his vow to the Best and Greatest God Salamates.
EDCS-81600024.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lupus Casius, a man from Arcobriga, dedicated to the god Salamates.

EDCS-81600025.translation: He is buried here, son of Elguenus, aged 60. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fil
EDCS-81600025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man identified as the son of Elguenus, aged 60.

EDCS-81600027.translation: Tancinus, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Gaius Rufus, son of ?, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. He had this made.
EDCS-81600027.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Tancinus and Gaius Rufus, with one of them having the monument commissioned.

EDCS-81800001.translation: Celtius, son of Maelo, is buried here.
EDCS-81800001.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Celtius, son of Maelo.

EDCS-81900042.translation: For the health of Avilia Antestia.
EDCS-81900042.summary: This is an inscription recording a prayer or dedication for the well-being of Avilia Antestia.

EDCS-82000028.translation: To the divine spirits of Flavius Ofelma.
EDCS-82000028.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the divine spirits of Flavius Ofelma.

EDCS-82300012.translation: To the divine spirits. Reburina, daughter of Reburus, aged 11, is buried here. Verna and Castor, freedmen, had this made according to the will.
EDCS-82300012.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a young girl, Reburina, and indicates that the monument was commissioned by her freedmen according to her will.

EDCS-82400121.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Iulius Peculiaris, aged 90, is buried here. Aemiliana made this for her excellent husband.
EDCS-82400121.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulius Peculiaris, aged 90, and states that the monument was erected by his wife Aemiliana.

EDCS-82400149.translation: Quintus Cassius Cassianus willingly erected this vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-82400149.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Cassius Cassianus dedicated to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
